properties.description: Over a period of four or five days, two massive flocks of starlings gathered over the city, one in the east and one in the west. On the 12 October, the two flocks took to the air, engaging in combat - covering the city ground with dead and wounded birds. Just before nightfall, both bird armies left the area, but returned for another major engagement and final battle on 14 October.
properties.location_name: Cork (County Cork) - Skies over the city

properties.name: Dog Walker
properties.description: Four friends camping on the beach decided to walk towards Corton in the early hours of the morning. Sitting down for a rest, two of the group spotted a man with a dog had suddenly appeared a short distance away. Man and dog stood completely motionless, looking out to sea. The two witnesses turned to their friends and tried to point out the man and dog, but they had vanished.

properties.name: Galloping Horses
properties.description: Walking along the lane, one man heard wheels moving along the road followed by the sound of galloping horses - the witness had to jump out of the way. Other people have reportedly heard farm animals in the area, although none are around.
properties.date: Unknown - late twentieth or early twenty-first century?
properties.location_name: Coventry (West Midlands) - Moat House Lane, Canley

properties.name: Unlucky Rabbit
properties.description: This white bunny is only seen just before a personal disaster strikes - the creature was the pet of a young girl killed by a couple of local thugs.

properties.name: Smoky Rabbit
properties.description: Several strange shades frequented the castle, including a rabbit that would turn into smoke, a small pixie-like bearded man, and heavy pacing.
properties.location_name: Cromarty (Highland) - Old castle (no longer standing, replaced by Cromarty House)

properties.name: Stealth Sheep
properties.description: Never appearing, this phantom flock can be heard moving along the roads, the occasional 'baaaa' betraying their position.

properties.name: WW2 Soldiers
properties.description: Soldiers dressed in Second World War uniform have been seen around the castle area, and the site is also said to be haunted by a horse and carriage. As a side note, Bram Stoker is said to have been partly inspired to write Dracula after seeing the castle while staying at Cruden Bay.

properties.name: White Owl
properties.description: An unnaturally large white owl took to hunting over an ancient battlefield, leading locals to believe the creature was magical and should not be interfered with. A visitor to Raglan castle who laughed at the story and killed the owl, himself died a few hours later, choking to death at a dinner table.

properties.name: Donkey
properties.description: This phantom white donkey appears at night.

properties.name: Hound Attack
properties.description: A night watchman reported being attacked by a man and his dog as they came out of the coal house - the watchman defended himself and tried to punch his attacker, only for his fist to pass through the figure and hit the wall behind. The pair of entities then went back into the coal house and vanished.
properties.location_name: Darlington (Durham) - Old Darlington and Stockton rail station

properties.name: Sow
properties.description: This phantom sow and her litter walk the area looking for food.

properties.name: David Davies
properties.description: Davies tended to the prison sheep between 1869 and 1929. He died soon after being released, but his ghost returned, walking the prison grounds on misty nights. The prison is also haunted by French PoWs from the Napoleonic wars, while some inmates believe jackdaws which fly around the area contain the souls of dead staff.

properties.name: Kitchen Horse
properties.description: The kitchen was built over the old stables, and the sound of a horse kicking a stable door is said to be occasionally heard. Horseshoes belonging to Shetland ponies have been found in the garden.

properties.name: Monk with White Face
properties.description: Seen by several people, this phantom monk was almost hit by a car before it vanished into the grounds. A light has also been reported floating around the courtyard, containing a strange face, while inside the hall itself a phantom cat and a woman in blue have been reported.

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A black dog haunted this stretch of road, although there have been no sightings for at least one hundred years.

properties.name: Coach & Four
properties.description: A phantom coach drawn by a team of four horses travel around this area. They are said to look quite solid, though is illuminated by an unknown source.

properties.name: Man with Dog
properties.description: It was said that this phantom man with his ghostly hound would walk quickly around the area.
properties.location_name: Drumcliff (County Clare) - Around the ruins of the round tower

properties.name: Monkey
properties.description: This yellow monkey has been seen several times, though only recognised as being a monkey recently. Two other ghosts have been seen here; Lady Anne Douglas walks carrying her severed head, while a little girl has been seen in a bedroom, floating a few feet off the floor.

properties.name: White Figure
properties.description: This pale phantom was described as wearing a white veil or having black patches around the head. One brave man touched the entity and went numb down his right side. A large black hound-like creature was also reported in the area, and a clanking chain heard.
properties.location_name: Dunwich (Suffolk) - Area around the 'red stile', between Dunwich and Heveningham, aka Devil's Field or Lane.

properties.name: Herds of Cows
properties.description: Herds of invisible grazing animals were once heard by locals moving along the roads in this area. The creatures are also said to be heard on the beach, where green pastures once lay.

properties.name: Unlucky Cat
properties.description: The family who lived here for six years reported a suppressive atmosphere and terrible luck. Their children reported a phantom cat, while the mother was too scared to enter certain rooms.

properties.name: Boy or Cat
properties.description: Local folklore said that the strange sounds reported in one room were created by the phantom of a boy flogged to death nearby. However, years later, the remains of a cat were discovered within the walls, and the sounds attributed to the frantic scrabbling of the creature before it died. Some will say that the poor cat was an apotropaic object; we'll never know for sure.

properties.name: Persian Cat
properties.description: This phantom feline haunted the original church prior to its destruction by German bombers during the Second World War. In one story, a small group rehearsing Christmas carols claimed to have seen a phantom woman sitting on a chair. The figure disappeared and was replaced by a black cat which also vanished soon after.
properties.location_name: EC3 (Greater London) - All Hallows by the Tower, Great Tower Street

properties.name: Bear
properties.description: After seeing a large bear approaching him, a guard tried to bayonet the creature; the weapon passed straight through the ghost, and it continued to move towards the soldier. The soldier collapsed and died a few days later.
properties.location_name: EC3 (Greater London) - Tower of London, Jewel House (now known as Martin Tower)

properties.name: Cat
properties.description: A phantom cat seen in this house was said to be the size of a mastiff hound, but it had no legs. The family were also subjected to a poltergeist outbreak, which opened doors, moved items around and lit candles.
properties.location_name: EC4 (Greater London) - House of Mr Edward Pitts, Puddle Dock

properties.name: Plague Victims
properties.description: A whole host of spectres haunt this area; women, children, old men and even animals have been reported haunting the site over the past 350 years. More recently, there have been reports of a ghostly little girl and an 'impish' young boy. The last occupant of the close was forced to move out in 1898 to make way for the City Chambers extension, which now stands over the area.
properties.location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Mary King's Close, currently buried under the City Chambers

properties.name: Shadowy Figures
properties.description: Several entities reportedly haunt this building, including cats, a dog, and possibly several people who were mistaken as 'real' by one witness.
properties.location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Private flat in Bruntsfield Place

properties.name: Thomas Holloway
properties.description: Founded by Thomas Holloway, the man himself is said to haunt the Founder's Building, though he manifests in the form of a black cat. Jane Holloway, Thomas's wife, is said to haunt the fourth floor of the same building.

properties.name: Jeffrey
properties.description: This building experienced phantom groaning, knocking, and invisible hands that pushed people around. Unlike most poltergeist cases, the entity also manifested in the form of a white figure and a headless badger. The presence left without warning.

properties.name: Plague Victims
properties.description: Several graves belonging to plague victims are thought to be haunted by their occupiers.

properties.name: Horse and Coach
properties.description: The sounds of a horse pulling a coach have been heard outside this inn, though never seen.

properties.name: Black Pig
properties.description: This porker is reputed to be the ghost of Judge Jeffreys, cursed to return in animal form after he died.

properties.name: Fast Moving Old Lady
properties.description: Limping, this OAP runs by witnesses at a rate of knots and disappears into the doorway of a house along the street. The same street is also haunted by a black dog which performs a similar action to that of the old woman.

properties.name: Final Bear
properties.description: This is said to be the location where the last wild bear in England was pointlessly killed; the creature's ghost now returns in protest.

properties.name: Headless Woman and Friends
properties.description: Locals once believed this area to be haunted by a headless woman, a woman on horseback and a couple of cats (one black, the other white).
properties.date: Likely to be pre twentieth century
properties.location_name: Fimber (North Yorkshire) - Site once known as Fimber Cross Road, where railway station once stood

properties.name: Distressed Horse
properties.description: Said to have been slaughtered in the abattoir, this horse is said to be heard every six months. There are also reports of the creature being heard galloping away from the area.

properties.name: Charlie Miles
properties.description: This old man tragically lost his son, and now walks the road with his dog, looking for new friends.
properties.location_name: Frimley Green (Surrey) - Old Guildford Road, and surrounding area

properties.name: Snuff Box
properties.description: The ghost of a farmer who committed suicide would manifest in the form of a calf. During an exorcism, the entity was trapped within a snuff box - the container was thrown into a pond, where ghost and box remain.
properties.location_name: Garnstone (Hereford & Worcester) - Unknown pond (may no longer exist) and Field's End

properties.name: Running Dog
properties.description: The small lane leading to the local churchyard is reputedly home to a phantom hound - the creature has been spotted running along the lane. It is thought that the location that the hound appears will soon be the site of a crime. A ghostly donkey seen here may be the same entity. The same area is also haunted by a haunted coach as it travels towards Bungay Castle.
properties.location_name: Geldeston (Norfolk) - Lover's Lane, leading to the churchyard

properties.name: Bull
properties.description: This lane is said to be haunted by a bull, containing the soul of the Jew who hanged himself on a tree along the road.

properties.name: Rose
properties.description: This shop is said to be haunted by a woman in black, named as Rose, who sits in a rocking chair with a ginger cat in her lap.
properties.location_name: Godshill (Isle of Wight) - The Bat's Wing Tea Shop

properties.name: Pistol Shots
properties.description: This was (briefly) the childhood home to E D Walker, a former mayor of Darlington. The mayor reported several ghostly encounters by his family, including the sound of pistol shots, a bed lifting and being dropped three times in quick succession, and invisible dogs fighting just outside the building. Walker's brother and a visiting joiner fled the site after watching a white man appear in a room and vanish a few moments later. A few years after the family moved out, a skeleton was discovered under the kitchen.

properties.name: A Grey Cat and Old Ruffy
properties.description: A grey cat has been seen in the building on several occasions, running down a corridor, only to disappear into a wall in the bathroom. Old Ruffy is the affectionate name given to the pub poltergeist, which once had a habit of breaking glasses.

properties.name: Labrador
properties.description: The history of this doggy spirit is unknown.

properties.name: Black Monk
properties.description: The monk that is said to appear at these ruins once a year has been rarely seen since the 1960s. A large chest of gold is also said to be buried in the area, watched over by a raven (or the monk, depending on the story source).
properties.date: January - first new moon of the year (reoccurring)

properties.name: Cloaked Man
properties.description: A couple of witnesses watched a man dressed in a cloak float into the room, being chased by a woman dressed in Tutor clothing. It is unclear whether the female spirit is the same as the woman in grey who has been seen in other areas of the estate, often with a small brown dog.

properties.name: Big Cat
properties.description: Invisible, large and friendly, this ghost cat would freak people out as it rubbed itself on their legs, trying to show affection.

properties.name: Cat
properties.description: A former landlord's cat which had died several years previous could be heard walking up and down on the keys of a piano.

properties.name: Soldiers with Mules
properties.description: A group of Roman soldiers with pack animals slowly make their way up these steps. Some believe that the ghostly posse will lead anyone who tries to follow them to gold.
properties.location_name: Harlech (Gwynedd) - Roman Steps leading up the Rhinog Fawr mountains

properties.name: Cat
properties.description: Northbrooke House is reportedly haunted by a cat, the reincarnation of a woman murdered by her lover.

properties.name: Woman with Horse
properties.description: While driving by the entrance of the hall, a police inspector spotted a horse being led by a lady with a bonnet and old fashioned clothing. He turned his car around for a second look, but both the animal and woman had vanished.

properties.name: Man in Grey Suit
properties.description: Not seen for many years now, the man in grey is thought to have been a previous manger that died in a fire on site. His dog also died in the blaze and has been seen running about in the corridors.
properties.location_name: Hastings (Sussex) - Royal Victoria Hotel, St Leonards-on-Sea

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A phantom man upon a shaggy horse was once said to haunt this hill, manifesting to other riders and causing their mounts to bolt.

properties.name: Dennis
properties.description: Amanda Hyde, writing for the Times Online, documented her less than convincing experiences during a ghost hunt on the site, during which she encountered Dennis (via a medium) and communicated with Edward using a glass. More convincing is the encounter a chef reported in 1998. He reported seeing a shadow the size and shape of a large dog in the kitchen a few minutes before feeling a nip on his right thigh. He cried out, three nearby staff asking what was wrong, although they were disbelieving when he told them what he had seen and felt. The following day a heavily bruised bite mark appeared on his leg.
properties.location_name: Hay-on-Wye (Powys) - Baskerville Hall Hotel (aka Clyro Court)

properties.name: St Bernard Dogs
properties.description: A pair of St Bernard dogs were spotted by a man walking his own dogs. Concerned that their owner was nowhere in sight, the witness closely followed them around a corner, where they vanished. The man later found out that disappeared at the rear of the house where they once lived, even though the alleyway leading to the house had been closed off.

properties.name: White Pig
properties.description: This village was the haunt for several years of a large phantom pig, said to contain the spirit of a local farmer who took his own life. A handful of people have also reported other ghostly creatures, including a sheep and a flying 'white thing'.
properties.location_name: Hoe Benham (Berkshire) - General area of village

properties.name: Horses' Hooves
properties.description: The hill is apparently home to the sound of horses moving around, the noise providing an uneasy feeling to any witness.

properties.name: Black Lady
properties.description: Workers in the castle were too scared to visit the attic, where it is said the ghost haunts. The basement in turn is haunted by a raven that attacks any source of light taken down there.

properties.name: Large Pig
properties.description: A large phantom hog is said to haunt this country lane, sometimes accompanied by a rolling barrel.

properties.name: Lady in Brown Dress
properties.description: Local legend says this ghost is a serving girl whose child was killed when the lord of the manor threw it from an attic window. A pair of disembodied hands have been reported floating around the building, also said to belong to the former servant, while on the odd occasion, a male figure in spurs has been observed. A visitor to the manor in 1995 felt a strong tap on her shoulder. Expecting it to be her boyfriend, she turned, but was alone in the room. There are several other local legends about the manor, including hauntings by a horse and carriage, a phantom woman, and ghostly birds.
properties.date: Mostly unknown, tap felt in October 1995

properties.name: Hare
properties.description: A phantom hare which ran around the area would once be hunted by locals, but it could never be killed.

properties.name: Feline
properties.description: This spectral cat has been seen running around the property, a shadow of its former self.

properties.name: Godstone
properties.description: The Godstone was said to have granted islanders with control over the weather, credited with increasing the speed of potato growth, and could also put out fires. The stone was cast into the sea at some point in the nineteenth century after a religious man took exception to it. The islands were (or are) also home to an immortal crane - the bird is said to have sat alone from the dawn of time, waiting until the end of the world.
properties.date: Godstone pre twentieth century, Crane still present?
properties.location_name: Inishkea Islands (County Mayo) - Exact areas unknown

properties.name: Gentleman in Top Hat
properties.description: Merely one of the spooks found here, the man in the top hat not putting on the Ritz shares the house with a woman dressed in silken clothes, a former pet dog, and a servant who is doomed to spend the rest of time ascending the staircase.
properties.location_name: Isle of Yell (Shetland) - Windhouse, near the Lumbister RSPB Reserve

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: While travelling along this road during heavy rain at night, a taxi driver encountered someone riding a white horse. The driver had to stop abruptly to avoid the rider, and when the driver looked up, man and horse had vanished. When the witness later mentioned the encounter to other people, they said it was not the first time the entity had been seen.
properties.location_name: Ivinghoe (Buckinghamshire) - Ivinghoe Aston to Slapton Road

properties.name: House Cat
properties.description: A phantom cat is reputed to haunt this building, though tends to be felt rather than seen.

properties.name: Rusty
properties.description: Paranormal investigation groups reported recording a laugh and a phantom dog barking after the landlady called them in. Locals named the dog as 'Rusty', a Jack Russell who died seventeen years previous.
properties.location_name: Keighley (West Yorkshire) - Bracken Arms public house

properties.name: Big Collie
properties.description: This spectral dog appeared briefly to prevent a robbery on the roadside, vanishing once the threat had passed.
properties.location_name: Keighley (West Yorkshire) - Woods just outside village

properties.name: Lost Girl
properties.description: The gatehouse is haunted by a little girl who asks for her daddy, a man dressed in black killed in a sword fight, and an old lady who breaks the same candle, time after time. A phantom little boy is reported in the stables, as are ghostly chickens and horses.

properties.name: Victorian Lady
properties.description: The ghostly Victorian was just one of several spirits thought to haunt this building; the sound of a small dog was often heard as it ran across the floor, and a man with his head dressed in blood soaked bandages (later named as John Leith, shot by an associate during a duel) was seen by one owner.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This area was the haunt of a headless horseman who died while racing the country roads. His ghost was put to rest after a local man was brave enough to let the decapitated figure ride by his side.
properties.location_name: Kilcummer (County Cork) - Hill in the area

properties.name: Black Cat of Killakee
properties.description: The Arts Centre found itself haunted by a violent manifestation during the 1970s, which destroyed rooms which the owners securely padlocked up. A short spectral man was seen to transform into a black cat before vanishing, leading to speculation that the building was haunted by a demon once summoned by the nearby Hell Fire Club.
properties.location_name: Killakee (County Dublin) - General area, including the Arts Centre

properties.name: Black Pig
properties.description: A phantom black pig made itself known to children. Adults standing in the same area could not perceive it, even when the children shouted that the entity was running around their feet.
properties.location_name: Kiltrustan (County Roscommon) - Road close to old plantation

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: Tourists staying in this cottage were said to have been awaken as the building shook due to a stampede of phantom horses. Footsteps have also been heard outside, and the face of a quickly vanishing sailor observed looking in through windows.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This woman leapt to her death from the battlements after discovering that her father had mistakenly shot dead her newlywed husband. Since her death, she has been seen walking through locked doors and was blamed for knocking unconscious at least two witnesses. The site was also haunted by a rabbit which attacked guards asleep on duty.

properties.name: Sheepdog
properties.description: A forgetful farmer left his dog out in the sheep's field, where it finally starved to death. The dog's shade now runs around the village, to warn all farmers never to forget their pets.
properties.location_name: Kirkby Overblow (North Yorkshire) - Area surrounding village

properties.name: Shaggy Foal
properties.description: Someone (apparently) encountered a large, shaggy, donkey-like entity in this area.

properties.name: Bell Ringer
properties.description: This ghostly figure is often spotted out of the corner of one eye, before disappearing. A local legend says that a large, evil bird set up home in the bell tower, interrupting services with its noise - the creature became displaced after the vicar cast a spell with the help of the local community.
properties.date: Twentieth century (bell ringer), bird not unknown

properties.name: Black Cockerel
properties.description: This creature is said to still be imprisoned within the walls of this public house - it contained the spirit of an angry local rector who died after falling (or being pushed) down a flight of stairs. In his cockerel form, the rector attacked everyone he met; a maid at the inn finally managed to trap the creature in an oven, and a builder bricked it up.

properties.name: Headless Chicken Runs Blindly
properties.description: With an unknown origin, a headless chicken appears and runs blindly around the gravestones.

properties.name: Headless Coachman
properties.description: A groom and his assistant heard galloping approaching Leam Hall. Expecting the lord and lady of the hall, the pair stood outside awaiting the coach. However, instead of the master's coach, the approaching vehicle was of a 'strange design' and was driven by a headless man. The phantom coach was driven straight past the witnesses and vanished into a closed gate.

properties.name: George
properties.description: Workmen demolishing this old house reported that 'something' was trying to stop them. Brookhurst had a history of hauntings, from the sounds of a large bird-like creature flying around to phantom footsteps and doors which unlocked themselves.
properties.location_name: Leamington (Warwickshire) - Brookhurst (demolished 1960), on site of Leamington Golf Club

properties.name: Disconcerting Monk
properties.description: There are mixed opinions to whether the library here is haunted by a grey monk or a white lady. A tormented Cavalier has been seen in the Great Hall, a phantom policeman haunts other parts of the building, a ghostly dog has been reported in the courtyard, a black cat in the Great Hall, and at least one witness claims to have been shocked to see a pair of legs manifest from the portrait of Henry Earl of Huntington hanging in the Major's Parlour. Ghost Scene Investigations and Leicester City Museums released video footage in 2006 of what appears to be a ghostly figure crawling across the floor in one room.

properties.name: Clark's Dog
properties.description: This malnourished hound, once owned by a criminal hanged nearby, spent the rest of its life as a minor celebrity and now is occasionally spotted lurking around the castle walls.

properties.name: Clark's Dog Too
properties.description: The hungry phantom hound reported to haunt the castle walls would sometimes try to enter this inn, scratching and barking at the door.

properties.name: Boy playing Flute
properties.description: This figure was seen and heard on the lanes, in trees and behind bushes in this village - the music he produced described as enchanting, but with an underlying danger. The same area is haunted by a pale white calf.

properties.name: Red Eyes
properties.description: Staying on what was a traveller's site, this witness felt a strange 'force' swarm over their van. The benevolent energy remained for around a minute before leaving. The witness ran out of the van and into their friend's coach to share the story, only to find out it had happened to other people who had parked in the same spot. Other travellers had reported red animal eyes looking through their windows, and one other person had seen a procession of ghostly figures down nearby Lake Lane.
properties.location_name: Liskeard (Cornwall) - Park Fenton, Liskerrett Road and surroundings

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This spook is reportedly a woman whose body was found on the nearby beach. A long dead cat is also reported to play on the beds at night, while a man in 'modern' dress is said to slowly materialise from the feet up.

properties.name: Stabbed Bulldog
properties.description: After saving a local girl from a friar who attempted to rape her, the dog was killed by her attacker. It can now be seen crossing the bridge.
properties.location_name: Littleport (Cambridgeshire) - Bulldog Bridge, which connects Littleport & Shippea Hill

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: Even the most fearless residents on the village felt terror when they encountered this phantom, snow-white mare along the roads at night.
properties.location_name: Llangynwyd (Mid Glamorgan) - Roads in the area

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: Witness Mr Morrison stopped his car suddenly when he encountered a white horse ridden by a Victorian man in the middle of the road. Both entities vanished quickly into the distance.
properties.location_name: Loch Dornoch (Highland) - Straight piece of road lined with trees on both sides

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: A white horse once haunted the area around the house. Another animal ghost was reputedly a mole - a former resident declared that she would haunt the garden she loved in the form of the furry mammal, and so she did.
properties.location_name: Lochgelly (Fife) - Old house (may no longer stand) on the banks of Loch Gelly, Powguild

properties.name: Sir John Plumley
properties.description: Hanged for his part in the Monmouth revolt, Sir John and his dog are now seen on his former property. His wife also puts in the occasional appearance - she killed herself after her husband's execution.
properties.location_name: Locking (Somerset) - Manor House, and Coach House public house (was once the manor house's stables)

properties.name: Hare
properties.description: This ghost is said to be a witch, who evaded capture by transforming herself into a hare.

properties.name: Dogs
properties.description: The house was haunted by the sounds of arguing, rapping, and an invisible force that felt like a dog pushing against people's legs. This 'dog' was quite common, many people felt it, and other pet dogs brought into the house appeared happy to play with an invisible friend. A phantom nun was also said to walk the garden.

properties.name: Hare
properties.description: This white creature has been observed running down the hill, disappearing as it reaches the public house. It is considered an ill omen if seen, and thought to be the spirit of a young woman who took her own life.
properties.location_name: Looe (Cornwall) - Hill between Talland and the Jolly Sailor Inn (A387 road)

properties.name: Cat
properties.description: People have reported being woken during the night when a small cat jumps on their bed, though they can no trace of it when they turn on the light to investigate.

properties.name: Black Pig
properties.description: This creature is said to be the ghost of Judge Jeffereys. Another ghost seen in the vicinity is that of Lady Howard.

properties.name: White Cow
properties.description: During times of drought or hardship, a white cow would appear in the middle of the circle and allow everyone in the neighbourhood to fill one bucket with milk. Michell, a local witch after whom the circle was later named, milked the cow into a sieve until the creature vanished.

properties.name: Winner
properties.description: A group of miners playing cards invited a passing stranger to join their game. He sat with them, quickly winning all hands, although the game quickly came to an end when the miners fled, having realised their guest had cloven feet.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: A legend once said that horses were driven around the area of the church by an invisible entity. Fire emerging from the horses' nostrils would be blamed for darkened grass and the flaking paint on fencing. In the same area, a phantom donkey was said to have been banished after a man kicked the creature.

properties.name: invisible Bird
properties.description: Two occupants of a house on this street felt the sensation of an invisible bird passing through their heads, and out a closed window.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: Walking her dogs, this witness heard and felt a large horse trotting towards her. She called the dogs to one side, to let the rider pass, but upon turning around, she could she nothing there.

properties.name: Paddy
properties.description: Seen in the garden of this house, this small black dog's grave is only a few metres away from the spot it haunts.
properties.location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - House at corner of Spath Road and Holme Road

properties.name: Headless Cat
properties.description: For a two month period, until they finally moved out, a family endured the sounds of a cat in terrible pain. During the haunting, the lady of the house was found unconscious - she said she had encountered a headless cat which pawed at her legs.
properties.location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Private house along Lower Seedley Road, Seedley

properties.name: Woman with Pig
properties.description: A former occupier of the rectory reported seeing a woman driving a pig over the lawn. She turned briefly to call for her husband, and when she looked again the woman and hog had vanished.

properties.name: Golden Coach
properties.description: Pulled by a team of four headless horses, this golden coach appears at The Hatches.

properties.name: Kitchen Worker
properties.description: It has been reported that this pub has three phantoms. A female form is said to work in the kitchen, a man has been observed in the garden, and a dog.

properties.name: Roman on Horseback
properties.description: This Roman centurion passes through the area on horseback.

properties.name: Mr Baker
properties.description: Mr Baker was a farmer murdered on the hill in 1694. His ghost returns on the anniversary of the crime, possibly driving a horse and carriage.
properties.location_name: Melbury Bubb (Dorset) - Dirt track along Bubdown Hill, known as Murderers Lane?

properties.name: Arthur Bryant Haig
properties.description: Former owner of the house, Arthur Bryant Haig, is still said to mount his (now phantom) chestnut horse and ride through his lands towards a nearby river.

properties.name: Goose
properties.description: This well was haunted by a ghostly entity which had taken the form of a white goose.
properties.location_name: Melsonby (North Yorkshire) - Berry Well (may no longer exist)

properties.name: Large Black Tomcat
properties.description: The pub (particularly room number 3) has been home to a ghostly cat. The phantom feline has a habit of laying across the throat of people sleeping, only to jump off and vanish once they awake.
properties.location_name: Melton (Suffolk) - The Horse and Groom Public House

properties.name: Greyhound
properties.description: This phantom greyhound began to haunt the area after a local lady drowned herself nearby.

properties.name: Echoing Footfalls
properties.description: These footsteps, associated with long dead feet, have been reported moving around the building. One group of people in the bar heard the swooping of wings close above them, though no birds could be seen. The pub is also haunted by the sounds of a wailing woman, said to cry on the anniversary of her death, caused when her runaway coach smashed into a wall of the building.
properties.location_name: Monkton Farleigh (Wiltshire) - King's Arms public house

properties.name: Lucky Venture?
properties.description: The stadium was briefly haunted by a horse named Lucky Venture which died on the track. The animal's shade was exorcised by a couple of local university students.

properties.name: Heavy Dog
properties.description: Three members of the family which lived here experienced the sensation of a heavy dog laying on them in bed. The property was also home to a lady in an old fashioned floor length dress, and a man in a suit and waistcoat who told a builder to 'get on with it' - the builder refused to re-enter the house and quit.

properties.name: Bacon's Chicken
properties.description: The site where Francis Bacon put theory into practice with refrigeration in 1626; the featherless, panicking chicken that has been seen running around the area on cold winter nights is all that remains of that early experiment.

properties.name: Mother Marnes
properties.description: Murdered many moons ago for her money, Mother Marnes now meanders around the building with her pet cat. A ghost hunting team claimed to have contacted Francis Bacon (philosopher, not painter) in the basement during an investigation.
properties.location_name: N6 (Greater London) - Ye Olde Gatehouse public house, Highgate

properties.name: The Blue Lady
properties.description: This young teenage girl once haunted the former priory along with her little dog, though sightings have been rare as of late. The dog outlived the girl by several years, was stuffed after its death, and is now kept in a glass case within the building. The haunting is said to grow quite intense if the hound is ever removed from the hotel.
properties.location_name: Nettlestone (Isle of Wight) - The Priory hotel

properties.name: Heavy Footsteps
properties.description: Just a small part of the phenomena, the footsteps were heard first. Over a period of weeks, these developed into bangs, whistling, the sounds of machinery, and finally a voice that spoke. Phantom animals were seen around the building. The family finally moved out, though the spirit stayed a little longer (but was much quieter).
properties.location_name: Newcastle upon Tyne (Tyne and Wear) - Willington Mill

properties.name: Sounds of a Horse
properties.description: While looking for two pet cats in a barn, this witness heard a horse wearing metal shoes walking directly outside. The witness thought the horses had come back from racing so left the barn to help, but nothing could be seen. Two different members of staff later told the witness that they had also heard horses moving around but could never find the creature responsible, and one said that they had been told by the former owner of the yard that the site was haunted by a racehorse.

properties.name: Fox
properties.description: A creature so cunning that it was never caught by the local fox hunting group, it now gloats by sitting on the golf club walls.

properties.name: Lion
properties.description: A zookeeper and his granddaughter came across the glowing apparition of a lion while out walking in the park at evening. The creature faded away as they approached it. The keeper knew that one of the lions at his zoo was ill, and when he went into work the following day, was told the sick lion had died the previous evening - at the time of the sighting. More recently, a glowing green figure was observed by a witness in the park after midnight and had actually caused the witness to flee; another person with the witness was unable to see anything. The witness returned to the scene the following day to try to understand what they had seen and realised the figure first manifested in a bush and had moved at a very fast pace.

properties.name: Dazzling Little Donkey
properties.description: This ghostly donkey reportedly radiates colour and light as it passes.
properties.location_name: NW9 (Greater London) - The Hyde, between Hendon and Kingsbury

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: A woman in the sitting in the passenger side of a car opened her window as the vehicle left the church. Suddenly both the woman and driver heard a horse trotting alongside the vehicle, even though no animal could be seen. As the vehicle reached the main road, the trotting ceased.
properties.location_name: Odstock (Wiltshire) - Lane adjoining St Marys Church

properties.name: Robert Leigh
properties.description: One of many ghosts belonging to this building, Leigh was reported to walk around the grounds carrying an unknown person's head under one arm. A woman in white rustling silk glides along the corridors of the building, the sad sounds of a harpsichord can be heard, and on occasion a black dog lurks outside.

properties.name: Knight
properties.description: A phantom knight upon a white horse was once thought to haunt this area.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Richmond (Greater London) - Kingston Hill area

properties.name: Raven
properties.description: For many years, this large black bird haunted the church and its grounds - it would tap and peck at coffins, sit on the pews, and generally create a nuisance.
properties.location_name: Outer London: West Drayton (Greater London) - West Drayton church

properties.name: Woman in Crinoline
properties.description: A night watchman spotted a man carrying a gun and accompanied with three dogs walk across the grounds and vanish into a pond. A year later, three guests at the house were awoken by persistent hammering on the door and walls of their room. The following night they spotted a woman wearing a long dress in the garden bathed in a strange light - she disappeared through a wall.
properties.location_name: Pembroke (Dyfed) - Bush House (currently a boarding house?)

properties.name: White Hare
properties.description: A former lord of the castle had an affair with a woman in a faraway land, who gave birth to a son. When the lord returned home (and back to his wife) the woman and child followed him. The lord threw his former lover and the child into the sea. The child survived and was picked up by a passing ship, but the woman drowned, returning as a white hare which haunted the lord for the rest of his life.
properties.location_name: Pengersick (Cornwall) - Former castle (on site of current castle)

properties.name: Horse & Dog
properties.description: A man who wagered that he could cross the track in record time fell from his horse and snapped his neck. The man on horseback, now minus a head, with a hound in tow, tries again and again to make the journey once a year.
properties.location_name: Penselwood (Wiltshire) - Sloane Track - between Penselwood and Stourton

properties.name: Woman with Dog
properties.description: A farmer and an aid chasing down stray cattle encountered a phantom woman dressed in black crossing the bridge. She was accompanied by a large dog, being led by a diamond chain.

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: One man was told by a gardener that a phantom monk could be heard ascending the staircase in the tower. The man went on to spend three years living in the tower and never heard a thing. Another local story states that the driveway of the building is haunted by a phantom coach and four.
properties.date: Monk in mid twentieth century, coach unknown
properties.location_name: Pewsey (Wiltshire) - Fyfield Manor, the Monk's Tower

properties.name: Dog that is not There
properties.description: Heard dragging chains as it ran, this phantom hound is said to be invisible, though it has been touched by witnesses as it passes them. A similar story is told in Stourpaine, Dorset.
properties.location_name: Pimperne (Dorset) - Road between village and Letton Hill

properties.name: Alarm
properties.description: Two employees would be woken at 02:15h by their alarms over a two week period, although neither had set their devices. The figure of a young woman quickly vanished after being spotted by one employee, and a cat would appear in the building before disappearing in areas with no possible exist. Finally, men (but never women) would find one room particularly disturbing, with builders refusing to work within and guests leaving during the night.

properties.name: A Man and his Dog
properties.description: The ghosts of a man and his dog haunt the site of former railway tracks just outside this bistro, where it is believed they were killed by a steam engine.
properties.location_name: Plumtree (Nottinghamshire) - Outside Perkins' Bar & Bistro (formally Plumtree railway station)

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: This park is said to be haunted by a figure wearing a habit near the gates of the lodge, a white lady who drowned in a lake while eloping, and a strange animal with very large eyes.

properties.name: Bloody Horse
properties.description: Driving past the mine in bad weather, a driver and his passenger felt a strange negative sensation wash over them. The driver slammed on the brakes and as the car stopped, they spotted a bloody horse standing in the lights. The driver started to climb out of his car to move the horse out of the road, but the creature had gone - there was a steep drop to one side of the road and a wire fence to the other side.
properties.location_name: Pool (Cornwall) - Road and tunnel close to South Crofty mine

properties.name: Fox Terrier
properties.description: Elliott O'Donnell wrote that a medium who receded here believed her home to be haunted by several cats, which were mistreated by a former owner of the property. The medium also detected the presence of a fox terrier.

properties.name: Scratching Monkey
properties.description: Home to several ghosts, the monkey was accidentally bricked up behind a wall and now tries to escape. Headless figures allegedly sit around the dinner table, duellists re-enact their last fights outside, and a grey lady walks through walls in the east wing.

properties.name: Miss Effie
properties.description: The (former) owner of Miss Ellie, a dead hamster which was buried on the goal line of this football ground, claimed that the animal's spirit had prevented Reading FC being relegated.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: It is not clear if the woman in white, a large black hound and a black hog which once haunted this area were all the same entity. The phantom (or phantoms) would all suddenly grow once seen, before pursuing any witness.
properties.location_name: Reagill (Cumbria) - Country lanes in the area

properties.name: Farm Animals
properties.description: Built on a farm, one house here was reportedly haunted by pigs in the garden. Poltergeist like activity was reported inside the building itself, including exploding cups and electrical equipment powering itself up and off.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: A farmer heard a galloping horse rapidly approaching him, even though he could not see the creature. Moments later, there was a crashing sound, and a large gust of wind passed the man, even though the creature remained invisible.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: This lane is reputed to be haunted by the phantom sounds of horses.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: A coach pulled by a team of frantic horses reputedly vanishes into a field after crashing through a wall on the roadside.
properties.location_name: Rockfield (Gwent) - Road between Rockfield and Monmouth (Ancre Lane?)

properties.name: Cat
properties.description: An old woman who lived in this cottage was convinced it was haunted by a black cat, which she believed was her mother, due to the way it walked.
properties.location_name: Ropley (Hampshire) - Cottage along Old Chapel Lane

properties.name: Shag Foal
properties.description: A ghostly donkey covered in long shaggy hair was said to haunt this lane.
properties.location_name: Roxby (Lincolnshire) - Road formally known as Goosey Lane (and/or Boggart Lane)

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A phantom black dog, unlikely to be a shuck, has been reported to walk around the corridors of the inn.

properties.name: Sister Lucy
properties.description: Sister Lucy ran away from her chapel, but realising she had made a mistake, tried to return. She slipped as she passed the well and drowned in its waters. Her ghost is now said to haunt the area. The same location is also said to be home to a phantom horse.

properties.name: Hare
properties.description: A phantom white hare was said to manifest when storms rolled through the area.
properties.location_name: Saint Martin (Guernsey) - Area around Profond Camp

properties.name: Ewe
properties.description: The phantom of a murdered man returned in the form of an ewe, appearing at the scene of the crime every morning at 1am.
properties.location_name: Sandwick (Orkney) - Nether Benzieclett farm (no longer standing)

properties.name: Tailless Dog
properties.description: This inn is haunted by a dog which lost its tail while trying to protect its master from press gangers.
properties.location_name: SE1 (Greater London) - Anchor Inn, Clink Street

properties.name: Boy with Cat
properties.description: The London Ghost Hunters group investigated this property after the owners reported strange knocking and phantom footsteps. A little boy with a cat was also spotted by one witness.
properties.location_name: SE13 (Greater London) - Private property along Roxley Road, Lewisham

properties.name: Dead Dog
properties.description: A woman from Bath saw a vision of her dog here, at the exact moment her pet died at home.
properties.location_name: SE3 (Greater London) - Blackheath, exact location unknown

properties.name: Fox Hunt
properties.description: A local legend says that the five trees are where an old witch coven would meet. One of the witches took the form of a fox and lead the local hunt over the edge of a cliff - the scene is repeated if the weather conditions are right.

properties.name: Irish Wolfhound
properties.description: A large phantom dog that has been reported running along the road is thought to be an Irish Wolfhound looking for his master.

properties.name: Heron
properties.description: Two animal ghosts were once said to roam here; a phantom heron would fly down into the lake and a spectral horse roamed the surrounding area.

properties.name: One Man and his Dog
properties.description: Two men witnessed a man with a hound, pursuing several horses along a steep rocky ridge. Man and beasts suddenly vanished - the witnesses were at first concerned that they had all fallen over the edge of a cliff, though were unable to find any bodies.

properties.name: Cow
properties.description: A Guernsey Cow said to haunt this location was once a woman who committed a 'terrible deed' and was transformed into this animal for 4,900 years as punishment.
properties.date: Circa late nineteenth century, said to appear at midnight
properties.location_name: South Tawton (Devon) - Hamlet just outside the village, exact location not known

properties.name: Lady with Dog
properties.description: Late at night, a phantom woman is alleged to appear walking about the hill with her dog.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A large black dog was said to haunt the lanes and the farms in this area, as far down as Copplestone.
properties.location_name: St Giles In the Wood (Devon) - Lanes in the area

properties.name: Snorting Boars
properties.description: The sound of running boars is reported here, snorting loudly as they go.
properties.location_name: St Leonards (Buckinghamshire) - Uphill and general area

properties.name: This Little Piggy...
properties.description: A sow and her litter were said to pass through the house, in protest of food to the poor being withheld.
properties.location_name: St Pierre du Bois (Guernsey) - House at Le Lorier

properties.name: The Birds
properties.description: A flock of screeching invisible phantom birds apparently attacked a witness walking along this lane.
properties.location_name: St Pierre du Bois (Guernsey) - Les Jaonnets Lane

properties.name: Horse and Cart
properties.description: The sounds of a horse and cart unloading stone has been heard around this former house. Another story says it is haunted by a sow and her piglets.
properties.location_name: St Saviour (Guernsey) - De Garis house (no longer present)

properties.name: Running Dog
properties.description: Upset by the continued yapping of the gravedigger's dog, the bell ringer poisoned the pet pooch. The animal died an agonised death, and some people now claim the dog runs around the church, floating slightly above the ground.

properties.name: The White Rabbit of Etruria
properties.description: This ghostly white rabbit appeared after the phantom cries and scream of a young boy. It was thought that the rabbit was the ghost of a fourteen year old lad murdered by another teenager while arguing over a wager.

properties.name: Piano Cat
properties.description: When the piano keys could be heard striking without human intervention, a ghost cat would be blamed. The phantom feline was said to be appeased when a small amount of beer would be left out for him.
properties.location_name: Stoke on Trent (Staffordshire) - New Inn, Basford (no longer open)

properties.name: Early Morning Rider
properties.description: While walking his dog, this witness heard horse's hooves walking alongside him for several minutes, never becoming and louder or quieter, or changing pace. The witness thought the sound may have been coming from behind a nearby hedge, though there was nothing visible.
properties.location_name: Stoke Trister (Somerset) - Field in the village

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A horseman with his caparisoned horse was said to travel over this bridge, the sound of his jangling armour causing unease to anyone unfortunate enough to be nearby.

properties.name: Flaming Carriage
properties.description: Surrounded by fields, this narrow path would have been quite spooky, especially if one encountered the entity which was said to haunt it. A phantom flaming carriage contained a headless woman would move at breakneck speeds, drawn by a team of six greyhounds. Why? No one knows.

properties.name: Dog That Still isn't There
properties.description: Heard dragging chains as it ran, this phantom hound is never seen. It is thought the dog died while escaping from a master who mistreated it. A similar story is told in Pimperne, Dorset.
properties.location_name: Stourpaine (Dorset) - Village square, heading out towards the hills

properties.name: Old Woman
properties.description: This house is reputedly haunted by the ghost of an elderly lady and her black dog.

properties.name: Ranulph
properties.description: When this knight returned from the crusades, he discovered his wife had thought him dead, and had remarried. He spent his remaining days living rough in the land around the castle, and his ghost can be seen in the former moat. One version of the story says that his ghost takes the form of a hare.
properties.location_name: Stratton (Cornwall) - Blanchminster Castle (were ruins in a field, current status not known), located at Binhamy grounds

properties.name: Pale Donkey
properties.description: A small white donkey haunts the area where its master was robbed and murdered while travelling across the heathland. While the owner's body was found, the donkey vanished without trace, now only appearing briefly before Christmas once a year.

properties.name: Bjorn
properties.description: Said to be a thirteenth century outlaw, the ghost of Bjorn wanders the area carrying a bag of living cats. He is easily recognisable, having lost his head.
properties.location_name: Styhead (aka Sty Head) Pass (Cumbria) - General area

properties.name: Old Man Marriott
properties.description: A ghostly figure of Old Man Marriott is said to walk down the road with his sheep dog and his gun.
properties.date: Unknown, said to occur around midnight
properties.location_name: Sutton Lane End (Cheshire) - Rossendale wood (known locally as Marriott`s Wood)

properties.name: Jeremiah Abershaw
properties.description: No wombles here, only a ghostly padfoot looking for victims after being hanged for his crimes two hundred years ago. On two occasions the ghostly entity has been seen wearing convicts clothing. Abershaw is sometimes heard on horseback, hooves heard thumping on the ground, yet the animal never appears.

properties.name: Cat
properties.description: A cat has been seen in the building running across the bar area. A member of staff who spotted the animal in July 2007 mistook it for a real creature, only to be told otherwise when she mentioned her encounter to others. There are also stories of a phantom man who sits by the fireplace with a beer.

properties.name: Black Cat
properties.description: A ghostly black cat was spotted by the occupants of this property.

properties.name: Mary on Piggyback
properties.description: Mary killed her husband's prized pig, so he drowned her in return. Mary is now said to ride the hog around the area of the river where her husband murdered her.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: There is said to be a secret passage connected this site and Dally Castle, with phantom horses heard running the route at midnight.

properties.name: Headless Group
properties.description: A headless team of horses driven by a headless coachman carries a headless lady in a coach along this road every night. As they reach the crossroads known as Sampson Cross, some ten miles from their starting point, they all disappear in a large explosion. Some people believe the entities have been laid, and now rest in a nearby lake.
properties.location_name: Tenby (Dyfed) - Between Tenby and Sampson Cross

properties.name: House with Glowing Windows
properties.description: After a murder of a newlywed bride, this house was left to fall into ruin. Some people reported that a glowing candle could be seen moving past various windows, while others claimed a ghostly wedding party occurred. The Hall is now lost below the waters of the flooded valley of Thirlmere, which is home to a phantom dog which swims through the water. In 2013 a witness camping in the area became unnerved after witnessing two silently moving lights along the road.
properties.date: 31 October (reoccurring), Wedding party occurs. Lights seen May 2013

properties.name: White Lass Beck
properties.description: The woman in white who haunted this body of water was thought to be a maid murdered in the area, her body discovered buried in a gravel pit. As well as manifesting as a woman, White Lass Beck would also take the form of a white dog or white heifer.
properties.location_name: Thirsk (North Yorkshire) - Stream in the area

properties.name: Two Monks
properties.description: A male witness reported cycling home towards the village at one night when he passed by two hooded figures in monks' garb and a white dog. The two figures raised a hand and wished the man goodnight, but when he directly looked at the pair, the witness realised the inside of the hoods were empty - the monks had no heads.
properties.location_name: Thornham Parva (Suffolk) - Road heading towards the village

properties.name: Beaten Donkey
properties.description: The area around the inn is haunted by a donkey that was beaten to death by its owner.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: One unnamed lane in the village is the haunt of a headless horse. The village must attract decapitated entities, as a headless pig is also said to haunt the general area.

properties.name: Headless Dog
properties.description: A ghost of a little headless dog wagging its tail was taken at a tea party in the village early in the twentieth century. The photographer, an ex-Scotland Yard Inspector, claimed he saw nothing when taking the image.

properties.name: Garden Monks
properties.description: Several shades have been reported here, including monks walking about the garden, a small dog that runs around the same area, a grey lady that stands by a fireplace and another female who haunts a bedroom. A phantom coach can only be seen or heard by members of the family and is an omen of impending death.

properties.name: White Rabbit
properties.description: The white rabbit is said to appear at different times of the year, running about the churchyard.

properties.name: Last Wolf
properties.description: A phantom white wolf, thought to have been the last one killed in the area, haunts the mountain where she was born. The creature has reportedly been seen leaping out at a car as it passed some bushes and also observed by a family out picnicking.
properties.location_name: Trillick (County Tyrone) - Forest and mountain in the area

properties.name: Headless Rider
properties.description: A beautiful white horse carrying a headless rider gallops across the valley when the night sky is right. The phantom is either a peddler or a soldier, depending on the legend.

properties.name: Pet Duck
properties.description: This tame bird was killed by a landlord after it took to attacking patrons. Unfortunately, death failed to prevent the ankle pecker continuing its work, and it fell on members of the clergy to exorcise the spirit.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: While picnicking with her daughter, this witness heard a stampede of horses close by, but no animals could be seen.
properties.location_name: Twechar (Dumbartonshire) - Antonine Wall - Bar Hill Fort

properties.name: Man in Black Cape
properties.description: More frequently seen in the nineteenth century, this ghostly gentleman, complete with cape and cane, could be seen walking his dog around the estate. It was thought that he had suffered a heart attack and broken his neck while falling down a set of steps. There is also a local story that a death of an heir of the Shrewsbury family is always preceded by the appearance of a ghostly old gypsy on the estate.

properties.name: Glowing Dachshund
properties.description: A pet belonging to two women, the dachshund disappeared one day, though its phantom manifested several times along the road for a period of weeks afterwards.

properties.name: Giant Black Bird
properties.description: Elliott O'Donnell wrote (albeit briefly) about a large, phantom black bird which appeared so frequently that the residents grew accustom to it.
properties.location_name: W1 (Greater London) - Unknown house, Dean Street

properties.name: Old Yella
properties.description: This establishment was haunted by a large mongrel with a shaggy yellow coat which lay asleep on top of a staircase. The creature was mistaken for a real dog by one member who tried to tap it with a stick - the stick passed through the dog and the entity faded away.
properties.location_name: W2 (Greater London) - The Motley Gentleman's Club

properties.name: White Rabbit
properties.description: When the moon is brightest, the ghostly rabbit bolts across the churchyard and vanishes into the boundary wall. Another entity that haunts the churchyard is that of a man who tried to shoot the rabbit with a shotgun but managed to kill himself with the weapon instead!

properties.name: Fallen Horses
properties.description: Walking her dog across the bridge, Amanda West was frozen with fear when the sound of horses passed her - with no horses to be seen. Without warning, she then heard a loud splash. Looking into the river below, the water was calm, but the sound of panicking horses could now be heard coming from below. She later found out that the original bridge had collapsed in 1836 and a team of horses pulling a cart had drowned.
properties.location_name: Wakefield (West Yorkshire) - Denby Dale Road bridge, and river below

properties.name: Terrier
properties.description: This small, yapping dog has attacked and scared creatures many times larger than itself - when touched the phantom pooch fades from view.

properties.name: Equine Entities
properties.description: Said to be heard only during the day, phantom horses gallop across the common. In addition, reports of a phantom man have been frequent since the 1940s, appearing both on the common and in the church ruins.

properties.name: Patter of Doggy Footsteps
properties.description: A woman living here at the time reported twice hearing what sounded like a small dog following her down the staircase, late at night. On both occasions, when she turned around nothing could be seen.
properties.location_name: Walsall (West Midlands) - Private building along Lysways Street

properties.name: Snowy the Cat
properties.description: Snowy's owner, the stationmaster, was killed by a train while out looking for his pet. The cat is doomed to haunt the area in which it disappeared until found by the stationmaster.
properties.location_name: Wansford (Cambridgeshire) - Yarwell tunnel, Nene Valley Railway (13m west of Peterborough)

properties.name: White Stag
properties.description: A white stag, which gained protection from the king who spent a day attempting to hunt the creature, remained to run through the area after its death.

properties.name: The Dun Cow
properties.description: Appearing at least once at the castle prior to a death in the family, the phantom 'Dun Cow of Warwick' appears a few weeks before a death in the family. Way back in history, a member of the Warwick family killed the giant cow when it roamed as a living creature that terrorised people across the land.
properties.date: Prior to death in the Warwick family
properties.location_name: Warwick (Warwickshire) - Warwick Castle and surrounding area

properties.name: Magpie
properties.description: A phantom magpie is said to tap on the window of a house along this street before manifesting inside. The bird then perches on a floating stick, both bird and baton vanishing the moment a sound is made.
properties.location_name: WC1 (Greater London) - Residence along Great Russell Street

properties.name: Fading Lady
properties.description: A woman clothed in an outfit dating from the Queen Anne period is reported to descend the staircase here, slowly fading before she reaches the bottom. A short scream is said to signify her complete disappearance. The female ghost has not recently manifested and appears to have been replaced by the sound of a phantom dolphin which, during life, was kept in a small tank under the stage in the 1930s.
properties.location_name: WC2 (Greater London) - Peacock Theatre (was Royalty Theatre), Dean Street

properties.name: Headless Horse
properties.description: The headless horse which runs along this road was said to have been a casualty during the civil war.

properties.name: Hare
properties.description: The hare that haunts this area is thought to be the ghost of a witch. In life, she enjoyed turning herself into a lepus. Another ghost at the gate was said to be a farmer murdered by a woman. For the crime she used a dagger, from which the gate takes its name.

properties.name: Dog in Cellar
properties.description: Delivery men at the White Hart Inn have complained of a dog darting under their feet as they worked in the cellar, though the landlords have always insisted that the area is devoid of animals.

properties.name: Pitch Forked Dog
properties.description: A farmhand discovered a dog laying in the hay - he poked it with his pitchfork, causing the entity to vanish. The sound of a coach has also been heard as it stops by the front of the house, though nothing is seen.

properties.name: Black Bird
properties.description: A member of the family which owned Whirlow Hall was said to haunt the lane, taking the form for a black bird with a white tail (or wings). It was capable of paralysing people; in one instance the entity froze the rider of a mule in the air as his mount continued walking along oblivious to its master's predicament.
properties.location_name: Whirlow (South Yorkshire) - Lane near Whirlow Hall (no longer standing)

properties.name: Horse?
properties.description: While staying in a house along this road, a witness heard a horse walking outside on the gravel - when they looked from the window, nothing could be seen.

properties.name: Footsteps
properties.description: Sounding like someone moving across flagstones, the echoing footfalls may belong to the owner of a ghostly dog also seen in the premises.

properties.name: Woman Wearing Sackcloth
properties.description: The ghost is said to be that of one Betty Price, executed for being a witch. The old room 14 of the Inn was also reportedly haunted, by a cat. Only one person has reported spooky occurrences since the pub has been converted, and that relates to an answer machine that switches itself on.
properties.location_name: Wickham Market (Suffolk) - The White Hart (currently a collection of shops and flats)

properties.name: Padding Dog
properties.description: This invisible hound has been heard walking around the building. It is said to leave a tranquil air in its wake.

properties.name: Old Jeffrey
properties.description: Once said to be the most haunted site in the north of England, the mill was haunted by several ghosts. While the most well known was 'Old Jeffrey', a bald man who floated a metre off the ground, other ghosts included a lady in lavender, another woman in grey, and a third woman with empty eye sockets. Phantom animals spotted on site included a rabbit, cat, and sheep.
properties.location_name: Willington (Northumberland) - Old Mill (no longer intact, though some of the Rope Works still use the old mill buildings)

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The misty white lady is not alone in her haunting of this building. A ghostly figure is also reported to sit by the fireplace smoking a pipe, a little phantom dog by his side.
properties.location_name: Woburn (Bedfordshire) - The Inn (was the Bedford Arms Inn)

properties.name: Cavalier
properties.description: Returning home one night, a driver encountered a man wearing Cavalier-style clothing. The figure was accompanied by a large black dog. The driver sped away, rather than investigate further.
properties.location_name: Woodborough (Wiltshire) - Road between village and Allington

properties.name: Grey Cat
properties.description: Perceived by both man and animal, this ghostly cat has been known to brush up against people, only to vanish once their attention has been attracted.
properties.location_name: Worcester (Hereford & Worcester) - Old Talbot public house

properties.name: Bear
properties.description: Ever since the Civil War there have been reports of a phantom bear that rises as if to attack. One guard is said to have taken a shot at the creature, which of course had no effect.
properties.location_name: Worcester (Hereford & Worcester) - Worcester Cathedral and College Green

properties.name: Little Girl
properties.description: Seen as she waits on the staircase, this entity is said to radiate peace and love. She met her death when a horse and cart delivering beer ran her over just outside the pub. The ghostly presence reported down in the basement is much more sinister and has been called evil by previous landlords. Finally, a phantom cat may rub itself against the legs of visitors.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Snickleway Inn, Goodramgate (was the Angler's Arms public house)

properties.name: Rabbit
properties.description: Seen moving around the aisles of the church, the ghostly rabbit always vanishes before it is caught. Some believed it to be the ghost of Dick Turpin who is buried nearby.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - St George's Roman Catholic Church

properties.name: Screams
properties.description: Another building which once housed injured Civil War troops, the basement is said to be the place where amputations occurred, which explains the sound of cries and screams. Two black cats are also reported to haunt the pub, and an old woman has been seen on the stairs.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - The Olde Starr Inn, Stonegate

properties.name: Roman Legion
properties.description: Harry Martindale was working on a ladder in the cellar of the building when a group of Roman soldiers walked past him along the old Roman Road which ran through the basement. Harry was so shocked that he fell from his ladder, and despite his terror, noted that the figures disappeared into the floor at knee height and was also later able to describe the uniforms and equipment of the Romans. At the time Harry was told that his description of the soldiers was wrong, and the uniforms and equipment did not match that which was used by the invaders, although later local archaeological discoveries resulted in Harry's observations being proved correct. Harry died in 2015 and never changed his story, nor made any money from sharing it. The building also claims to be home to several other ghosts, including a dog, a black cat, George Aislaby (killed in a duel), and Frank Green, the man who converted the interior into what it is today. The Tapestry Room is reputed to have an oppressive atmosphere and is where the wife of a former owner murdered him after he conducted one extra-marital affair too many (so the story goes).
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Treasurer's House, Minster Yard

properties.name: Dean Gale
properties.description: This gentleman loved the Minster so much that in death he refused to leave and has been seen sitting while listening to sermons. A crisis apparition has also manifested here; the ghost of an officer appeared to his sister as she prayed in the church in the 1820s - at the exact moment he had died on far away shores. Also reputed to haunt the building is the sound of a dog barking.

properties.name: Wellington
properties.description: A witness outside their home heard engines but could see anything. They stepped inside their doorway but as the sound of the engines grew louder the witness stepped outside again. Looking up, the witness spotted a twin engine, dark coloured Wellington bomber fly over and past, as 'real looking and sounding as any solid aircraft'. At the time, the witness thought nothing was strange about the encounter as they were unaware that there were no flying airworthy examples of a Wellington that existed.
properties.location_name: Aberdare (South Glamorgan) - Sky over Cwmbach estate

properties.name: RF398
properties.description: The last remaining Avro Lincoln Bomber situated here is said to be haunted by a pilot seen in the cockpit, while a tape recorder left on board overnight picked up the sounds of a busy airport, though the hangar and the area were devoid of life.

properties.name: Landing Craft
properties.description: This ufo report was based on a story made up by two young people returning late from a leave pass. As time went on, the story grew, and it eventually became that an UFO had touched down near a hangar on the base, and was recovered and removed for reverse engineering.

properties.name: Run and Shout
properties.description: Having just locked the main hangar, the key orderly heard a shout and the sound of running from within. He unlocked the hangar, but no one was could be seen.

properties.name: Pilot
properties.description: A figure wearing a Second World War pilot's suit walked past one witness as he stood by a photocopier. The figure then passed through a pair of closed doors leading outside and disappeared in a field. The pilot is rumoured to be a Polish airman who hanged himself.

properties.name: Lancaster Bomber
properties.description: Around thirty witnesses claimed to have seen a silent, grey coloured aircraft resembling a Lancaster Bomber moving silently through the sky. The accounts were virtually all isolated and spanned the month.
properties.location_name: Barnoldswick (Lancashire) - Area near Rolls Royce's Bankfield factory, and area towards Craven

properties.name: Young Men
properties.description: A witness who works on the site has reported the smell of old aftershave in the corner of a warehouse which is only detectible at 20:30h, and the sounds of young men in disused buildings. A loud crash and the shaking of a concrete floor resulting in one employee running to a warehouse, convinced that racking units must have collapsed, but nothing was disturbed. On one summer night, the sound of young children playing and singing could be heard by the main gate at 23:00h, a location many miles from the nearest residential site.
properties.location_name: Barton under Needwood (Staffordshire) - Former RAF Tatenhill, now a business park

properties.name: RAF Pilot
properties.description: Media outlets reported that Rob Davies and Chris Felton had filmed a figure which resembled a 'RAF pilot' standing on the roadside late at night. Although the footage was poor, the witnesses said the figure was dressed all in beige and held his arm out as if hitchhiking. When they turned the car around and drove back for another look, the figure had vanished.
properties.location_name: Belsay (Northumberland) - A696, just outside of village

properties.name: Spitfire
properties.description: Observed flying overhead, and sometimes heard, a phantom Spitfire haunts the skies of Biggin Hill - January would appear to have become its favourite haunting month. Airmen dressed in trench coats have been reported in the village; they stop people and ask directions before disappearing.
properties.location_name: Biggin Hill (Kent) - Biggin Hill Airport & village

properties.name: Clubfoot
properties.description: The nickname given to an Australian worker on the base who blew himself up trying to sabotage a Lancaster bomber during the Second World War was maybe a bit too unkind, and he was seen for years after his death walking around on the perimeter road. A ghostly NCO also made a comeback at the former base. Sergeant Sinclair managed the loading of aircraft bombs during the Second World War; a mistake caused a bomber to explode on take-off, killing Sinclair while he was trying to flag the aircraft down to alert them to the error. For a short while his ghost was seen on the runway, arms waving wildly in the air.

properties.name: Three Airmen Playing Squash
properties.description: Many ghosts are said to exist here, mostly coming into being during the Second World War. During the war, a car full of 'merry' (ie, very drunk) pilots crashed into a hangar, killing the driver and all the passengers; the scene is now re-enacted at irregular periods. Another group of ghosts were called into being when a bomber that crashed on the landing strip lost three of its crew - they now return to play their favourite racquet game! The sound of a squash ball echoing in the deserted building, and that of soft footfalls are more common than seeing the airmen, though one man in officer's clothing has been seen a few times.
properties.location_name: Bircham Newton (Norfolk) - Wartime Airfield (owned by Construction Industry Training Board)

properties.name: Man in Flying Kit
properties.description: The warehouse was constructed at the end of the runway at Bitteswell. It is reported that in the toilets is a permanent stain on the floor in the shape of a man, and an airman in flying kit has been repeatedly seen in there.
properties.location_name: Bitteswell (Leicestershire) - Asda distribution warehouse, Magna Park (site of former airbase)

properties.name: Trolley Lady
properties.description: Throughout the 1950s, patients would report a ghostly woman in a green apron pushing a trolley through the sick quarters, disappearing through a door. In 2008, during a visit to Bletchley Park, four visitors sitting in a car heard a disembodied voice giving instructions to fill in log sheets.

properties.name: Avro Lancaster
properties.description: A large aircraft moved silently over the treetops, so low that the witness thought it would land or crash into a field. It was later identified by the witness as an Avro Lancaster.

properties.name: Wet Footprints
properties.description: A pilot killed when his new aircraft crashed into the sea was heard walking about his hut at night, his wet footfalls slopping on the floor.

properties.name: Manchester
properties.description: It is thought that the sound of droning sometimes reported over the former airbase is that of a Manchester bomber - a high loss ratio ensured that these aircraft were later replaced with Lancasters. A phantom light source has also been reported moving in the tower, and a pilot in full kit was seen once.
