properties.name: Hazy Airman
properties.description: A man and his twelve year old son watched a hazy figure dressed in a Second World War uniform walking towards them, even though the figure did not actually move closer. The uniformed man then reached down to the ground as if to pick something up and slowly faded away. Other people have seen an airman near the former Hangar One and have also heard an air raid siren. A photograph taken in 2012 is said to show a phantom figure.

properties.name: Airman
properties.description: This pub is said to be home to a phantom airman wearing a great coat, most likely to be seen on the fourth Sunday of Lent.
properties.date: Unknown, but said to be most likely to appear on Mother's Day

properties.name: Pipe Smoking Man
properties.description: When farmers worked the land where a Second World War airfield once stood, it was reported that they would occasionally be approached by a young man smoking a pipe. This figure would wish them a good evening before vanishing. It was said that this ghost was once a pilot who worked here, though no one can be quite sure of his true past.

properties.name: Headless Airman
properties.description: An airman has been observed standing around with no head - it is thought that he was decapitated as he tried to bail prior to crash landing.

properties.name: Helicopter
properties.description: A phantom helicopter was reported flying around the area at night several times over a two year period. Press and police were unable to discover who or what was behind the mysterious craft.
properties.location_name: Buxton (Derbyshire) - Skies above Harpur Hill area

properties.name: Second World War Pilot
properties.description: A few buildings on this site have reports of phantom pilots in their flying gear or RAF uniforms. Sounds of footsteps in empty areas are not uncommon, and there is also a story of ghostly singing.

properties.name: Wellington
properties.description: A man sitting in his living room at home heard an old fashioned aircraft approaching. The sound grew louder and louder, finally deafening and items within the house began to shake. The man went out to investigate, and found other people outside trying to spot the aircraft. Despite the weather being clear, no one could see the aircraft, although one old man was convinced that the sound was of a wellington bomber.

properties.name: Sounds of Driven Coach
properties.description: It was once said that when the head of the household hears a phantom coach approach but is unable to see it, a member of the family will die within a year. The hall is also reported to be haunted by a hand, possibly belonging to Lady Catherine Hungerford. During 2020 a figure was spotted on the road outside, vanishing when approached, and an air hostess wearing an old fashioned hairstyle silently moved along a hallway.
properties.date: Prior to death (coach), man and woman in January and November 2020
properties.location_name: Castle Donington (Leicestershire) - Donington Hall (currently Nottingham East Midlands Airport HQ)

properties.name: Officer
properties.description: A defunct airliner once stood at the perimeter of the airfield and was used for training. An engineer spotted lights on and returned to the flight deck where he saw a figure in the co-pilot seat who vanished in an instant. The figure was thought to be the first officer who died in a crash.

properties.name: German Pilot
properties.description: This pilot is said to have died during the Second World War when his aircraft crashed near the village. He now makes the occasional appearance in the field where he died. He was last seen by a woman driving towards the village.
properties.location_name: Chalvington with Ripe (Sussex) - Ripe Lane, heading towards village

properties.name: Silent Bomber
properties.description: A bomber thought to be from the Second World War has been observed moving silently over the skies in this area.
properties.location_name: Church Broughton (Derbyshire) - Skies over old air force base

properties.name: USAF Pilot
properties.description: Standing against the monument, this young man has been known to talk to members of the public, only to vanish after his first sentence.
properties.location_name: Cirencester (Gloucestershire) - Queen Anne's Column, in the park

properties.name: Three Pilots with Pale Faces
properties.description: Seen by two children, the three pilots are believed to be German, the sandpit being a crash site for a Messerschmitt 110 shot down during the Second World War. The area is currently a car park for a nearby factory.

properties.name: Droning of Aircraft
properties.description: Since the city was levelled by German bombers during the Second World War, some people have reported hearing 'old' aircraft over the ruins of the cathedral. In 1893, witnesses spotted a human arm holding a blue torch in the north aisle. The arm vanished when one man grabbed a poker and approached it, although manifested again a short time later.

properties.name: Passenger
properties.description: Workers on the 007 film Casino Royale reported seeing the ghost of a woman moving along the aisles of a 747 they used as a set. It is believed the phantom is of a female passenger who died from a heart attack at least thirty years previous.

properties.name: Freddy's Photograph
properties.description: A group photograph taken here in 1919 shows the face of an engineer who died three days before; Freddy Jackson had tripped and fallen into the blades of an aircraft ready for take-off. Issue 91 of Fortean Times has a good copy of the photograph.
properties.location_name: Cranwell (Lincolnshire) - Runway of post-First World War Naval Airport

properties.name: Bomber
properties.description: Looking out from their kitchen window, two witnesses watched a large grey aircraft pass silently overhead. As the aircraft was low, the witnesses thought it was going to crash, and stepped outside to investigate, although nothing could be heard. They later found out that a bomber had crashed nearby during the Second World War.

properties.name: Pilot
properties.description: The ghost of a German pilot shot down over the area during the Second World War is said to remain in the neighbourhood. One story says that a man looking for his lost wallet along Queen Elizabeth's Drive had the object handed back to him by the pilot, who appeared quite solid.
properties.location_name: Croydon (Surrey) - Area around New Addington, and Queen Elizabeth's Drive

properties.name: Pilots
properties.description: It was said a chambermaid working at this hotel quit after spotting a phantom RAF pilot. A guest also reported seeing a group of pilots sitting and singing around a fireplace in the restaurant.

properties.name: Mynarski
properties.description: Mynarski was awarded the VC for trying to save a rear gunner when the bomber they were in caught fire. His ghost dressed in flying kit has been seen around the airport's hotel.

properties.name: War Staff
properties.description: Staff have reported seeing figures dressed in Second World War clothing around the airport.
properties.location_name: Derry / Londonderry (County Derry / County Londonderry) - City of Derry Airport (formally Eglinton air base)

properties.name: Officer on Bike
properties.description: Cycling along on an old bike, this phantom RAF officer once stopped and asked two guards for the control tower to be opened for him. When they agreed, the officer vanished. On other occasions, lights were reported moving around the control tower, though it would be locked and empty.

properties.name: Aircraft of the Future
properties.description: A lost pilot recalled flying over disused airfield in the 1930s and saw it operational, with yellow RAF aircraft on the ground - these did not exist at the time. He later checked and found the airfield really was unused. A few years later, the airfield was reopened for training purposes, and aircraft based there painted yellow.

properties.name: B17 Crew
properties.description: The control tower is haunted by crew from a B17 which hit the building while attempting a landing. Other witnesses report hearing aircraft flying low overhead, though nothing can be seen, while hangar 5 is another paranormal hotspot.

properties.name: Headless Airman
properties.description: Seen parachuting down from the skies, this decapitated airman is thought to date from the Second World War.
properties.location_name: East Cowes (Isle of Wight) - Skies above area

properties.name: Tall American
properties.description: This USAAF officer has been seen several times in the old watch tower. He is thought to have been killed when a Flying Fortress crash landed near the site in 1944. The same figure is thought to have been seen on the runway - dragging his parachute behind him, he slowly moves towards the control tower.
properties.location_name: East Kirkby (Lincolnshire) - East Kirkby Watch Tower (now an aviation heritage centre)

properties.name: Fred
properties.description: During the 1980s and 1990s, workers at the MOD transport section would say 'hello' to Fred, the resident ghost. It was said that if Fred were not acknowledged, the phantom would open lockers and move things around. It was thought that Fred was a worker at the site during the Second World War; he died after walking into a propeller. The ghost of a young man in a grey RAF uniform was encountered in 1974, as it walked over to a witness and touched their forehead before vanishing.

properties.name: Morse Code
properties.description: A family living in the tower after the war reported hearing Morse messages being tapped out, seeing pilots dressed in flying gear, and even once watching a large aeroplane taking off from the disused runway, baring the code letters PM.
properties.location_name: Elsham Wold (Lincolnshire) - Former RAF base and flight tower (no longer standing)

properties.name: Hitchhiker
properties.description: For several days, a car driver found himself giving a lift to a Second World War pilot, who would suddenly appear in the back seat of his car when he reached a certain point of his journey. This stopped once the driver started taking a different route.

properties.name: Voices
properties.description: Left empty a few years after the war, locals reported still hearing voices coming from the control tower. Figures in RAF uniform were also reported in the area, as were men dressed in aircrew gear. An inter-denominational prayer team visited the site in 2006, and no reports of haunting activity has been reported since.
properties.location_name: Fiskerton (Lincolnshire) - Former RAF Fiskerton control tower (no longer standing)

properties.name: Tail End Charlie
properties.description: The headless rear gunner that haunts this area accidently walked into the propellers of a Lancaster.

properties.name: Pilot
properties.description: A phantom pilot dressed in his RAF clothing has been reported haunting the site of his old base.
properties.location_name: Grangemouth (Falkirk) - Inchyra Road, site of former RAF airport

properties.name: Tiny Harris
properties.description: Joe 'Tiny' Harris, a former telephone operator during the Second World War, has been seen standing in the shadows, heard walking down corridors, and is blamed for flicking lights on and off.
properties.location_name: Grantham (Lincolnshire) - Old RAF Spitalgate, now Royal Corps of Transport base?

properties.name: German Pilot
properties.description: The site of a former RAF base, investigators belief that the pilot was shot down whilst on a bombing run over the area.
properties.location_name: Gravesend (Kent) - Private house on the River View Park estate

properties.name: Parachutist
properties.description: A pilot who bailed from his aircraft above the airfield landed on the tower while in a controlled descent, though died falling from the tower onto the ground. His shade has been seen slowly parachuting onto the building.
properties.location_name: Great Driffield (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Driffield RAF base

properties.name: Three Men
properties.description: Three men wearing long coats and large boots were spotted on the road by a driver. Convincing they were going to hit one of the men, the driver slammed on their brakes; the figures vanished, leaving the witness quite shocked. The witness was later told that the area was once a US air base during the Second World War, and that other people had encountered phantom airmen and even heard old aircraft in the area.
properties.location_name: Great Waldingfield (Suffolk) - Road to Sudbury, near the public house

properties.name: Airman with Hand Grenade
properties.description: Several former pilots walk this old RAF base, both along the aging airstrips and the perimeter road. One of the ghosts that has chosen the perimeter to haunt is said to have blown himself up with a grenade after being declared unfit to fly (and may have been seen in a bungalow in 1969). Another spirit has appeared by the memorial laid for the Number 100 squadron. More recently, a dull warm white has been spotted drifting along the old runways, and a man walking his dog was later asked about the person dressed in dark clothing who had earlier walked by his side; the man had not encountered any other person during his walk.
properties.location_name: Grimsby (Lincolnshire) - Former RAF Waltham (aka RAF Grimsby)

properties.name: Headless Airman wanting Lift
properties.description: After losing his head in a flying accident, the apparition of an American pilot has been seen thumbing a lift on the roadside.
properties.location_name: Hadstock (Essex) - The B1052 leading into Hadstock

properties.name: US Airmen
properties.description: Heading towards an old USAAF airfield, this car full of military personnel slowly fades from view as travels the road.

properties.name: Furniture Mover
properties.description: A family living in a semidetached house were kept awake for several nights by loud knocks coming from their neighbour's home. One night the sounds were incredibly loud, so the following morning they went next door to complain, only to find their neighbour now arriving home after spending the evening away. Upon investigation, it was discovered that all the furnishings in the house were moved around, though no money or credit cards were stolen. The RAF took the case so seriously they called in a bomb squad to ensure that an explosive device had not been planted by the mysterious intruder.
properties.location_name: Haverfordwest (Dyfed) - Unnamed house in RAF Brawdy

properties.name: Engine
properties.description: Sounding like it belongs to a Second World War aircraft, the engine has been heard in the skies, but nothing ever seen. One witness claimed it sounded like a doodlebug - a Second World War German rocket.

properties.name: Bomber?
properties.description: Driving home for lunch, this witness watched a large aircraft, possibly a Second World War bomber, pass overhead and descend towards a railway line. The witness thought the craft had crashed, so climbed out of the car and ran over to a brick wall which had obscured the view of the falling aircraft. There was nothing there, and then the witness realised the aircraft had been completely silent as it passed above.

properties.name: Pilot on Fire
properties.description: Two ghosts have been reported at this former Second World War airbase. A pilot who crashed on landing runs down the runway with his clothes on fire, while an engineer who lost his arm in machinery staggers around screaming. A ghostly rendition of the Missouri Waltz is also reported.

properties.name: Henry
properties.description: Spoken about by locals, the ghost known as Henry, an USAF pilot in flying kit, is said to have ginger hair.

properties.name: Pilot?
properties.description: Several dog walkers had seen a man dressed in black with a white stripe on his outfit along this road, his silence when spoken to a topic of discussion between the pet owners. One dog owner then reported that the man had disappeared in front of their eyes and refused to walk his dog in the same area. One person researched the area and found that a German aircraft had crashed here, speculating that the man in black was a pilot.

properties.name: Aeroplane Crash
properties.description: A person called police after hearing a loud crash and screams for help in the woods, which they thought to be an aircraft crash. On investigation, nothing could be found.

properties.name: Cigarette Smoking Man
properties.description: In 1983 two RAF police officers spotted a man dressed in Second World War flying kit smoking a cigarette between two barbed wire fences. They challenged the man, who turned, walked through one of the fences, and faded away. Three years prior to this encounter, a converted H block was home to an entity which could be heard breathing heavily and would occasionally attack people in their beds. A local story reports that a USAF bomber exploded on take-off during the war, and one of the crew's bodies was never found.

properties.name: Second World War Aircraft
properties.description: Witness Tony Ingle watched a totally silent aircraft fly past him, a mere 15 meters above the ground, before it dived behind a hill. Expecting to find the crash site, Mr Ingle investigated but found nothing there.
properties.location_name: Hope (Derbyshire) - Aston Lane, near the railway bridge, skies over

properties.name: Flying Fortress
properties.description: A ghostly B-17 Flying Fortress reportedly passes overhead to land on the remains of the runway at this former airfield.

properties.name: Children Playing
properties.description: One witness reported hearing schoolchildren playing on the site, although it was late at night, and seeing pilots walking around the area.
properties.location_name: Hornchurch (Essex) - Site of RAF Hornchurch (no longer operational)

properties.name: Hitching Airman
properties.description: The man dressed in an old RAF uniform tries to hitch a lift to the now ruined airbase. There are also reports of the sounds of an old aircraft in the skies and along the former runways.
properties.location_name: Kelstern (Lincolnshire) - Road leading to the former RAF Kelstern

properties.name: Screaming
properties.description: The phantom screams of pain are thought to date from an air raid which killed many French pilots. One of these men has also been observed lurking by an old hangar and may have been spotted during the early 1990s by two witnesses in the clubhouse.
properties.location_name: Kirton in Lindsey (Lincolnshire) - Former RAF base

properties.name: Smoking RAF Pilot
properties.description: A phantom hitchhiker was seen along the road, dressed as a RAF pilot. An American policeman picked him up, only to find that the pilot had disappeared a few minutes later.
properties.location_name: Lakenheath (Suffolk) - Lakenheath Air Force Base, perimeter road

properties.name: Australian Pilot
properties.description: Seen walking across the airfield on bright moonlit nights, the ghost here is believed to be that of an Australian pilot who died when his bomber crashed during the Second World War.

properties.name: RAF Guard
properties.description: In 1978 one witness watched a man in a uniform walk down a set of steps. The figure smiled at the witness before walking through a wall. While driving away from the site in 1983, a director of a business operating from the former base thought he had hit a uniformed man who had appeared in front of his car. He stopped and looked for what he assumed was an injured person but could find nothing. The same thing happened to him again a few weeks later.

properties.name: Crash and US Airmen
properties.description: The sound of a crashing aircraft has been reported amongst the trees, and misty figures dressed in US AAF uniform have been seen - always dissipating when approached. Screaming was once heard coming from one end of a former runway, though when the sound was investigated, nothing was found.
properties.location_name: Langham (Norfolk) - Apple Orchard, once the World War 2 airbase

properties.name: Officer
properties.description: A witness entering the control tower briefly spotted an RAF officer before the figure vanished. The site ceased being used by the RAF in 1939.

properties.name: Crew
properties.description: The crew from a bomber have been seen walking together here, and when not seen they have been heard laughing and joking.

properties.name: Sounds of Action
properties.description: A witness recalled how he and a friend were walking across the deserted airfield at night and seeing a strange light in the control tower. Both knowing that the building should have been empty, the illumination unnerved them slightly. As they passed by a hangar, they were suddenly surrounded by the sounds of activity - they could hear the hangar doors screeching open, the engines of aircraft starting up, and the patter of many pairs of feet running by them, though nothing could be seen.
properties.location_name: Lindholme (South Yorkshire) - Former Second World War Air Force Base

properties.name: Pete the Pole
properties.description: A Polish airman who died when he crash-landed, 'Pete the Pole', aka 'Lindholme Willie', turned up at the airbase several times in the years following his death. Even when the former airbase was replaced by a prison in later years, the pilot reportedly turned up in prisoner's cells. In 1957 a misty shape was seen walking along the runway before vanishing, while in 1977 a shuffling figure crossing the parade square was spotted by an Air Traffic Assistant. The figure disappeared into the shadows by a sealed door.
properties.date: Reputed mid-to-late-twentieth century, sightings in November 1957 and 1977
properties.location_name: Lindholme (South Yorkshire) - Former Second World War Air Force Base

properties.name: WO Hodgson
properties.description: A former pilot at the base, Hodgson's ashes were scattered across the runway and a small plaque erected in the 1950s. When the memorial was moved thirty years later, a spectral pilot was seen several times around the control tower. The officer's mess is also thought to be haunted, this time by a pilot who died in 1978 when his Provost crashed.
properties.location_name: Linton on Ouse (North Yorkshire) - RAF Linton

properties.name: Halifax
properties.description: This phantom bomber only made itself visible to a crew once inside their aircraft, if they were all going to die together.
properties.location_name: Lissett (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Former Second World War airfield

properties.name: Captain Scholz
properties.description: Fifty years after dying when his Mustang crash landed, Captain Scholz, dressed in his flying suit, appeared in the back seat of a car as it drove past the airfield. The apparition was only visible for a few seconds.
properties.location_name: Little Walden (Essex) - Little Walden Airfield, near Saffron Walden

properties.name: Thomas Campbell Black
properties.description: Black died in an accident on the runway of the airfield in 1936 - his shadowy form has been seen walking around the area where the old hangars stood.
properties.location_name: Liverpool (Merseyside) - Liverpool Airport, formerly known as Speke

properties.name: Wellington Bomber
properties.description: A training zone for Second World War crews, one assumes this phantom belongs to a group that did not make the grade.
properties.location_name: Llandovery (Dyfed) - Towy Valley, between Llandovery & Llandeilo

properties.name: Bomber
properties.description: A father and his young son were walking along the misty runway at this disused airfield. They paused to drink tea and noticed that there was a red flashing light on the control tower, just visible through the mist. The wind suddenly picked up, and as the mist cleared, they both spotted the silhouette of a four engine Second World War bomber, moving silently, banking over the airfield.

properties.name: Wartime Bomber
properties.description: A family on holiday in the area watched a Second World War bomber fly over the sea and vanish inland, diving behind a hill as if going to crash. They noticed there was no sound accompanying the aircraft. A similar, or the same, aircraft was observed by two witnesses some thirty years later.
properties.location_name: Llysfaen (Clwyd) - Colwyn Bay area, skies over

properties.name: Bomber
properties.description: A wife and her husband camping here heard the droning of what they believed was an old wartime bomber coming into land. They could not see anything, and the sound ceased without warning. When talking to the campsite owner the following morning he said they were not the first to report such sounds.

properties.name: RB-B29 Bomber
properties.description: In 1997, a dozen witnesses in different areas of the valley called emergency services after they watch an aircraft crash in the valley one spring night in 1997. A search of the area revealed no evidence of an accident, and locals blamed the ghost of RB-B29 'Over Exposed' that crashed just after the Second World War. Since 1997, a number of hikers have reported seeing a large black and silver aircraft in the area. The captain of that aircraft is also said to walk the area in which he died. Other sources say the plane is a Skytrain transport or a F13 which crashed in 1946.

properties.name: Two Former Airmen
properties.description: A training base for fighter pilots during the Second World War, a couple of the trainees have refused to go away post-mortem and are blamed for moving chairs and removing small items left laying around. The site has now been converted into a holiday village, with most of the original buildings destroyed.
properties.location_name: Maes Artro, Llanbedr (Gwynedd) - Former Second World War airbase

properties.name: Figure in Greatcoat
properties.description: Formerly RAF Manby, the area is haunted by a Second World War pilot. He is sometimes seen wearing a long coat, and at others wearing his flying gear. The drone of aircraft engines is sometimes heard over the old runways.
properties.location_name: Manby (Lincolnshire) - Tedder Hall, and surrounding area

properties.name: Old Bare Footed Man
properties.description: Observed in what was once the barracks of the 613 RAF Squadron, this phantom old man has been reported by cleaners and late working staff. The occasional scream has also been heard coming from the building.

properties.name: Former Pilot
properties.description: A MoD fireman was on kitchen duty in the early hours of the morning when he spotted a semi-translucent figure wearing a flying helmet, Irvin jacket and Mae West. The witness quickly left the area and was later told that others had seen the entity. It was generally thought the ghost to be a pilot who crashed into a snow drift during the Second World War.

properties.name: Pilot
properties.description: This pub is said to be haunted by the ghost of a pilot who crashed en route back to his airbase located at Martlesham.

properties.name: Long Dead Airman
properties.description: The site of a major airfield during the Second World War, the Police Headquarters and the surrounding heathland has a reputation of being haunted by long dead pilots. Several police officers have seen such shades, while more recently, owners of a shop reported seeing a detached human shadow outside their establishment.
properties.location_name: Martlesham Heath (Suffolk) - Police HQ, heathland, and shopping area

properties.name: Catherine Bystock
properties.description: Killed in a motorcycle accident near the base during World War II, Catherine is said to have returned; she flags down motorists along the road and asks them to help her boyfriend who is injured down the road, only to vanish if assistance is granted.
properties.location_name: Metheringham (Lincolnshire) - Perimeter track of the former RAF base

properties.name: Old Roger
properties.description: Old Roger is a figure that is said to primarily haunt the Norfolk Breckland, playing on a beaten up flute that summons storms. During the war, one man claimed to see Roger in the RAF base, the storm following the sighting resulted in an impending German bombing raid on Mildenhall being cancelled. An aircrew also reported seeing an old First World War biplane land on the runway, before taking off again, during a particularly foggy night during the 1940s.

properties.name: Trainee Pilot
properties.description: This World War I trainee died while out on his first solo flight - his ghost appeared in the base commander's room three times, before the room was locked up and no longer used. He was also seen flying around in his biplane, up until the end of the Second World War. A phantom Second World War RAF Officer was seen around the base during the latter years of the conflict. It was thought that he was killed when a mechanic with a grudge tampered with his aircraft's engine. In 2010 it was reported that an old radio set at the heritage centre continued to pick up Second World War transmissions, including Churchill speeches.

properties.name: Silhouette
properties.description: A phantom airman walks around these roads, vanishing if a car slows down close to him.
properties.location_name: Newton (Nottinghamshire) - RAF Newton (no longer operational) and roads in the area (including Fosse Way)

properties.name: Fordson Tractor
properties.description: A visitor to the site heard what he first thought was an old Fordson tractor ticking over, though the sound later changed to an aircraft carrying out pre-flight checks.
properties.location_name: Normanton (West Yorkshire) - RAF Bottesford (aka RAF Normanton) - no longer operational

properties.name: Tobacco
properties.description: The smell of tobacco is said to fill the air in the top hangar, even though no smokers are present. Mild poltergeist activity is reported, and occasionally a phantom man is seen wearing dark coveralls.

properties.name: Roar of Engines
properties.description: The sound of old aircraft engines warming up has been reported coming from a hangar on this site, even though the building in question is empty.
properties.location_name: North Pickenham (Norfolk) - Hangar at Second World War Airfield

properties.name: WAAF member
properties.description: Killed in a bombing raid, this member of the Woman's Auxiliary Air Force was heard walking around the area where the bomb hit for a dozen years after her death. Another ghost was blamed for removing the handset off the telephones on the base - a heavily wounded airman died during the war as he picked up the receiver of a phone, and it was thought he continued to try to make the call for many years later. Finally, the base is also reputedly haunted by a man who was pinned to a tree after being caught in an explosion.

properties.name: S for Sugar
properties.description: The Lancaster Bomber 'S for Sugar', which is kept on site here, still retains its crew from the Second World War in spirit form. A gunner has been observed sitting in one of the turrets, while the sounds of people working on the aircraft have been recorded late at night.

properties.name: Officer
properties.description: This witness spotted an airman in officer uniform standing at ease and watching them. The witness speculated that the ghost was related to a Lancaster bomber which crashed nearby in 1943.
properties.location_name: Old Leake (Lincolnshire) - Private location, Chapel Road

properties.name: Second World War Officer
properties.description: The Newcastle Journal reported that lights would turn themselves on and off in the sickbay, and an internal door which was kept locked would be open by morning. Personnel near the sickbay would hear footsteps and other strange noises at night, even though the area was secure. Some blamed the ghost of an officer killed on site during the Second World War.

properties.name: German Pilot
properties.description: Dying after his aircraft crashed nearby, this the Second World War pilot is still occasionally seen walking around this former village.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Addington (Greater London) - General area

properties.name: Digger Harry
properties.description: Harry was so upset when his wife died that he buried her close to his cottage without telling anyone. The authorities, believing Harry had killed her, locked him up for several months before releasing him because of his age. During his incarceration, Harry forgot where his wife was buried, and spent the remainder of his days trying to find her. His tragic phantom was said to continue to roam the area carrying a shovel.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Addington (Greater London) - Wooded area near Courtwood Lane

properties.name: Airmen
properties.description: Several witnesses were said to have seen a couple of airmen standing on the hard shoulder of the A2. One wore a leather flying helmet while the other appeared to have a medal or piece of metal near his throat. A local tale says that a Heinkel bomber crashed nearby, killing the crew who could not bail out in time, and it has been speculated that the figures belong to this aircraft.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Bexley (Greater London) - A2 London-bound between Heath turn-off and Black Prince turn-off

properties.name: Heavy Breathing
properties.description: This ghost was heard several times, breathing heavily around female members of staff, but never being seen. A second entity wears a bowler hat and has been known to ask about the whereabouts of his briefcase - both case and man were in an aircraft that crashed in the late 1940s, killing all on board. A phantom Dick Turpin rides a brown horse across the runways (or cargo sheds), while cleaning staff during the 1970s reported a presence which held them down, applying pressure around their throats. Finally, prior to the airport being built, a phantom Saxon prince reputedly haunted the area close to a pond where he drowned.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Heathrow Airport (Greater London) - General area

properties.name: Lost Pilot
properties.description: Seen walking along the east/west runway of this former fighter base, a ghostly figure wears a pilot's uniform dating back to the Second World War. The residence of the station commander is also reputedly haunted, with one occupant hearing someone sitting on a creaky seat throughout the night and feeling its presence (although it was described as non-malevolent). One story says the Officers' Mess is home to a couple of ghosts, while on a road outside the base, a phantom Polish pilot from Second World War asks for directions - the man died when his aircraft crashed a short distance away.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Northolt (Greater London) - RAF Northolt

properties.name: Wet Footprints and Droning
properties.description: Locals have reportedly heard the droning of phantom aircraft over the disused airfields, while the control tower has reappearing 'wet footprints', which begin and end in inexplicable places. One female witness reported seeing the reflection in glass of a man in USAAF uniform who smiled at her. The witness said 'hi' and turned to the figure, but no one was there.
properties.location_name: Parham (Suffolk) - Disused Airfield, and Control Tower

properties.name: Burning Spitfire
properties.description: A phantom burning spitfire was claimed to haunt this airfield. Another ghost reputedly at home here is a pilot who frequents the control tower, although his history is unknown.

properties.name: Spitfire
properties.description: The story of a ghostly spitfire appearing here was carried in the local press; it was actually Spitfire BM597, owned by the Historic Aircraft Collection, and was operating from Pent Farm Aerodrome on the day in question.

properties.name: Black Cat
properties.description: Two Czech pilots died here on 22 October 1941, crashing in the field as they ran out of fuel. A local farmer rushed to the field trying to save them but was beaten back by the flames. He did, however, see a black cat emerge from the burning wreckage. The creature made its home with a local old woman, and when she died, the cat vanished, but appears once every ten years at the crash site.
properties.date: 22 October, every ten years since 1941 (next in 2021) (reoccurring)
properties.location_name: Poynton Green (Shropshire) - Field in the village

properties.name: MP
properties.description: Seen by huts near the former Officer's Mess, a ghostly Military Policeman is said to walk around the area. There are also reports of phantom aircraft, including a P-47 and a B-17.

properties.name: Crashing Aircraft
properties.description: Now little more than a collection of old huts, the area is haunted by the sounds of crashing Second World War aircraft, shouting airmen, and other wartime noises.

properties.name: Hugh Ripley
properties.description: The town has a famous ritual which involves blowing the Charter Horn, traditionally used to warn against Viking raiders before its use became ritual. Hugh was the first mayor of Ripon and was obsessed with ensuring that the horn was blown correctly, watching over its use every day. If the horn is not blown correctly, Hugh will appear and gaze out of the attic window disapprovingly. Some say the town will be hit by disaster if the horn is not respected.

properties.name: Lancaster Bomber
properties.description: A father and daughter driving eastbound spotted a Lancaster bomber flying nearby, smoke coming from a left right hand engine. They passed under Scammonden Bridge, and after coming out were no longer able to see the aircraft.

properties.name: Disappearing L'il Butch
properties.description: Towards the end of the Second World War, L'il Butch was shot down during a bombing run on Germany, but briefly reappeared at the air base a few months later, as if it had just landed. Little exists of the airfield now - an industrial site now stands on the area, and fields cut off access to the runways.

properties.name: Lancaster Bomber
properties.description: Richard and Helen Jephson were reported in local news as having witnessed a Lancaster bomber which flew low and silent above them. They expected the aircraft to crash into a nearby wooded area, but it did not happen.
properties.location_name: Rowsley (Derbyshire) - Road by an unnamed retail outlet

properties.name: B-17 Crew?
properties.description: There were a handful of reports of a phantom aircrew made during the 1960s, at this base once used by the USAAF. The B-17 may have been seen in 1994, moving silently overhead. More recently, a man who lived on the site of the former airfield reported seeing a pair of legs in dress uniform which manifested in his home, and the sounds of aircraft starting up.

properties.name: Pilots in Hangar
properties.description: A disastrous bomber landing resulted in almost a dozen deaths, a wrecked farmhouse, and three more bombers ruined as they sat on the ground - the crew of the aircraft responsible have been observed in a hangar on the site.

properties.name: USAAF Pilot in Car
properties.description: Exactly fifty years after dying in a flying accident (1943), the ghost of an USAAF pilot appeared in the back seat of a woman's car, as she drove past the old crash site of the plane in which he flew.
properties.location_name: Saffron Walden (Essex) - B1052, between Saffron Walden and Hadstock

properties.name: Pilot
properties.description: This former Second World War radar station, and later nuclear war bunker, is reputedly home to a pilot who walks around the staircase after dark. Disembodied footsteps have also been heard.

properties.name: Sitting Airman
properties.description: This lonely airman sits by the side of the road, patiently waiting for a lift which has never yet arrived.

properties.name: Lt Peter Salter
properties.description: During the First World War a pilot reported greeting Lieutenant Salter as he walked into the officer's mess, and the Lieutenant responded in kind; unbeknown to the witness, Salter had just died in an air crash many miles away.

properties.name: Pilot in Lifejacket
properties.description: This pilot, thought to date from the Second World War, has been seen in the control tower at the air force base. He may have been the source of the disembodied voice which said 'hello!' to a man walking through an empty hangar. Chatting has been heard in the crew room, though when investigated it has been found devoid of life. Guy Gibson's dog is also reported to haunt the base, killed on the road outside in 1943.

properties.name: Shadow-like Man
properties.description: Two men travelling back to Waddington spotted a figure wearing a long cloak or tailcoat disappearing into a hedge row towards the airbase. One witness said the figure was transparent or shadowlike and did not appear to have any legs.
properties.location_name: Scopwick (Lincolnshire) - Road parallel to RAF Digby

properties.name: RAF Pilot
properties.description: This pilot died when his spitfire crashed into the marshes during the Second World War. This tall, dark uniformed man has been reported in several buildings along the road. Some also report that the sound of a German aircraft crashing into the marshes here can still be heard.
properties.location_name: SE28 (Greater London) - Abbey Road and Tavy Bridge (former Erith Marshes), Thamesmead

properties.name: Pilot?
properties.description: A man spotted a figure wearing a flying jacket and scarf walking across the foggy, open playing fields. While walking towards the witness, the figure suddenly vanished.

properties.name: Downed Vickers
properties.description: In 1929 an old Vickers aircraft crashed in this field, killing its two crew members. The crash was heard several times over the next few years by locals and was even reportedly seen, glowing blue in the pale moonlight.
properties.location_name: Shepperton on Thames (Surrey) - Field near Ferry Lane

properties.name: Fred
properties.description: Nicknamed 'Fred', this phantom resembles a young RAF pilot in overalls. Due to the proximity of the cafe to the former RAF base, it is speculated he was based there during the Second World War.

properties.name: Guilt Ridden Officer
properties.description: This phantom officer in RAF uniform is thought to return briefly around May time, in regret for sending dozens of young men off to their deaths, in particular a close friend of whom he was particularly fond. The remaining control tower is thought to be particularly haunted, with footsteps and crashes heard and a poltergeist like entity throwing stones and broken glass at people who venture too close.
properties.location_name: Sleaford (Lincolnshire) - Colbey Grange - former RAF base

properties.name: Vanishing John
properties.description: A female witness reported interaction with the ghost of a Second World War RAF officer at this site. The figure in brown walked up to her, introduced himself as 'John' and, as he touched her hand, vanished.

properties.name: Crashing Aircraft
properties.description: Several people driving together during the Second World War witnessed a plane crash here - when they investigated, all they could find was the monument to the first two members of the Royal Flying Corps to die in a plane crash.
properties.location_name: Stonehenge (Wiltshire) - Small monument to two pilots

properties.name: Lancaster Pilot
properties.description: This decapitated shade nicknamed 'Strubby' is the result of a Lancaster bomber crash near the hangar area during the Second World War.
properties.location_name: Strubby (Lincolnshire) - Second World War RAF base

properties.name: George & Co
properties.description: Several entities are reputed to haunt this museum. The bottom half of a man has been seen walking around the hangars, wearing black trousers but no shoes. A phantom hound reputedly remains here, and poltergeist activity has also been reported - one witness watched an old fashioned gramophone, part of a static display, start to play unaided.
properties.location_name: Sunderland (Tyne and Wear) - North East Aircraft Museum

properties.name: Pilot in the Fog
properties.description: An RAF police officer encountered the apparition of an RAF pilot in full flying gear in the fog. The phantom dissolved before him. The witness tried to report it as an incident and was told not to bother as it had been seen before. Others on site had heard an aircraft flying low as if to land, though no aircraft would touch down.

properties.name: Screaming Women
properties.description: Killed by a bomb during the Second World War, members of the Woman's Auxiliary Air Force can still be heard screaming on this site.

properties.name: Andrew Mynarski
properties.description: Mynarski died after trying to save a trapped pilot in a Lancaster bomber as it dived towards earth. He was awarded the VC for his efforts. Mynarski's restless spirit currently walks the area he was stationed during the war and was seen outside the control room and hangars in the 1950s. Another version of the story names the phantom as a Canadian pilot named Mullen. In 1982 a pilot reported feeling a weight on his bed which vanished when he told it to go away, and although this could be sleep paralysis, the pilot researched further and found 20 more people who had experienced the same event. Another pilot reported many crews would sleep with the lights left on, and after waking to the smell of burning flesh (which swiftly dissipated), refused to stay in the hotel again.
properties.date: 1950s onwards, pilot seen in hotel 1982 and detected 1990s
properties.location_name: Teeside (Durham) - Teeside International Airport (was Middleton St George airfield), hangars and nearby St George Hotel

properties.name: Time for Breakfast
properties.description: One man reported smelling a full English breakfast being cooked, though he was in the middle of an empty car park. He was later told that the location where he stood was once the site of mess tents. Other places on the airfield have their own ghosts. The Officer's Mess is home to ghostly footsteps, while policemen patrolling the airfield once peered into an empty hut, only to see a group of wartime airmen playing cards!

properties.name: Voice over the Tannoy
properties.description: This echoing voice has been heard on the ground here using a non-existent tannoy, barking orders at crews who died over fifty years ago. The sound is sometimes accompanied by the sound of a bomber warming up before take-off.
properties.location_name: Tibenham (Norfolk) - Old Second World War Airbase

properties.name: Halifax
properties.description: A witness watched a low flying, four engine aircraft from the Second World War fly overhead. The witness later realised it was a Halifax bomber - there are no operational Halifaxs left.

properties.name: Airman with Silk Scarf
properties.description: Thought to be another casualty of the Second World War, this spectral airman was last seen by the base's cook.

properties.name: Lost Pilot
properties.description: This shadowy pilot was killed when his First World War aircraft crash landed in nearby fields. He was reported several times by men on guard duty.
properties.location_name: Upavon (Wiltshire) - Perimeter around former RAF Upavon

properties.name: Flash of Person
properties.description: While out walking with her husband, this witness briefly spotted the flashing image of a person. The witness said the area was completely void of birds, insects or other wildlife.

properties.name: Faceless Motorcyclist
properties.description: The motorcyclist was seen early in the morning, dressed in old clothing. It is thought he was stationed on this site during the Second World War, which was a RAF base. Other airfield ghosts reported around the area include a Dutch pilot killed when his airplane crashed in fog in the 1930s and three nuns who died when their plane crashed in 1947. Phantom singing has also been reported on the estate.

properties.name: Bomber Crewman
properties.description: Staff at the private company which took over this industrial unit frequently reported a ghostly figure dressed in flying kit or feeling his presence. It was speculated that he was a local man killed when his bomber crash landed nearby - the payload exploded, killing everyone on board.
properties.location_name: Wantage (Oxfordshire) - Former site of RAF Grove (currently an industrial unit)

properties.name: Lancaster Crew
properties.description: The crew of a Lancaster bomber which crashed shortly after take-off have been observed crossing the old runway. The Glenn Miller hangar is one of the last places the big band member played before being killed; one account says the place is eerily quiet with local wildlife shunning the area, while another says band music has been heard coming from the hangar.

properties.name: Navigator
properties.description: This member of the RAF ran into the rotating blades of a stationary aircraft - his shade has been seen and heard retracing his final steps.

properties.name: Kitted Out Airman
properties.description: The haunt of a Second World War airman, this figure may also be to blame for throwing bricks at passing vehicles.
properties.location_name: West Malling (Kent) - Old World War 2 Airfield

properties.name: Dark Form
properties.description: An officer taking part in a night exercise on the airfield with a group of ATC cadets encountered several dark shapes. The warm night air quickly turned icy cold, and the officer watched as one of the dark shapes ran towards him. He sidestepped the figure, and it vanished as it passed him. The man later mentioned his experience to a colleague and was overheard by a nearby airman who previously had a similar encounter.
properties.location_name: Weston on the Green (Oxfordshire) - RAF Weston on the Green, south-western side of the airfield on the perimeter track

properties.name: Biplane
properties.description: The plane was blown out of the sky in during a storm in the mid-1930s - now when the weather conditions are bad, the aircraft can be heard struggling in the wind.

properties.name: Pop
properties.description: Michael Bentine, of The Goon Show fame, worked here during the war. After a period of leave, Bentine headed towards his hut and walked past a friend and fellow officer known as 'Pop', whom he greeted. Pop nodded his response and continued walking. The following morning, Michael discovered Pop had died two nights previous. A nearby building is also reported haunted, this time by a WAAF officer who killed herself after her boyfriend died during a bombing raid over Germany.

properties.name: Morse
properties.description: There are a few reports (or rumours) of the faint tapping of Morse code coming from this empty building. A driver who passed the tower in the mid-1990s briefly spotted an airman in beige along the road.

properties.name: Bomber Trying to Land
properties.description: Crashing into the control tower during the Second World War, the bomber is said to re-enact the fatal accident, either silently descending until it reaches ground where it disappears or else it hits the Control Tower. Ghostly airmen have also been seen around the site of the crash, and a former MP reported feeling a presence and lights coming on and unexplained loud bangs in the control tower. They also heard footsteps in the empty building corridor in what was the old hangar for 1 squadron, together with observing shadows moving into rooms.
properties.date: Second World War onwards, last witnessed 1990?

properties.name: White Haired Woman Standing in the Corner
properties.description: The club has a reputation for being haunted by ex-service personnel, but an old woman dressed in a night-shirt has also been seen here, standing in the corner of the bar.
properties.location_name: Woodbridge (Suffolk) - RAF Woodbridge, Bar and Club

properties.name: Pet Photograph
properties.description: A photograph of a group of airmen revealed a black dog standing at the end of the line-up that was not spotted at the time by neither the photographer nor any of the pilots. It was later suggested the entity was Guy Gibson's pet dog that had been run over a few months before. During the 1980s, a team of engineers working at night spotted a pilot in 'old flying gear' walking along a corridor. One of the team went to investigate, only to find that the pilot had disappeared.
properties.location_name: Woodhall Spa (Lincolnshire) - Former Second World War airfield

properties.name: Bomber
properties.description: A ghostly bomber, covered in flames, is said to land on this airfield once a year (although the date is not known).
properties.date: Unknown, but said to be a yearly occurrence

properties.name: Helicopter Pilot
properties.description: A woman photographed alone in a helicopter found, after her prints her developed, a figure sitting next to her in the pilot's seat. Though the phantom figure could have been created by lighting and reflection, others are less sure.

properties.name: The Good Musician
properties.description: A small band of musicians were performing around the city when they found themselves joined by another unannounced member. He played the violin particularly well, and as the band completed a song standing by Beedham's Court, their new-found violinist vanished, leaving only the strong smell of brimstone.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Passageway once known as Beedham's Court, off Skeldergate

properties.name: Drunken Airman
properties.description: Towards the end of the Second World War, a drunken Canadian pilot fell from an upstairs window in the building and broke his neck on the pavement below. Since then, this ghost has reportedly haunted the bedroom from which he fell.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - The Golden Fleece public house, Pavement Street

properties.name: Bell Ringing
properties.description: The East of Scotland Paranormal Society investigated this building and claimed to have heard footsteps, whispering, and the sound of a bell.

properties.name: Lost Town
properties.description: Swallowed by the sea, church bells belonging to a town lost off the coast here can still be heard on quiet evenings. They say that at low tides one can see sunken tree trunks.
properties.date: Weather Dependent: Calm nights, or summer (reoccurring)

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: Much of Aldeburgh has been taken by the sea, leading to the belief that the bells from sunken churches could sometimes be heard under the waves.

properties.name: Singing
properties.description: Though disused, locals have reported singing and bell ringing from this aging chapel.

properties.name: Ringing
properties.description: The echoes of phantom bells have been heard coming from beneath the waters here.

properties.name: Stubborn Bell
properties.description: When the priory bells were removed from the site, the Great Bell did not want to leave its location. Eventually the bell was dragged away (after a single unhappy peal) by man and ox to its new home in Exeter Cathedral, where it promptly cracked once mounted.

properties.name: Chime
properties.description: The sounds of footsteps and the soft ringing of a bell haunt this old building.

properties.name: Kilmigrol
properties.description: A town lost off the coast of Blackpool many years ago, the bells can be heard ringing on stormy nights. The bells may have last been heard by a mother and daughter walking near Sandcastle Water Park.
properties.date: Weather Dependent: stormy nights. Last heard 21 May 2013?

properties.name: Bell for the Dead
properties.description: If the phantom bell is heard to ring three times at midday or midnight, someone of importance is due to die.
properties.date: Before a death in the village

properties.name: Murdered Monks
properties.description: Killed by a group of bandits, the monks of this abbey can occasionally be seen drifting across their land. The phantom pealing of the abbey bell accompanies them.

properties.name: Moving Coffins
properties.description: Music, crashes, thumps, bell ringing and footsteps have all been reported emerging from the church after it has been locked up for the night. A local tale involves the coffins in the Waldegrave family crypt mysteriously moving on their own accord between the times the tomb was opened.

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: Bells made for this church were cast and blessed, but as the ship carrying them approached the church, the captain claimed that he, not God, had transported the bells safely. A storm suddenly blew in, sinking the ship and taking the bells to the bottom of the sea.
properties.location_name: Boscastle (Cornwall) - Off coast, close to Forrabury Church?

properties.name: Tolling Bell
properties.description: The tolling of a phantom bell said to either foretell a shipwreck in the area, or the sound itself comes from an old sunken ship.

properties.name: Underwater Bells
properties.description: When Danish raiders took the bells from the church, they pushed the Lord too far - he sunk their boat, and now the bells can be heard ringing in celebration.

properties.name: Monks
properties.description: A monastery once stood near these woods, and even though it has long since vanished, the bells can be occasionally heard ringing out and a ghostly monk walks between the trees.
properties.location_name: Braidwood (Lanarkshire) - Wooded area between Braidwood and Crossford, near St Oswald's Chapel

properties.name: Woman in Black
properties.description: A mystery woman in black is said to haunt the dining room. Bells have been heard ringing in other parts of the hotel after dark.

properties.name: Ringing
properties.description: The sound of a ringing bell would be heard on the top floor of this building, normally by a caretaker who was alone on site. Another witness claimed to have seen a woman on the staircase waving a handbell.

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: A customer and his parents at the facility heard bells ringing and echoing around the corridors - they spent some time trying to find the source of the discordant sounds but failed.

properties.name: Tolling Bells
properties.description: A set of church bells were hidden in the forest during the Reformation and forgotten about. They are said to ring at night, and were heard during the Second World War, a time when bell ringing was illegal.

properties.name: Vanishing Figure
properties.description: A gardener spotted a shadowy figure in the locked building, which vanished after he gained access. The bell of the HMS Bude which hangs on site supposedly rings unaided.

properties.name: Monk's Chanting
properties.description: The sound of plainchant has been heard coming from the ruins of the old priory, accompanied by the ringing of bells that have now been long gone.

properties.name: Monk in Brown
properties.description: The phantom monk is only one of several spooky occurrences at this site; another is an unearthly bell that makes itself heard at two every morning, and the dead gamekeeper who patrols the area with his rifle.

properties.name: Lost Village
properties.description: An old village was submerged when the reservoir was constructed in 1898. The church bells are supposed to occasionally still ring.

properties.name: Heavy Bell
properties.description: Two bell ringers with first-hand experience of every bell in town heard an unfamiliar ringing coming from the direction of the Audi supermarket, the sound different to the nearby town hall clock and that of Saint Modwens, a church with bells that could be occasionally heard from the area. The supermarket was built on the site of the Holy Trinity church, which had a heavy bell that would have had a similar note depth to the sound the bell ringers heard.
properties.location_name: Burton upon Trent (Staffordshire) - King Edward Place, heading towards corner of Rangemore street

properties.name: Ringing
properties.description: Two bell ringers standing outside the church waiting to ring for Sunday service heard the peeling of a set of eight bells. The next nearest church only had six bells, while the only two churches with eight bells both denied their bells were operating at the time.
properties.location_name: Burton upon Trent (Staffordshire) - Outside Saint Chad's church, Hunter Street

properties.name: Ringing Bell
properties.description: The gentle pealing of a small bell could be heard by many people over a period of years. It was widely believed to indicate the presence of a kindly unknown entity.

properties.name: Clanging
properties.description: This large house was subjected to the continuous ringing of any bell on site. The occupier eventually tired of the strange phenomenon, and no one would take up residence after his departure.
properties.location_name: Chesterfield (Derbyshire) - Rosehill House (since demolished and replaced by the town hall)

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: It was once believed that bells could be heard ringing deep within the earth if one placed one's ear on the ground.
properties.location_name: Cleethorpes (Lincolnshire) - Unidentified church with a well

properties.name: Singing
properties.description: The ruins of this church are said to be haunted by the gentle ringing of bells (long since removed) and of hymns being sung. Candlelight has also been spotted in the church, though upon investigation the building is always empty and unlit. A strange moaning is heard underground and was thought to come from a concealed tunnel.

properties.name: Bell Overboard
properties.description: After the nearby abbey fell into ruin, the bells were taken across the lake towards Wrenbury church - one bell fell overboard and can sometimes be heard ringing.
properties.location_name: Comber Mere (Cheshire) - Waters of the lake

properties.name: Sunken Church
properties.description: An area of rock was known as the Sunken Church, with some people believing it was the place where a church and the congregation were consumed. The bells could be heard pealing once a year.
properties.date: Unknown, but bells can be heard once a year on an unknown date
properties.location_name: Conisby (Lincolnshire) - Area outside the village called Sanclif or Sancliff

properties.name: St Patrick
properties.description: Climbers in the region sometimes hear the ring of a hand bell, which encourages them to keep climbing to the summit, though they never see the bell or bell ringer. The consensus of belief is that the bell is rung by St Patrick himself.

properties.name: Shipden
properties.description: The church of Saint Peter is reputed to exist off the coast of Cromer, where a town called Shipden once stood before being taken by the sea. The church bells could be heard ringing just prior to a storm.

properties.name: Buggane
properties.description: This monster, an ogre-like entity, was stirred by the ringing of the bells of this church - it tore off the roof while trying to silence the peals. Another story says that the roof is missing because demons would throw it to the ground after each repair until either the locals eventually gave up trying to fix it, or local tailor Timothy Clucas tricked the Buggane into exploding its own head.
properties.location_name: Crosby (Isle of Man) - St Trinian's Church

properties.name: Lost Bell
properties.type:original_category: The Devil and All His Works
properties.description: Having stolen the church bell, the Devil dropped it on the common, where it sunk into the earth. The bell is said to still be buried there today.

properties.name: Black Tom of Scothill
properties.type:original_category: The Devil and All His Works
properties.description: Black Tom is the name of the bell in the church and is tolled the number of the year (ie, in 2007 it was tolled 2007 times) to ensure Old Nick does not visit the village during the following twelve months. The last peal must occur on the stroke of midnight to ensure success.

properties.name: Twenty Five Clangs
properties.description: A local policeman reported the bells of the locked church rang 25 times, marking the anniversary of the death of a landowner. The officer entered the church to investigate, finding the bell rope swinging, but no one there.

properties.name: Church Bells
properties.description: Once a busy port town, sea erosion has reduced this location to only a few dozen houses. The church that once stood on the cliffs has been washed out to sea, and it is said the bells still ring on still moonless nights. Divers exploring the ruins of the old town that once stood on the cliffs have often reported a strange feeling that they are not alone under the sea.

properties.name: Missing Steeple
properties.type:original_category: The Devil and All His Works
properties.description: Night after night, during the construction of the church, the Devil appeared and pulled down the steeple. The builders finally gave up building the tower, and the bells now stand in a little bell house.

properties.name: Gabriel
properties.description: Gabriel, one of the bells in the church and once at home in Avenbury (Herefordshire), is reported to ring when death strikes the Rector of its old parish.
properties.location_name: EC4 (Greater London) - St Andrew by the Wardrobe, Queen Victoria Street

properties.name: Clergyman
properties.description: Haunting the All Soul's chapel area, the holy man is wears archaic clothing, thought to be hundreds of years old. His whistling is known to be so loud it upsets people who have come to the church to find peace. There are also reports of children crying which sounds as if it comes from the far east section of the crypt, and the sound of footsteps in various parts of the building, heard when there should be no visitors to the site. In 2019, a staff member working in the North Transept reported a figure or figures on the South Transept walking along behind the iron barrier, fifty feet from the cathedral floor. The figure(s) appeared holographic, slightly luminous and were not fully formed, and moved from west to east twice within five minutes. St Paul's is also home to the bell Great Tom, which is said to strike incorrectly prior to the death of a royal.

properties.name: Tolling
properties.description: Sunken bells on a long forgotten chapel can still be heard ringing from the mere. One version of the story says they ring to mock Oliver Cromwell, who pulled down the church, while another version say they ring on St Helen's day, the patron saint of the chapel.
properties.location_name: Ellesmere (Shropshire) - Colemere (aka Cole Mere, aka Crose Mere, aka Crosmere) lake

properties.name: The Rector's Wife Bell
properties.description: The wife of the rector was the only person in the village to hear the bell, but it always foretold a death within a couple of days of the tolls.
properties.location_name: Elm (Cambridgeshire) - Elm Vicarage near Wisbech, now known as The Old Shires

properties.name: Sound of Silver
properties.description: Hidden in the river when the local abbey was closed for business in 1539, the silver bells still ring their song around Christmas.

properties.name: Speeding Cyclist
properties.description: A squeaky bicycle with a ringing bell has been heard approaching various witnesses at speed - they move out of the way and turn to see where the sound is coming from, but nothing can be seen. A few people have reported spotting the phantom cyclist, who quickly vanishes from view.
properties.location_name: Eyam (Derbyshire) - Road between Eyam and Stoney Middleton, and dales in the area

properties.name: Dancing Girl
properties.description: Though long since removed, the bell that was once housed in the castle tower can still be heard to chime. A shadowy figure haunts the guard room, while the long gallery is home to a phantom monk. A knight n armour appeared in certain rooms, and a lady in white glided along corridors. Lastly, a little dancing girl haunts a staircase, where she collapsed and died. Outside the castle, on the hill where it stands, disembodied footsteps are heard.

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: It was believed that the bells from sunken churches could be heard ringing off the coast here.

properties.name: Lost Village
properties.description: An ancient village is submerged under these waters, and sometimes the toiling of the old church bell can be heard from within the estuary.

properties.name: Discarded Bells
properties.description: After the bells were looted from churches across Edinburgh and tossed into the estuary, they continued to ring at Christmas.
properties.location_name: Firth of Forth (Fife) - Under the estuary

properties.name: Sunken Rings
properties.description: These bells can sometimes still be heard to peal - they were lost as the ship due to deliver them sunk just off the coast.

properties.name: Former Waitress
properties.description: This former worker killed herself after being victimised by her boss. For many years after the event, bells would ring without reason throughout the building.

properties.name: Kitchen Clanger
properties.description: Another famous case, the bells in the dining room and kitchen rung erratically for a two month period, while carefully watched by the occupiers looking for a rational explanation. None could be found.

properties.name: Radley
properties.description: The buried village of Radley is hidden somewhere in this area, and long vanished church bells can be heard on Christmas Eve.

properties.name: Whistles and Bells
properties.description: When in use, some police officers reported strange occurrences in the building, including whistling, doors opening and closing themselves, cold spots, and ringing bells that were connected to empty cells.
properties.location_name: Halifax (West Yorkshire) - Former police building, Prescott Street

properties.name: Submerged Bells
properties.description: The sound of sunken church bells is said still to be heard coming from the sea.
properties.location_name: Hayling Island (Hampshire) - Off the south coast

properties.name: Ringing Bells
properties.description: Now little more than ruins, people still say they can hear the ringing of the bells, once pealed by monks. Before the Dissolution, the destruction of the abbey was prophesied in a manuscript, although the paper was destroyed in the mistaken belief that the prediction would be stopped.

properties.name: Matilda
properties.description: The opening of doors and ringing of bells resulted in the owners of this house calling the 'ghost' Matilda, suspecting the spirit to be that of a child.
properties.location_name: Hockley (Essex) - The Old School House, currently a private residence.

properties.name: Missing Town
properties.description: Legend has it that a town once stood here, but an earthquake swallowed it up as punishment for the inhabitants' ungodliness. The old church bell still rings, and sometimes the blacksmith can be heard at work. Or so they say.

properties.name: Marching Troops
properties.description: A phantom bell begins to toll as the sun sets here, slowly growing louder until midnight comes and a small army of men pass through the area. The castle is said to have been a resting place for King Harold's men as they marched to Hastings.
properties.location_name: Horley (Surrey) - Thunderfield Castle and Haroldslea Drive area

properties.name: Beyond Last Orders
properties.description: Though nothing has been seen here, the bar bells have been heard ringing when the building is empty, and pet dogs refuse to go down into the basement.

properties.name: Thirsty Bells
properties.description: At midday, the church bells were said to grow thirsty and cross the lane to drink from the village pond.

properties.name: Ringing Bell
properties.description: A bell on a tree, hung by Saint Maree, would remain silent all week until dawn on Sunday mornings, when it spontaneously bust into life until sunset. The bell was removed and taken to another church but failed to ring unaided again.
properties.location_name: Isle of Skye (Highland) - Northeast coast, place once known as Ashig

properties.name: Lost Bells
properties.description: The bells of Jedburgh abbey were said to have been lost when being moved across the river. Thankfully they can be heard ringing so they are never forgotten.
properties.location_name: Kelso (Borders) - River Tweed, close to the town

properties.name: Bell Ringing
properties.description: Legend says that the pool covers a lost town from which one can still hear the church bells ringing on stormy nights. The truth is likely to be based on the nearby town of Kenfig being lost to shifting sands during the sixteenth century. Other local legends say that the pool is bottomless and that a whirlpool pulls unsuspecting swimmers to their deaths.

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: The old servant bells were said to ring themselves in the early hours of the morning.

properties.name: Magic Bell
properties.description: This bell possesses healing properties and would travel (flying self-propelled!) across the country when needed.

properties.name: Three Tolls
properties.description: Driving late home from King's Lynn, a witness heard the distinctive sound of a huge bell toll three times. Not being able to see anything nearby that would be chiming so late, the witness interpreted the tolls as a warning and took additional care driving home.

properties.name: Sunken Church
properties.description: The church of the village sunk beneath the ground - whether God's hand or earthquake is unknown. One is said to be able to hear the bells if an ear is placed on the earth, while another version of the story says the ringing only occurs on Christmas Day.

properties.name: King's Home
properties.description: The King of the pixies once lived in this area, but the ringing of the church bells drove him away.

properties.name: Blonde
properties.description: A blonde woman has been observed shrouded in mist at the base of the stairs, and children have been heard singing Greensleeves. The sound of church bells has also been heard, even though the nearby church has been silent.
properties.location_name: Lavenham (Suffolk) - Tickled Pink tearoom, Tickle Manor

properties.name: Gaveston
properties.description: Piers Gaveston was murdered on this site in 1312. A local legend claimed strange sounds filled the air after dark, and that at midnight, bells would be heard moments before a ghostly man riding a horse manifested.
properties.location_name: Leek Wootton (Warwickshire) - Blacklow Hill and area around Gaveston's Cross

properties.name: Cleric
properties.description: This seventeenth century holy man has been seen climbing the steps at the front of the cathedral. The last witnesses to see him reported a chain hanging from his neck. The bells have also been pealed by an unseen hand, while legend says a procession of praying monks haunt the cloisters. A suicidal woman named Constance who jumped from the tower during the 1960s returns to re-enact her final, desperate action. Finally, an immovable bloodstain is said to remain on the floor where a stained glass window master landed after leaping from the gallery - he became depressed after his apprentice created a superior window from the master's off-cuts.

properties.name: Sunken Village
properties.description: The ringing of the bells from this sunken village are said to be heard by those in the right place at the right time (and in the right state of mind).
properties.date: Certain times of the year, though no further details known

properties.name: Cathedral Bells
properties.description: A cathedral once stood in this spot before it was flooded - the tower bells now ring out on select days of the year.

properties.name: Auditory Warning
properties.description: The ringing of this phantom bell can be heard over the house or building where the death shall occur. Before the funeral of the person, strange noises can be heard in the church - if anyone is brave enough to peep into the building, they can see the forms of everyone who is due to die during the year.
properties.date: Before a death in the neighbourhood
properties.location_name: Llantwit Major (South Glamorgan) - General area, and church

properties.name: Jester
properties.description: A figure has been reported dancing about the woods, their clothing adjourned with bells. The grave of the last 'real' jester in England is housed in this wooded area, and it is believed that the shade is he.

properties.name: Singing
properties.description: Both the sounds of bells and singing can be heard coming from this small body of water.

properties.name: Thieving Mermaid
properties.description: An accident sent a church bell into the waters of the local river - a passing mermaid took the opportunity to steal the bell and can sometimes be heard ringing it. As a sidenote, a bell was recovered from the water in 1848.

properties.name: Hunters
properties.description: The fairies that frequent this remote area dress in green and often carry hunting weapons - the last one seen possessed a whip. It was once said that under the site was a lost city which sunk for some unnamed reason - church bells could be heard on still nights.

properties.name: Dog Hater
properties.description: The family troubled by a noisy spirit in this house first reported their dog becoming extraordinarily excited, to such an extent their pet had to be rehoused. Soon after a certain door in the building would never remain closed, and the doorbell would ring on its own accord.

properties.name: Summoning Servants
properties.description: This presence would vigorously ring a set of disused servant bells located in the kitchen.
properties.location_name: N6 (Greater London) - Private House along Archway House

properties.name: Former Owner
properties.description: A former owner of the hotel killed himself with a shotgun after discovering his wife was having an affair. His ghost was blamed for ringing bells in the kitchen after it had been closed for the night.

properties.name: Blind Peter
properties.description: On Halloween three misty women appear and walk along the ruin's walls. The sounds of bells can also be heard on this night. Another presence is Blind Peter. He is less choosey on the date on which he appears and just wanders around the ruins of the abbey. Finally, there is a legend that a secret underground passage was sealed after the last man to enter it emerged shortly after, screaming 'block it up!' before dropping dead - the passage may be connected to the tales of treasure buried on site.

properties.name: Drinking Rocks
properties.description: Upon hearing the ringing of the local church bells, these standing stones move to a nearby pool and start drinking.
properties.location_name: New Radnor (Powys) - Standing Stones east of village

properties.name: Devil's Hoof
properties.type:original_category: The Devil and All His Works
properties.description: A hoof print is visible in a stone by the gate. The Devil was once so upset by the sound of the church bells, he climbed the steeple with the intent of stealing them; he slipped and fell, leaving the single piece of evidence behind.

properties.name: Ringing Bell
properties.description: Workers in the basement at the hotel would hear a servant's bell ring at the same time early Sunday morning, even though the bells were disconnected. Other workers would hear a little girl calling out their names, and a child would occasionally be seen running around.
