It is also claimed that phantom nurses haunt the site, from the time during the Second World War when the RAF used the building as a hospital.

properties.name: Peals
properties.description: The gentle peals of a bell are said to emerge from under the bay's waters.

properties.name: Ringing
properties.description: A policeman cycling through the village was surprised to hear the church bell ringing. The bell rang a total of twenty five times and stopped as the officer approached the church. The door was locked, and there was no one inside, but the bell rope was gently swinging.

properties.name: Ringing Vicar
properties.description: The bells are said to be rung occasionally by the ghost of a former vicar, who has also been seen on site.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: Several entities were said to have taken up residence around the castle, including a phantom coach which would arrive outside the front door, an older man seen on the first floor, ringing bells and a phantom monk. A gate on the site would open itself; even after concreate had been used, the following day the gate was found open and bent.
properties.location_name: Paulstown (County Kilkenny) - Shankill castle (aka Shankill Castle)

properties.name: Anne West
properties.description: Anne feared being buried alive, so she requested a bell be placed in her crypt when her time came. When she was buried, the bell rang for several nights, and her corpse was seen walking down the road towards her former home. She continued to haunt the area for many years afterwards.
properties.location_name: Pembury (Kent) - Great Bayhall Manor house, and churchyard (no longer standing)

properties.name: Captain Martin
properties.description: Drowned at sea, the former bell ringer of St Gluvias who became a captain returned from his watery grave and now silently stands in the church. On occasion, he has been heard ringing the bells in the early hours of the morning.

properties.name: Post Mortem Marriage
properties.description: A married woman fell in love with a young sailor. Rather than undergo lengthy and unfavourable divorce proceedings, the woman murdered her husband. Not long after, she was arrested, and while awaiting trial, the woman and the sailor promised to marry regardless of the outcome. The woman's court appearance was brief; she was found guilty and quickly hanged. The sailor returned to work on his fishing boat and one night shortly after her death, the crew watched him jump into the sea. Even though his body was never found, the sailor's voice would be occasionally heard in the area whispering, 'I will...'. There are also reports of phantom bells that still peal under the sea.
properties.location_name: Penzance (Cornwall) - Beach and sea, Mount's Bay

properties.name: Buzzing
properties.description: A priest staying in the castle reported hearing the buzzing of a bell that woke him up several times during the night - he was told that a former mistress had an electric buzzer connected to her maid's room should she require assistance, though this was removed when she had died. The priest had spent the night in the former maid's room.

properties.name: Watch the Timing
properties.description: Concerning the bells of the church and the cathedral, an old rhyme says, 'when the church and the abbey they strike together, they'll either be a death or change in the weather'.

properties.name: Ringing Bells
properties.description: Eight bells were lost, as were the entire crew, when a storm sunk the ship carrying them. The bells are still said to peal at night, together with the wails of the drowned sailors. The harbour is also named as one of many places where Arthur threw Excalibur.
properties.date: Unknown, but said to occur at night

properties.name: Ringing Bells
properties.description: Local legend says that a church which once stood here sunk into the earth and its bells can be heard to ring on Christmas Eve.
properties.location_name: Preston (Lancashire) - Site of old monastery (estimated to be A59 / Friargate area)

properties.name: Priscilla Rawdon
properties.description: The seventeenth century Priscilla is now said to manifest as a grey lady, though she is more likely to be heard walking around with a rustling of her dress and gentle ringing of small bells. Her voice, speaking in little more than a whisper, is also heard coming from the priest hole.

properties.name: Bell Ringing
properties.description: Midnight marks the start of the ghostly bell ringer's duties in the area, though the building in which he worked has not stood for many years.

properties.name: Magic Bell
properties.description: A knight being held captive in the castle was given a magic bell and told its power would break down the wall of his cell. The knight rang the bell but the wall remained intact, much to the amusement of the prison wardens. Soon after, the castle was struck by lightning, destroying everything other than a wall upon which the magic bell hang.

properties.name: Grey Shadowy Girl
properties.description: Appearing in front of the fireplace and in the men's toilet, this fleeting female form is also blamed for ringing the 'last orders' bells in the early hours of the morning.

properties.name: Sunk Church
properties.description: After the church sank into the ground, the only 'evidence' of its existence is the phantom ringing of the bells.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: Her reasons unknown, this mermaid returns once a year to ring a bell hidden deep underwater and sing.

properties.name: Sweeping Brush
properties.description: A yard brush swept a path without human help and a lawnmower rolled uphill at this residence. Other more 'normal' poltergeist occurrences included the ringing of doorbells and self-opening and closing doors. Some attributed the paranormal happenings to the ghosts of three people killed in an aircraft crash that occurred in 1938 on the corner of Fortescue Lane and Green Lane.

properties.name: A Knock and a Ring
properties.description: Mr H T Graham was dared to knock on the door of this empty church late one night - the moment he did so, the bells began to toll.

properties.name: Caretaker wearing Green Overalls
properties.description: The haunting here is reportedly caused by a former caretaker electrocuted onsite. He has been seen on the upper floor of the building and is also held accountable for the pealing of a bell that is heard around dinner time.
properties.location_name: Shoreham (Sussex) - Adur Civic Centre, Ham Road

properties.name: Lost Bell
properties.description: Several stories surround this pool. In one, a village once stood here, but the villagers mocked God, He sent forth a storm that flooded the area. The church bell can now be heard peeling once a year. Another story says the pool is home to a massive fish - it was caught only once, but escaped, taking the sword of the fisherman (or knight) with him. He now uses the sword to cut any net which falls near him. The final story says the pool is bottomless, and that when any attempt to drain the pool is made, the waters always return overnight.

properties.name: Runner
properties.description: An invisible being has brushed past several staff and customers of this public house. The entity has also disturbed people by running down corridors and ringing the front bell.

properties.name: Former Occupants?
properties.description: There are many spooky stories told by past and present staff; the empty lift moves between floors at night, many reports of phantom figures exist, and sounds of footsteps are heard along deserted corridors. The bells for the call system have also gone off in empty rooms.

properties.name: Raleigh
properties.description: Beneath a small valley located between these two places is the lost town of Raleigh, swallowed up by an earthquake in 1185. A local legend says the church bells can sometimes still be heard on Christmas Day.
properties.location_name: Southwell (Nottinghamshire) - Road between Southwell and Oxton

properties.name: The Toll
properties.description: The ringing of church bells from a church taken by the sea many years ago can be heard prior to a storm.
properties.location_name: St Annes on Sea (Lancashire) - Off Coast

properties.name: Chef
properties.description: The poltergeist found in this establishment enjoys ringing a bell in the kitchen which then sounds a different tone. The bottoms of female staff are pinched by an unseen hand and voices have also been heard in an empty bar. A clairvoyant identified the entity as a former chef.
properties.location_name: St Blazey (Cornwall) - Cornish Arms public house

properties.name: Stolen Bell
properties.description: The largest bell was stolen from the building by imps disguised as men (or according to others, Cromwellian soldiers) and transported out to sea where it was dropped into the Whitesand Bay. The bell still rings just before a storm, as a warning to nearby fishermen to return home. Another version of the tale says the bell was rested on several rocks during the heist, and it is the rocks that now ring before the weather changes.
properties.location_name: St David's (Dyfed) - Cathedral and Whitesand Bay

properties.name: Ringing Rock
properties.description: When the church bell was stolen and dropped into the sea, it was recovered by supernatural means and concealed within solid rock. When the rock is tapped, the ringing of the bell can be heard. Some have speculated St Govan was in fact Sir Gawain, who became a hermit after Arthur's death. Another legend says anyone who tries counting the steps which lead to the Chapel will reach varying totals depending on whether they ascend or descend. Finally, the chapel was home to an expanding, hollow stone that would change shape and size depending on who attempted to climb inside. Once inside, a wish would be granted.
properties.location_name: St Govan's Head (Dyfed) - St Govan's Chapel (and surrounding area)

properties.name: Bells
properties.description: It is said that in 1551 all the church bells in Jersey were removed and shipped to France. Unfortunately, they did not make it; the vessel carrying the bells sank shortly after departing. It is now said that anyone who hears the bells while out at sea will not be returning to land.
properties.location_name: St Helier (Jersey) - Off the Coast, heading towards Minquiers

properties.name: Returning Bell
properties.description: In the forgotten past, an English king was said to have taken the bell from this church. He died soon after, but wished the bell returned from the original location. The horse carrying the bell immediately set off back to St Illtyd without human intervention, the bell's rings summoning other horses to aid in the quest. Once the horse reached the church, the bell fell from the horse's neck, breaking a stone which, it was said, remained in the churchyard for many years as a reminder that the miracle had really occurred.

properties.name: Neptune
properties.description: This old ship has been reported off the coast many times. It is also believed that before the Neptune sunk here, the area was haunted by another unnamed ghost ship. Phantom bells have also been reported coming from the deep waters.

properties.name: End of the World
properties.description: St Levan hit this rock with his bare hands and broke it in two large pieces. He declared that when the gap grew big enough to let a horse through, the world would come to an end. In another part of the graveyard, one can find the tombstone of Captain Wetherel, who can be heard ringing his ship's bell from within his coffin.
properties.location_name: St Levan (Cornwall) - St Levan's Stone, in village churchyard

properties.name: Cat Lover
properties.description: The rear of this public house is reportedly frequented by a spectral old lady who fed all the local stray cats. The ringing of phantom bells has been heard coming from nearby moorland.
properties.location_name: Stanbury (West Yorkshire) - The Old Silent Inn

properties.name: Ringing Bells
properties.description: Over a two and a half hour period, every bell in the house constantly rang out. The strange activity ended as suddenly as it had begun.

properties.name: Ringing Bells
properties.description: A spiritualist told the occupants of this hotel that the reason bells would ring unaided by human hand was that a spirit had taken up residence there, unable to leave having dropped dead at the bar.
properties.location_name: SW9 (Greater London) - Gresham Arms Hotel (no longer open)

properties.name: Maiden
properties.description: A fairy maiden lived in the waters of this lake. A story also claimed a large town once stood here but it was swallowed by the lake (after the locals insulted Saint Patrick) and then used by the fairies to construct their kingdom. A slight variation says the fairies were once the townsfolk before they were submerged. Bells could occasionally be heard coming from the water.
properties.location_name: Swansea (West Glamorgan) - Crumlyn Lake (aka Llyn Crymlyn), now Crymlyn Bog

properties.name: Silver Bell
properties.description: A silver bell was briefly found in this pool, but as one man blasphemed, the bell fell back in and could not be recovered.
properties.location_name: Tintagel (Cornwall) - Nathan's Keeve (pool of water and waterfall two miles south of Tintagel, name may have changed since report)

properties.name: Witch
properties.description: The church bell is rung between 11:00h and 11:15h every Shrove Tuesday. During this time, if one places their ear to the ground on Conger Hill, a witch can be heard boiling her cauldron and preparing her pancakes.

properties.name: Marshy Bells
properties.description: The Devil once appeared in the village, stealing the church bells and sinking them into the nearby marsh. Bubbles in the marsh were said to be the result of the bells continuing to sink into the bottomless pit.
properties.location_name: Tunstall (Norfolk) - Church, and marsh known as Bell Hole (and Hell Hole)

properties.name: Chimes
properties.description: Heading towards a shrine, the invisible procession of pilgrims can be heard ringing small hand bells as they move.

properties.name: Pealing Bells
properties.description: The church at Walton was taken by the sea in the late 1790's, and now its bells warn of incoming storms. The church is said to have reappeared briefly in 1928.

properties.name: Brown Gardener
properties.description: The short, brown coloured figure seen in the abbey grounds is thought to be a former gardener. A few ghostly women (some of whom are nuns) in different colours and forms also haunt the abbey, including a headless woman known as Elfreda. During the mid-twentieth century a team of workmen camping on the grounds heard the bell pealing, even though it had been removed several years previously.
properties.location_name: Watton (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Watton Abbey

properties.name: Ringing
properties.description: A ship carrying bells stolen from the abbey was sunk at the Black Nab while trying to escape. On Halloween, one can stand close to the rock and call out their true love's name; the wind repeats the name, and the submerged bells can be heard to ring.
properties.location_name: Whitby (North Yorkshire) - Off coast, near Black Nab

properties.name: Swinging Doors
properties.description: Doors have been reported to open unaided, bells ring, and items fly from the walls and shelves since the extension of the bar area.
properties.location_name: Woolfardisworthy (aka Woolsery), Torridge (Devon) - Farmer's Arms public house

properties.name: Hovering Head
properties.description: The ghostly form of a severed head belonging to a murdered young bride reputedly hovers around this building. There are also reports of a phantom man whose clothes are saturated with water.

properties.name: Mistletoe Bride
properties.description: A grey lady that walks on the grass in front of the rectory is said to be the mistletoe bride (the nearby Knowle Hall one of several places in the UK that claims the legend as its own). Poltergeist activity has also been reported in the rectory in the past.

properties.name: Newly Wed Bride
properties.description: Convinced she had made a bad wife after waking up and finding her new husband gone, this girl leapt off the battlements to her death - unfortunately, her husband had only gone hunting without informing her. She now appears to others, not wanting them to make the same mistake.

properties.name: White Woman with Mistletoe
properties.description: Not just home to the ghostly bride (who suffered the fate of hiding in a chest from her husband while playing hide and seek, only to become stuck and suffocate) who wakes people up who spend the night there, but also to a grey lady who haunts the library and a green man who hovers by the lake - it is reported that he has no legs. Finally, a man in a flannel suit was observed walking through a wall by a security guard in the 1970s.

properties.name: Mistletoe Bough
properties.description: The hall is one of several sites which is supposedly the site of the legend of the Mistletoe Bough - a bride who died on her wedding day after being locked in a chest during a game of hide and seek was found many years later in skeletal form.

properties.name: Bride in White
properties.description: Left standing at the altar, this bride who never was now haunts the place where her reception should have been. A maid who saw her ran screaming from the building, while another witness said the ghost was cut off at the knee. A more recent ghost is that of a man in a blue pinstripe suit, seen by two different proprietors.

properties.name: Bride in Black
properties.description: Murdered by a former suitor on her wedding day, this bride now returns on that fateful date. Dressed in black, she has been seen standing in the doorway of a bedroom.

properties.name: Sarah
properties.description: Stamping and thuds emanating from an empty room have been reported from this shop, as well as strange wailing. Lights flicker on and off, and doors open and close by an unseen hand. The source of the haunting is said to be Sarah, a jilted bride who hanged herself, though her presence and poltergeist activities are said to be 'friendly' in nature.
properties.location_name: Chester (Cheshire) - Thorntons Chocolate shop (was once House of Bewlay Tobacconist), Eastgate Street

properties.name: Woman in Wedding Dress
properties.description: Said to frequent room 6, this bride was jilted at the altar, and still awaits her partner's return.

properties.name: Bride Dressed in White
properties.description: The bride committed suicide in the building after being jilted at the altar, and her pallid form has existed since.

properties.name: Mistletoe Bough
properties.description: The decaying hall is one of several sites which is supposedly the site of the legend of the Mistletoe Bough - a bride who died on her wedding day after being locked in a chest during a game of hide and seek was found many years later in skeletal form.

properties.name: Wedding Party
properties.description: To celebrate a wedding, the guests, bride and groom went out hunting; it resulted in a fight that killed most of them, and now they all travel back to the castle once a year, covered in blood.
properties.location_name: Featherstone (Northumberland) - Route between Pynkinscleugh & Featherstone Castle

properties.name: Tall Man glowing Blue
properties.description: This ghostly man with blonde hair and a blue tint stood in the road; an incoming vehicle had to violently swerve to avoid the figure. When the driver realised the man had vanished, he quickly drove off. There has also been a report of a phantom woman with long blonde hair along the A229, said to have been a bride killed one day prior to her wedding day.

properties.name: Pink Bride
properties.description: Wearing a pink wedding dress, the ghost of a former owner named Maggie who died in 1934 is thought to haunt this hotel. A local legend said that two coffins were buried under the hotel, one containing Maggie's body and the other filled with her money, though an excavation under the building in 2008 found nothing.

properties.name: The Bride
properties.description: This woman killed herself the day after being wed - her ghost loiters around the Purfleet area before screaming and throwing itself into the quay waters. Other screams, belonging to fighting soldiers, can also be heard in the area, during which the water runs red with blood.

properties.name: Small Lady
properties.description: Shortly after a wedding, the bride was stabbed to death by her new husband in the hotel. Since then, a short woman in a long white dress has been spotted and phantom footsteps heard.
properties.location_name: King's Lynn (Norfolk) - The Tudor Rose Hotel, 11 Nicholas Street

properties.name: Honeymooner
properties.description: This bride found herself alone after her husband ran away before their first night together - her shade still waits for his return.

properties.name: Bride on Other's Photographs
properties.description: Appearing on three wedding photographs (though none have been published to the author's knowledge), this ghostly bride is said to have died of a broken heart after discovering her husband to be had been killed on route to their wedding, after his horse had bolted. In addition, chanting has sometimes been heard coming from the church as it stands empty.

properties.name: Bride
properties.description: The old house which once stood here before the hospital was the scene of the ultimate wedding day disaster in which the bride fell to her death from an upper story window while her guests partied on below. Her shade has been reported by nurses in the newer building.

properties.name: Water Horse House
properties.description: This freshwater monster had a house built for him and his mortal bride - the general area of the property is known as the water there never freezes because of the home's open fire!

properties.name: Carrickogunnel
properties.description: Holding a bright lantern to lure passing ships onto the rocks, the repulsive ghost of Carrickogunnel lurks around the castle. The other phantom found here is that of a bride who mysteriously vanished shortly after being married.
properties.location_name: Newmarket on Fergus (County Clare) - MacAulliffe's Castle

properties.name: Bride
properties.description: This woman in white is said to be a bride murdered by her husband on their wedding night hundreds of years ago. She may have last been seen by a married couple who spotted a figure with folded arms standing amongst trees. Two years prior to this sighting, two woman passing Castle Hill observed the phantom woman in white.
properties.location_name: Newton le Willows (Merseyside) - Willow Park (and other meres and woods in the area)

properties.name: Silent Woman
properties.description: Dressed in black and wearing a veil, this figure has been seen sitting quietly and disappearing without warning. She is believed to be a bride whose new husband had stuck his head out of a window and was decapitated. Found holding his body once they reached London, totally insane, her spirit has been making the journey since.
properties.location_name: Other London (Greater London) - Railway line between London & Carlisle

properties.name: Jane Seymour
properties.description: The Seymour family once owned this property, and Jane is thought to haunt the surrounding grounds. The shade of Anne Boleyn has also been reported loitering near a row of nearby yew trees. Finally, the hall is reputedly the location where the Mistletoe Bough bride story originated, with the chest still (?) being present today.

properties.name: Newly Married
properties.description: One witness watched a man and woman in wedding attire run the forest and then vanish into thin air.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A phantom woman who has been spotted on the staircase is reputed to be a bride killed in the hall.

properties.name: The Bride
properties.description: Once a year, on an unconfirmed night, a team of six horses draw a coach containing a faceless bride. During her lifetime, the bride is said to have jilted at the altar, resulting in her suicide a short time later. Now in death her ghost drives around the area looking for the man who betrayed her.

properties.name: Marge in a Bridal Gown
properties.description: Legend says that the chest next to the west door once contained the remains of a bride trapped inside after a game of hide and seek went wrong. She is reported to emerge once a year to join in with the church choir.
properties.location_name: Terrington St Clement (Norfolk) - Parish Church, the 'Lovell' pew

properties.name: House with Glowing Windows
properties.description: After a murder of a newlywed bride, this house was left to fall into ruin. Some people reported that a glowing candle could be seen moving past various windows, while others claimed a ghostly wedding party occurred. The Hall is now lost below the waters of the flooded valley of Thirlmere, which is home to a phantom dog which swims through the water. In 2013 a witness camping in the area became unnerved after witnessing two silently moving lights along the road.
properties.date: 31 October (reoccurring), Wedding party occurs. Lights seen May 2013

properties.name: Bride in a Chest
properties.description: In a story that appears several times throughout the UK, a newly wedded bride played hide and seek with her guests, only to disappear... A few years later her mummified body was found in a chest in the loft, the spring mounted lid trapping her inside.

properties.name: Headless Bride
properties.description: The decapitated ghost caused one witness to faint as it ascended the staircase and headed towards the viewer. A local legend states that the ghostly woman and her lover both committed suicide the day before she was due to be married to another man. The building has a history of phantom footsteps and doors that open themselves.
properties.date: Twentieth century, particularly active around Christmas

properties.name: Shaggy Creature
properties.description: Driving towards Thetford from the direction of Watton, this witness spotted a large, long haired creature coloured greyish white. It had small ears, a long snout, large eyes, and stood on four legs. The witness drove past the beast three times in total, ensuring a good look at it; on the third pass the creature rose on its hind legs, taking an almost human form, and stood between six to eight feet tall. The driver quickly returned home, only returning two days later to look at the area again - the creature had gone, but there were tracks at the site suggesting something had been dragged. Another witness, a similar creature in December 2007, but said it was greyer in colour with black patches.
properties.location_name: A1075 (Norfolk) - Between Thetford and East Wretham, half a mile from the level crossing

properties.name: Earth Hound
properties.description: Earth hounds are said to be the size of a rat, but with a dog-like head and feet of a mole. They live in churchyards and eat the recently buried. A dead earth hound may have been ploughed up in the Mastrick churchyard.

properties.name: Urisk
properties.description: This cave and the surrounding area are said to be the meeting place of many Urisk.
properties.location_name: Aberfoyle (Stirling) - Goblin's Cave, north base of Ben Venue

properties.name: Genaprugwirion
properties.description: Also known as a cenaprugwirion, this lizard is around thirty centimetres long with an orange head and large eyes. Karl Shuker has speculated that the creature is a tuatara, a living fossil found in New Zealand.

properties.name: Bathing Mermaid
properties.description: Twelve people watched a beautiful pale woman washing herself in the sea, with what appeared to be a black tail splashing around behind her.

properties.name: Scaled Creature
properties.description: One witness said that this lake monster looked like a dinosaur, with scales running down the back. Another viewer observed the monster on the lake bank - he described it as four metres in length, with a long neck and a sheep's head. It moved in little 'hops'. A photograph taken in 1968 of the monsters is unconvincing.

properties.name: Humped Creature
properties.description: While fishing, two teachers watched a small headed creature with a single hump swim past their boat some thirty-five metres away. It surfaced three times before disappearing.
properties.location_name: Achmore, Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Loch Urabhal

properties.name: Nuckelavee
properties.description: A hybrid, sea living creature, the Nuckelavee was described as having the features of both a mighty horse and its rider. The rider was legless and appeared to grow straight out of the horse's back. Its head was ten times the size of a normal human head, possessing a very wide mouth that jutted out like a pig's snout, and a single red eye that blazed with flame. It was both hairless and skinless. Nuckelavee's breath was toxic and it was responsible for the 'Mortasheen', a fatal disease of cattle.

properties.name: The Otter King
properties.description: Martin Martin recorded the tradition of a large otter with a white spot upon its breast. It was said that the king otter was always accompanied by seven normal otters. The pelt of the Otter King was said to offer magical properties to those who owned it.
properties.location_name: All over the region (Outer Hebrides) - No exact location given

properties.name: Horse Dog
properties.description: Previously spotted in 1975 by two women, the 1990s witness reported seeing a strange horse with the legs of a dog prancing around the woods in broad daylight. The entity quickly vanished.

properties.name: Bigfoot
properties.description: Local press reported that Charmaine Frase joined the British Bigfoot Research team having, as a child, twice encountering a tall figure in woodland. The creature was said to be seven foot (2.13 metres) with wide shoulders and no neck.

properties.name: Drowned Man
properties.description: While the specifics may vary from tale to tale, the loch is named as home to a water horse that drowned a local man known for his dissolute lifestyle.
properties.location_name: Aros, Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Loch Frisa

properties.name: Shadowy Creature
properties.description: Walking through the woods after dusk, a couple found themselves chased by a large shadowy quadruped that stood just over a metre tall. One of the witnesses said it did not move like a deer or a dog. The woods is also reputedly home to a phantom highwayman.

properties.name: Lost City
properties.description: The first town of Bala is said to be concealed under the waters of this lake. In addition, a monster seen in 1995 and nicknamed 'Teggie' by locals, was described as resembling a humped crocodile, although a story published in the same year claimed recent sightings of the creature were a hoax. Teggie sightings date back to the 1960s, and the creature may have first been encountered by two men boating in the lake.

properties.name: Stoned Monster
properties.description: Three boys watched a weird water creature playing in the lake - they threw stones at the creature and ran when it approached them. They stated it had a hairy, horned head.

properties.name: Great Fish
properties.description: A fishing vessel reported being attacked by a dark fish the size of a whale. The fish rammed the boat several times, only ceasing the assault when the captain ordered the engines to be killed; after half an hour, the fish disappeared.
properties.location_name: Ballycotton (County Cork) - Somewhere between Ballycotton and Old Head of Kinsale

properties.name: Large Eel
properties.description: A long eel-like creature remained stuck under this bridge for two days.
properties.location_name: Ballynahinch (County Galway) - Bridge near to Ballynahinch Castle

properties.name: Thick Eel
properties.description: Two people in a two man kayak quickly headed towards the shore after spotting a long black creature under the water with a body the thickness of two human legs. A local legend says that Lough Neagh is also home to fairies, who can be heard laughing, and that the lough was created when someone forgot that they had left the cover off a magic well (with towers from the lost town visible at dusk).
properties.location_name: Ballyronan (County Derry / County Londonderry) - Lough Neagh

properties.name: U-28 Creature
properties.description: Commander of the U-28, Freiherr George G von Forstner claimed that after torpedoing the steamer Iberian, the ship sank and exploded, throwing a huge animal out from the sea. The creature was described as resembling a sixty foot long crocodile, with four large webbed feet, a long, pointed tail and a pointed head.
properties.location_name: Baltimore (County Cork) - 17 km south west of Fastnet Rock

properties.name: Humanoid Beast
properties.description: Incredibly ugly to look at, this monster would sit for many hours on the rock. One man went too close, and he was later found with his flesh clawed off.
properties.location_name: Balvaig (Argyll and Bute) - Area in and around sea, and Grador Rock

properties.name: Long Serpent
properties.description: Eight crewmen of a lugger reported seeing a sea creature three hundred feet (ninety metres) long, with three huge humps. The beast made as much noise as a steamer while moving.

properties.name: Shape Changers
properties.description: The mermaids said to live in this area would be capable of changing their shape as they came on to land. They would also be seen sitting on the rocks playing harps.

properties.name: Snake-like Creature
properties.description: A ship was attacked by a sea serpent, which wrapped itself around the ships mast until the crew hit back - it fell back into the sea but followed the vessel for two more days before disappearing.

properties.name: Sea Monsters
properties.description: Several sightings of various strange creatures have been over recent years, from the traditional sea serpent to a creature resembling a giant turtle with an egg shaped head and two spines jutting from its back. A blurred photograph taken in January 2016 that national press claimed was the monster was more than likely to have been a log.
properties.date: Late twentieth century / early twenty-first century

properties.name: Shagfoal
properties.description: Similar to (or maybe identical to) a shuck, the Shagfoal is reported to resemble a large, black longhaired bear.

properties.name: Women with Child
properties.description: Crofter Colin Campbell raised his rifle to fire at what he thought was an otter eating a fish but stopped when he realised the creature was a mermaid holding a child. The creature dived into the sea after Campbell made a noise.
properties.location_name: Barra (Outer Hebrides) - Reef in Caolas Cumhan

properties.name: Hominid
properties.description: There was a brief flap of monkey (or chimp) sightings here, the creature said to stand between 3 - 4 feet high (90 - 120cm) and had bright rings around its eyes. Trees were damaged and residents reported their pets to be terrified. On one occasion, a taxi driver spotted a chimpanzee on the pavement, although the creature fled before the police arrived.
properties.location_name: Bath (Somerset) - Brassknocker Hill and surrounding area

properties.name: Tall Hairy Creature
properties.description: A man walking his dog reported seeing a man like creature, just over two metres tall and covered in ginger hair, drinking from a pond in the woods.

properties.name: Pterosaur?
properties.description: A witness reported a large pterosaur-like creature flying above Nursery woods.

properties.name: Blobster
properties.description: A dead four metre long creature was washed up on the beach, with diamond shaped fins sitting along its back - the creature could not be identified.

properties.name: Green Shark
properties.description: A 1.8 metre long shark was discovered in this river, green in colour and no doubt a long way from home.

properties.name: Tailed Bigfoot
properties.description: Two girls walking from school reported seeing a large hairy figure running into nearby woods, complete with an unmistakeable tail.

properties.name: Sandwell Vallygator
properties.description: An American snapper which had either escaped or been set free was first spotted at a valley lake in 1999, though it took a further two years to catch the creature.

properties.name: Good Looking Woman
properties.description: A wife and husband witnessed a mermaid sitting on the rocks, described as 'good looking' by the wife. The couple tried to capture the creature, but it swam away.

properties.name: Dragon Train
properties.description: Baron Ryves was said to have had a vivid dream of a fiery dragon which passed roaring over his land, its chest filled with holes in which people could be seen. The creature disappeared into the ground. The area of land later became a railway line and tunnel. Prophetic or coincidence?
properties.location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Fields in area which were replaced by railway station (now Edward Street / Alexandra Street area)

properties.name: Tall Monkey with Glowing Eyes
properties.description: A late night fishing party reported seeing an eight foot creature standing upright, with radiant eyes.

properties.name: Unseen Tarbh-uisge
properties.description: During one dark and stormy evening one of several men sheltering in a hut wished that he had a fat ox. No sooner had he wished for one, there could be heard a bull just outside. However, all in the hut were too terrified to look and see what had appeared. It was only next morning when they ventured out, they found plenty of cloven hoof track but no sign of what had left them.
properties.location_name: Borerary, St Kilda (Outer Hebrides) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Tasmanian Wolf
properties.description: A strange cat-like creature spotted by a female driver was later identified by her as a Tasmanian wolf, though the creature is thought extinct.

properties.name: Gliding Hound
properties.description: The passenger of a van observed an Alsatian-sized dog as it glided towards the vehicle before reaching a hedge, where it stood upright on its rear legs. The passenger and dog watched each other until the van turned a corner and out of sight.

properties.name: Big Bird
properties.description: A large bird seen in the skies above the town was originally identified as an eagle or an escaped condor by its witnesses; twenty years later a few of them claimed it to have been a pterodactyl.

properties.name: The Kerry Carabuncle
properties.description: This lake is home to a mythical snake-like beast which is covered in jewels and gold. The monster is said only to appear once every seven years.
properties.location_name: Brandon (County Kerry) - Mount Brandon - Lough Veagh and/or Lough Geal

properties.name: Grey Horse
properties.description: Home of a Welsh water horse, this creature who tempt people to sit on it before terrifying the rider by dragging them over rough land and through the air.

properties.name: Red Flying Squid
properties.description: Constable John Morrison kicked a squid he found on a beach, only for the creature to wrap a 180 centimetre tentacle around his ankle. The policeman managed to remove his boot and killed the cephalopod with rocks and a set of shears.
properties.location_name: Broadford, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Coast near the area

properties.name: Monstrous Fish
properties.description: A twenty-two yard (twenty metre) long fish washed up on the beach, the creature's roar being heard over a mile away before dying. One of the creature's eyes was said to be 'more than a cart and six horses could draw'. It is very likely the 'fish' was a whale.
properties.location_name: Broadstairs (Kent) - Sandy area, was known as Fishness

properties.name: Monster's Grave
properties.description: The people of the village once found a water monster in a nearby lake - they took the creature and buried it on the mountain slope.

properties.name: Buckland Shag
properties.description: This water monster set up home in this small body of water, until driven away by a local clergyman. A large rock located in the North Downs is still stained red with the bloody of the Shag's victims.
properties.location_name: Buckland (Surrey) - Stream near the village, and rock in the North Downs

properties.name: Sea Serpent
properties.description: This creature was reported by the vicar of the local parish - he reported it moved at around 25 miles per hour and left an oily wake.

properties.name: Smelly Creature
properties.description: Several reports of a creature with a sail-like body and a flipper on the end of its nose exist in this region of water off the Shetland Isles. A fishing crew who encountered it stated the beast destroyed several of their nets and came so close to their boat they could detect a pungent stench.
properties.location_name: Burra Haaf (Shetland) - Waters in the area

properties.name: Swan Eater
properties.description: An unknown large creature was seen to attack several swans from beneath the water of the lake - it is believed to be a type of large catfish, which can grow up to sixteen feet in length. There has also been at least one mermaid sighting in the lake, and Sir Lancelot's body is supposed to be buried nearby.

properties.name: Water Monster
properties.description: Rumoured to be a bottomless lake, the waters are reportedly home to a water monster that once drowned a man foolish enough to try to swim across it.

properties.name: Famh
properties.description: This author is not sure if this is a joke, but... the Famh is said to be a strange dog/mole hybrid (Dole? Mog?), which possesses a vicious set of teeth and a disproportionately sized mouth. It lurks in the mountain range, shying away from human contact. The area is also haunted by a piper, who is seen standing on the tallest peaks, and is heard playing at night.

properties.name: Mer Children
properties.description: Three men from Douglas found two merchildren on the rocks. One was already dead, but they saved the second child and took it to their town. The creature was described as around 60.3 centimetres in length and brown in colour, although the scales around its tail were slightly violet, and the hair on its head light green.
properties.location_name: Calf of Man (Isle of Man) - Rocky area, exact location not known

properties.name: Child Killer
properties.description: The water horse in this loch was blamed for the deaths of children who were attempting to cross the water.

properties.name: Wallaby
properties.description: Seen from a distance of one metre, the wallaby left the witness and was last seen heading towards the allotments.

properties.name: Goat Headed Monster
properties.description: Lady Eveline Camilla (Newton) Gurdon claimed this monster encounter possibly happened in Suffolk, while the 'Every Day Book' placed it in Cambridge. They were both wrong - it comes from Poland; the story is from 'The Doome Warning All Men to the Judgemente' and reads: Fishers toke a disfigured divell, in a certain Stoure (which is a mighty gathering togither of waters from some narrow lake of the sea), a horrible monster with a goat's head, and eyes shyning lyke fyre, whereuppon they were all afrayde and ranne awaye; and that ghoste plunged himselfe under the ice, and running tippe and downe in the stoure made a terrible noyse and sound.
properties.location_name: Cambridge (Cambridgeshire) - River Cam close to Stourbridge Common (or not, as the case may be)

properties.name: Shorted Armed Mermaid
properties.description: Watched by a farmer for two hours, this mermaid had a pale upper body and a red/grey tail covered in hair. Its face was human, with deep-set eyes and short neck. The creature combed the hair on its head with what looked like short, stubby arms before diving into the water and washing its torso.

properties.name: Long Necked Creature
properties.description: Two local men reported this creature after watching it splashing around in the waters. They described it as having the head and neck of a giraffe, and although the body remained submerged, a dorsal fin could be seen.
properties.location_name: Campbeltown Loch (Argyll and Bute) - Kilkerran Bay

properties.name: Gorilla?
properties.description: A group of friends walking through the area in the early hours of the morning spotted a tall dark figure which quickly ducked down - one of the group approached the figure, only to hear it run off. Walking back towards their car, two of the group heard a roar which they believed sounded like a silver backed gorilla. One person also reported red eyes glaring at them from the bushes.

properties.name: Red Sea Monster
properties.description: Two unidentified sea creatures were found dead on the island within a year of each other. Both were less than a metre in size, brown-red in colour, possessing sharp teeth, large eyes, gills, and what appeared to be two legs and feet.

properties.name: Coat Tearer
properties.description: One man had a lucky escape from a water horse that emerged from the loch. As the man fled, the creature paused to rip apart a coat that had been left behind, which gave the potential victim enough time to reach his home where his barking dogs drove the water horse away.
properties.location_name: Caolas, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Loch an Air

properties.name: Whirlpool
properties.description: A water monster was once thought to live in this river. A whirlpool would be seen to form as the monster consumed drowned bodies.

properties.name: Eel-like Creature
properties.description: The lough is said to be home to a lake monster which resembles a rather large eel. The site featured in a few news outlets in 2019 after Adrian Shine, a Loch Ness Monster hunter, was invited to investigate the waters.

properties.name: Growling
properties.description: While moving equipment ready for a rave, this witness and several friends reporting hearing growling from the darkness which appeared to follow them. The witness described the sound as 'seriously weird', a cross between a snarl and a howl.
properties.location_name: Carlton-in-Cleveland (North Yorkshire) - Hills in the area

properties.name: Fiery Water Horse
properties.description: The water horse which would wait by the banks of the Towy had balls of fire for eyes, and breath of a furnace.

properties.name: Grey Water Horse
properties.description: This water horse waits on the shores of the bay for a gullible person to approach. One man took the horse and used the creature to drive his cart. Shortly after, the horse dragged man and cart into the sea where they were never seen again.

properties.name: Large Black Figure
properties.description: A woman walking her dog encountered a seven foot (2.13 metre) tall black figure with wide shoulders and no neck. The dog became excited; the witness screamed and fled.
properties.location_name: Carmyllie (Angus) - Close to a disused sandstone quarry (area now redeveloped)

properties.name: Strange Creature
properties.description: A large animal was said to have made this location its home. Although no description of the creature was properly given, it was said to have been large and hunted for food on land. Local men tried to capture the beast but were unable to do so. After several years, the creature left the area, thought to have gone out to sea.

properties.name: Teenager
properties.description: Henry Reynolds spotted a merman off the coast which he initially believed to be a pale skinned teenager before noticing its brown tail which waved about. While the body and arms looked human, the arms and hands looked too short and thick, while the creature's nose was long and sharp. Reynolds went to find other witnesses, but by the time he returned, the merman had gone.
properties.location_name: Castlemartin (Dyfed) - Off the coast in the area

properties.name: Horse-Like Monster
properties.description: Sir Walter Scott wrote that people he had known had encountered a horse-like monster in this loch.
properties.location_name: Cauldshiels Loch (Borders) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Dark Blue Bird
properties.description: Three people reported seeing a large flying creature, 'much larger than a bat', slowly glide past them as they explored the first cavern of these caves.
properties.location_name: Chapel le Dale (North Yorkshire) - Cave system in village

properties.name: Pointy Humps
properties.description: Two witnesses reported seeing seven pointed humps coming out of the sea, together with a serpentine head.

properties.name: Large Bird
properties.description: A witness watched a huge bird, with a fourteen foot wingspan, pass overhead. Moving with great haste, it quickly disappeared over nearby shops.

properties.name: Bear
properties.description: A bear was said to have taken up residence in the surrounding woodland. Described as dark brown and the size of a sheepdog, the creature's footprints were also discovered, although it is not known whether they were formally identified as belonging to a bear.

properties.name: Hare Fox
properties.description: A father and daughter spotted a grey creature which resembled a fox, although it had long brown ears with white tips which were hare-like. They watched it jumping across a field until it disappeared into a hedge.
properties.location_name: Chelmsford (Essex) - Outside of town, exact area not known

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: This mermaid was so upset at the way she was treated by onlookers, she drove to the bottom of the pool and never returned. It was thought that she guarded a mass of treasure.

properties.name: Green Faced Monkey
properties.description: This four foot high fella was seen over a dozen times over the summer months, running along the floor of the woods. He may not have survived the winter, as no reports were forthcoming the following year.

properties.name: Flapping Serpent
properties.description: A man rowing towards Portishead claimed to have been attacked and pushed overboard by a long snake-like creature which jumped out of the water at him. He managed to climb back on board his boat and row onwards.

properties.name: Loathsome Creature
properties.description: After spotting this strange water creature, one of the witnesses had nightmares for weeks after the event and refused to return alone to the lake. She said the creature was long and black, with smooth skin and a head like a shark.

properties.name: Sea Creature with Horses Ears
properties.description: This sea monster was seen briefly off the coast.

properties.name: Petticoat Loose
properties.description: The monster known as Petticoat Loose has a body like that of a horse, while the head takes the form of a woman. When it appears (which is rare), the creature asks, 'When will the day of judgement come?' before disappearing back under the water.
properties.location_name: Clogheen (County Tipperary) - Large pond in the Knockmealdown Mountains

properties.name: Each Uisge
properties.description: A local girl had a narrow escape after encountering a water horse which had taken the form of young man by the edge of the loch, only realising his true nature after spotting sand and mud in his hair. She fled home and told her brother, who killed it with his sword the following morning.

properties.name: Multi-Winged Bird
properties.description: More paranormal than a physical cryptid, a large white bird the size of a goose but with multiple wings appeared before a girl in the village. She circled the creature nine times and it vanished.
properties.location_name: Coal Ashon (Derbyshire) - Exact area not known

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: This phantom dog is said to hide around the base of the building.

properties.name: Red eyed Figure
properties.description: The CFZ website reported that a couple walking along a dark lane encountered a seven foot tall, black, man-shaped figure with glowing red eyes. The couple watched it for around fifteen seconds before it screamed and dropped from view.
properties.location_name: Cold Hesledon (Durham) - Lane leading away from the Pemberton Arms

properties.name: Red Monkey
properties.description: For years, this creature was reported in the bushes of the area, attacking anyone who came too close.

properties.name: Macphie's Captor
properties.description: A mermaid kidnapped a man by the name of Macphie and held him in a cave along the island's shore. Macphie was given any item he wished for but remained unhappy until he managed to escape. The mermaid gave chase, but Macphie tossed his dog into the sea. Dog and mermaid fought until they both died from their injuries.
properties.location_name: Colonsay (Argyll and Bute) - Unknown cave along the coast

properties.name: Something Large
properties.description: Something tore a large hole in a pike net, and a diver searching for a drowned body encountered something that frightened him to such an extent that he said he would never return to the lake.

properties.name: Crocodile
properties.description: A lizard-like creature just under a metre in length was seen by several users of this quarry when it was converted to a water sports centre.
properties.location_name: Congleton (Cheshire) - Old quarry (now Astbury Mere Water Park)

properties.name: Horned Eel
properties.description: A family watched this creature, almost four metres long, swimming around the lake. It was described as dark and long without hair or eyes, and possessed two horns at the top of the head.
properties.location_name: Connemara (County Galway) - Lough Nahioon (also spelt Nahooin)

properties.name: Giant Cow
properties.description: A giant skeleton, around 6.5 metres in length, which was dug up on the banks of the river was believed (at the time) to belong to a rather large cow.

properties.name: Dog Attack
properties.description: A group of men with a dog were watching over cattle when they heard strange sounds, resembling a herd of sheep, although nothing could be seen. As the sounds passed, the dog chased the invisible entity. The dog later returned but died soon after, its hair scraped off with skin ripped and bloodied.
properties.location_name: Cornaigbeg (aka Cornaig Beag), Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Land around Cornaigbeg Farm

properties.name: Brocshee
properties.description: Several local farmers reported seeing a giant badger swimming in this lake, and the story made the front page of the local newspaper.

properties.name: Hairy Man
properties.description: A family returning home from a Christmas party in December 2015 were driving along Owthorpe Road when they witnessed a tall, hairy figure standing by the side of the road. British Bigfoot Research visited the site in January 2016 and investigated nearby Cotgrave Forest, where they heard howls from an unidentified creature.
properties.location_name: Cotgrave (Nottinghamshire) - Owthorpe Road and Cotgrave Forest

properties.name: Long Neck with Series of Humps
properties.description: The witness described the creature as having a head like a seal, with a long neck and several humps. He also said that the event occurred very quickly...

properties.name: Playing Creatures
properties.description: Four people watched several strange black creatures playing and diving around the surface of the lake. They said the animals were larger than horses, which they could see standing on the shoreline.
properties.location_name: Croagh Patrick (County Mayo) - Lough na Corra

properties.name: Buggane
properties.description: This monster, an ogre-like entity, was stirred by the ringing of the bells of this church - it tore off the roof while trying to silence the peals. Another story says that the roof is missing because demons would throw it to the ground after each repair until either the locals eventually gave up trying to fix it, or local tailor Timothy Clucas tricked the Buggane into exploding its own head.
properties.location_name: Crosby (Isle of Man) - St Trinian's Church

properties.name: Creature from Curridge
properties.description: A sixty centimetre tall, long necked creature was seen by Don Prater as he walked his dog. He described the creature as being dark or grey in colour, having a head like a deer, a neck similar to a swan and a bushy tail. The picture he sketched of the creature resembled an alpaca, although all local alpacas were accounted for.
properties.location_name: Curridge (Berkshire) - Passageway behind the Women's Institute Hall

properties.name: Devil's Footprints
properties.description: Thousands of cloven footprints covered Devon on the evening of 8 Feb 1855, resembling that of a donkey - apart from they ran in a straight line, covered roofs as well as roads, and stretched around one hundred miles across the county - Dawlish is just one of many places touched by the strange event.

properties.name: Mermaids
properties.description: These women of the water were seen dozens of times during the last twenty years of the nineteenth century. One was said to have been shot, but the body was not recovered.

properties.name: Piranha
properties.description: The carcasses of two dead piranha were found on the shore of the lake causing concern to people in the area. The environment department of the local council said it was unlikely that the toothed fish had lived in the lake.

properties.name: Gorilla?
properties.description: A teenager photographed a strange creature close to her home in parkland, before it ran up a tree and disappeared. The teenager said the creature resembled and moved like a small gorilla, although the nearby Monkey World attraction denied they were missing any of their animals.

properties.name: Wildmen
properties.description: This area was once thought to be the home of large hairy men, who abducted women.

properties.name: Humanoid Creature
properties.description: A man who spotted a half-man, half-animal entity was so scared he called the police. Various postmen in the village talked of seeing strange things, but it is unclear whether these are the same entity as the November sighting. It has also been speculated that the sighting may be connected to an old Anglo-Saxon burial ground close to the bridge. Reports of the area being haunted by a Shag Foal go back almost one hundred years.
properties.location_name: Dorrington (Lincolnshire) - Area near the Railway bridge

properties.name: Vampire Birds
properties.description: The People newspaper reported that a farmer lost three sheep to 'vampire' birds during the harsh winter conditions. Other newspapers were a little less dramatic, blaming starving crows and foxes.
properties.location_name: Driffield (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Kester Farm

properties.name: Centaur?
properties.description: Two people travelling by car had to stop and wait for a couple of minutes after a horse with a human's face blocked the road. The terrified couple were frozen with fear and when the creature suddenly vanished, they turned around and drove home.
properties.location_name: Drogheda (County Louth) - Unnamed road in the area

properties.name: Armed Creature
properties.description: A farmer claimed to have shot at a creature in this lough after it passed within two metres on his boat. The monster, described as being seven-foot (2.13 metres) in length, had two arms that had claws (or webbing). The creature dived under the surface.
properties.location_name: Drumate Lough (also referred to as Dromate) (County Monaghan) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Playful Creature
properties.description: A father and daughter watched a sea monster playing in the waters just off the coast for 30 minutes.

properties.name: Treasure Guardians
properties.description: A team of mermaids and mermen are reported to guard a hidden cave full of treasure.
properties.location_name: Duncansby Head (Highland) - Cave system off coast

properties.name: Ape-like Creature
properties.description: Driving home from work, this witness watched as a tall, dark brown hairy figure crossed the road in three strides. The driver had to stop to avoid hitting the figure.

properties.name: Hairy Man
properties.description: A driver reported in the local press that he had encountered a tall (around 2.7 metres) dark figure in woodland as he drove past.
properties.location_name: Dundee (Angus) - A92, approaching Tay Road Bridge

properties.name: Tall Hairy Man
properties.description: While out with her dogs, a holiday maker saw a three metre tall man, with red glowing eyes and long grey hair that covered his body. The creature moved away, and the woman thought it better not to pursue.

properties.name: Muirdris
properties.description: When Fergus mac Leti, a king of Ulster, first encountered the monster known as Muirdris, his face was left permanently contorted in terror. To prevent the king from finding out, all mirrors were banned in his company. Seven years later, Fergus discovered the truth and returned to Dundrum Bay to kill the monster. After a battle which lasted two days, the king killed the beast but himself died soon after from exhaustion.
properties.location_name: Dundrum (County Down) - Dundrum Bay, was known as Lough Rudraige

properties.name: Enchanted Eel
properties.description: This eel must have been of great size, as it terrorised the neighbourhood, eating sheep and cattle. A man tending the graveyard encountered the creature one morning and severed its tail with a hook. Enraged, the creature attacked but the man managed to run away. What happened to the creature after that is undocumented, but the man in question died a few days later, and one of the headstones in the graveyard is said to be decorated with a hook and an eel.
properties.location_name: Dunkettle (County Cork) - Lake close to small graveyard

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: Mr W J. Wood was trout fishing at this lough when he observed a three metre long dark brown creature some ninety metres away. Wood said that the creature had a head like a calf which was held above the water line. After a couple of minutes, the creature sank beneath the water. A Japanese film crew visited the lake in 2004 to search for the monster, without success.

properties.name: Captured Fisherman
properties.description: A good looking fisherman once caught the eye of a mermaid who fell in love with him - she kidnapped the fellow and held him in a secret cave under the sea, where he remains to this day...

properties.name: Long Serpent
properties.description: Observed several times around this period, this creature would remain visible for long periods of time.

properties.name: Dun Cow
properties.description: Said to have been used to scare children into going to bed, the Dun Cow of Dunsmore Heath was of massive size, and sported eighteen teats. The beast was slain by Guy of Warwick.
properties.location_name: Dunsmore Heath (Warwickshire) - Fields in the area

properties.name: Hairy Creature
properties.description: During an evening fishing trip, Michael Kent reported encountering a dark hairy figure, standing around four foot tall. The creature vanished into the bushes.
properties.location_name: E11 (Greater London) - Hollow Ponds area, Epping Forest, Wanstead

properties.name: Short Hairy Creature
properties.description: A local paper reported that Irene Dainty encountered a four foot, hairy creature with large feet and animal-like eyes close to her home. The creature ran off and jumped over the wall of the nearby Three Jolly Wheelers public house.
properties.location_name: E18 (Greater London) - Love Lane area, Woodford Bridge

properties.name: Swan Eater
properties.description: Users of the river had reported the swan population had been dropping, and in November 2011 one witness watched a large Canada goose being quickly pulled under the water. Opinions of what is snacking on the local bird life have ranged from the exotic (escaped or released alligator or python) to the reasonable (catfish or pike).
properties.location_name: E20 (Greater London) - Lea River, Olympic Park

properties.name: Bear Hunt
properties.description: Four children reported seeing a bear in the marshland at Hackney in 1981- investigators discovered large paw prints in the snow but failed to find the animal. In 2012, student Helen Murray took two photographs of a large black haired creature in the area.

properties.name: Croc
properties.description: After witnesses watched a Canada goose pulled under the water never to reappear, several people declared the attack must have been by a crocodile. However, experts said the attacker was much more likely to have been a large pike.

properties.name: Green Serpent
properties.description: This sea creature was snakelike, possessing a flat head. It appeared to gasp for air as it skimmed across the waves.
properties.location_name: Easington (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Off coast

properties.name: Large Wormlike Creature
properties.description: Observed by the Mayor, a MP and four other people, a creature twelve metres long moved like a worm on the surface of the water. One witness estimated the speed to be up to ninety miles per hour.

properties.name: Wish Granter
properties.description: A local man caught a mermaid on the rocks, only releasing the creature after she agreed to grant three wishes. One of the wishes ensured the man became a skilled doctor, another granted the gift of prophecy, but the third wish, to become a great singer, apparently failed.

properties.name: Pale Ape?
properties.description: A lorry driver reported seeing a hairy, light grey creature on all fours moving through grass around ninety metres away. The driver could see the creature had forward facing eyes, a long snout, and upright ears. The creature then stood on its hind legs and approached the driver, moving like an ape, before dropping once again to all fours and running off, peering over its shoulder as it left.

properties.name: Brenin Llwyd
properties.description: The Brenin Llwyd, or Grey King, could be classed as a hairy man entity. It has scared dogs and is weary of any kind of light.

properties.name: Hungry Worm
properties.description: A group of men swimming across this lake were attacked by a large worm-like monster. One of the people was grabbed and consumed by the beast.

properties.name: Fish Man
properties.description: Fishermen on the shore caught a four foot tall humanoid, with duck-like feet and a tail protruding from its back. The creature tried to escape but was killed when the fishermen beat it with sticks. Another fish-man was caught a few months later in the same area, though this one was described with more seal-like qualities.

properties.name: Giant Bat
properties.description: A witness walking down the road late at night stated he watched a giant bat, with a wingspan of 1.2 metres, swoop around the churchyard along this road.

properties.name: Capybara
properties.description: This large rodent, not native to the UK, was spotted in 2006, sitting in the middle of the A1. Another creature, or possibly the same one, was spotted three years later at Reston.

properties.name: Vanishing Twitchers
properties.description: Two birdwatchers that vanished without trace on the island after arriving with 86 other people were thought by locals to have been taken by merfolk. An extensive search by the coastguard could find no trace of the missing people.

properties.name: Morgawr Monster
properties.description: This huge lizard was seen several times during 1976 - one witness managed to take a couple of photographs of the creature. Several years prior to this sighting, two fishermen reportedly caught a hairy monster about six metres in length, five kilometres off the coast. In the mid-1980s a student volunteer witnessed what he described as a 'dinosaur powering out to sea'.

properties.name: Hopping Creature
properties.description: Several witnesses came forward over the first few months of 2009 claiming to have spotted a strange creature in bushes and trees in the area. Descriptions varied slightly between sightings, with one witness describing it as slightly larger than a dog, with a cat's face, walking on its hind legs. Another witness said it hopped away.

properties.name: Sea Beast
properties.description: A coastguard reported a large sea monster with large glowing eyes off the coast here, its neck reaching 250cm from the water, its body a further 10 metres in length. A local legend tells how a dragon was killed by locals close to the location where the monster was seen.

properties.name: Humped Creature
properties.description: A female witness reported seeing a strange creature with two humps in the lough.

properties.name: Giraffe's Neck
properties.description: A partly submerged creature with a neck not dissimilar to that of a giraffe, though it had the head of a camel, was seen in the waters by several fishermen.
properties.location_name: Firth of Clyde (Argyll and Bute) - Cumbrae Islands - waters off Great Cumbrae

properties.name: Black Creature
properties.description: The earlier sighting here lasted around fifteen minutes, and the serpentine creature was seen by over one hundred people. The twentieth century sighting was of a totally different creature, this time brown in colour and possessing a horse's head with large eyes.

properties.name: Small Bear
properties.description: This displaced animal was seen by several witnesses in different places, all within the same evening.

properties.name: Luminous Water Horse
properties.description: The water horse found here helped an old man cross the marshland without incident. Within hours of appearing, the marsh was flooded by a freak tide. Another story says that people can often feel frightened in the area for no real reason.
properties.location_name: Flemingston (South Glamorgan) - River Thaw and marshy land in the area

properties.name: Moose-Pig
properties.description: A massive wild boar was said to live in the forest, large enough to knock down trees. A group of farmers hunted the creature but were unable to find the beast.

properties.name: Little Man
properties.description: 'A little, brown, man-like creature' was found at this mine - witnesses used sticks to move it, but it was 'lost' a short time later.
properties.location_name: Forest of Dean (Gloucestershire) - Poolway Colliery (no longer operational)

properties.name: Well Dweller
properties.description: An old story, most likely told to prevent children playing too close to the well, said a mermaid waited in the water at the bottom. She was ready to drown children who touched the water's edge.
properties.location_name: Fornham All Saints (Suffolk) - Well in the village (exact location unknown)

properties.name: Nygel
properties.description: The Nygel was a water horse that appeared to hang around locations with water wheels, using its teeth to stop the wheel turning. It had a tail that arched over its back. One story said the creature entered a home of a local man; the occupant fled, and the building was left empty for thirty years.

properties.name: Monster Attack
properties.description: The fishing boat Glengrant reported coming under attack by a sixty metre long beast, with a horse's head, though the teeth were long and pointed. The monster only stopped ramming the boat after being shot by a crewman.

properties.name: Bigfoot
properties.description: After hearing of hairy hominid sightings in this area, the British Bigfoot Research Organisation visited the reserve and took a photograph which they later discovered showed a shadowy figure standing behind a tree.

properties.name: Water Cow
properties.description: To kill the water cow, said to be the size of an upturned boat, the landowner tried to drain the loch. When this failed, he poured quicklime into the water to poison the creature.

properties.name: Gallan Whale
properties.description: Martin Martin reported that a large mystery whale terrorised the fishing boats of Gallan Head. It would deliberately sink the boats before returning to eat the crew.
properties.location_name: Gallan Head, Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Off coast

properties.name: Sea Monster
properties.description: Captain J.C. Robinson, famous for assisting in the rescue of 712 Titanic survivors, encountered a sea monster while acting Chief Officer of the Campania. The creature, which Robinson sketched, had a neck which rose eight or nine feet (243 - 274 centimetres) out of the water, the trunk of which was twelve inches (30 centimetres) thick.
properties.location_name: Galley Head (County Cork) - Sea off Galley Head, heading towards Queenstown

properties.name: Pig Fish
properties.description: A sea monster caught by a fishing crew was seen by thousands of people. The creature was described as nine and a half feet (2.9 metres) long, weighed 136 kilograms and had silvery skin. The head had a long pig-like snout, cow-like eyes, and two rows of teeth, each tooth around two inches (5 centimetres) in length. The creature also had an underbelly of sharp thorns. The Dublin Museum later identified the creature as a bramble shark.

properties.name: Charging Beast
properties.description: Two fishermen out collecting lobster pots reported seeing an unidentified creature which rose thirty feet out of the water and charged towards them. They quickly rowed ashore. A sixteenth century text states that a water horse the size of a greyhound emerged and killed three men with as many swipes of its tail; the remainder of the group survived by running away or climbing the trees.
properties.location_name: Gare Loch (Argyll and Bute) - Mouth of the loch

properties.name: British Bigfoot
properties.description: A small group of teenagers sitting in a car spotted a hairy humanoid in woodland close by. They drove off at speed, only to be pursued by the creature, even though it remained in the tree line.

properties.name: Dead Sea Serpent
properties.description: When fishermen discovered the rotting body of a creature just under ten metres in length, possessing a head of a camel and a neck resembling a giraffe's, the media immediately compared the creature with the Loch Ness Monster. However, natural history scientists dismissed the creature as either a decaying whale or basking shark.

properties.name: Fachan
properties.description: This mythical creature has set up home in Etive, though it is said to travel for miles around the area of the Glen. It is described as possessing only one leg, a single hand which protrudes from the middle of its chest, and a solitary eye in the centre of the head. The famed hunter MacChallum a Chronain made his home in the Glen and was reputedly the last man to understand the fairy ways, using his skill to track a woman who had been kidnapped by the little people.

properties.name: Beithir
properties.description: A beithir is an unknown savage animal, sometimes thought to be a serpent or dragon. One such creature was said to have made its home in the mountainous region.
properties.location_name: Glencoe (Argyll and Bute) - Ben Vair (aka Ben Vehir), in a mountain to the south of the area

properties.name: Drowned John
properties.description: Struggling to maintain his farm, John MacInnes was told by a strange being that help would be provided, but certain conditions would have to be followed. John agreed, and a short time later found a stray horse which he used to plough his land. He would bless the creature every evening and it continued to work, but unfortunately on one occasion forgot. The horse picked John up and dashed into the loch, where man and beast vanished forever.
properties.location_name: Glenelg (Highland) - John MacInnes' Loch (six miles out of village)

properties.name: Humped Creature
properties.description: A party of seven people rowing across the water encountered a black, multi-humped creature. It was described as moving almost erratically, sometimes showing six humps, sometimes only revealing its head. The beast moved quite close to the small boat, startling one of the passengers to such an extent that she refused to make the return journey by sea and walked the thirteen miles journey back home by moonlight over wild tracks. The creature was reported several more times during the same month by other witnesses.
properties.location_name: Glenelg (Highland) - Waters between town and Lochourn

properties.name: Rhino-Like Creature
properties.description: A cow-sized creature was hooked by a man and his son out fishing along the river - the monster emerged from the water after the younger of the two snagged it with his hook. The strange beast was described as having short stumpy legs, small ears, and a single horn protruding from its nose.
properties.location_name: Glinsk (County Galway) - Lough Dubh, aka Black's Lake, between Glinsk and Roscommon

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: A traveller once climbed on the back of a white stallion in this area, which flew him many miles before fading into mist and dissipating.
properties.location_name: Glyn Neath (West Glamorgan) - Area where rivers meet

properties.name: Yellow Monster
properties.description: Several witnesses reported seeing a long necked sea monster along this coastline. It was estimated to be between twelve and fifteen metres in length, and brown and yellow in colour.

properties.name: Turtle-like Monster
properties.description: This creature was described as turtle-like, having large front flippers, small back flippers and a shell, but was also said to have a small head on a thin, two foot long neck. It was around 27.5 metres off the coast, and it was said that fish beached themselves to escape the creature.
properties.location_name: Goodrington (Devon) - Off coast at Saltern Cove

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: The lake is said to be home of a mysterious water monster resembling a horse.

properties.name: Giant Eel
properties.description: At least one swimmer in the Lough has been chased by a wriggling water monster, named by locals as a giant eel.

properties.name: Serpent
properties.description: St. Finbarr arrived in this area and found a serpent living in the lake. He caught the beast and threw it to one side. It landed many miles away, the impression of its body in the earth filling with water and becoming Lough Allua.

properties.name: Rotting Carcass
properties.description: The decaying body of a strange creature, with neck as long as the body, was discovered by two local council workers. They estimated the length to be around twenty-eight feet (just under nine metres). The men were ordered to destroy the corpse by the Royal Navy, who were using the area at the time. Another serpent sighting in the area occurred in 1913; this time the creature was alive and well and swimming in the river.

properties.name: Sea Horse
properties.description: This creature possessed a long neck (the only part of the beast above the water line), down which a lengthy mane ran. The total length of the monster was estimated to be nine metres.
properties.location_name: Gower Peninsula (West Glamorgan) - Waters of Fall Bay

properties.name: Guardian Griffin
properties.description: This mystical beast once set up home at the well and attacked anyone who tried to draw water. A wandering knight fought the creature, killing the part lion, part eagle beast with arrows from afar.

properties.name: Hopping Roo
