properties.description: Three motorists reported seeing a 1.5 metre tall kangaroo jumping down the road. The creature narrowly missed a car and vanished over a wall.

properties.name: Fox-Headed Creature
properties.description: Sue Langham reported seeing a creature around the size of a small dog with a fox-like head and muscular body. The witness said the animal was making the sound of a 'strangled wolf'. Earlier in the month the creature (or something similar) was spotted along Belmont Road.

properties.name: Wild Man
properties.description: One man reportedly saw a large hairy creature with pale eyes, which resembled a man. Other sightings have come from a nearby quarry. One theory is that these creatures are the ghosts of Neanderthals.

properties.name: Long Creature
properties.description: Seen as it crossed the beach and vanished into the sea, the creature was said to have a head around a metre in length. Large footprints were left in the sand. A strong unpleasant smell also remained in the air, and the witness's dog was in an 'upset' state after the sighting.
properties.location_name: Helensburgh (Argyll and Bute) - Eastern part of the beach, mouth of the Clyde

properties.name: Monster Pursuit
properties.description: Crowds of holiday makers watched several boats chase a sea serpent across the bay. The creature was said to be brown and yellow in colour, and about twenty feet (six metres) long. It managed to evade and escape the pursuers.

properties.name: Grampus
properties.description: A tree in the churchyard was home to a Grampus, a kind of water loving animal like a dolphin. After upsetting the locals (by harassing their teenage daughters), a priest was called in and banished the creature for a thousand years.

properties.name: Wallabies
properties.description: Police warned motorists to watch out for wallabies on the road, after several reports of the small marsupials were made by motorists.

properties.name: Long Sea Monster
properties.description: Two sightseers watched a fast moving long creature in the sea here, with a small head and several humps, travelling northwards.
properties.location_name: Hilston (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Off coast

properties.name: Scapasaurus
properties.description: The rotting corpse of a basking shark that washed up on the beach was mistaken as a Scapasaurus or sea monster, killed by depth charges.

properties.name: Boar
properties.description: Wild boar have been reported living in this area of woodland.

properties.name: Bigfoot
properties.description: According to the local press, a man walking his dogs through this woodland encountered a seven foot (2.13 metre) tall hairy biped. The creature walked across the track ahead of the witness and could be heard moving through the undergrowth.

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: This creature would attack anyone using the bridge over the burn at night.

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: The water horse that lived on the hill would attack people travelling along the road to Longhope.

properties.name: Soft Landing
properties.description: A young lad sitting on the rocks claimed he was attacked by a sea monster with a horse's mane - it tried (unsuccessfully) to clamp its teeth around his legs. Many years later, a diver reported descending to investigate a sunken German ship, only to realise he was standing on the back of a large sea monster that had made its home in the wreak.

properties.name: Thick Necked Creature
properties.description: While travelling along the coast in heavy fog, the crew of a steamship spotted what they first mistook for a mast jutting out of the water. The 'mast' then moved to the side of the ship, the men realising that it was the neck of a large creature. It then drew a large black body from out the water, then dived and vanished. Sightings of the creature continued for several years.
properties.location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Off coast

properties.name: Headless Bird Man
properties.description: A group of teenagers ran from a creature that emerged from behind a tree and approached them. It was described as man shaped but headless, completely black, had webbed feet, and a pair of large bat wings. Prior to this, they had watched a mysterious light flickering about the sky.

properties.name: Sea Folk
properties.description: The area around the island was said to be home to many mermaids and kelpies.

properties.name: Four Metre Eel
properties.description: This oversized eel (or eel-like creature) was seen swimming in the river - locals have claimed the creature has been around for the last ten years or so, both here and by Loch Assynt.

properties.name: Coiled Serpent
properties.description: This creature was seemingly disturbed by a passing boat and swam away, revealing rows of black fins that ran down the side of its twenty to thirty metre long body.
properties.location_name: Instow (Devon) - Water between town and Bideford Bay

properties.name: First Nessie Sighting
properties.description: Contrary to popular belief, it was in the River Ness and not Loch Ness that St Columba drove away a water monster which had attacked and killed a local man. When told about the attack, the Saint asked one of his followers to swim across the river to fetch a boat on the opposite bank. As the creature surfaced to eat its latest victim, St Columba made the sign of the cross and the creature fled.

properties.name: Kangaroo
properties.description: A driver and passenger reported seeing a young kangaroo travelling along a busy Ipswich road.

properties.name: Man Eaten
properties.description: A Quebec trading vessel claimed to have had a man consumed by a sixty-foot (18.3 metre) long serpent, as thick as a hogshead cask.
properties.location_name: Isle of Arran (Ayrshire) - Thirty miles (south?) from Island

properties.name: Resting Creature
properties.description: This sea monster was observed resting on rocks - it was described as looking like a seal, though it had a parrot-like beak and was bigger than an elephant.
properties.location_name: Isle of Arran (Ayrshire) - Unknown beach on the island

properties.name: Long Necked Creature
properties.description: This creature, mistaken for a rock when first observed, was seen at various times by up to thirteen fishing boat crews. It was described as between 70 and 80 foot in length (21 - 24 metres) and possessing a long neck which it kept underwater whilst swimming.
properties.location_name: Isle of Canna (Highland) - Off the coast, and around surrounding islands

properties.name: White Figure
properties.description: Neil McIntosh, steering a boat between the two islands in stormy weather, glimpsed a white human figure in the sea. McIntosh thought the creature to be a mermaid, although admitted he did not have much of an opportunity to examine it.
properties.location_name: Isle of Canna (Highland) - Sea, about quarter of the way between island and Skye

properties.name: Dead Creature
properties.description: The remains of a creature some 8.5 metres long were originally named as being that of a Scapasaurus, although later revealed to be a carcass of a basking shark.
properties.location_name: Isle of Hunda (Orkney) - Hope of Hunda

properties.name: Serpent
properties.description: A sea serpent spent a couple of weeks at this location, playing in the water. The creature, described as between 18 and 24 metres in length, had a mane like a horse and appeared white in colour.
properties.location_name: Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Broad Bay area

properties.name: Feolagan
properties.description: The Feolagan was a small mouse-sized creature (or fairy) that would paralyse sheep by walking across the animal's back. The sheep would die unless the Feolagan was forced retrace its steps, or, if many sheep were affected, by capturing the Feolagan in a jar filled with salt and using the grains to cure the sheep.
properties.location_name: Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Kebock Head

properties.name: Searrach Uisge
properties.description: Resembling a capsized boat, this creature has been reported swimming around for one and a half centuries. Locals say lambs were once offered annually to the creature.
properties.location_name: Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Loch Suainbhal

properties.name: Bumpy Beast
properties.description: A German ship 15 kilometres off the coast reported a sea serpent around 40 metres in length, several bumps protruding from the water, along the creatures back. Sea serpents have also been reported at the southern side of the island.
properties.location_name: Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Sea off the north of the island

properties.name: Snakelike Beast
properties.description: This creature, when first observed, possessed no fins, and was estimated to be twenty five metres in length. When seen again almost sixty years later, its speed was estimated to be around 15 kph, and was seen by several independent parties.
properties.location_name: Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Sea east of the island, and other nearby areas

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: A mermaid perched on a rock threw herself into the sea when approached by two local men walking along the beach. After the mermaid entered the sea, the witnesses could see the creature's upper body resembled a woman.
properties.location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - Beach on north end of island

properties.name: Hybrid
properties.description: Folklorist Walter Traill Dennison recorded in 1893 that there were decedents of a union between a male selkie and a woman living on the island - you could tell who the offspring were because they had webbed hands and feet.
properties.location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - Exact area not known

properties.name: Black Eagle
properties.description: Scottish chronicler Martin Martin wrote about a black eagle that hunted deer. The eagle was not the largest bird in the area but would bring deer down by grasping their antlers and flapping its wings in front of its prey's eyes. The confused deer would then injure itself, allowing the eagle to feast on the prone animal.
properties.location_name: Isle of Skye (Highland) - Exact area not known

properties.name: Cro Sith
properties.description: After attempting to round up Cro sith, fairy cows that normally live under the sea, a farmer was found dead after the fairy herdsman turned up and reclaimed his cattle, leaving only the trampled body of the farmer behind. Another cryptid that has made its home on Skye is the Biasd na Srogaig, a large creature that lives in lochs, possesses a single horn emerging from its forehead, and has long legs.

properties.name: Rotating Light
properties.description: This creature, which sounds very much like a bizarre submarine, possessed a long 'neck' that had a shining light from the top, which turned from side to side.

properties.name: Great Auk Returns
properties.description: Years after shooting a mystery water bird, a hunter said the creature may have been a Great Auk. If correct, this is certainly the last sighting of a Great Auk, decades after it was declared extinct.

properties.name: Fillan
properties.description: Martin Martin documented a small worm that lived between the flesh and skin, known as either a Fiollan or Fillan. It was about 2.5 centimetres in length, as wide as a goose quill, and it had little legs either side of the body that enabled it to crawl around under the skin. Once there, it would cause tumours and abscesses.
properties.location_name: Isle of Skye (Outer Hebrides) - No exact location documented

properties.name: Heavy Breather
properties.description: While fishing off the coast of the island, two fishermen reported coming across a large reptilian creature, the size of a cow, though it appeared much larger beneath the water level. The witnesses were so close they could quite clearly hear the creature breathing.
properties.location_name: Isle of Soay, Skye (Highland) - Sea around the island

properties.name: Halibut-Tailed creature
properties.description: Three (or six) fishermen claimed to have caught a ninety centimetre long mermaid while at sea. The creature had arms around 23 centimetres in length with webbed fingers, and while it had blue eyes and nostrils, no ears or chin could be seen. It possessed a tail similar to a halibut and had two fins on its shoulders. After listening to the creature wail for three hours, the fishermen threw it back into the sea.
properties.location_name: Isle of Yell (Shetland) - Thirty miles off the coast

properties.name: Sea Monster
properties.description: Travelling to Kinsale (Co Cork) in his sloop, Patrick Dolan spotted a worm-like creature, some twelve metres long, with a two metre neck lifted above the water. The creature remained visible for 20 minutes.
properties.location_name: Isles of Scilly (Cornwall) - 30 miles NNW of islands

properties.name: Transforming Water Horse
properties.description: A woman managed to escape a water horse which had taken the form of an attractive man after spotting sand in his hair. Looking back as she ran, the woman could see the furious creature tearing up the earth. At least one batch of cut peat remained uncollected after a rumour began that the water horse had returned to the area.
properties.location_name: Keoldale (Highland) - Loch Meadaidh (Referred to as Loch Meudaidh)

properties.name: Long Beast
properties.description: A sea monster, sixty foot in length with thirty 'blobs' running along its back, has been seen three times during the twentieth century. The first sighting was documented by the daughter of author H. Rider Haggard, which she described in a letter to her father on 20 July 1912. The most recent sighting occurred in 1978. It has been speculated that the creature was a procession of seals leaping in and out of the sea.

properties.name: Bassie
properties.description: A humped creature between twelve and thirteen feet (3.7 - 4 metres) long, with a head like a python featured in a couple of newspaper reports. This sighting should not be confused with reports of 'Eachy', a known hoax attached to the lake in 2006.

properties.name: Mermaid?
properties.description: A mermaid which was spotted several times in this area was described as a 'normal' woman, leading one to conclude that maybe she was..?

properties.name: Beast with Horse's Head
properties.description: A sea monster spotted several times during the nineteenth century was reported to have a head like a horse, a mane of seaweed, and a large body which was hidden under the water.
properties.location_name: Kilkee (County Clare) - Off coast and near location known as Diamond Rocks

properties.name: Bruckee
properties.description: A demonic badger known as a Bruckee was said to live in this area. One witness who claimed to have observed the creature said it was hairy, brown and had eyes the size of turnips.

properties.name: Pale Eel-Thing
properties.description: Cryptozoologist Jonathan Downes and his wife spotted a nine-foot (2.74 metre) long, pale eel-like creature swimming in these waters. One piece of folklore says the fairy queen of Kerry created the lake to punish a former lover.

properties.name: Bloodied mermaid
properties.description: A mermaid who frequented the lake would often steal wine from the crypt beneath the church (or the cellar of Newhall, depending on the storyteller). She was stabbed (or shot) when caught but managed to drag herself back to the lake before dying. The waters are said to turn red with her blood every forty years.
properties.date: Every forty years, exact date unknown
properties.location_name: Killone Lake (County Clare) - Waters of the lake

properties.name: Photographed Monster
properties.description: After brushing against a swimmer, the creature thought to lurk in this lough was accidently photographed by local man Sean Walsh. The beast has been described as over two metres long with a spiky head. Others have speculated the creature is not a monster but a Wells catfish or a pike.

properties.name: Slain Creature
properties.description: A water horse which took the form of a man was killed by the brothers of a woman who it tried to court.
properties.location_name: Kilmoluaig (aka Kilmoluag), Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Loch Bhasabol and farm (no longer standing)

properties.name: Mistaken Horse
properties.description: A lad working on the fields near the lough spotted a stray pony and tied it to his harrow (a large rake). Unbeknown to the boy, the pony was in fact a water horse - the creature bolted to the lough, taking the lad with him. The lad's screams brought people running to the area, but it was too late - the lough was red with the boy's blood.
properties.location_name: Kiltubrid (County Leitrim) - Lough Caogh (aka Blind Lake)

properties.name: Ape-like Bear
properties.description: A party of three boys out exploring came across a large creature with green eyes, like those of a cat. Its body resembled a bear, though it stood upright like an ape.
properties.location_name: Kings Nympton (Devon) - Woods near the village

properties.name: The Thing of Delamere
properties.description: Many locals reported a dog-like creature in the forest here, standing sixty centimetres tall. While the beast was described as having a tail like a fox, it looked more like an Alsatian dog.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: A report exists of a seven foot long mer-creature being seen here, bathing on the rocks.

properties.name: Black Fish
properties.description: A ship managed to bring a strange black fish to shore. The fish measured seven foot (2.1 metres) in length, half of which was a horned head with a mouth filled with sharp teeth. When the side of the fish was pieced, several flounders escaped. The black fish was set free.

properties.name: Not a Seal
properties.description: Two children out picking whelks were shocked when they encountered a mystery sea creature at the water's edge. They described it as being black in colour and far bigger than a seal.

properties.name: Long Necked Creature
properties.description: A married couple in their yacht encountered a serpent with a neck as tall as their boat's mast. It moved towards them, but slowly sunk beneath the water before it came too close.
properties.location_name: Kyle of Lochalsh (Highland) - Water separating Skye to mainland

properties.name: Hairy Man
properties.description: Standing almost two and a half metres tall and covered in long brown hair, a family watched this creature from the roadside. A passing car scared the man-like figure off, and it vanished into nearby woodland. More recently, a group of friends reported a figure in the trees accompanied by small green lights.
properties.date: Family sighting in 1991, figure in the trees reported Early 2006

properties.name: Eel
properties.description: Little is known about the legend of the eel depicted in Kilmacreehy Church, other than it feasted on corpses.

properties.name: Pair of Dadharchu
properties.description: Translated their name means 'Hound of the Deep'; two Dadharchu lived in this lake until one of the beasts killed and ate a young bride. Her husband exacted revenge by shooting one of the creatures and taking a sword to the other. Regardless whether this is myth or legend, there have been reports in the twentieth century of four metre long creatures seen swimming in the lake.
properties.location_name: Lake Glenade (County Sligo) - Waters of the Lake

properties.name: Tizzie-Wizzie
properties.description: This creature, reportedly part hedgehog, part squirrel, and part bumblebee, has been reported on and off over the past one hundred years.

properties.name: Giant Eel
properties.description: In 2006 Steve Burnip and his wife observed an eel-like creature, estimated to be up to six metres long, in the waters of the lake. Steve said the creature had a little head and two humps which protruded from the water. In 2011 Tom Pickles photographed a multiple humped creature in the lake, although the image looks like waves caused by the wake of a boat.
properties.location_name: Lake Windermere (Cumbria) - Waters of the lake

properties.name: Thick Monster
properties.description: The crew of an American ship reported seeing a long snakelike creature with a thick body and fierce looking teeth in the water off the coast.

properties.name: Lion Cubs
properties.description: A pair of lion cubs were reportedly seen playing on the tip.

properties.name: Yellow-eyed Creature
properties.description: While driving through the village, a married couple spotted a large black animal in the middle of the road. They were forced to stop the car, as the creature did not move. It was about two metres high and just under a metre tall, covered in black bristly hair and yellow coloured eyes. One of the witnesses reported that the beast resembled a large cat but was not feline nor dog. The creature eventually moved slowly from the road and stood on the grass verge, enabling the witnesses to drive off.

properties.name: Shaggy Figure
properties.description: A driver began to feel overwhelmingly terrified while passing through a valley along this road. To quote, 'stood at the side of the road and looking directly at me, was a very tall, shaggy, brown or black, fur covered shape. At my estimation, it must have stood over two metres tall, around 1.2 metres wide, with no visible limbs or eyes, but I sensed that the eyes were there somewhere, as I could feel them boring into the car'. The witness accelerated away, but the feeling of dread lasted until morning, even after arriving home. Earlier the same year a large black cat was observed crossing the same road.
properties.location_name: Leeds (West Yorkshire) - Outer ring road, between Horsforth and Rodley

properties.name: Lynx?
properties.description: Appearing only at night, a lynx or similar cat is reported to traverse this area.
properties.location_name: Leigh-on-Sea (Essex) - Area around the railway station

properties.name: Red Adder
properties.description: The snake known as the Red Adder was said to be the colour of burning coals and anyone bitten would die.
properties.location_name: Leith Hill (Surrey) - Chalk hills in the area

properties.name: Wolverine
properties.description: A farmer in this area twice reported seeing a wolverine on his land, blaming the creature for killing two lambs.

properties.name: Water Kelpie
properties.description: This thirteen metre long creature was described as eel-like, with huge fins that broke the water as it periodically emerged. Witnesses watched the beast eat a blanket that had accidentally been left on the beach.

properties.name: Buckshaw Beast
properties.description: Locals reported an animal prowling the area, which may have been responsible for killing several deer. A creature photographed in the area on a camera phone was too poor to be properly identified, although a boar, hyena, wolf and big cat have all been named as the 'beast'.

properties.name: Emu
properties.description: A female jockey reported seeing a large bird in the woods near Lifton, which tried to attack her and her horse.

properties.name: Humped Creature
properties.description: While docked at Limerick, Captain Hugh Shaw and several of his crew reportedly spotted a creature with a three to four metre long neck and single hump moving up the Shannon.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: An inland mermaid was once said to live in this pit. Another story says the pit would be used to lay troublesome spirits.
properties.location_name: Little Wenlock (Shropshire) - Dary Pit (pool at the foot of the Ercall), likely now known as Dairy Pit

properties.name: Green Eyed Monster
properties.description: Six girls stood around 180 meters from a three meter long creature which crossed the beach and entered the sea. Dusk at the time, it is difficult to see how they could see it clearly enough to describe the creature's eye colour as green.

properties.name: Fad Felen
properties.description: Maelgwn Gwynedd was said to have died after looking through the keyhole of the church door and spotting the beast known as Fad Felen. The monster was said to have emerged from the saltwater marsh and have gold-like eyes, teeth and hair.

properties.name: Long Sea Monster
properties.description: From the safety of the town pier, a small group of people watched a sixty meter long sea monster travelling at 50 kph as it moved towards the Great Orme.

properties.name: Koala
properties.description: Reports of koala bears came in during the 1990s, seen on the roads in this area. No physical evidence has been found.

properties.name: Flying Pig
properties.description: Witnesses reported a large flying creature over the town; it resembled a pig of tremendous size, with stumpy webbed feet.

properties.name: Healing Eel
properties.description: The sick would dangle their feet into the waters of the well, hoping that the touch of the eel would increase their chances of recovery.
properties.location_name: Llangybi, near Pwllheli (Gwynedd) - St Cybi's Well

properties.name: Beithir
properties.description: This large water snake was said to be around three metres in length.
properties.date: Unknown, but active during the summer months
properties.location_name: Loch a' Mhuillidh (Highland) - Area around the loch

properties.name: Gabriel
properties.description: This water beastie, nicknamed Gabriel, is reputed to be reptilian and some fifteen metres in length.
properties.location_name: Loch Achanalt (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Man Shaped
properties.description: Strange sounds heard coming from the loch were blamed on the water horse. A shepherd once encountered a stranger by the loch who had water weed on his head. The stranger walked into the loch, resulting in people believing the water horse had taken human form.
properties.location_name: Loch Anlaimh, Coll (Argyll and Bute) - General area

properties.name: Basking Beast
properties.description: This creature, with a head like that of a horse and a single hump, rested ten metres from the shoreline, before slowing sinking beneath the water. A legend says that the Jacobites once hid a collection of treasure in the waters of the loch, where it remains.
properties.location_name: Loch Arkaig (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Horse Headed Beast
properties.description: Seen twice during the 1850s by the same witness, the man claimed the creature had a horse's head.
properties.location_name: Loch Assynt (Highland) - Waters of the Loch

properties.name: Huge Monster
properties.description: The loch is another large body of water in Scotland that is said to contain a large beastie, this time reputed to resemble a twelve legged eel. The creature is said to come ashore during winter. Another version of the story says the creature resembles a large bull.
properties.location_name: Loch Awe (Argyll and Bute) - Waters of the Loch

properties.name: Drowned Rider
properties.description: Upon discovering a horse on the moors, a man mounted the creature only for it to bolt and run at breakneck speed around the area. Both man and beast eventually plunged into the loch. The rider did not survive.
properties.location_name: Loch Bhasapol(?), Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Loch and nearby moorland

properties.name: Fantastic Horse
properties.description: This loch monster will disguise itself as a radiant white horse, enabling it to walk up to victims before pulling them into the loch's murky waters.
properties.location_name: Loch Borralan (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Water Kelpie
properties.description: When two fishermen vanished along the banks of the loch, the only items a search party could find were a brace of fish, two fishing rods, and large hoof prints in the sand which came from and disappeared back into the loch.
properties.location_name: Loch Borrolan (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Monster
properties.description: The loch is said to be home of an unidentified water beast.
properties.location_name: Loch Canisp (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Horse Man
properties.description: A water horse that lived in the loch would often be seen floating around on its side. On one occasion the creature took the form of a man and tried to enter a former soldier's home. Bullets did not harm the water horse, but a silver coin fired from the gun drove the creature away.
properties.location_name: Loch Chuaich (aka Loch Quoich) (Highland) - General area

properties.name: Kelpie
properties.description: This lake monster would take the form of a beautiful horse which would try to drown anyone who tried to mount it.
properties.location_name: Loch Coruisk (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Screaming Monster
properties.description: While out looking for a horse that escaped his farm in the mist, a farmer saw what he thought was his missing beast in the loch. As he approached, he realised he was looking at a water monster; the creature gave an unearthly yell, sending the farmer running home and disturbing all the wildlife around the water.
properties.location_name: Loch Duvat, Eriskay (Outer Hebrides) - North side of the loch

properties.name: Each Uisge
properties.description: This water horse would entice people to sit upon its back before drowning the unsuspecting victim. One story says that the creature lived in Loch Tay but was driven out so moved here.
properties.location_name: Loch Earn (Perth and Kinross) - Waters of the Loch

properties.name: Long Necked Monster
properties.description: A creature with a neck between four and five feet high popped up next to a man rowing his boat across this loch.
properties.location_name: Loch Feith an Leothaid (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Tall Serpent
properties.description: This creature was reported to be the size of a ship's mast when it surfaced from the water. At a much later date, a carcass of an unidentified creature was found on the banks of the loch.
properties.location_name: Loch Fyne (Argyll and Bute) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: Another body of water thought to be home to one of these formally unidentified creatures, this loch monster was described as a cross between a horse and a black bull which possessed incredible strength.
properties.location_name: Loch Garten (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Water Horse House
properties.description: This freshwater monster had a house built for him and his mortal bride - the general area of the property is known as the water there never freezes because of the home's open fire!

properties.name: Black Creature
properties.description: Four witnesses observed a ninety-six foot (twenty-nine metre) long black beast swimming in the loch, its body forming humps as it moved.
properties.location_name: Loch Hourn (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Water Beast
properties.description: Seen by staff and residents of the hotel, this three-humped creature swam on the surface of the loch. Reports of a monster here date back to 1930 and before.
properties.location_name: Loch Lochy (Highland) - Corriegour Lodge Hotel, Fort William

properties.name: Hipotam
properties.description: An early description of the loch stated it had 'waves without wind, fish without fins and a floating island' - this is thought to be a reference to the water horses, or hipotam, that swam in the waters, although crannogs, small artificial islands, would be constructed in lochs by wealthy families. A Mr and Mrs Haggerty spotted a humped back crossing the loch on 22 September 1964, while in 1980 another couple watched a head and neck emerge from the water before submerging thirty seconds later.
properties.location_name: Loch Lomond (Argyll and Bute) - Waters of the loch, especially where it is joined with the River Enrick

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: This loch is another traditional haunt of the mythical water horse.
properties.location_name: Loch Lurgainn (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Upside Down Boat
properties.description: This creature possessed a hump very similar to that of a rowing boat turned upside down. A local man gained some notoriety when he tried (unsuccessfully) to drain the loch while searching for the beast. Until around the 1750s, bulls were sacrificed on 25 August to appease the water monster. The water of the loch was said to cure 'lunacy', but nothing could ever be taken from the area in case the illness returned.
properties.location_name: Loch Maree (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: This loch has been named as another traditional haunt of water horses.
properties.location_name: Loch Meiklie (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Morag
properties.description: Home of yet another Scotland lake monster, a close eyewitness described the creature to be 10 meters in length, with brown skin. Three humps broke the water, as did a snake-like head. Two fishermen reportedly had a fight with it in 1969 - one hit it with an oar, while the other let off a couple of rounds with a rifle.

properties.name: Monster Killer
properties.area:normalized: Loch Na Mna, Isle Of Raasay
properties.description: A local man lost his daughter to the water horse that resided in this lake. In revenge, he roasted a pig by the loch, waited for the beast to take the bait, and killed it.
properties.location_name: Loch na Mna, Isle of Raasay (Highland) - Shore of the loch

properties.name: A Vain Search
properties.area:normalized: Loch Nan Dubhrachan, Isle Of Skye
properties.description: Laird MacDonald of Sleat is said to have dragged the loch in 1870, failing to locate the kelpie which he believed lived here.
properties.location_name: Loch nan Dubhrachan, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: World Famous Monster
properties.description: A creature that needs no introduction, Nessie varies in size and shape, and with countless books and websites dedicated to proving and disproving its existence, I can offer little more information on the (possible) creature. One nineteenth century account says that the loch was the home of a demonic horse. The saddled creature would wait by the roadside, and if mounted by an unsuspecting passerby, would drag the rider into the water and drown them. According to an 1898 feature in the Llangollen Advertiser and North Wales Journal, a slightly less fantastic creature, the roe deer, would occasionally take to the waters of the Loch to escape man. While it cannot explain all Nessie sightings, perhaps a bobbing deer isolated in the dark waters could freak out the occasional tourist to the area.
properties.location_name: Loch Ness (Highland) - Water and surrounding banks

properties.name: Dark Water Horse
properties.description: This creature would wait until a bather submerged their head, before moving in to drown them. It was particularly fond of children, and in one attack managed to drown nine simultaneously.
properties.location_name: Loch Pityoulish (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: This saltwater beastie is said to swim these waters.
properties.location_name: Loch Poit na h-I (aka Loch Pottie), Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - General area

properties.name: Water Beast
properties.description: Seen as it moved towards a small boat, the crew reported that the tail of this creature thrashed about above the water, while the rest of the body remained submerged.
properties.location_name: Loch Scavaig, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Three Humped Monster
properties.description: With a sail running along its back, this serpentine 'Seileag' has even once been seen on land. A sailor, while transporting two old ladies across the water on an old boat, paused to take a pot-shot at it with a rifle he had at hand. In 1926 the creature was seen emerging from the water at Sandy Point.
properties.location_name: Loch Shiel (Highland) - Waters and land around the loch

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: This creature was friendlier than many of the other Scottish water horses and assisted in the construction of a local church. It may have been yellow in colour.
properties.location_name: Loch Shin (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Lamb Eater
properties.description: It has been reported that lambs were thrown into this loch to feed the creature which lived within.
properties.location_name: Loch Suainaval, Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Kelpies
properties.description: During the construction of the Laggan dam, some divers requested transfers after encountering something under the water. One legend says that the water horses at Treig were the fiercest in the world and would tear anyone who strayed too close into a thousand pieces.
properties.location_name: Loch Treig (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Buarach-Bhaoi
properties.description: This eel-like creature would wait for horses to trot through the water before looping itself around their legs, drowning the horse before drinking its blood.
properties.location_name: Loch Tummel (Perth and Kinross) - General area

properties.name: Wattie
properties.description: Peter Haining wrote that Colonel Arthur Trimble had spotted a creature with a long neck and large head was seen in this loch. Karl Shuker has written a rather detailed article on his website which dismisses the encounter as highly likely to be fiction.

properties.name: Stretching Horse
properties.description: Ten children caught a horse and, one by one, mounted the creature. The horse's back stretched to accommodate the children. The last child to try to climb on the horse had a bible in his pocket; the horse bolted as the child touched it, running into the loch. The nine children were never seen again.
properties.location_name: Lochaber (Argyll and Bute) - Lochan na Dunaich

properties.name: Big Slug
properties.description: Mr John Davies reported having to stop his motorbike to let a giant slug like creature cross the road - he compared the monster to a whale.

properties.name: Two Saurians
properties.description: A pair of green sea monsters were observed chasing fish off the coast. They were said to resemble Chinese dragons.

properties.name: Monster
properties.description: Three witnesses observed the giant black snake as it crossed the lough. The creature was estimated to be some ten and a half metres in length with its head held clear of the water. Its body formed two large loops breaking the surface of the lake as it moved. The creature was said to have developed a taste for the local sheep.

properties.name: Peist
properties.description: The lough is said to be home to a water monster, although no further information is available. One local folktale says the lough was created when a mortal left the cover off a fairy well, submerging a village.
properties.location_name: Lough Allen (Aillionn) (County Leitrim) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Large Swimming Creature
properties.description: A local farmer spotted a grey creature in the lough during the sixties, while in 1969 Royal Netherlands Air Force Commodore Kort claimed to have witnessed a life form with a pointed back swimming through the water.
properties.location_name: Lough Auna (County Galway) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Long Creature
properties.description: Stories of this creature date back many years, thought the description varies considerably. The more recent sightings imply the monster to be a giant eel, greater than four metres in length.
properties.location_name: Lough Bran (or Brin) (County Leitrim) - Waters of the Lough

properties.name: Pig-like Creature
properties.description: This area was visited in 2004 by a Japanese TV crew looking for the monster said to live here. The entity has a similar appearance to a black pig but blowed water from its back like a whale. It was spotted in 1954 by a farmer taking his cattle for milking, while in 1979 two more farmers spotted a black three metre long creature swimming in the waters.
properties.location_name: Lough Brin (County Kerry) - Water near Moll's Gap

properties.name: Hump
properties.description: Tony 'Doc' Shiels watched a hump glide through the water, the sighting lasting several seconds.
properties.location_name: Lough Cullaun (County Clare) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Brief Visitor
properties.description: Said to resemble a giant eel, this monstrous creature appears only once every seven years.
properties.location_name: Lough Graney (County Clare) - Waters of the Lake

properties.name: Peist
properties.description: This lough is said to be home to a lake monster.
properties.location_name: Lough Leane (County Kerry) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Dobhar Chu
properties.description: A man walking along the bank of Lough Mask had a narrow escape when a water beast jumped from the water and tried to drag him into the water. The man managed to escape and ran for his life. Another version of the story says the beast killed him. A creature seen in the lough during the 1960s was described as eel-like with two humps. Folklore claims the lake to have been created by either fairies or a giant.
properties.location_name: Lough Mask (County Mayo) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Peist
properties.description: This Site of Special Scientific Interest is home to another local peist.
properties.location_name: Lough Melvin (County Fermanagh) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Frightful Beast
properties.description: One very old story says that a swimmer was torn to pieces by a monster after trying to leave a small island in this body of water. A creature was seen in 1954 by several people while out fishing. One of the witnesses, Georgina Carberry, suffered nightmares weeks after the event. Possibly because of this sighting, Captain Lionel Leslie set out to capture the beast using explosives in the first instance and a long net in the second, but with no success. In 1959, two English fishermen claimed their boat had been pulled around the lough by a creature (they escaped by cutting their fishing line) and in 1960 three clergymen observed a serpent-like beast. More recently, it has been speculated that the lake is home to a giant eel.
properties.location_name: Lough Ree (County Westmeath) - Waters of the lake

properties.name: Abducted Woman
properties.description: A farmer spotted a beautiful woman leave the lough and forced her to return to his farmhouse. Eventually she agreed to stay with him, on the condition that she never glazed upon the lake again. Twenty years passed and the farmer and the mermaid had several children. While waiting for the farmer to return from the fields on a clear day, the mermaid spotted the lough in the distance and fled towards it, never to be seen again.
properties.location_name: Lough Sheelin (County Meath) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Suileach
properties.description: Suileach was a huge creature with many eyes and spread terror throughout the county. The creature is thought to have been killed by Saint Colum Cuille in the sixth century.
properties.location_name: Lough Swilly (County Donegal) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Unknown Creature
properties.description: A fisherman is said to have caught a strange brown creature covered in creamy blotches here.
properties.location_name: Lough Waskel (County Donegal) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Lough Ness Monster
properties.description: A duck was seen being pulled underwater by an unidentified creature. The attacker was christened the 'Lough Ness Monster', though is most likely a pike.

properties.name: Circular Creature
properties.description: Two friends watching the Lough spotted a large, dark grey circular body rise from the lake, almost four metres in circumference. The head, positioned slightly away from the body, stood ninety centimetres out of the water.
properties.location_name: Lower Lough Bray (County Wicklow) - Waters of the lake

properties.name: Peist
properties.description: A type of monster which could only be slain by a hero or saint, little more is known about the peist said to lurk in the area.
properties.location_name: Lower Lough Erne (County Fermanagh) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Cro Sith
properties.description: The Cro sith were fairy cattle that lived under the sea, occasionally coming ashore. When they did reach land, local farmers would try and catch them to add them to their own herds.

properties.name: Blind Creature
properties.description: Maurice Knowles, a farmer, reported encountering a strange dog-like creature on his land. It resembled a Great Dane, though it had a snout like a pig's, and only slits where eyes should have been. The creature ran away only when Maurice shouted at it.

properties.name: Humped Creature
properties.description: The crews of two fishing boats were reported to have encountered a humped creature several metres in length moving through the water. A column of spines ran down the monster's back and it possessed a head like a tortoise with a large mouth.

properties.name: Eagle Man
properties.description: Two women and their partners out hillwalking were hounded by a large eagle-like figure. All four people were left terrified by their encounter, and one of the women collapsed with shock. One man with whom they discussed their encounter with suggested it was a 'gatekeeper entity'.
properties.location_name: Malvern Hills (Hereford & Worcester) - Exact area unknown

properties.name: Sea Horse
properties.description: A pair of swimmers watched as a 60cm neck rose from the water close to their location - they reported the serpent had horse like ears.
properties.location_name: Margate (Kent) - Sea near the Lido Baths

properties.name: Cornish Owlman
properties.description: This man-sized creature was seen several times over a period of years - a giant owl with glowing red eyes and pincers for feet. All the witnesses would appear to have been teenage or younger girls.

properties.name: Strange Carving
properties.description: A carving in the church shows a strange creature entangled in a thicket.
properties.location_name: Melbury Osmund (Dorset) - Church of St Osmund

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: A sailor once fell in love with a mermaid here. The woman gave him a golden ring, informing the sailor that she would be back in five days to meet him. Five days later, the man died.

properties.name: Kelpie
properties.description: After the sun goes down, a water beast was said to emerge from the river and start hunting. It was said to take at least one human life per year.

properties.name: Long Snake
properties.description: A small group at the pub watched a snake-like creature, between 10 - 15 metres in length, a short distance off the shoreline.
properties.location_name: Milford Haven (Dyfed) - Off coast, viable from the Shipwright public house

properties.name: Battling Bull
properties.description: A small village close to Loch Nan Buadh was terrified by an Each-uisge that lived there. Eventually the villagers unleashed a specially bred bull upon the Each-uisge - after an epic struggle, both animals disappeared below the waters.
properties.location_name: Monach Isles (Outer Hebrides) - Loch Nan Buadh

properties.name: Two Metre Tall Neck
properties.description: The crew of the Rosa reported this monster's appendage which rose from the sea by their boat. It looked around before diving again.
properties.location_name: Montrose (Angus) - Just over a kilometre offshore, 16 kilometres southeast of town

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: This fishy female comes to the surface of the pool at night, ready to pull any witnesses down to the dark waters where they remain forever.
properties.location_name: Morridge Moor (Staffordshire) - The Black Mere, also known as the Blake Mere

properties.name: U Boat Sighting
properties.description: The German submarine U108 encountered a giant marine saurian, estimated to be 18 to 24 metres in length.

properties.name: Shaggy Dog
properties.description: An extremely large shaggy grey coloured dog darted out ahead of this witness travelling to work on a clear morning. The creature, around 1.8 metres in length, had short, pointed ears, and oversized snout and feet.

properties.name: Large Creature with Five Humps
properties.description: While walking along the beach, a couple watched the creature move at fantastic speed through the water.

properties.name: Chicken Eater
properties.description: After the local population of chickens took a dive, the problem was blamed on an unpleasant creature which one local man spotted in the nearby woods. The witness said he passed the monster as it sat on a rock - the size of a dog, the creature resembled a cat, though it had a tapering, bald tail.
properties.location_name: Myross (County Cork) - Forests in the area

properties.name: Forty Foot Creature
properties.description: This sea serpent reportedly took a liking to the local seal population, though the pinniped's objected to the new arrival and their fleeing was an indication the creature was near.

properties.name: Sea Cows
properties.description: A herd of sea cows that immerged from the sea were prevented from returning by locals, who moved to the shoreline with weapons and stood between the animals and their home. The sea cows, which looked identical to regular cattle, spent the remainder of their days on a nearby farm.
properties.location_name: Nisabost Beach, Harris (Outer Hebrides) - General area

properties.name: Man Monkey
properties.description: A man driving a horse and cart crossed this bridge in the evening. A black creature with white eyes leapt from a bush upon the horse, causing the horse to panic and bolt. The rider used his whip to lash out at the creature, only for the whip to pass through the entity. The man eventually arrived home, with little memory of how he managed to shake off the attacker. Sharing the experience with another local man, the witness discovered that the 'creature', known locally as a man-monkey, had come into being after someone drowned in the river.

properties.name: Sea Monster
properties.description: This saltwater creature was observed by the steward on a small boat.

properties.name: Whale like Creature
properties.description: Fishermen reported a whale with characteristics of a boar in the sea in this area.

properties.name: Therianthropy
properties.description: The Goodman of Wastness, a farmer by trade, stole a selkie's seal skin and hid it, forcing the woman to become his wife. After many years of marriage and a house full of children the selkie found her seal skin and ran off back to the sea, never to be seen again.
properties.location_name: North Ronaldsay (Orkney) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Long Black Creature
properties.description: After setting off from North Shields, the crew of a boat spotted what they believed to be a piece of wreckage floating in the sea. The men investigated and realised the object was alive and actually the head and neck of a black creature. The creature swam away and dived, revealing a nine-foot (2.74 metre) wide body.
properties.location_name: North Shields (Northumberland) - North Sea (exact area not known)

properties.name: Giant Spider
properties.description: A local librarian driving along Stonebridge Road at night caught sight of a giant spider. The creature was over sixty centimetres at the legs with a body the size of a dinner plate. The spider crawled across the road and disappeared into a disused factory, leaving the witness in shock.
properties.location_name: Nottingham (Nottinghamshire) - Stonebridge Road, close to site of Wrights & Dobson factory (no longer standing)

properties.name: Salamander-Que
properties.description: Cryptozoologist Adam Bird reported fishermen believed a large creature to reside in the lake, around fifteen feet (4.5 metres) in length and resembling a salamander.
properties.location_name: Nottingham (Nottinghamshire) - West Lake, Colwick Country Park

properties.name: Mermaid's Grave
properties.description: The area reportedly contains the grave of a mermaid killed in the early nineteenth century. The mer-creature had been seen for a couple of days, before a teenage boy threw a rock at it, killing the entity. Accounts stated that the upper part of the creature was the size of an infant, while the bottom resembled a salmon.
properties.location_name: Nunton, Benbecula (Outer Hebrides) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Knight or Bear?
properties.description: A strange entity was rumoured to frequent NW8 and the surrounding areas during the 1830s. Some reports said it wore chainmail, others saying it was more bear-like.
properties.location_name: NW8 (Greater London) - St John's Wood - general area

properties.name: Road Crossing Serpent
properties.description: Two independent motorists reported a six metre long legless creature alongside the road, grey in colour. One witness said it propelled itself along like a giant caterpillar.
properties.location_name: Oban (Argyll and Bute) - A85 between Oban and Perth

properties.name: Coiled Serpent
properties.description: A one hundred foot (thirty metre) long black creature was observed swimming in the bay for around fifteen minutes. A story which may be a hoax states that the same or similar creature was killed in 1877, and the locals dragged the carcass above the high water mark to use as fish bait.

properties.name: Boar
properties.description: Odiham and the surrounding area appeared to be home to one or more wild boars during the autumn of this year.

properties.name: U-28 Monster
properties.description: U-boat commander Freiherr George G von Forstner claimed to have encountered a giant 'crocodile-like' creature as the U-28 torpedoed the British steamer Iberian. As the Iberian sank, a huge explosion occurred from under the sea. Moments later, the monster appeared, blown out of the water before travelling back down and sinking beneath the sea.

properties.name: Rostron's Monster
properties.description: Sir Arthur Rostron, while the Chief Officer of the Campania, sketched a long necked sea monster off the coast of Cork. Rostron later became the Captain of the Carpathia, involved in rescuing Titanic survivors.
properties.location_name: Off coast of Cork (County Cork) - Sea

properties.name: Wolves
properties.description: A local has claimed that several people in the area have spotted wolves stalking the forest. There is an unsubstantiated report that a boy disappeared in the woodland and was eventually found torn apart.
properties.location_name: Ogmore Vale (Mid Glamorgan) - Forest near the former Abba school

properties.name: Mermaid's Stone
properties.description: The concave nature of a stone on the road leading to the church was attributed to the foot of a mermaid (an untraditional untailed one) escaping from her jailor.
properties.location_name: Old Cambus (Borders) - Road leading to Church of St Helen

properties.name: Albino Wallaby
properties.description: After spotting a white creature with red eyes, IT trainer Stacey Purdy pulled out her phone and used it to photograph and video an albino wallaby. Even though wallabies live in several places over the UK, it is estimated by experts that the chances of a white one being born is 100,00-1.

properties.name: Tarroo Ushtey
properties.description: The entity known as Tarroo Ushtey, or water bull, was a bovine version of the water horse - the creature looked like a normal bull, although had fairy traits and would live under the water. In Onchan, one was seen crossing the road, and was recognised as a Tarroo Ushtey due to the fire burning in its eyes.
properties.location_name: Onchan (Isle of Man) - Exact Location not known

properties.name: Capybara
properties.description: Normally found in South America, a capybara was spotted by actor David Dayan Fisher and his nephew and niece while walking through this area.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Barnet (Greater London) - Darlands Lake

properties.name: Shark Attack
properties.description: A woman swimming in this reservoir was attacked by a creature under the water that grabbed and held her foot with its mouth. She managed to kick the creature off, and when the bite marks were examined by marine biologist, she was told the creature must have been a shark.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Heathrow Airport (Greater London) - Unnamed reservoir south-east of Heathrow Airport

properties.name: Selkie Prince
properties.description: A group of hunters who arrived at the island killed and skinned many seals before a violent storm began to batter the island - unbeknown to the hunters, a selkie prince was amongst the seals. The hunters finally returned the skins to the remaining seals, after which the storm ceased.
properties.location_name: Papa Stour (Shetland) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Great Auk
properties.description: Reports of an extinct Great Auk being spotted alive on a beach 142 years after the last confirmed sighting turned out to be nothing more than a hoax.

properties.name: Wallabies
properties.description: A small colony of Red Neck wallabies exists in the Peak District, after escaping their private zoo after the Second World War.

properties.name: No Mermaid Here
properties.description: A Canadian newspaper reported that the Mayor of Peel and others had seen a mermaid off the coast and were offering a reward for the creature's capture. The story turned out to be a fake.
properties.location_name: Peel (Isle of Man) - Sea off Peel

properties.name: White Animal
properties.description: While travelling home to Cilgerran, this witness spotted a large white animal behind a fence which was described as a cross between 'a deer and a big cat'.

properties.name: Dog Faced Beast
properties.description: The body of water was home to a sea creature with a long thick neck and a small head resembling that of a dog. It swam under one witness's boat.

properties.name: Tall Creature
properties.description: Observed by a lone lighthouse keeper, this sea monster was seen to rise almost ten metres clear of the waves. A smaller creature had been seen fifteen years earlier by a fishing boat crew and a holiday maker.

properties.name: Mermaids
properties.description: This area is another haunt for the Cornish merfolk.
properties.location_name: Perranuthnoe (Cornwall) - Cudden Point, off the coast

properties.name: Big Black Horse
properties.description: Having seen a big black horse grazing on the banks of the Ugie, a local man was warned that it was a kelpie. He found a 'waith-horse' bridle and managed to control the creature, setting it free once it had carried enough stones to make a bridge over the river.
properties.location_name: Peterhead (Aberdeenshire) - River Ugie by Inverugie Castle

properties.name: Serpent
properties.description: While likely fictitious, one story written by Roger W Travers and published in the Cork Constitution claimed that he and four others had encountered a large serpent with no visible appendages. The creature's eyes were about nine inches (23 centimetres) across, while a grooved shell-like substance covered the upper part of its back. The men shot at the creature, which promptly swam off.

properties.name: Tall Creature
properties.description: Standing on the shore, a witness spotted a seven foot (2.1 metre) tall, round, object moving down the channel. The shape disappeared and reappeared several times, slowing moving away from the witness.
properties.location_name: Port Eynon (aka Port Einon) (West Glamorgan) - Around one mile west of Eynon Point

properties.name: Protected Boats
properties.description: Local boat builder Roderick Mackenzie managed to sneak up on a sleeping mermaid and grabbed her by the hair. She requested that he let her go and in return would grant him a wish. Mackenzie wished that any boat he made would never sink, a tradition that was said to hold true throughout his career.
properties.location_name: Port Henderson (Highland) - Rocks along the coast

properties.name: Merman
properties.description: This figure normally warned of ill fortune at sea - if the crew of a fishing vessel spotted him, they would return to port for the day. Two fishermen spotted two of the creatures in 1814 - one they described as black in colour, with curly hair and long arms, while the other was female with long hair.

properties.name: Mermaid or Whale
properties.description: Following a storm, a large creature was discovered on the beach. It was alive, until concerned locals beat and stabbed the creature to death. The creature measured just under 49 feet (15 metres) from head to tail, had a back tougher than a turtle's shell, and two stubby arms. While some of the creature's killers named it as a whale, others were not so sure and declared it to have been a mermaid.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: One story says that a mermaid came ashore here but died soon after being discovered by local people heading to church.

properties.name: Veasta
properties.description: This sea monster is reported to have the head of a seahorse but the tail of a fish. Robert Westwood, in his 'Mysterious Places of Dorset', points out that the earlier sighting in 1457 was said to be a giant cockerel which emerged from the sea and crowed three times before submerging.

properties.name: Creature
properties.description: A water monster has been reported inhabiting the loch.
properties.location_name: Portree, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Woman's Loch, by Sithean Beinne Bho'idhich

properties.name: Battered Serpent
properties.description: Caught by two fishermen who found the beast wrapped around their offshore crabbing equipment, they proceeded to hit it with an oar before finally killing the creature on nearby rocks.

properties.name: Pooka
properties.description: This valley may have been home to a pooka before being flooded to create the reservoir.
properties.location_name: Poulaphouca Reservoir (County Wicklow) - Beneath the waters

properties.name: Rotting Sea Serpent
properties.description: The body of what was thought to be a sea monster was later identified as a rotting basking shark.

properties.name: Strange Dog
properties.description: Following the death of sheep in the area, a large number of people hunted and killed a strange dog of 'unusual size and very strongly made'. The creature also had forelegs as thick as a man's wrist.

properties.name: Serpent?
properties.description: Sheila Smith reported seeing a serpent in the waters of the river. It was thought that the creature was likely to have been an eel.

properties.name: Black Shape
properties.description: Staff at the Minydon hotel watched a ten meter long black shape swim by, both the front part of the creature and the end of the tail protruding from the water. Several other sightings of strange sea creatures were reported in the area throughout the year.

properties.name: Fake Bear
properties.description: In what was later to be revealed to be a hoax, local press carried a story that a family spotted what they first thought was a large dog moving through the trees. As they watched it, they realised that the creature was larger than any dog. Experts talking to the local press said it was unlikely that a bear could survive in the forest.

properties.name: Rake
properties.description: This freshwater mermaid was reputed to be armed with a rake, ready to drag in any children who strayed too close.

properties.name: Cockatrice
properties.description: This mythical creature, made up of pieces of dragon, serpent, and cockerel, took up residence in the church when it fell into ruin. A local man named John Tallantire (or Tallantine) killed the creature using a branch from a rowan tree (or hawthorn), though some locals say it is still seen flying about at dusk.

properties.name: Beast of Bodalog
properties.description: Thirty five sheep were killed over several months by an unknown creature which dispatched livestock with a bite just below the neck. It was speculated that the creature came from the nearby river at night. Due to flattened grass around the kills, some speculated it was the work of a large snake.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: There are several entities said to haunt this small island, including a mermaid, the sound of rattling chains, and water horses. A woman supervising cattle on the island was attacked by something tall and hairy, with no skin upon its face. She managed to survive by throwing boiling water over the entity and creating a magic circle, remaining inside it.
properties.location_name: Rinns of Islay (Argyll and Bute) - Small island off Rinns of Islay

properties.name: Afanc
properties.description: Afanc was a monster, thought to resemble a beaver, which attacked locals and dragged them to the bottom of the river. The beast was defeated after being tricked into sleeping by a fair maiden.
properties.location_name: River Conwy (Clwyd) - Llyn yr Afranc (aka Beaver Pool)

properties.name: Gipping Girl
properties.description: The mermaid that frequented the Gipping would prey on children who played near the deeper waters of the river.

properties.name: Strange Animals
properties.description: An ostrich was seen by a driver during the 1990s near the haunted hill, but when he stopped to investigate, the creature had changed into a large black cat which ran away. The cat (or a similar one) had been previously seen in the area, as has a large hairy beast, thought by the witness to be a gorilla.
properties.location_name: Rochester (Kent) - Blue Bell Hill, near Rochester

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: Her reasons unknown, this mermaid returns once a year to ring a bell hidden deep underwater and sing.

properties.name: Rothesay
properties.description: Three men spotted a mermaid carefully combing her long black hair along the shoreline. One of the witnesses also reported a large sea monster nearby, which had the body of a man 'but with the head of a brute'.
properties.location_name: Rothesay (Argyll and Bute) - Shoreline within a mile of town

properties.name: Horse o' Hillaroo
properties.description: A winged horse was said to be found atop of the mound of Hillaroo. The horse would swoop down on unlucky travellers, favouring children, and carry them off to an unknown fate.

properties.name: Nuggle
properties.description: Appearing as a beautiful black horse, this creature would hide amongst 'real' horses near the water's edge. True to water horse form, if anyone mounted the beast it would head straight for the water to drown the unsuspecting rider.

properties.name: Kentish Apeman
properties.description: A two and a half metre tall hairy hominid with glowing red eyes has periodically been reported in this area since the Second World War.
properties.location_name: Royal Tunbridge Wells (Kent) - Tunbridge Wells Common

properties.name: Lizard
properties.description: It has been reported that woodland around this area is home of a green and red lizard the size of a large dog. The creature is also said to have been seen digging in the parish church...

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: Legend says this island was home to a water horse, although nothing more is known.

properties.name: Cockatrice
properties.description: This medieval creature which killed people with its eyes took up residence near the town, only to be defeated by a man wearing mirrored armour.

properties.name: Barking Beast
properties.description: A serpent like creature which was reported in the area was claimed to be a large conger eel, though this would not explain the strange barking sounds that were also accredited to the sea monster.

properties.name: Catgarookey
properties.description: This sixty centimetre tall creature, christened 'Catgarookey' because it looks like a cat-kangaroo-monkey hybrid, was spotted several times in the region. The fact that the animal possessed a black and yellow tail with a white tip suggests the 'beast' is little more than an escaped racoon family member.

properties.name: Wild Man of the Woods
properties.description: A wild man who lived in the woods was shot at when he attacked and tried to kidnap a local farmer's wife. The creature was never seen again.

properties.name: Naked Man
properties.description: A naked figure reputed to lurk within forested areas was said to be a 'wild man of the woods'.

properties.name: Green Fish
properties.description: The yacht Valetta reported encountering a long green fish. While the creature's full length is undocumented, the tail was said to be eighteen foot (5.5 metres) in length. Local fishermen blamed the creature for the destruction of their nets.
properties.location_name: Sannox, Isle of Arran (Ayrshire) - Sea around the area

properties.name: Emu
properties.description: A train driver reported spotting an emu crossing the Ipswich to Lowestoft track.

properties.name: Remains in the Bay
properties.description: The remains of a large mystery sea creature drifted into Scalloway Bay. Described as a looking like an upturned vessel, it remained in the bay for two weeks before disappearing - during this time, no one was brave enough to approach it for a closer look.

properties.name: Kelpies
properties.description: This area was thought to be home to humanoid sea creatures that drowned their victims before partaking of the deceased flesh.

properties.name: Humped Creature
properties.description: Four witnesses in a yacht reported seeing two humped creatures swimming off the coast of the island. One witness estimated the length of the serpents to be at least forty feet (twelve metres).
properties.location_name: Scalpay Island (Highland) - Waters off the island

properties.name: Kangaroo
properties.description: Footprints resembling those of a kangaroo were reported in the park, while another witness reported the creature had kicked her dog. No sightings of the roo have been reported since.
properties.location_name: SE13 (Greater London) - Beckenham Place Park, Lewisham

properties.name: Pterodactyl
properties.description: A Pterodactyl-shaped creature with a six metre wingspan flew around six metres over the top of houses. The creature moved at around 30mph and made absolutely no sound.
properties.location_name: Seaton Burn (Tyne and Wear) - Skies over the area

properties.name: The Deaf Adder
properties.description: This terminator-like serpent was said to be completely deaf, but if the snake spotted anyone, it would pursue its victim, never giving up the chase until it had bitten its prey. The bite was always fatal.

properties.name: Peist
properties.description: Saint Senan engaged a peist in this body of water. The creature, with fiery eyes and venomous mouth, fell after the saint made the sign of the cross, and was bound and thrown into Doo Lough (or Doolough).
properties.location_name: Shannon Estuary (County Clare) - Close to Scattery Island

properties.name: Scaly Horse
properties.description: A creature with the body of a horse was observed by several fishermen, who reported it had scales like a fish, and a spotted effect over its body.

properties.name: Each Uisge
properties.description: Two cousins, Dark Mary and Fair Mary, spent the night in a shieling in a valley near to Shawbost. One night an each-uisge (a water spirit) appeared in the guise of little old lady and tricked the cousins into letting her spend the night with them - Fair Mary was murdered by the each-uisge, and it escaped into the night.
properties.location_name: Shawbost, Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Valley in the area

properties.name: To Be Seen Alive
properties.description: This pub featured in local news as it offered a sea serpent 'to be seen alive'. The creature, pulled from the River Dun, weighed around twenty-two kilograms, a head resembling a mastiff, and fine mouse-like hair. Music would calm or anger the creature, depending on the number of instruments played.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Rawson's Arms, Tenter Street (no longer operating), and River Dun

properties.name: Flying Lizard
properties.description: It was said that there were three types of lizard in this area, the Running Asker, the Water Asker, and the Flying Asker, the latter being the smallest of the three.

properties.name: Bench End
properties.description: A carved wooden bench arm is in the shape of a mermaid. A notice in the church said that, in days of olde, a mermaid tried to enter but was turned away by a mortified crowd. She later snuck in undetected and remains to this day.

properties.name: Upright Beast
properties.description: A local teenage myth says that the forest is home to a tall hairy man, whose eyes glow red in the dark. There is a chance that this is a modern version of the tree spirits that were once said to live here.
properties.location_name: Sherwood Forest (Nottinghamshire) - Deep within the forest

properties.name: Shoepultie
properties.description: This creature was said to be like traditional water horses, other than it would use its tail like a propeller once in the water.

properties.name: Cursed
properties.description: A fisherman who caught a mermaid while out at sea killed the creature with a knife. It was said that from that moment, he was cursed.

properties.name: Noggle
properties.description: The Noggle was a type of water horse that could be found stopping water wheels. When a miller appeared to chase the beast off, he would often be tricked into mounting it - being taken to deep water and his doom.

properties.name: Upset Sea Creature
properties.description: The crew of the fishing vessel Bertie fired several shots at a sea monster they passed - this angered the beast, which created waves of such force that they washed over the boat, taking away fishing gear and other supplies. The creature continued to stalk the crew until night fell.
properties.location_name: Shetland Islands (Shetland) - Southeast of the island of Fetlar

properties.name: Killer Octopus
properties.description: An octopus was said to have killed several people along the coast here during a year in this decade, although the story is likely fake.

properties.name: Green Creature
properties.description: Joan Borgeest spotted a large green creature off the coast of Skeffling. She described the beast as having a flat head, long mouth and protruding eyes. It dived when she shouted for others to come and see it.
properties.location_name: Skeffling (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Off coast

properties.name: Serpentine Beast
properties.description: This creature, or possibly creatures, has been reported on and off for many years now - its humped form moving quickly and quietly through the water.
properties.location_name: Skegness (Lincolnshire) - Area of sea between town and Maplethorpe

properties.name: Camel Headed Creature
properties.description: The arrival of this creature was introduced by a school of whales leaping from the water and fleeing the area. The head and neck of the sea monster reached out of the water at the height of a small sailboat.
properties.location_name: Skerries of Work (Orkney) - Sea in the area

properties.name: A Sea Man
properties.description: A man was caught in the sea by fishermen and kept for many weeks in a cottage where he lived on a diet of fish. After a while, the 'sea man' returned to the sea, waving goodbye to people standing on the coast before diving down and never returning.

properties.name: Bear
properties.description: A man reported seeing a dark brown bear standing 150 centimetres high. No trace of the creature could be found later.

properties.name: Washed up
properties.description: Following a storm, a sea creature (curiously described as a sea orangutan) washed up on the beach. From head to toe, the creature measured 4.14 metres, and when stretched, the creature's arms spanned 3.4 metres. A newspaper said the creature to be 'a frightful looking devil'.

properties.name: Afanc
properties.description: Witness Oliver Vaughan spotted the pale head of strange animal in Llyn Glaslyn, which was thought to be an Afanc, a lake monster from Welsh mythology.

properties.name: Speared Serpent
properties.description: After finishing off the local fish population, a large serpent came ashore and started snacking on the local population. Survivors set a trap for the creature; a long line of sharpened wooden stakes were placed along the waterline, which the beast impaled itself upon.
properties.location_name: Solway Firth (Dumfries and Galloway) - General area

properties.name: Glassy Eyed Mermaid
properties.description: Becalmed in their skiff after setting out from the Sound of Mull, two men spotted a creature in the water which had the head, neck and upper torso of a woman, although the hair was coarser and the eyes glassy.
properties.location_name: Sound of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Sea

properties.name: Monkey
properties.description: On two separate occasions, two different police officers reported seeing a monkey along this road. Both described the creature as 'a similar size to a squirrel, dark brown in colour with a long, straight, upright tail'.

properties.name: Man-Like Monster
properties.description: A man crossing the bridge watched as a human-like creature rose from the ground and leapt over the bridge, vanishing into the mist. The witness fled.
properties.date: Circa late nineteenth / early twentieth century
properties.location_name: South Mimms (Hertfordshire) - Bridge along Wash Lane

properties.name: Long Grey Creature
properties.description: While casting their nets, two fishermen watched a long creature around thirty metres away. They stated that the beast was grey in colour, moved at great speed, and estimated the length to be around twenty metres. However, a story later appeared in the local press that the 'creature' was nothing more than several pieces of lumber bound together.

properties.name: Wild Man
properties.description: The wild man is thought to have been a hermit rather than a large hairy hominid. A local pub was named after the man.

properties.name: Pure White Monster
properties.description: Fishermen spotted a pure white creature swimming eastwards close to the castle. The creature was four foot (1.2 metres) wide with only twelve foot (3.7 metres) of its width visible at any one time. A flock of seagulls and two fishermen followed the creature, but it eventually escaped.
properties.location_name: St Andrews (Fife) - Off the coast close to the castle

properties.name: Crocodile
properties.description: Measuring around one hundred and forty centimetres in length, a crocodile was said to have been killed by a local man in this cave.

properties.name: Rabbit Hunting
properties.description: John Corbett reported seeing a wolverine hunting rabbits close to St Audries.
properties.location_name: St Audries (Somerset) - Unnamed area near the town

properties.name: Large Water Horse
properties.description: After being caught by a ploughman, the water horse allowed itself to be used as a plough horse on nearby fields for a few weeks. Once it grew tired of the work, the creature dragged both plough and man into the sea from which they never emerged.

properties.name: Tarbh-uisge
