properties.description: Raised in secret by a local lass after she found the creature on the roadside, this dragon grew up and began to eat the locals and their livestock; the beast never touched its surrogate mother. The creature was finally killed, though the stories differ to whom carried out the slaying.
properties.location_name: Mordiford (Hereford & Worcester) - Village and surrounding countryside

properties.name: Slain Dragon
properties.description: A local story tells of how Sir Thomas Venables shot and killed a dragon here by shooting an arrow into the eye, just as it picked up a small child to eat.

properties.name: Red Dragon
properties.description: It was at this site that Vortigern, King of the Britons, encountered a red dragon and a white dragon fighting in a deep underground. The white dragon fled, leaving the red dragon victorious - the king took the creature to be his emblem.
properties.location_name: Nant Gwynant (Gwynedd) - Lake hidden under hillside

properties.name: Impenetrable Dragon
properties.description: A dragon whose armoured scales rendered it virtually invincible was killed by a soldier who managed to shoot the creature in its one weak spot - up the bottom. When the dragon's body hit the nearby river venom gushed out, killing all fish.
properties.location_name: Newcastle Emlyn (Dyfed) - Ruined castle in the area (may no longer be standing)

properties.name: Fulk's Fire Breather
properties.description: Born from a pile of decaying bodies left in the hillfort, this creature was finally killed by the brave knight Fulk Fitzwartine.

properties.name: Self-Healing Dragon
properties.description: The dragon which set up home near here possessed the ability to heal itself and spit poison. It was finally defeated by Sir Peter Loschy and his dog; as Sir Peter cut pieces of the monster off, the dog picked the segments up and ran away with them. Alas, the knight also died when his dog jumped up and licked his face in celebration, rubbing some of the dragon's poison on to his master.
properties.location_name: Nunnington (North Yorkshire) - Exact location unknown - Loschy Hill?

properties.name: Sea Dragon
properties.description: Several fishermen were attacked by a winged crocodile-like creature which they snagged in their nets while off the coast in this area. The beast killed one man and disabled another before being slain. The creature measured around 1.2 metres in length (though was said to be larger when alive) and possessed two legs with cloven feet.
properties.location_name: Orford (Suffolk) - Sea between Orford and Southwold

properties.name: Tamed Dragon
properties.description: Saint Petroc is said to have tamed a horrific dragon by placing a girdle about its neck before taking the beast to the beach and setting it free. The dragon swam away and was never seen again.

properties.name: Crested Dragons
properties.description: The dragons (or winged snakes, according to other sources) here were once said to be quite common, if not a little shy. They snacked on the local produce, especially chickens, and sparkled and shimmered when escaping farmers. They were hunted to extinction. It has been suggested that the winged snakes were misidentified pheasants or peacocks and not native to the area until introduced by the English.
properties.location_name: Penllyn (South Glamorgan) - Penllyn Castle, and surrounding woods

properties.name: Winged Snakes
properties.description: An old woman claimed that her grandfather had fought and killed a winged serpent in this area. The man had skinned the creature, though the evidence had been thrown away after his death.

properties.name: Tricked Dragon
properties.description: Paid by a nobleman to rid the area of a dragon, a local lad dug a deep pit and at the bottom he placed a brass mirror. The creature fell into the hole and, believing it had met a rival, fought its own reflection before falling exhausted. This enabled the boy to decapitate the monster with no further problems.

properties.name: Winged Snake
properties.description: A flesh eating serpent that lived on top of the waterfall was killed by locals who antagonised the beast until it attacked a scarlet cloth that concealed jagged spears.

properties.name: Cattle Eaters
properties.description: Before the town was built here, legend says the area was home to a huge fire breathing dragon that was slain by a local blacksmith annoyed at the large number of cattle he and his friends were losing.

properties.name: Gold
properties.description: A dragon was said to protect hidden gold in this barrow.

properties.name: Gas Breather
properties.description: A winged fire breathing dragon once terrorised this area. Demanding the milk of nine cows every day, the creature took residence on a hill. As well as breathing fire, the creature released poison gas which killed anyone venturing too close. It was finally killed after a long battle by a wandering knight who then went on his way without demanding a reward or revealing his name. The skin of the dragon was stored in the local church for many years, but what happened to the item is not known.

properties.name: Sleeping Dragon
properties.description: Some locals say that the two hills are a sleeping dragon. Another legend connected with the area says that either the king of Mercia or the king of Wessex lost a gold arrow here in the seventh century, and it can only be found by a seventh daughter of a seventh son.
properties.location_name: Shrewsbury (Shropshire) - Earl's Hill and Pontesford Hill

properties.name: Poisonous Lizard
properties.description: A great lizard that lived along this road, feasting on lonely travellers at night, was killed by a local hero and his pet dog. Unfortunately, both the victors died soon after the battle due to the delayed effect of the dragon's poison. One story says that the dragon was over a mile long.
properties.location_name: Slingsby (North Yorkshire) - Road from Hovingham to Malton

properties.name: Dragon Death
properties.description: Sir John Conyers killed this worm by stabbing it under a wing, marking the spot with a stone and donating the used sword to Durham Cathedral.
properties.location_name: Sockburn (Durham) - Area marked by the Greystone

properties.name: Undying Prince
properties.description: An immortal prince was bound by magicians and remains trapped in the hole. No one knows what he looks like, but animals which were led to the mouth of the hole would become terrified and become covered in sweat. One man was said to have seen a dragon with burning eyes enter the hole, followed by terrible groans emerging from below the ground.
properties.location_name: South Barrule (Isle of Man) - Devil's Den (was a hole in the ground, current status not known)

properties.name: Dragon of Wormenhert
properties.description: Abbot Ealdred of St Albans rebuilt his abbey using the ruins of Verulamium, a Roman city located nearby. During the course of the work the Abbot destroyed the lair of the dragon of Wormenhert.
properties.location_name: St Albans (Hertfordshire) - Exact location unknown, said to have been a cave within a gorge in a nearby hill

properties.name: Dragon Attack
properties.description: Recorded on old documents, a large dragon attacked a house in St Ostwyth, the air surrounding the creature so hot that it set the area alight.

properties.name: Well Monster
properties.description: A dragon which guarded a well managed to eat nine maidens who came one by one to draw water. It was finally slain by a man named Martin, due to be married to the youngest maiden.
properties.location_name: Strathmartine (Angus) - Nine Maiden's Well (now buried, exact location not known)

properties.name: Water Dragon
properties.description: This crested monster came from out of the local river and terrorised Sudbury and the neighbouring villages. Eventually, all the townsfolk gathered and chased the beast back into the river.

properties.name: Curse
properties.description: Treasure believed (by some) to be concealed in the area around the Iron Age hillfort has yet to be fully discovered - death and serious accidents have befallen all those to have tried. Another legend says that the treasure is protected by a dragon.

properties.name: Guardian
properties.description: A gwiber, or large lizard, protects this ancient tumulus.
properties.location_name: Trelech a'r Betws (also spelt Trellech a'r Betws) (Dyfed) - Prehistoric tumulus

properties.name: Slain Beast
properties.description: Little is known about the dragon said to have been killed in this area.

properties.name: Spilt Blood
properties.description: Parts of this hill are bare and chalky - some legends say that a dragon was slain on top of the hill, and where the beast's blood fell, the grass died.

properties.name: Hungry Dragon
properties.description: A local dragon which feasted on the local women and children was killed by the local landlord who fired a dagger at the creature using his musket.

properties.name: Skeleton
properties.description: A long barrow located here is said to contain most of the bones of a dragon killed by Sir Hugh Bardolph in the area. The dragon's severed head was sent to the king.

properties.name: More of More Hall
properties.description: When a fire breathing dragon visited his area, More of More Hall was called upon to slay the creature. As protection, More's armour was covered with fifteen centimetre long spikes. The fight between man and monster dragged on for two days and one night, before More had the opportunity to kick a spiked boot into the dragon's bottom (or mouth), which killed it.

properties.name: Latimer the Dragon Slayer
properties.description: The dragon of Well was slain by a knight name Latimer who was a local landowner. A dragon is featured in the Latimer coat of arms.
properties.location_name: Well (North Yorkshire) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Bishop Jocelyn's Wyrm
properties.description: Bishop Jocelyn drove away a dragon that had been terrorising locals around seven holy springs - the cathedral was later built in the area.

properties.name: Dragon Slayer
properties.description: A dragon which was given the locals a hard time was killed by an ex-soldier and his dog - the dog leapt onto the creature's face, while the man decapitated the wyrm.
properties.location_name: West Clandon (Surrey) - Exact location unknown, but woodcarving in local church

properties.name: The Dragon of Wantley
properties.description: This dragon made a nuisance of itself until the local hero Moore of Moore Hall donned a suit of spiked armour and kicked the creature between the legs. The dragon left the area with tears in its eyes.
properties.location_name: Wharncliffe Crags (South Yorkshire) - Exact location not known

properties.name: The Devil's Mount
properties.description: When the church in Wievliscombe was being rebuilt, the devil manifested riding a green dragon and began hurling rocks at the construction. Saint Andrew materialised and used a crucifix to drive both Devil and dragon away.

properties.name: Brace of Dragons
properties.description: Several dragons were reported to live in the area, though nothing more is known about their activities.

properties.name: Drake's Home
properties.description: An ancient document gives reference to this place has being home to a dragon.
properties.location_name: Wolverley (Hereford & Worcester) - Drakelow, adjacent to an SSSI woodland called Kinver Edge

properties.name: Coil Marks
properties.description: The marks on the hill near the church were said to be the location where a dragon once laid. The church is named after Margaret of Antioch, who was swallowed by a dragon but escaped when her crucifix caused irritation.

properties.name: Dragon Slaying
properties.description: The local church gives the account of how a local dragon that was immune to arrows was put to death in a nearby field by Sir George de la Haye. A virtually identical tale exists from nearby Bures.

properties.name: Guarded Secret Tunnel
properties.description: Leading to an untold amount of wealth, the tunnel hidden in this hillfort is guarded by a pair of snake-like dragons. The ghost of a druid is reported to haunt the site looking for this treasure, and the sound of phantom galloping horses have also been reported. Finally, another legend says the structure was formed by earth falling off the Devil's shovel as he tried to dig a hole in the South Downs.

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Spotted briefly from the cliffs, the fairy-enchanted island appeared and disappeared.

properties.name: Strange Mist
properties.description: Before a large part of this boggy land was drained, it was renowned for strange shapes, vaguely human in appearance, which appeared at night. One man who walked across the area encountered a strange cloud which enveloped him, filling the poor man with terror, before it drifted off.

properties.name: House
properties.description: A young girl walking on the hill came across a horse which appeared to be empty. She later discovered that the building had been demolished many years before her encounter. Twenty years previous, three shepherds watched a phantom funeral procession, complete with a horse-drawn hearse and figures carrying burning torches. Upon the coffin sat a golden crown. As a side note, the hill once had a carved white horse upon it.
properties.date: House seen 1962 or 1963, coffin seen around 1940, and previously in 1870s

properties.name: Clashing Blades
properties.description: A member of the Ghost Club and his partner reported being surrounded by the sounds of men fighting, sword clashing with sword, and the moans of the dying. There are also tales of a golden coffin and stolen treasure buried in the area.

properties.name: Screaming
properties.description: The battle, fought in 1691, is said to have been the most brutal on UK soil, with up to seven thousand people killed. Folklore says that the screams of dying soldiers can still be heard in the area.
properties.location_name: Aughrim (County Galway) - Site of Battle of Aughrim

properties.name: Burning
properties.description: This manor house was consumed by fire around the start of the twentieth century. Ten years after the fire, a witness reported seeing a house burning where the manor had stood, and again another witness reported the same occurrence a few years later.
properties.location_name: Baginton (West Midlands) - Manor House (no longer standing)

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Fishermen reported seeing the mysterious vanishing island that travels around the coast of Ireland.

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Another place where a magical vanishing island reputedly appears and disappears.
properties.location_name: Ballinaleame (according to source, but cannot place on modern maps) (County Galway) - Mouth of bay

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: Fishermen claimed to have seen an enchanted island off the coast before it vanished

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Local fishermen awoke to see a strange new island out to sea, covered with trees and rocks. As dozens climbed into their boats and set off towards it, the island faded from view.

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: The bay at Ballydonegan is another place where an enchanted island occasionally makes an appearance before vanishing.

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: One author claimed the entire population of Ballyheige witnessed an enchanted island which vanished soon after appearing.
properties.location_name: Ballyheige (County Kerry) - Tralee Bay, between Kerry Head and Brandon's Head

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Fishermen were aware of an enchanted island that would briefly manifest off the coast.

properties.name: Buildings
properties.description: A woman taking a polaroid photograph of the beach was amazed to see two buildings and a shadowy post on the developed image - no buildings were in the frame as she pressed the camera shutter. An analysis of the image suggested that it was an under exposed image of a farm.

properties.name: Lake with Sword in a Stone
properties.description: A family driving in the New Forest while on holiday passed a mist covered lake, with a sword set in a large stone a few metres off the shoreline. The lake had disappeared when they drove back and, to the best of our knowledge, has never returned.
properties.location_name: Beaulieu (Hampshire) - Exact location unknown, somewhere in the New Forest

properties.name: Line of Monks
properties.description: A ghostly procession of monks is said to haunt this area, and the ruins of the priory return to their former pristine state. The wife of Joseph Conrad was one witness to this event.

properties.name: Strange Trees
properties.description: Sounds of footsteps can be heard in the wood, even when no one is immediately visible. The trees are also said to dramatically change appearance.

properties.name: Phantom Fair
properties.description: A man riding through the hills saw a fair from the distance, though it faded from view as he approached. As he passed through the area, it is reported he 'felt' the event happening around him.

properties.name: Cottage and Chopper
properties.description: A young girl ran ahead of their family and came across a stone cottage with an older man chopping firewood outside. The man had white hair and long moustache. The girl briefly spoke to the old man, who spoke back to her. Something then made the girl uneasy, so she ran back to her family. When the family passed the area where man and cottage were, all that could be found were old foundation stones of a long-gone building.

properties.name: Georgian Red Brick Dwelling, Surrounded by a Beautiful Garden
properties.description: First seen in 1860, the Georgian house was said to look quite normal, until it dissolved into mist. It was also seen around 1900, though no recent sightings exist.
properties.location_name: Bradfield St George (Suffolk) - North of the church

properties.name: Wall and Gates of a Grand Building
properties.description: Observed in 1926, the witnesses were new to the area and did not think much about the building until they returned later in the day and realised it had disappeared.
properties.location_name: Bradfield St George (Suffolk) - The lane from Little Welnetham which turns right at the church

properties.name: Victorians
properties.description: While walking along the seafront, two sisters watched Victorian figures walking around and listened to the sound of music and laughter. The seafront was lit up as if daytime, and a young man and a boy walked out to sea along a rope bridge. As the sisters walked away, they realised the scene they had watched was impossible, and turning back they saw only the darkness over the sea. One sister later discovered that there was a chain bridge on the spot which led to the pier, but it was destroyed in 1896.

properties.name: Roadside Cottage
properties.description: Three lost girls on the moor at night claimed to have come across a cottage. Peering through an un-curtained window, they could see an old man and woman sitting by a fire. Before they had a chance to move, cottage and contents vanished without warning.

properties.name: Sounds of Fighting
properties.description: Once a year the sounds of clashing swords and Roman and Saxon screaming can be heard in this area. Another ghost reportedly observed here during dark nights is a figure that plummets from the ramparts.

properties.name: Heavy Bell
properties.description: Two bell ringers with first-hand experience of every bell in town heard an unfamiliar ringing coming from the direction of the Audi supermarket, the sound different to the nearby town hall clock and that of Saint Modwens, a church with bells that could be occasionally heard from the area. The supermarket was built on the site of the Holy Trinity church, which had a heavy bell that would have had a similar note depth to the sound the bell ringers heard.
properties.location_name: Burton upon Trent (Staffordshire) - King Edward Place, heading towards corner of Rangemore street

properties.name: Ringing
properties.description: Two bell ringers standing outside the church waiting to ring for Sunday service heard the peeling of a set of eight bells. The next nearest church only had six bells, while the only two churches with eight bells both denied their bells were operating at the time.
properties.location_name: Burton upon Trent (Staffordshire) - Outside Saint Chad's church, Hunter Street

properties.name: House of the Dead
properties.description: The building which infrequently materialises on the hill was demolished long ago and was the site of several chilling murders.

properties.name: Island City
properties.description: Witnesses who spotted an enchanted island which briefly manifested off the coast claimed they could see it featured a city.

properties.name: Thick Mist
properties.description: A group of friends in the woods at dusk reported a thick mist that quickly descended the area. Footsteps and other noises surrounded them as they ran towards the car park. As they ran clear of the woods, the mist lifted, and silence returned once more.

properties.name: Cottages
properties.description: Some believe that several ghostly cottages periodically appear in the village, though it is not known whether they were once 'real'.
properties.date: Every ten or twelve years, though date not known

properties.name: Butler & House
properties.description: A lost woman driver stopped at this building and asked the butler for directions, which he gave. However, she soon found out that the house had been empty for many years. She returned and found the building in a disused state, completely different from when she first called in.

properties.name: Sounds of Battle
properties.description: Two friends were walking through the area on a cold frosty morning. There was a sudden gust of wind, followed by the sounds of shouting, horses galloping, and the clash of swords. Both witnesses were scared by their encounter.
properties.location_name: Cotgrave (Nottinghamshire) - Colston Gate, open road flanked by fields

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: The bay at Courtmacsherry is another place where an enchanted island occasionally makes an appearance before vanishing.

properties.name: Vanishing Cottage
properties.description: An OS surveyor working on the moor spotted a cottage with smoke rising from the chimney. When he went to investigate, no sign of the building could be found, although he did meet another person who had also spotted the cottage but was unable to find it.

properties.name: Drumming and Shouts
properties.description: A dog walker in the area reported hearing drumming and shouting men. The sounds continued for 15 - 20 minutes before fading, and nothing out of the ordinary could be seen. The witness said it was like being on the set of a movie where an army were about to charge into battle.

properties.name: House Party
properties.description: A manor house has been seen in the area, a party in full flow visible through its windows. However, the house was demolished many years previous. Even when not seen, locals have reported hearing music coming from the site. A large black knight on horseback is still occasionally seen, once a year.
properties.date: 31 December knight seen (reoccurring), house reported in the twentieth century
properties.location_name: Downend (Isle of Wight) - Area near Knighton Gorges

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: The mysterious appearing and vanishing enchanted island briefly became visible here.

properties.name: Aircraft of the Future
properties.description: A lost pilot recalled flying over disused airfield in the 1930s and saw it operational, with yellow RAF aircraft on the ground - these did not exist at the time. He later checked and found the airfield really was unused. A few years later, the airfield was reopened for training purposes, and aircraft based there painted yellow.

properties.name: Fighting Armies
properties.description: The ghostly figures found here when Britain is at war generate screams, horses and the clashing of swords as they move over the hills overlooking the coast. The apparitions are said to have last appeared during the Second World War.
properties.location_name: East Lulworth (Dorset) - Flower's Barrow and Grange Hill

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Many of the townsfolk were said to have spotted an island which appeared and disappeared off the coast.
properties.location_name: Enniscrone (aka Inniscrone, aka Inishcrone) (County Sligo) - Off coast

properties.name: Flashback
properties.description: On three occasions between 1996 and 1997, a witness (and on one occasion, their friend) looked out the window of their flat and watched the street below fill Victorian figures, including ladies with long dresses, workmen carrying hods and horses and carriages. Each time the scene lasted for around ten minutes.
properties.location_name: Glasgow (Lanarkshire) - High Street, just after The Royal Infirmary and to the Tolbooth Clock

properties.name: Civil War Skirmish
properties.description: Six workers claimed they heard charging horses, the clash of swords, and gunfire, though could see nothing strange. A local historian said that a small fight had happened nearby, between Roundheads and Cavaliers.
properties.location_name: Grafton Regis (Northamptonshire) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Disappearing Shop
properties.description: A coin collector in Yarmouth reported visiting a shop to buy envelopes for his collection. He thought the shop looked old fashioned, as did the lady who served him, but thought no more of it until he returned a week later - and found the place had gone.
properties.location_name: Great Yarmouth (Norfolk) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Georgian House
properties.description: While walking a familiar path, a brother and sister spotted a strange Georgian house that they had never seen before. A girl and a dog walked down the driveway and passed the siblings. Neither brother nor sister were later unable to locate the house, even on old maps. M H Coleman, writing in the Journal of the Society for Psychical Research (vol 63, no 853) offered compelling evidence that the house is real, and the witnesses were mistaken in their belief that what they had seen was ghostly.
properties.location_name: Hadleigh (Essex) - Wood between Leigh-on-Sea and Hadleigh

properties.name: Radley
properties.description: The buried village of Radley is hidden somewhere in this area, and long vanished church bells can be heard on Christmas Eve.

properties.name: Edwardian Dance
properties.description: A woman staying at in a spare room on the ground floor walked into the room and found herself looking upon a dinner party, the attendees wearing Edwardian attire. The diners looked at the woman in surprise, and then vanished. She was later told that the spare room was once part of the dining room. A phantom man in riding gear has also been observe din the same room.

properties.name: Old New Castle
properties.description: An image of Hastings Castle has been reportedly seen floating over the sea, flags flying and in the condition that one would imagine it to be in several hundred years ago.

properties.name: Crowd with Torches
properties.description: A couple out in their car found themselves surrounded by hundreds of people running across nearby fields holding burning touches. As they drove to the nearby village, one of the figures stepped out in the road and passed straight through their car.
properties.location_name: Havenstreet (Isle of Wight) - Road leading to village, and area near Hare & Hounds Inn

properties.name: Cottage
properties.description: Said to come and go irregularly, the cottage matches none that can be remembered or placed in the area.

properties.name: Burning Windows
properties.description: The windows of this ruined castle were said to glow with blue fire when observed from a distance - when approached, the illusion would vanish.

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Inhabitants of this island spotted another island identical to their own, which vanished a brief time later.

properties.name: Vanishing Island
properties.description: Islanders spotted another island which disappeared without trace.
properties.location_name: Inishark (aka Inishshark, aka Shark Island) (County Galway) - Off coast

properties.name: Battleground
properties.description: A total of five witnesses in two different places watched a battle over the skies of the castle, between the Highlander regiment and garrisoned French troops. This mirrored real events in America, where 300 Highlanders were killed assaulting a fort.
properties.location_name: Inveraray (Argyll and Bute) - Skies around Inveraray Castle

properties.name: Time Slip
properties.description: A clergyman visiting the island reported seeing the abbey in its former glory - he almost drowned when approaching the building, believing that he was walking on a causeway that no longer existed. Friends managed to save him. There are also stories that say Viking longboats sometimes appear off the coast, depositing phantom raiders who go on to attack the abbey, while people still report hearing singing coming from the empty abbey. At least one guest reported seeing a transparent, motionless, cowled figure standing by their bed, while a much older story concerns a tailor who works through the night and is troubled by a phantom fleshless hand.
properties.location_name: Iona, Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Abbey

properties.name: Island Covered in Buildings
properties.description: This island, covered in fantastic white buildings, has appeared twice in the sky just off the coast. On one instance it remained viable for most of the day. Some people believe it to be the Fairy Isle, while others say it was a mirage of a part of Norway.
properties.location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - In the sky off coast of the island

properties.name: Ambush
properties.description: For years after an ambush which left many RIC men dead, locals reported hearing the screams of wounded people in the area.

properties.name: Castle
properties.description: A passenger in a vehicle briefly spotted a castle while passing through the area. After looking away and then realising that there should be no castle in the area, they looked again - the building had gone.
properties.location_name: Kilsyth (Lanarkshire) - Likely to be near the grounds of Auchinvole farm

properties.name: High Visibility Man
properties.description: A teenager with a group of friends exploring the sheds on the site spotted a man wearing a high visibility jacket standing in a doorway. The teenager pointed the figure out to the rest of the group, but they could not see anything. They all left the site. About a month later, the teenager returned with another friend, and realised that the doorway in which the man had stood did not exist. The witness was later told that a friend's father had encountered a ghostly man along the road close to the sheds. The man appeared to be 'normal', until he suddenly vanished as the witness walked close.
properties.location_name: Kimberley (Nottinghamshire) - Disused buildings on the hill leading into Kimberley, Main Street (private property)

properties.name: Battle of Edgehill Repeating
properties.description: A bloody battle of the English Civil War, the fighting repeated itself for several successive nights in 1642, in front of dozens of witnesses. More recently, sounds of battle have been reported, though the visuals are not forthcoming. The date of the more recent battle sounds is said to be 23 October.
properties.date: 23 October (reoccurring), also 23 December 1642, lasting several nights

properties.name: Kilstiveen
properties.description: The town of Kilstiveen was submerged by a giant tidal wave, its inhabitants washed away to drown. The ghost town occasionally is glimpsed by fishermen.
properties.location_name: Lahinch (County Clare) - Sea west of town

properties.name: Raided Building
properties.description: Over the past two hundred years, there have been several reports of this building seen to be on fire with soldiers running around its base - the vision vanishes if investigated. Another tale of the area tells of a headless coachman who drives a phantom coach.

properties.name: Battle
properties.description: This battle fought around the 1300's is still reportedly heard in May - over 3000 people are said to have been killed. The exact date may vary, though the phantom event always occurs in late May.

properties.name: Sounds of Action
properties.description: A witness recalled how he and a friend were walking across the deserted airfield at night and seeing a strange light in the control tower. Both knowing that the building should have been empty, the illumination unnerved them slightly. As they passed by a hangar, they were suddenly surrounded by the sounds of activity - they could hear the hangar doors screeching open, the engines of aircraft starting up, and the patter of many pairs of feet running by them, though nothing could be seen.
properties.location_name: Lindholme (South Yorkshire) - Former Second World War Air Force Base

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: Shortly before an enchanted island appeared to the population of Ballyheige, it made a brief manifestation off the coast of Lisneakeabree.
properties.date: Late nineteenth or early twentieth century?

properties.name: Cathedral Bells
properties.description: A cathedral once stood in this spot before it was flooded - the tower bells now ring out on select days of the year.

properties.name: Isolated Cottage
properties.description: This cottage vanished into thin air before the witnesses could reach it - apparently the small building stood on the water's edge at the start of the twentieth century but was submerged and lost forever as the loch's water level rose.

properties.name: Battle Cries
properties.description: A woman reported waking up in the early hours and hearing the distant shouts and cheers of hundreds of men. She awoke her husband who also heard it, and the sounds appeared to upset the local dogs which began to bark.

properties.name: Island Village
properties.description: When a magically appearing and disappearing island manifested in the bay, inhabitants of Maas could see a village that resembled their own.

properties.name: Fighting Armies
properties.description: During a great storm, two armies marched through the sky and fought with firearms. The army which came in from the north was first to vanish, but only after the accompanying storm caused much damage on the ground, scattering and killing livestock.
properties.location_name: Malton (North Yorkshire) - Skies over the area, covering around twelve miles

properties.name: The Demon Oak Tree
properties.description: An oak tree which was once used to conceal a dead body was considered haunted; locals were fearful of walking by the tree at night. The tree eventually fell and was destroyed, though some say that the oak sometimes comes back.

properties.name: Gibbet
properties.description: A young girl walking home encountered a gibbet with the body of a bearded man swinging from it at this junction. She ran off, and looking over her shoulder once more, realised that gibbet and man had vanished, replaced by the normal junction signpost.

properties.name: Cottage with Pond
properties.description: A lad cycling down Sandy Lane recalled seeing a picturesque farmhouse with a pond just in front of it. Years later the (now grown) man tried to find the dwelling but could find no evidence the property or pond ever existed.

properties.name: Fordson Tractor
properties.description: A visitor to the site heard what he first thought was an old Fordson tractor ticking over, though the sound later changed to an aircraft carrying out pre-flight checks.
properties.location_name: Normanton (West Yorkshire) - RAF Bottesford (aka RAF Normanton) - no longer operational

properties.name: Crowned Man
properties.description: A witness walking towards a camp site encountered a man wearing a crown on his head dancing around strangely, like a 'village idiot'. The man stopped in front of the witness and stared hard into his eyes. The witness tried to push the man away, but his hands passed straight through the figure. As the witness moved away, he turned and saw many people in Victorian clothing pushing prams. The witness was said to be shaken up for days after the experience.

properties.name: Cricket Pavilion
properties.description: Walking his dog through the park, this witness spotted what looked like a cricket pavilion with a veranda about 100 yards from him. The building was quite brightly lit, illuminated from the outside. The witness was a little surprised never to have seen the building before, but being new to the area, was not perturbed. Walking back the same way a little later, the building had gone. Neighbours told him that they could not recall a pavilion ever being on the site.
properties.location_name: Outer London: East Barnet (Greater London) - Oak Hill Park

properties.name: Battlefield
properties.description: The bloody battleground between English Government and Jacobite Highlanders, the anniversary of the battle is marked by the ground turning blood red. Reports of soldiers in English and Scottish regalia, both lying dead and others fighting, have also been recorded. As if this was not enough, the area is also haunted by the drifting head of a woman murdered on the site during the seventeenth century, and the tall white spectre who tries to grab people passing along the roads; anyone the ghost touches dies within the year...
properties.location_name: Pitlochry (Perth and Kinross) - Pass of Killiecrankie

properties.name: Dying Highwayman
properties.description: A large spectral tree has been seen here, together with a highwayman pinned to it by several swords. On other occasions, an elderly phantom woman is spotted; thought to be a gypsy woman, she accidentally set herself on fire with her pipe while sleeping.

properties.name: Lamps
properties.description: It has been said that sometimes one can see box pews and brass lamps within the church, even though the items were removed in the nineteenth century.
properties.location_name: Poole (Dorset) - United Reformed Church, Skinner Street

properties.name: Overlord
properties.description: While walking his dog early one quiet May morning, Mr Murphy suddenly found himself surrounded by tanks, jeeps and American soldiers walking around. Then, as suddenly as they had appeared, the scene vanished, leaving both Murphy and his dog shaking. The witness speculated that he had fleetingly witnessed soldiers making final preparations for the D-Day landings.

properties.name: Old Brazils
properties.description: A strange island, likely to be a Fata Morgana, was said to periodically appear off the coast. The island was covered in trees, ruins and tall towers, and would always disappear at sunset. It is not clear whether this phantom land was 'Old Brazils', an island which appeared once per year along the Antrim coastline.

properties.name: Double Fronted Georgian House in Mist
properties.description: 'In the days before automobiles' a house with a beautiful garden appeared along Kingshall Street. A witness had never seen the building before, so went to investigate, only to see the house engulfed by mist and disappearing to leave an empty field.

properties.name: Coffee Shop
properties.description: A woman in a supermarket spotted an old fashioned coffee shop frequented by several smartly dressed people, through a doorway in the shop. A little while later she returned to the site with her husband but was unable to find the coffee shop. After a little research, they discovered a bar had existed on the site many years previous.

properties.name: Battle
properties.description: The sounds of a ghostly battle, complete with screams, horses and cannon fire, have been heard here.

properties.name: Monks and Priests
properties.description: A fisherman caught in a storm sought shelter on the island and went to pray at a ruined church. He encountered many monks and priests within the building, although when he closed his eyes and opened them, the figures had vanished, and the church once more appeared ruined.
properties.location_name: Scattery Island (Inis Cathaig) (County Clare) - Church of St. Mary near the Round Tower

properties.name: Spencer
properties.description: A man walking home witnessed a figure entering a small cottage. A few seconds, a couple of screams followed, and two figures ran from the cottage, one with a head injury. The witness panicked, convinced he had watched a robbery and ran off. Lost in the dark, he ran in a circle, returning to the scene of the crime, only there was no cottage... It is speculated he witnessed the murder of a gatekeeper and his wife, killed by John Spencer in 1779. The ghost of Spencer, or maybe the gatekeeper, has also been seen standing on the roadside.

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: The mouth of the River Shannon is another place where an enchanted island has made an occasional appearance before vanishing.

properties.name: Chip Shop
properties.description: Walking home one evening, a mother and daughter spotted a chip shop which they had thought was new, as they had not seen it before. The shop was open, lights were on, and people were inside. A couple of nights later, they decided to visit the takeaway, but it was not there - a DIY shop stood on the spot, twice the size of the chip shop they had seen.

properties.name: Hooves on Gravel
properties.description: While walking home through the nearby countryside, a woman reported hearing the overworked horses and the sound of crunching gravel. The site of the occurrence, upon investigation, proved once to be a brick yard.

properties.name: House
properties.description: A phantom manor house with large arched doors appeared here to a witness who described it as 'perfect but without substance'.

properties.name: Army Marching On
properties.description: Over two dozen witnesses saw a mass of troops ascending the mountain on horseback, though there were no real soldiers in the area. The ghostly army had been seen twice before, always on the same date.
properties.location_name: Souter Fell (Cumbria) - Side of Mountain visible from Wilton Hall

properties.name: Chapel
properties.description: A slightly dubious source claimed that as a man named Allan turned the corner of this street, he could see the large chapel (which had not existed for many years). Allan entered the building in time to see a monk being decapitated by an assailant. Allan then found himself sitting on a nearby wall with a grinning skull by his feet. The skull briefly gnashed its teeth before flying into the sky and vanishing.
properties.location_name: St Andrews (Fife) - North Bell Street (now Greyfriars Garden)

properties.name: Enchanted Island
properties.description: The bay is another place where an enchanted island has made an occasional appearance before vanishing.
properties.location_name: St Finian's Bay (aka St Finan's Bay) (County Cork) - Off coast

properties.name: Altered Interior
properties.description: A passing gentleman noticed the church doors open and went in to investigate - he said he found the altar adorned with flowers and set up for a Roman Catholic service, even though it was an Anglican church.

properties.name: Army on Manoeuvres
properties.description: Several people reported seeing a forest in the sky, with soldiers in black uniforms moving around in formation. More soldiers wore white clothing, but appeared to be allied to the other troops, rather than in conflict.
properties.location_name: Stockton on the Forest (North Yorkshire) - Skies above area

properties.name: Thatched Cottage
properties.description: A female cyclist caught in a storm took cover in a thatched cottage along this road, warming herself by an open fire with an old grey haired man for company. She later discovered that the cottage had been overgrown and dilapidated since the previous century.

properties.name: Gothic House
properties.description: Two cyclists passing through the village on a winter's evening spotted a large gothic-style manor house where they knew a bungalow should have been. Speaking later to a local, they were told no house had ever stood on the site.

properties.name: House with Glowing Windows
properties.description: After a murder of a newlywed bride, this house was left to fall into ruin. Some people reported that a glowing candle could be seen moving past various windows, while others claimed a ghostly wedding party occurred. The Hall is now lost below the waters of the flooded valley of Thirlmere, which is home to a phantom dog which swims through the water. In 2013 a witness camping in the area became unnerved after witnessing two silently moving lights along the road.
properties.date: 31 October (reoccurring), Wedding party occurs. Lights seen May 2013

properties.name: Changed Landscape
properties.description: What could have been a mirage, someone reported that the landscape along the coast had totally changed. The witness almost fell over the edge of the cliff, mistaking the phantom landscape as real.
properties.location_name: Torbay (Devon) - Off Coast of Churston Ferrers

properties.name: Mansion
properties.description: While walking their dog, a witness watched a mansion appear. The more the witness looked, the clearer the vision became, before it suddenly vanished.

properties.name: Shuck and Friends
properties.description: This wooded hollow is particularly haunted around Halloween, when it is said to change into dense woodland with demonic creatures, including a red eyed dogs and rams.

properties.name: Execution
properties.description: A witness living near here reported looking from a window to see dozens of people standing around the scene of an eighteenth century execution. The Bayswater Road area is also said to be haunted by strange shadowy figures which quickly disappear once seen.
properties.location_name: W2 (Greater London) - St George's Field, area near Marble Arch

properties.name: Battle
properties.description: Several thousand men were said to have been seen in the area, the sound of battle filling the air. The event is said to have been witnessed by around one hundred people.

properties.name: Fire
properties.description: Several witnesses have reported seeing large burning fires on the hills, with figures dancing around them, talking and singing loudly in an unrecognised language. When investigated, there is never any evidence of the fire or figures. A legend says the hill was created by the Devil, who threw down a pile of dirt on the spot after being led to believe that it would take him several years to reach Devizes.

properties.name: Monks in a field
properties.description: One visitor to the site stated she looked across the fields and watched a group of monks approaching the barrow. She left for a few minutes, and on her return realised that the field now looked different; shorter grass, fewer trees, and the figures had vanished. The area around the barrow is locally acknowledged to be 'strange'. The road running by Belas Knap is said to be haunted by a blue figure which unnerves drivers as it steps on into their paths.
properties.location_name: Winchcombe (Gloucestershire) - Belas Knap, a long barrow situated above the town

properties.name: Kidnapping
properties.description: The Rev Robert Kirk is said to have been kidnapped by fairies here. He appeared in ethereal form a couple of times after the event, begging for help in escaping from the fairy world.

properties.name: Goblins
properties.description: The same is supposed to have been derived from 'Puck walls' and is speculated to come from long-gone haunted ruins.

properties.name: Coffin Makers
properties.description: The fairies which lived at this fort were said to have borrowed a man's hatchet to make a coffin for a neighbour's child two weeks prior to his death.

properties.name: Dwarf with Pointed Hat
properties.description: The witness to this encounter stated that they once bumped into a four foot high figure wearing a green pointed hat feeding the plants.

properties.name: Little Folk
properties.description: Fairies once made their home here and rewarded a local man with food after he helped one of their kind repair a kitchen utensil.

properties.name: Dancing within Limits
properties.description: Children would dance around a fairy circle here, although it was said if they circled the site more than nine times, fairies would spirit the offending child away.
properties.location_name: Alnwick (Northumberland) - Fairy ring at Chathill Farm (ring may no longer exist)

properties.name: Fairy Hill
properties.description: Famed for its fairies, these pesky fellows kidnapped a local baker's wife - she was only returned after her husband broke the fairy spell by accidentally standing on one leg.

properties.name: Secret Fairies
properties.description: The fairies here once travelled to and from the barrow using underground passageways leading to a nearby churchyard (at Maughold).
properties.location_name: Andreas (Isle of Man) - Shan Cashtal barrow

properties.name: Evil Fairies
properties.description: A palace concealed within this hill was run by an army of evil fairies - they would kidnap anyone foolish enough to venture close to their land.

properties.name: Trolls
properties.description: Living beneath the ravine, the trolls are said to emerge at night and track down their prey - humans. At least one farmer was lured to his death by their ability to trick people into straying from the road.

properties.name: Short Men in Green
properties.description: A fifteen year old boy spotted two figures sea fishing, each just over a metre tall, dressed in green and wearing brown shoes. The figures chattered to each other in Irish before jumping up and vanishing. One of the figures left a small pipe behind which the witness took, although it later disappeared when 'safely' locked away.
properties.location_name: Aran Island (County Galway) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Tree
properties.description: Jim Sweeney appeared on national television sharing the story of the fairy tree on his land. Sweeney claimed that when the tree fell, he would be dead within a week, and as the last remaining member of the family, Jim worked tirelessly to keep the dying tree propped up to ensure his survival.

properties.name: Leprechaun's Shoe
properties.description: A small shoe said to belong to a leprechaun was found near the village by a cow herder. Unfortunately, the man forgot to take the shoe home, and when he went to retrieve it the following day, the shoe had gone.
properties.location_name: Ardrah (County Cork) - Field in the area

properties.name: Pooka
properties.description: A witness who awoke in the middle of the night encountered what she believed to be a fairy entity known as a Pooka, sleeping over the pelmet of a landing window. The creature resembled a little piglet, but with a longer, pointy nose and no visible tail. The part of the house where the sighting happened is on the path of a ley line.
properties.location_name: Ashby de la Zouch (Leicestershire) - Private residence

properties.name: Bagpipes
properties.description: Played by the fairy folk, the sound of bagpipes could once be heard emerging from the stones. A tree in the centre of the stone circle is considered to belong to the fairies.
properties.location_name: Athgreany (County Wicklow) - Stone circle, aka Piper's Stones

properties.name: Robin's Well
properties.description: Robin Goodfellow and his friends were thought to frequent this spring.
properties.location_name: Aynho (Northamptonshire) - Spring known as Puck Well

properties.name: Red Cow
properties.description: Dugald Campbell noticed a strange little red cow being attacked by the rest of his herd. The red cow ran off with the rest of the herd giving chase. Campbell followed and watched as the red cow entered a solid piece of rock, closely followed by one of his cows. The rest of the herd, intercepted by Campbell, were prevented from vanishing into the rock.
properties.location_name: Balephuil, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Farm in the area

properties.name: Phynnodderee
properties.description: This fairy creature would be observed digging around the ruins, although when the area was examined, there was never any evidence of soil disturbance.

properties.name: Fairy Home
properties.description: A large sandbank was once thought to be home to fairies that borrowed the local blacksmith's cauldron every night, returning it in the morning.
properties.location_name: Ballevulin, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Large sandbank (no longer exists)

properties.name: Beiceacan
properties.description: Described as sounding like a calf at slaughter, this entity would prey on lone travellers at night, throwing them into the marsh to drown. One man tracked down Beiceacan, and while wearing spurs, mounted the beast and kicked at its flanks. Beiceacan tossed the man onto the roadside and ran off, never to be see again.
properties.location_name: Ballinagree (aka Ballynagree) (County Cork) - Marshland in the area

properties.name: Sprites & Pixies
properties.description: On this day, the fairy population comes out in force. If they are not acknowledged by anyone crossing the bridge, the fairy folk have no hesitation in making their presence felt.

properties.name: Bankruptcy
properties.description: According to the Belfast Telegraph, former billionaire Sean Quinn moved the Aughrim Wedge Tomb in 1992, which was home to fairy folk. These little entities cursed the man responsible for the relocation - Sean was at one point worth just under five billion euros, and as of November 2011, he only had eleven thousand euros in the bank.

properties.name: Bagpipers
properties.description: The fort in this field was home to four fairy bagpipers dressed in red.

properties.name: Tiny Footballers
properties.description: A worker returning home took a shortcut across a marshy field and watched several tiny men playing football. The worker was told to kick the ball, but he was unable to touch it. When he finally did kick the ball, the worker was knocked to the ground. When he recovered, the tiny footballers had vanished.
properties.location_name: Ballyroan (County Laois) - Marshy field west of village

properties.name: Black Goat
properties.description: This goat, though by some to be a fairy being, resides over the tombs in the area. The creature causes anyone who threatens the land to develop a hunchback.
properties.location_name: Ballyvaughan (County Clare) - Poulaphuca, area around dolmen

properties.name: Free Money
properties.description: Lucky people are said to be able to find money which has been left by the fairies, but if they failed to add a coin of their own to the hoard they had found, everything would slip away.
properties.location_name: Bamburgh (Northumberland) - Rocky outcrop on which castle is built

properties.name: Idris
properties.description: Idris was said to be a wise giant, who may have had links with King Arthur. People would avoid the area at night, convinced that fairies would drive them mad. Another legend says that the site was the hunting ground of Gwyn ap Nudd (Ruler of the Otherworld) who searched the area with a pack of Cwn Annwn, looking for souls to take back home. Finally. Strange lights are said to appear around the summit for the first few days of a new year.

properties.name: Tiny Green People
properties.description: Two children digging on the mound said they unearthed a small cottage with a slate roof, though when they returned a short time later, the construction had vanished. A few days later their father saw two small people in green standing on the hill - they vanished into it.

properties.name: Fighting Fairies
properties.description: A farmer from Sheemore (Co Leitrim) who once helped the fairies had his cattle taken by bailiffs. The bailiffs drove the cattle for a few miles before being intercepted by the fairies at a bridge, where the bailiffs were beaten by invisible foes and scared away. Legend says this is how Battlebridge received its name.

properties.name: Butter Makers
properties.description: The little folk would use water in this area in the creation of their butter.
properties.location_name: Baysdale (North Yorkshire) - Unidentified spring or well in the area

properties.name: Fairy Home
properties.description: The moorland in his region was considered a place where the little people could hide.

properties.name: Moving Fairies
properties.description: A group of fairies carrying household items were observed walking up the mountain, disappearing into the mists which circled the top. It was thought they moved home to escape the sound of a newly built mill.
properties.location_name: Beary Mountain (aka Bearey Mountain) (Isle of Man) - Upper part of the site

properties.name: Water Fairies
properties.description: A witness watched the little people playing merrily in the water at the pass. Nearby is the grave of Gelert (the dog that was mistakenly killed by its master after saving a child from a wolf) where the hound's ghost has been reported. Finally, the ghostly woman in white who haunts the area is an ill omen, with death said to follow a sighting.

properties.name: Knockers
properties.description: Some believe these creatures to resemble mutant dwarfs (two foot high, large eyes with deformed and twisted heads), while others call them the ghosts of those killed while being forced to work for the Romans... Either way, they can be heard mining and picking away at the rocks underground in these areas.
properties.location_name: Bedruthan (Cornwall) - Beach, cliff top, & Bedruthan Steps

properties.name: Sounds of Hunters
properties.description: Evan Thomas claimed to have scared away invisible fairy hunters by drawing his knife (it was said that fairies were afraid of knives and iron).
properties.location_name: Bedwellty (Gwent) - Mountain in the area, close to Ebbw Fawr

properties.name: Helpful Fairies
properties.description: The little people here are traditionally helpful in nature, and once fixed a plough for a farmer. When archaeologists tried to dig up the barrow in 1850, a storm blew up and drove them away. A golden coffin is said to be buried in the area.

properties.name: Red Caps
properties.description: In an unnamed field in this area treasure is reputedly buried. Two fairy-like entities wearing red caps are said to occasionally appear, searching for the hoard.
properties.location_name: Beelsby (Lincolnshire) - Unnamed field in the area

properties.name: Small Staircase
properties.description: Too small for normal human use, the stairs are believed to have been designed for fairy use. A local legend says if you can climb them without touching the stone cliffs on either side, you will be granted a wish.

properties.name: Home
properties.description: This mountain was home to fairies, one of many sites in Scotland.
properties.location_name: Beinn a' Ghlo (mountain) (Perth and Kinross) - General area

properties.name: Apologise to the Tree
properties.description: If the fairy tree found near the course is hit by an errant golf ball, the player is advised to apologise to avoid the wrath of the little people. Even if not struck, it is said that golfers nod at the tree as they pass. Just in case.

properties.name: Young Girl
properties.description: Having traversed old drover track on western shoulder of Ben Alder, a walker was looking for a suitable spot to cross a stream. The walker noticed the image of a smiling young girl, dressed in blue/green watery outfit, sitting on a slab of rock amidst the fast moving water. She was pointing to the water, enticing the walker to cross. The walker panicked and moved further downstream, crossing at a point where the water was calmer.
properties.location_name: Ben Alder (Highland) - Below the mountain, en route for Loch Ossian

properties.name: Grinding
properties.description: This hill was home to fairies who could be heard grinding their corn and singing deep within.
properties.location_name: Ben Feall, Coll (Argyll and Bute) - General area of the hill

properties.name: Gathering
properties.description: A child walking this path with his aunt watched many people moving around a sheep pen, including a pale woman wearing a red jacket and a crown. When the child mentioned it to his aunt a little later, she said he must have been dreaming, as only ruins stood in that field.
properties.location_name: Berriew (Powys) - Field between Hafodafel and Pen-y-Llwyn

properties.name: Fairy Song
properties.description: Local superstition states that if you place your ear to the ground here at midday, you can hear the soft singing of fairy folk.

properties.name: Fairy Rings
properties.description: This area was widely believed to be the haunt of fairy folk, where they would come and dance in the pale moonlight.

properties.name: Fairy Guardians
properties.description: Though the castle is long gone, the area is still a place where fairies hide - anyone trying to steal earth or soil from the land is warned off by whispering voices.

properties.name: Fairy Fair
properties.description: Fairies were once believed to hold annual fairs on the hills, singing and dancing throughout the night.

properties.name: Little Man
properties.description: Farmer Mick Purcell was forced to take his cow to Cork to sell. While on the road Purcell met a little man and swopped the cow for a small bottle. Understandably, returning home with a bottle instead of money upset Purcell's wife, until he opened it - out sprang two figures which covered the table with gold and silver dishes.
properties.location_name: Bottlehill (County Cork) - Road heading towards Cork

properties.name: Figures in Brown
properties.description: Seen in a garden on this estate, several little people dressed in brown played in and around the bushes before being scared off.

properties.name: Puck
properties.description: The name of this Bournemouth suburb is a corruption of Puck's Down, or Fairy Down, although the reason for this name is forgotten.

properties.name: Herbivore Heist
properties.description: Fairies stole a white cow but returned to the farmer's field every night to let it graze. The farmer and his dog would give chase, but the fairies would escape by throwing bread that the dog would stop to eat.
properties.location_name: Brackletter (Highland) - Field of the White Cow

properties.name: Glashtin
properties.description: A short phantom with a hairy and evil face, this entity would haunt the churchyard. It could only be safely passed if bowed to three times.

properties.name: Fairy Home
properties.description: This is another site said to be home to the fairies, and it is considered unlucky not to say hello or wave to them as you cross the bridge.

properties.name: Fairy Photographs
properties.description: Becoming world famous, the handful of fairy photographs taken here by the girls Elsie & Frances are still a bone of contention in the fortean world. The book by Joe Cooper, 'The Case of the Cottingley Fairies', is worth reading for further (and very detailed) information.

properties.name: Pins
properties.description: Children in the area would drop pins in the village wells for luck. Each well had a fairy protector who would know whether a child had not performed the ritual. The following day, children had glass bottles tied around their necks on string; if a child had not dropped a pin, the fairies would break their bottle.
properties.date: Easter Sunday (tradition died out nineteenth century?)

properties.name: Long Haired Woman
properties.description: The long-haired female fairy that followed herds of cattle around the island would be appeased with milk poured into a large rock.
properties.location_name: Brae (Shetland) - Fields in the area, and stone (once?) known as MacQueen's Big Rock

properties.name: Fairy Stone
properties.description: A kelpie crept into the mill at night and used a fairy stone to grind a sack of flour to give to a mortal woman he had taken a liking to. The miller awoke and heading out to his workplace, spotted kelpie leaving the site with the sack of flour over his shoulder. The miller picked up the forgotten fairy stone and lobbed it at the kelpie. The stone struck the kelpie's leg, breaking it, and causing the entity to roll into the River Dee where it drowned.
properties.location_name: Braemar (Aberdeenshire) - Mill (unlikely to stand) near the River Dee

properties.name: Fairy Mine
properties.description: A man crossing the mountain watched a fairy coal mine at work. The operation was taking place in complete silence, and the witness knew that no mortal mine existed in the area.
properties.location_name: Brecon Beacons (Powys) - Exact area unknown, but possibly mountain closest to Aberbeeg

properties.name: Secret Door
properties.description: Once a year, a secret door opens here which will take mortals to the fairy realm. The door was once always open, but after a man stole a fairy flower, access was restricted. The area is also home to an old woman who uses music to lure the weak willed into the lake waters where they drown - once she has killed nine hundred people, she will regain her youth and become mortal.
properties.location_name: Brecon Beacons (Powys) - Llyn Cwm Llwch (somewhere around the area)

properties.name: Hobbes
properties.description: Hobbes lived in a secret cavern under the hill and would travel to a tavern after dark to churn the milk. The entity left the area after his coarse woollen apron was replaced with a cotton one.
properties.location_name: Breedon on the Hill (Leicestershire) - Hill in the area

properties.name: Goat Man
properties.description: An Urisk banished to the mountain by a saint has remained here since and may lurk in a nearby waterfall.
properties.location_name: Bridge of Orchy (Argyll and Bute) - Ben Doran

properties.name: Music in the Air
properties.description: Fairy music was once not uncommon in this area. While last heard in 1939 by a group of school children, one hundred years previous it was said a young girl danced herself to death to the tunes.

properties.name: Market Place
properties.description: The hill and the area around it are said to be a haven for the fairy folk - one man once stumbled upon a fairy market from which he tried to steal gold. By the time he reached home, the coins had changed into pebbles.

properties.name: Water Lovers
properties.description: Fairies in this parish could be seen playing on the surface of a pond and within the plants on the banks.
properties.location_name: Brington (Northamptonshire) - Unnamed pond in the area

properties.name: Little Red Capped Man
properties.description: The author Violet Tweedale reported seeing a twelve centimetre tall man dressed in green, sitting beneath a small plant. He vanished after a minute of her observing him.

properties.name: Dying Cattle
properties.description: A man whose cattle were dying mysteriously was informed by a passing gypsy that his home was blocking a fairy path, and by leaving the doors open at night, the fairies could pass happily pass through. The man did so, and the deaths stopped.
properties.location_name: Brosna (County Kerry) - Former croft at Knockeencreen (currently a cattle shed?)

properties.name: Gnomes
properties.description: Appearing at all times of the day and night, locals have blamed the gnomes and other fairy folk for a multitude of tricks. While several sightings have been reported, none in living memory.

properties.name: Uniformed Pixies
properties.description: These little people, standing a couple of feet tall, wore red military uniforms, as if guarding the burial site. Other people have seen the little people dancing over the dolman.

properties.name: Fairy Battle
properties.description: The town is said to have become a battleground for a fairy war - red fairies with pointed ears engaged little green pixies until the former group were driven out.

properties.name: Fairy Bridges
properties.description: It was said that the Fair Folk danced upon the Fairy Bridges, which are actually naturally formed sea stacks. There is also another formation close by known as the Wishing Chair.
properties.location_name: Bundoran (County Donegal) - Roguey Walk, Tullan Strand

properties.name: Bee Hole Boggart
properties.description: Years before the area was developed, the entity patrolled the area looking for people - it once kidnapped and murdered an old woman, leaving her skin to be discovered on a rose bush.
properties.location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - Area around Burnley Football Club

properties.name: Fairy Hangout
properties.description: Pixies, fairies and other members of the little people are still said to live in and around the hill.

properties.name: Last Home
properties.description: The last fairies in England are said to have resided here, until chased away in the 1900's by archaeologists.

properties.name: Sound of Little People
properties.description: Sounds attributed to goblins have been heard underground in this area.
properties.location_name: Butcombe (Somerset) - Fairy's Toot, aka Nempnett Thrubwell

properties.name: Water Spirit
properties.description: Appearing just before the witness went skinny-dipping, this thirty foot tall water elemental scared the potential swimmer away.

properties.name: Jenny Greenteeth
properties.description: Parents warned their children to stay away from pits in the area, as Jenny Greenteeth would ensure the child would never return home.

properties.name: Music
properties.description: Fairies could once be heard making music under this artificial hill.

properties.name: Dancing
properties.description: People once believed the fairies came out at dusk and celebrated by dancing around a blindfolded midwife (also a fairy) in this field.

properties.name: Teleporting Goats
properties.description: A goat keeper on the island engaged the Ghost Eire team to investigate why three young goats would move between two pens separated by two six-foot walls, on six different nights. The goat keeper speculated it may have been fairies, upset that he had placed an iron roof on the goat shed.
properties.date: Early 2000s, for a five year period
properties.location_name: Cape Clear Island (County Cork) - Goat house

properties.name: Little People
properties.description: Fairies were once said to frequent this locale.
properties.location_name: Capel St Mary (Suffolk) - Old house on edge of woods

properties.name: Milk Snatcher
properties.description: The brownie that took up residence in the MacDonald's home would steal their milk and occasionally create loud noises. The entity had a reputation for hating any Campbells and would also spend time at Largie Castle in Tayinloan.
properties.location_name: Cara Island (Argyll and Bute) - Old MacDonald house

properties.name: Carraig
properties.description: There was a flurry of Leprechaun activity on this mountain after a green suit and several gold coins discovered in 1989. Two years later, 'Leprechaun Whisperer' Kevin Woods claimed have contacted Carraig, elder for the 236 remaining leprechauns in the area. The little people are now protected in the area, and while there is an annual Leprechaun Hunt, the only things found are tiny cauldrons which have been left by the fairy folk themselves.
properties.date: Coins found 1989-2002, hunt occurs second Sunday of May

properties.name: Little People
properties.description: Once a popular place for pixies to frequent and dance, they have not been seen in recent years. They are said to be protecting a concealed hoard of gold. A large rock in the area was said to feature a tiny footprint.

properties.name: Changeling
properties.description: Mary Scannell discovered that her own healthy baby had been replaced by a sickly child which did not stop crying - a changeling! A wise woman told Mary to underfeed the changeling and to beat it. Within a week the new child had gone, and the fairies returned Mary's own baby.
properties.location_name: Castlemartyr (County Cork) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Dancing Folk
properties.description: The round barrow on this site was home to a hundred or so dancing fairies (according to Mary Howitt), with nine harp players to provide music.
properties.location_name: Cauldon Low (Staffordshire) - Ribden Lowe (round barrow on top of a hill)

properties.name: Music
properties.description: On quiet nights, nineteenth century villagers said that fairy music and the patter of little feet could be heard from the surrounding area.

properties.name: Watching Pixies
properties.description: These little fellows use the rocks as a lookout area, the jagged crags forming a natural defence.
properties.location_name: Challacombe (Devon) - Area between village and Chapman's Barrows, locally known as Pixie Rocks

properties.name: Dunnie
properties.description: A troublemaking entity, the Dunnie was said to take the form of a horse and allow itself to be ridden, only to disappear when the rider was above a muddy puddle.

properties.name: The Funny Man
properties.description: Many have complained of being harassed by an unknown force while at the building, reports blaming an ugly little dwarf said to haunt the area, particularly the churchyard.

properties.name: Fairy Home
properties.description: As with Elwin Stream near Melrose, this small body of water was once thought to be the home of the fairies, the circular pebbles within considered elf plates.

properties.name: Elfin Music
properties.description: This site was said to often be filled with the sound of fairy music and singing.
properties.location_name: Chillingham (Northumberland) - Area around the Hurle Stane (aka Hurl Stone)

properties.name: Dwarf
properties.description: This little man is said to stand guard over a fabulous wealth, once concealed from greedy men.

properties.name: Strange Sprite
properties.description: This religious building was due to be built in a slightly different position, but every night a large fairy appeared and moved the walls. The builders finally gave in and finished the construction where the sprite recommended.

properties.name: Little Old Men with Long Beards and Pointy Hats
properties.description: Two girls watched a couple of fairy folk, described as looking like the average garden gnome, dig a hole in the school's playing field.
properties.location_name: Clacton-on-Sea (Essex) - Jaywick's Frobisher Primary School, school field

properties.name: Fairy Music
properties.description: A Bronze Age cemetery, the haunting sound of fiddling heard in this area has led some to say that the music is produced by fairies. A few people who have removed stones from the site are reported have returned them after suffering streaks of bad luck.

properties.name: Dancing Trolls
properties.description: Small troll-like creatures are said to party and play jovial music in this wooded area. They welcome anyone who shows no fear of them.

properties.name: Urisk
properties.description: The cascade of water is named after the human/fairy hybrid that once lived there.
properties.location_name: Clifton (Perth and Kinross) - Eas Na H'urvisg - waterfall

properties.name: Chatterer
properties.description: An entity here was said to keep a former owner, an older woman, company - when the woman sat by the fireplace, the entity would join her and sit by her side. An old newspaper reported a poltergeist outbreak here in 1891, complete with loud knocks and items being thrown around. The 'poltergeist' ceased after a servant was caught moving items around by police, and later confessed to being the source.

properties.name: Dancing Pixies
properties.description: The little people have been seen in this area for many years now, often dancing the night (and day) away.
properties.location_name: Clocaenog Forest (Clwyd) - General area, & Craig Bron Bannog hill

properties.name: Pwca
properties.description: A water spirit, Pwca was once thought to hide out in this deep pool of water located under the waterfall here.

properties.name: Party
properties.description: The little people were said to use this area as a meeting ground.

properties.name: Hidden Fairies
properties.description: Children digging in the area were said to have unearthed a small fairy house - it later vanished, along with their digging equipment, though two small figures were observed in the area for several days afterwards.

properties.name: Fairies Playing around Trees
properties.description: An inhabitant of the road watched a group of fairies dancing around an old tree trunk.

properties.name: Little Man
properties.description: A little man-like figure was found in crawling around in a coal scuttle. The figure was carried out and left on the driveway, never to be seen again.

properties.name: Angry Dwarf
properties.description: This entity, part fairy and part ghost, was seen many times and drove out several families, until exorcised by clergy. The bleeding severed arm was said to be the most noticeable and terrifying feature of the manifestation. Phantom cavaliers were also reported, as were violent poltergeist outbreaks.

properties.name: Fairy Led
properties.description: Local man Jan Brewer found himself lost while walking home, despite walking the route thousands of times previous. While trying to find a familiar landmark, Jan stumbled across dozens of tiny people who formed a circle around him and compelled the man to dance. Remembering a piece of folklore concerning the local piskeys, Jan turned his coat pockets inside out; the piskeys vanished and Jan could see his home a short distance away.

properties.name: Tool Mover
properties.description: Fairies would be blamed when gates were found left open and tools removed from their sheds, only to be found hanging in nearby trees.
properties.location_name: Coolcreedan (County Louth) - Farms in the area

properties.name: Ugly Fairy
properties.description: This little creature, standing no taller than one metre, was seen sitting upon one of the gravestones. It was once seen by two women - one fainted, the other traumatised. A third woman with them saw nothing.

properties.name: Brownie
properties.description: This friendly entity would help keep the building clean and tidy, though it left for good after a servant criticised the quality of its work.

properties.name: Home
properties.description: This scenic route, popular with walkers, is said to be home to fairies.

properties.name: Leprechaun
properties.description: This little fellow wore a red cap, a black jacket, long grey stockings and narrow shoes with turned up toes. His home was in a row of bushes which grew close to the fort. Most people knew not to go near the bushes, but Mr Sullivan decided that they needed a trim. After a few minutes cutting away, he severed the top of his finger, so quit the task.
properties.location_name: Crookstown (County Cork) - Fort in the area

properties.name: Goblins
