The Upsall man said that he had never heard of the place and quickly returned home to dig the treasure up himself. A similar story which is said to have occurred in several places across the UK.
properties.location_name: Upsall (North Yorkshire) - Elder bush near the castle ruins (not known if bush remains)

properties.name: Bird
properties.description: Under this stone is said to be a golden container. A large black bird protected the treasure, scaring away any tomb raiders. There have been reports of unnatural cold spots in the area.
properties.location_name: Verwood (Dorset) - The Verwood Stone (aka Hoarstone, aka Stephen's Stone)

properties.name: Treasure Boat
properties.description: A golden boat with silver oars was once believed to be buried in the area. The craft was used by the Cornish giant Gerennius. The barrow was opened in 1855, and no treasure found.

properties.name: Wandering Saxon
properties.description: A phantom Saxon warrior was once said to wander throughout this area. Sighting ceased after a large hoard of Saxon coins were discovered by a farmer and either sold or taken by the Government.

properties.name: Cardinal
properties.description: Appearing in the dining hall, this former Cardinal was reportedly seen many times drifting around. It is believed that he could stand guard over a hoard of hidden treasure. The writer William Cobbett reported seeing a large grey cat while visiting the abbey, possibly an early Alien Big Cat encounter.

properties.name: Gamekeeper's Treasure
properties.description: Shortly after dying, a gamekeeper would manifest under a tree opposite a set of gates at midnight. A parson was told the story and investigated, visiting the site at night. Calling out for the ghost, the parson was met with instruction to dig at the site. The parson did so, and found a bag filled with coins. The gamekeeper was never seen again.
properties.location_name: Weedon Bec (Northamptonshire) - Tree opposite park lodge gates

properties.name: Treasure
properties.description: This stone, animated by the sound of a cockerel's crow, is said to cover a secret treasure.

properties.name: Buried Treasure
properties.description: The original building which stood here was haunted by three ghostly figures, though they departed after one old lady spoke to them. Before leaving, as a reward for her kindness, the ghosts informed the woman that a wealth of treasure was buried under the farmhouse. Unfortunately, a series of accidents prevented the hoard being discovered, and now the area has been concreted over.

properties.name: Jack o'Legs
properties.description: Jack o'Legs was said to be a very tall man (perhaps fourteen foot tall!) who, armed with bow and arrow, robbed from the rich and gave to the poor. It is also said that a cave somewhere near here may still contain some of his ill-gotten gains.

properties.name: Cerberus
properties.description: This three-headed hound stands guard over a hoard of hidden treasure somewhere on this site.

properties.name: Hideous Bird
properties.description: A cave hidden under the castle contains a vast collection of gold and gems. The wealth is protected by a large bird which never sleeps and attacks any who come too close to discovering the cave.

properties.name: Cromwellian Treasure
properties.description: A Cromwellian soldier stands guard over a hidden hoard of treasure somewhere in the area.

properties.name: Cursed Hoard
properties.description: Somewhere in the village, a fabulous treasure is hidden; searching for it will do little good, however, as anyone who finds it will die.

properties.name: Concealed Treasure
properties.description: Arthur hid much of his loot in this cave, which Merlin then protected with a spell, ensuring the treasure would never be found.

properties.name: Coffin
properties.description: One of the barrows in this area is said to contain a Golden coffin containing a Roman body.

properties.name: Clawed Stone
properties.description: The stone was finally buried by a farmer, who dug a hole next to the boulder and pushed it in. A failed previous attempt had resulted in the Devil appearing who scratched the stone with his claws. The stone was believed to have been placed on top of treasure, however when the stone was finally moved to one said, no treasure (or Devil) was found.
properties.date: Moved to the side of the field in 1970
properties.location_name: Winceby (Lincolnshire) - Boulder near area known as Slash Hollow

properties.name: Wagon
properties.description: The wagon that haunts this spot was supposedly crushed as its owner tried to move aside the heavy monolith located here, to steal the treasure beneath.

properties.name: Coffin
properties.description: It was once thought that a golden coffin was buried here, though a dig in 1858 discovered a lead one containing a Roman body.

properties.name: Golden Coffin
properties.description: Another location said to contain a coffin made of gold, though its location is not really known (nor, for that matter, its existence).

properties.name: Pixie Party
properties.description: A human gatecrasher reported joining a pixie party that was occurring within this barrow - he soon ran off, not wanting to fall under a fairy spell. Another man was said to have picked up a golden coin each day from the barrow, left there for him by a fairy who liked him, although she stopped her act of generosity when the man told others of her. Finally, another story says there is treasure here, although it can never be removed.
properties.location_name: Wold Newton (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Willy Howe barrow

properties.name: Dunny
properties.description: Either a former Border reiver or someone who committed suicide (or maybe both), this entity would manifest as a dun coloured horse and scour the area for his lost treasure.
properties.location_name: Wooler (Northumberland) - Cave in Cockenheugh Hills, and Fowberry Bridge (and occasionally Tower)

properties.name: Golden Table
properties.description: A small team of treasure hunters were searching for a golden table, fabled to have been buried in the area. One man found the top of the table on the barrow, but as he called out to his friends, it sank beneath the ground and could not be rediscovered.
properties.location_name: Wootton Rivers? (Wiltshire) - Barrow near a farm?

properties.name: Guarded Secret Tunnel
properties.description: Leading to an untold amount of wealth, the tunnel hidden in this hillfort is guarded by a pair of snake-like dragons. The ghost of a druid is reported to haunt the site looking for this treasure, and the sound of phantom galloping horses have also been reported. Finally, another legend says the structure was formed by earth falling off the Devil's shovel as he tried to dig a hole in the South Downs.

properties.name: Well Rich
properties.description: An old local poem stated, 'Near the brook of Bell, there is a well, which is richer than any man can tell'.
properties.location_name: Wroxeter (Shropshire) - Buried well near Bell Brook

properties.name: Hidden Gold
properties.description: The hoard of treasure hidden under rocks on the top of this mountain is protected by demonic entities which attack anyone close to discovering the gold.

properties.name: William Nevison
properties.description: The woods were a popular place for Nevison, a highwayman, to lurk before riding out to commit his crimes. After he was hanged in York, his shade returned to the scene of better times.
properties.location_name: Ardwick le Street (South Yorkshire) - Hanging Wood

properties.name: Lady in Brown
properties.description: A previous landlady and her friend observed a woman wearing old fashioned brown clothing walk through the closed front door. The ghost is said to be a woman who lost both her children outside the inn in a coach accident. A highwayman is also reputed to haunt the building.

properties.name: Headless Highwayman
properties.description: This decapitated spectral bandit remains lurking in the shadows of his old ambush area. One version of the story says he remains on his horse.
properties.location_name: Atwick (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads in the area

properties.name: Shadowy Creature
properties.description: Walking through the woods after dusk, a couple found themselves chased by a large shadowy quadruped that stood just over a metre tall. One of the witnesses said it did not move like a deer or a dog. The woods is also reputedly home to a phantom highwayman.

properties.name: Tom Faggus
properties.description: Two people witnessed the ghost of a man riding a large horse in this area. The witnesses carried out some research and believe they had encountered the ghost of Tom Faggus, a Robin Hood-like figure who operated in the area.

properties.name: Veiled Woman
properties.description: A woman with a red veil has been reported here, sometimes accompanied by two male escorts. It has been speculated that one of these men could be a highwayman by the name of William Nevison. Civil war soldiers are also said to haunt the area.
properties.location_name: Batley (West Yorkshire) - Howley Hall ruins, and surrounding golf course

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: Killed in the seventeenth century, this highwayman refused to leave and continued to ride around the area to the annoyance of locals.

properties.name: Man in Tricorn
properties.description: A phantom highwayman is reputed to haunt this road. There is also a story that a ghostly horse and coach have been heard along the Beckhampton Road.

properties.name: Black Tom
properties.description: A nineteenth century highwayman, Black Tom was executed and buried at this junction with a stake driven through his heart to prevent his return. The ritual failed, and both he and another unknown ghost were seen countless times for years following his death. A 1960s sighting of Tom took place in broad daylight, with witnesses stating they saw a gruesome figure with a broken neck staggering down the road, vanishing into thin air seconds later. The witness who spotted Tom during the 1990s thought he was just a drunk man in fancy dress, until he suddenly disappeared.
properties.location_name: Bedford (Bedfordshire) - Junction of Tavistock Street, Union Street and Clapham Road

properties.name: Turpin in Green Velvet
properties.description: Local legend says Dick Turpin's ghost was seen in an upstairs room sitting at a desk, writing with a quill. Another story says a woman in eighteenth century garb also haunts the upper part of the building. The pub hit headlines in September 2013 after it was reported a phantom French Soldier called Barnard scared away two bar staff who were staying overnight.
properties.location_name: Bickley (Kent) - The Chequers public house, Southborough Lane

properties.name: George Lyon
properties.description: Riding on his horse, this former highwayman passes through the village where he once hid out.

properties.name: Murph
properties.description: A former highwayman, Murph can sometimes be heard mumbling and moaning at the bar late at night.
properties.location_name: Blackpool (Lancashire) - Eagle and Child public house

properties.name: Woman in Black
properties.description: Wearing a black crinoline dress, this ghostly woman has been observed on the first floor. One of the cleaning staff spotted a woman in black enter a room, but upon investigation, the room was devoid of life. The hotel is also home to a phantom highwayman who hangs about in the courtyard.

properties.name: Female in Long Dress with Highwayman
properties.description: Sometimes appearing with a horse, the unknown woman and highwayman have been reported a few times over the past two hundred years.
properties.location_name: Blythburgh (Suffolk) - A12 from Ipswich, towards Kessingland, near Blythburgh common

properties.name: Noosed Highwayman
properties.description: This shade, mistakenly hanged for highway robbery, still has the hangman's noose tied around his neck when he appears. They also say his eyes look as if they're ready to pop out of his head. Another report says that the road is also haunted by a fatal car crash from the mid-twentieth century which killed a family - the screech of brakes followed by screams is heard but no accident can be seen.

properties.name: Tom Hoggett
properties.description: This former highwayman, wearing a coat so long it almost drags along the ground, is still thought to lay in wait for the unwary along this stretch of road.
properties.location_name: Boroughbridge (North Yorkshire) - Great North Road (A1), between town and Scotch Corner

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: Roads running in and through the village are reportedly home to several phantom entities. A highwayman, a cavalier, a small group of Tudors have all been reported here.

properties.name: Pocock
properties.description: Pocock, a highwayman, was badly injured in a fight. Pocock managed to escape and hide, only to die of his wounds in a cave which has never been discovered. His ghost has been seen on horseback, curiously galloping on the spot, and has been blamed for several accidents in the area.
properties.location_name: Bridgwater (Somerset) - A39 between Street and Bridgwater

properties.name: Grizelda the Highway Woman
properties.description: Dressed as a male highwayman, this girl has been seen still riding her midnight black horse. She reportedly held up a stagecoach carrying an execution order condemning a friend of hers, which bought him enough time to escape the noose.
properties.date: At the end of every month (reoccurring), last seen 1987

properties.name: Rex Bacon and Friends
properties.description: An investigation by a medium revealed this building was haunted by 24 ghosts (!), one of whom was Mr Rex Bacon, a highwayman from the eighteenth century whose base of operations was the inn.
properties.location_name: Bungay (Suffolk) - Ye Olde Three Tuns public house

properties.name: Whistling
properties.description: A group of friends exploring this area heard a loud, tuneful whistling with no discernible source. One of the witnesses was later told the site was the haunt of a phantom highwayman who would whistle while he worked.
properties.location_name: Calne (Wiltshire) - Area around Black Dog Hill

properties.name: Naked Man
properties.description: A naked man is said to haunt a road in this area. One story says that the figure was a highwayman who stripped and oiled himself up, to ensure that no one could grab him.

properties.name: Whitfield
properties.description: An infamous highwayman, Whitfield's capture resulted in his hanging for murder - but the rope, not tight enough, did not kill Whitfield outright. Several days later a passing coachman heard Whitfield crying and moaning, still dangling from the noose. To end the suffering, the coachman shot the hanged man; Whitfield's ghost can still be heard today crying for help.
properties.location_name: Carlisle (Cumbria) - A6, Barrock Hill near Carlisle

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: This public house was said to be haunted, possibly by the ghost of Dick Turpin, although there is a distinct lack of accounts of any sightings. A headstone which stood in the beer garden most likely added to its spooky atmosphere, as did its location, close to Blue Bell Hill and its infamous vanishing hitchhiker (but more on her another day). One paranormal group claimed to have photographed 'orbs' on this site.
properties.location_name: Chatham (Kent) - Upper Bell Inn (no longer standing)

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A woman out walking in the area reported seeing a phantom highwayman who rode past her - the terrified witness ran back to her family.
properties.location_name: Chingford (Essex) - Area around Richmond Avenue and The Avenue in Highams Park

properties.name: Jenkins Protheroe?
properties.description: A driver and passenger witnessed a dark figure standing in the middle of the road with long arms stretched out in a 'stand and deliver' stance. Several days later the driver found out about the ghost of Jenkins (or Jenkin) Protheroe, a former highwayman hanged in the area.

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A man on horseback, considered to be a highwayman, reportedly haunts this building. It may have been this entity which created the loud heavy footsteps from above which followed one witness along the corridor to the back rooms; when asked, the owners said no one else was updates at the time.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: This cloaked figure has been known to stand by the cross, empty eye sockets watching the road ahead.

properties.name: Clibbon
properties.description: Found guilty of highway robbery, Clibbon was tied to the back of a horse and dragged around the town until he was dead. His screams are sometimes heard. The same area is also home to a headless horse, though it may not be the same creature associated with the highwayman.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: Two friends out sledging spotted a figure on horseback, resembling a highwayman in tutor style clothing, staring at them. The friends were terrified and left the area. They returned the following day and although their sledge marks were visible in the snow, there was no sign of any horse tracks. The area is said to be haunted by Black Dick of the North (aka Sir Richard Beaumont).
properties.location_name: Dewsbury (West Yorkshire) - Cow field, Thornhill Edge

properties.name: Black Dick of the North
properties.description: Known locally as Black Dick's Tower, this building is said to be the home of a phantom highwayman once a year.
properties.location_name: Dewsbury (West Yorkshire) - The Temple, aka Black Dick's Tower

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A horse-mounted highwayman stood on the roadside, looking towards Durham. The legs of the horse vanished at the knee, and the ghost vanishes if approached.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: This sixteenth century inn is said to be home to a phantom highwayman.

properties.name: Jangling Highwayman
properties.description: The sound of chains that can be heard crossing the road as the church bells ring are attributed to a phantom highwayman. He prowled on people using the road between Beccles and Great Yarmouth, and when caught the man was chained and hanged.
properties.location_name: Gillingham (Norfolk) - Road past the War Memorial

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A ghostly highwayman is said to haunt this site, which also contains occasional poltergeist activity.

properties.name: Woman in a Crinoline Gown
properties.description: The history of this building goes back to 1138, and several ghosts have attached themselves to it and the surrounding area. The woman dressed in a crinoline gown drifts silently through the house, while a woman with blonde hair sits in front of the fireplace. This may be the same ghost who rides a white horse down the lane outside. A cavalier has been observed in the lounge, and a phantom monk walks a foot above the ground outside in the garden. Finally, just around the corner of the building the spectre of a highwayman still hangs from a tree.
properties.location_name: Great Holland (Essex) - Treasure Holt - farmhouse in marshes

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A phantom highwayman is said to haunt this pub's cellar.

properties.name: Could be Dick
properties.description: Thought to be Dick Turpin by the witness, no real evidence exists to back this claim up.
properties.location_name: Hastings (Sussex) - The Stag Inn, All Saints Street

properties.name: Dancing Robert
properties.description: The site where the body of Robert Snooks lays, the last highwayman to be hanged in England. Anyone who dances around the grave twelve times will summon him to join the jig.

properties.name: Headless Highwayman
properties.description: This ghostly figure still rides his horse, still seeking his head that was taken from him as punishment for his crimes.
properties.location_name: Hickleton (South Yorkshire) - Area near the church

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A phantom highwayman with his cloak thrown over one shoulder is reputed to haunt this road. The figure disappears as any witness approaches.
properties.date: June / July over several years in 2000s
properties.location_name: Horsford (Norfolk) - Holt Road, north of village

properties.name: Robert the Wizard
properties.description: A highwayman of local fame and folklore, the headless ghost of Robert appeared to a local woman who had recently moved to her new home.

properties.name: Highwaymen
properties.description: A popular place to display the corpses of highway robbers, their shades now walk where the bodies hung.

properties.name: Ned King
properties.description: A highwayman caught at the public house and hanged nearby, Mr King returned post-mortem for several years after the event, knocking on doors and windows late at night.
properties.location_name: Hurst Green (Lancashire) - Punch Bowl Inn (demolished June 2021), and general area

properties.name: Headless Shape on Horseback
properties.description: The Archbishop of Sudbury (or another senior religious figure) was decapitated near to this point by his enemies, the man now returns on dark nights to retrace the route of his death. Another story names the headless horseman as a highwayman, or one of three rebels executed nearby for the murder of the Archbishop.

properties.name: Highwayman in Grey Coat
properties.description: Mounted on horseback, this figure stands in the way of oncoming traffic, though by all accounts only the driver can see him. The last known witness to see the entity only saw the man and not the horse.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A highwayman hanged on gallows that once stood outside this building is still said to make his presence known in the area.

properties.name: Cooper
properties.description: Cooper was a highwayman who, so successful at robbing the rich, died a wealthy man. Cooper was buried with his treasure along the road he committed many of his crimes, and the place post-mortem he continues to dwell.
properties.location_name: Lacey Green (Buckinghamshire) - Coopers Grave (area of road), two stones at the junction of a bridleway

properties.name: Hoggett
properties.description: A highwayman during his life, Hoggett drowned in this pool while running from the law. It is now said that his ghost ensures no one ever swims in it without drowning.
properties.location_name: Langton (North Yorkshire) - Hoggett's Hole, River Swale

properties.name: Suicide
properties.description: Several spooky phenomena have been reported here, including a floating ball of light, a girl in a mop cap, the sound of a gunshot, a faceless highwayman, and food that would jump off a plate in the restaurant. A young man who took his life in the building is blamed for most of the occurrences.

properties.name: Highwayman Jacques
properties.description: This robber was shot by lawmen in room six, and his spirit is said to remain. It is also thought that he can be heard travelling along the roads in the village.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A figure dressed as a highwayman has been observed in the garden of the building and crossing an old bridge.
properties.date: Unknown, but said to appear around dusk
properties.location_name: Little Abington (Cambridgeshire) - The Old Cottage and nearby bridge

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: The phantom highwayman reputedly haunting this pub was hanged from a nearby tree.
properties.location_name: Little Marlow (Buckinghamshire) - Crooked Billet public house

properties.name: Dick Turpin Followed by a Ghost
properties.description: Turpin's ghost is said to ride down this hill, with a ghost of an old woman hanging onto his back. In life, Turpin tortured her until the woman told him where she had hidden her money, so now her ghost haunts his.
properties.date: Three times a year (exact dates not known)

properties.name: Frozen Nick
properties.description: The area is said to become so cold at night that the Devil died of exposure and is buried under a hill. Phantom highwaymen are also said to ride around the countryside.

properties.name: Gilbert
properties.description: Gilbert, an eighteenth century highwayman who tried to rob a coach, was stabbed to death by one of the occupants after she recognised him as having killed her brother. The killing drove the woman mad. The scene was said to silently re-enact every Christmas Eve.

properties.name: Catherine Ferrers
properties.description: Seen riding her horse topless, and swinging around tree branches without a stitch on, the ghost of the highwaywoman Ferrers continues to create the same amount of havoc that she did whilst living.
properties.location_name: Markyate Cell (Hertfordshire) - Area around the priory

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: The ghost of a highwayman is said to ride through the area and is more likely to be heard than seen. The highwayman is said to follow the route of a road that no longer exists.

properties.name: Hooves
properties.description: The sound of hooves heard passing this way are thought to belong to the horse of highwayman. Galloping Dick, who lived locally.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This phantom rider is named as local highwayman Galloping Dick.

properties.name: Black Bess
properties.description: A former hideout of the highwayman Dick Turpin, both he and his dark horse were once seen in the area - even after his death.
properties.location_name: Milton Keynes (Buckinghamshire) - Woughton on the Green, general area

properties.name: Maxi Major
properties.description: The highwayman Maxi Major is said to have been buried at Hunter's Hill - he can sometimes still be heard in the area.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A phantom highwayman would ride around this pit before heading towards Callow Spong (where he would vanish). The pit itself was said to be the home of the Devil, who dragged one man down into its waters after he tried to fish out a chest of coins.
properties.location_name: Moulton St Mary (Norfolk) - Callow (or Caller) Pit

properties.name: Humphrey Kynaston
properties.description: Wild Humphrey Kynaston, a local highwayman who robbed the rich to give to the poor, is said to haunt the building where his chair remains.
properties.location_name: Nesscliffe (Shropshire) - Old Three Pigeons public house

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: This phantom figure (who looks like Dick Turpin) stands at the end of the bar and has been mistaken for a paying customer. A ghostly woman in black reportedly walks down the staircase, while bottles and glasses fall from the shelves without just cause.

properties.name: Landlord
properties.description: Unable to handle the high pressure role of running the pub, this man took his own life - the shade remains in the building. A highwayman is also suspected of haunting the inn. The caves under the pub are said to be haunted by a young child, a four year old girl.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: One version of this tale says that, wearing a jacket with sleeves of bright crimson, a phantom highwayman lurks along the roadside with a tricorn hat hiding the face. Another version names him as Dick Turpin and when last seen, the phantom rode his horse across common land. It may have been the same entity seen in 1979, reduced to a shimmering shape which swirled like water. The witness to this event had an accident close to the spot later in the day.
properties.location_name: Nuneaton (Warwickshire) - A5 between town and Hinckley (Watling Street)

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: Thought to be none other than Dick Turpin, this ghostly robber rides around the heath after dusk on his jet black horse. Another ghost on the common land is that of a nineteenth century Member of Parliament who swallowed poison nearby; his shade can be identified by its top hat. A third ghost was spotted by two boys out playing on the heath, who reported watching an old man silently digging a hole. When they returned to the spot the following day, the ground was undisturbed. In 2005 a man encountered a large, winged creature which tore off the tops of trees as it passed overhead.
properties.date: Sighting of ghosts unknown, cryptid in 2005

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: The common was said to be one of the homes to the ghostly form of Dick Turpin.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Barnet (Greater London) - Finchley Common (no longer exists, now suburbs)

properties.name: Turpin and Bess
properties.description: Reportedly once owned by Dick Turpin's grandfather, the highwayman would visit the family when he needed to hide out. His ghostly form has been seen both inside and out, sometimes accompanied by his horse.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Enfield (Greater London) - Rose & Crown public house, Clay Hill

properties.name: Heavy Breathing
properties.description: This ghost was heard several times, breathing heavily around female members of staff, but never being seen. A second entity wears a bowler hat and has been known to ask about the whereabouts of his briefcase - both case and man were in an aircraft that crashed in the late 1940s, killing all on board. A phantom Dick Turpin rides a brown horse across the runways (or cargo sheds), while cleaning staff during the 1970s reported a presence which held them down, applying pressure around their throats. Finally, prior to the airport being built, a phantom Saxon prince reputedly haunted the area close to a pond where he drowned.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Heathrow Airport (Greater London) - General area

properties.name: Water Spirit
properties.description: This destructive entity, named as a highwayman hanged just across the road, is blamed for turning on taps and smashing barrels of beer.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Hounslow (Greater London) - Bell Inn

properties.name: Dick
properties.description: The highwayman Dick Turpin, who is just as busy in death as he was in life, is reported to haunt this area of urban heathland.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Hounslow (Greater London) - Hounslow Heath

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: This figure, complete with dark clothing and cape, has been observed loitering around the cellar doors.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Hounslow (Greater London) - Hussars public house

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: Once the haunt of footpads and robbers, the woodland in the area is still said to be home to a ghostly highwayman prowling the area.
properties.location_name: Oxney Bottom (Kent) - Woodland in the area

properties.name: Dying Highwayman
properties.description: A large spectral tree has been seen here, together with a highwayman pinned to it by several swords. On other occasions, an elderly phantom woman is spotted; thought to be a gypsy woman, she accidentally set herself on fire with her pipe while sleeping.

properties.name: Hanging Highwayman
properties.description: This unpleasant footpad murdered a serving girl in the pub and was hanged in the yard for the crime. His shade still ensures he is not forgotten.
properties.location_name: Pocklington (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Feathers Inn

properties.name: Tom King
properties.description: The sound of hooves heard several times from outside the public house are thought to belong to the horse of Tom King, a local highwayman who once frequented the bar.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: Buried under the gibbet that housed his body for many days, the highwayman is said not to have completely left this earth. . .

properties.name: William Boulter
properties.description: A highwayman by trade, Boulter was hanged for his troubles. His phantom is said to run along various parts of the A30.

properties.name: Highwayman's Girlfriend
properties.description: This woman was murdered by a jealous partner after he discovered that she was having an affair with a highwayman; the ghostly lady in white now walks the staircase and the first floor.

properties.name: Highwaymen
properties.description: This inn was built where a much older inn once stood. The latter building was demolished soon after three men were arrested within for a murder and highway robbery. It is said that one of the robbers tried to repent but was hanged regardless. The entity is now said to make his presence known by throwing things around the room.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: Wearing the clothing of a robber, this figure was seen lurking in the shadows prior to mysteriously disappearing.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Mail Coach public house, 149 West Street

properties.name: Headless on Headless
properties.description: The headless highwayman seen around this area rides a headless horse. A road on a hill south of the town is haunted by a woman (who may also be headless) wearing black, who quickly vanishes just before reaching a hill.
properties.location_name: Skipsea (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads around town

properties.name: Flagging Figure
properties.description: Several motorists (and a vicar on his bike) encountered a figure who stood on the roadside flagging down travellers, only to vanish seconds later. More generally, the roads in the area where once said to be haunted by the ghost of Dick Turpin.
properties.location_name: South Mimms (Hertfordshire) - Unnamed road in the area

properties.name: Sobbing
properties.description: The sound of a lady's gentle sobs has been heard diffusing through the fabric of the pub. The common outside the pub is home to a highway woman on horseback, after whom the building is named.
properties.location_name: St Albans (Hertfordshire) - Wicked Lady public house

properties.name: Murdered Landlord
properties.description: The shade seen here is thought to be a former landlord murdered on the site, along with his wife. Another story has the highwayman Claude Duval as haunting the site.

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: It was once believed that the ghost of Dick Turpin passed by this inn on Wednesday nights - the thud of horse's hooves could be heard, and some people reported seeing a man on horseback just outside of the building.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A ghostly highwayman was observed by one person at a site along this road.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A highwayman, some say Dick Turpin himself, hid some of his takings in this deserted medieval village. He died before managing to retrieve the money, and now he rides towards the village on horseback.

properties.name: Highwayman with Horse
properties.description: Appearing under the light of the full moon, the ghostly highwayman on horseback is reported to say, 'Your money or your life', and other such cliche expressions.

properties.name: John Alcock
properties.description: Highwayman John Alcock stashed much of his ill-gotten gains in a cave under this hill, in a chest protected by three locks and guarded by a cockerel. The bird will only let treasure hunters pass if they possessed one of Alcock's bones. Another legend says the Devil created the hill; out picking nuts on Devil's Nutting Day (21 September), Old Nick stumbled across the Virgin Mary who ordered him to drop the nuts. The Devil did, thus creating the hill.
properties.location_name: Temple Grafton (Warwickshire) - Alcocks Arbour (aka Rolls Wood Hill), Croft Lane

properties.name: Swift Nick
properties.description: An old highwayman, Swift Nick has a habit of relighting fires which have gone out hours beforehand.

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A phantom highwayman has been sighted in this village.

properties.name: Actor or Highwayman?
properties.description: This tall, dark stranger has often been observed standing in the shadows, his face obscured to make identification impossible, though it is speculated he is either a highwayman or a thespian.
properties.location_name: W1 (Greater London) - Sheppeys Restaurant, Shepherd Market

properties.name: Jack Diamond
properties.description: Jack Diamond was a highwayman - his ghost, and maybe those of others, have been seen here on the unluckiest dates in the calendar.

properties.name: Gladys Saxton
properties.description: Gladys Saxton and her maid Margaret Harrison were killed in a fire at the building in 1945. Since that date, a woman wearing a long black dress has been seen by guests at the building. There is also a report of a phantom highwayman being spotted standing in a doorway.

properties.name: Hiding Highwayman
properties.description: Said to have been seen hiding in the trees around the ruins, the highwayman's target can occasionally be heard galloping up the nearby road. A black soldier in a red uniform has also been reported from the churchyard, and a phantom monk has been observed moving above the ground on nearby private land.

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: The ghost of a highwayman, thought to be Dick Turpin, haunts the Bury Lane approach towards the Old Swan Inn that once acted as one of his many hideouts.
properties.location_name: Woughton on the Green (has also mistakenly been called 'Wroughton') (Buckinghamshire) - Bury Lane

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: This figure wearing a tricorn hat appears to be pacing along the roadside, as if waiting for someone to join him.
properties.location_name: Wroughton on the Green (Buckinghamshire) - Road through the village

properties.name: Highwayman
properties.description: A driver returning home from work reported passing a figure on horseback, dressed in highwayman's clothing, stood by the wall of the pub. When the witness looked in his rear view mirror, the figure had vanished. There is a local belief that the highwayman is Dick Turpin, and sightings of the highwayman are said to go back to the mid-twentieth century. A sandstone cave in the area is said to have been one of Turpin's hideouts.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Four All's public house, A64

properties.name: Box Carriage
properties.description: Travelling home in 2007, a driver watched a horse drawn box carriage cross the road around 100 metres in front of him. The witness reported that he could not make out too many details as it was raining hard, though he was able to see a dim lantern towards the front of the carriage. As the driver reached the spot where he had seen the vehicle pass, he realised there was no side road where the coach and horses could have crossed. Another witness in the 1960s spotted a female figure with long hair, wearing a white dress, standing by the roadside. The witness stopped to see if she was lost or needed a lift, but she had vanished. Mentioning the encounter to a work colleague the following day, the colleague described the figure which was encountered and said the area was haunted.
properties.location_name: A31 (Surrey) - Hogs Back, Guildford bound. 1 mile Northeast of Tongham junction

properties.name: Cloaked Gentleman
properties.description: One of several ghosts reported to haunt the house, the cloaked figure disrupted a video unit sent to shoot several TV scenes here to such an extent, the crew refused to work after dark. The other ghosts comprise of a phantom horseman and two spectral women, one of whom appears before a death. The building is also thought to have once contained part of the Holy Grail.

properties.name: Coach with Headless Driver
properties.description: Racked with guilt after deliberately running over his daughter's lover in 1780, this man now continues to drive his coach and horses down the lane, though he has lost his head since death.
properties.location_name: Abington (Northamptonshire) - Pig Lane (lane may no longer exist or has been renamed?)

properties.name: Middle Aged Man
properties.description: Passing the turn off to Halvergate towards Acle, a driver watched as a middle aged man walked out into the middle of the road (from the right hand side) in the path of their car. The figure turned to look at the driver, and the car passed straight through him. The area is traditionally known to be haunted by a horse and cart.
properties.location_name: Acle (Norfolk) - Heading west into Acle from Halvergate turnoff

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: Midnight, at an unspecified date, and the park gates are said to burst open, letting in a ghostly carriage pulled by four horses which race to the area known as the Nursery Corner. Local legend states it is here that once a vicious battle took place between Romans and Britons.
properties.date: Unknown date, but appears at midnight

properties.name: Pony
properties.description: A white pony silently galloped past two men as they returned to the village. It has been speculated that the pony was King Arthur's mount Sigral (ridden at the Battle of Baydon) or belonging to a local man who committed suicide.
properties.location_name: Aldbourne (Wiltshire) - Road leading to the village from Braydon

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Possibly the same entity that haunts the park, a coach and team of horses are said to erratically travel along the road which skirts the woodland.

properties.name: Crump
properties.description: Sir John Dutton, nicknamed Crump because of his hunched back, is said to drive a phantom coach and horses under the gates to the park. A white stag may also haunt the parkland.

properties.name: Dreadful Eyes
properties.description: A few entities were said to have made their homes here, including a black dog with dreadful eyes, a ghostly horseman, and a wagon containing a coffin pulled by four phantom horses.

properties.name: Mr Rimmington
properties.description: The ghost of Rimmington reportedly takes several forms, including a man on horseback accompanied by two hounds, and the slightly less intimidating form of a robin.
properties.location_name: Almondbury (West Yorkshire) - Woodsome Hall golf course

properties.name: Sinking Coach
properties.description: A coach pulled by a team of four horses which sunk into the pond drowning all on board is seen repeating the tragic accident once a year. In addition, a stone wolfhound which stands by the gateway is said to have once been flesh and blood - the dog gave a warning to an advancing sneak attack upon the castle before turning into rock. It now safeguards the Clotworthy family name on the condition it is never removed. Finally, the sound of heavy breathing can be heard coming from the ruined building.

properties.name: Stable Lad
properties.description: Murdered to prevent the continuation of an affair of the heart, this young stablehand was then buried at a crossroads to conceal the crime. His ghost is reported to still travel around on horseback.
properties.location_name: Arbigland (Dumfries and Galloway) - Roads in the area

properties.name: Richard Costen
properties.description: Richard died trying to escape from the law for a crime he did not commit. He can be heard screaming, choked to death on the reins of his horse. The spectral Jeremiah Coghlan reportedly rides on the back of a white horse - if seen, the viewer will die within a year. Finally, Sir Francis Prendergast, a psychotic murderer, stalks nearby woodland.

properties.name: Michael
properties.description: Michael Morey murdered his grandson and, trying to hide the crime, set fire to the body. Morey's deeds were soon discovered, and his executed remains displayed on the gibbet that stood upon the barrow. At midnight, his ghost on horseback is said to ride three times around the barrow, crying out 'Michael Morey's Hump'.
properties.date: Said to happen at midnight, last sighting unknown
properties.location_name: Arreton (Isle of Wight) - Barrow known as Michael Morey's Hump

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: No longer seen since extensive roadwork, a ghostly horseman once ran around the area.

properties.name: Pale Horse
properties.description: In the process of demolishing the hotel, workmen reported encountering a phantom pale horse and hearing disembodied footsteps walking along empty corridors.
properties.location_name: Ascot (Berkshire) - Royal Ascot Hotel (no longer standing - site now a roundabout along the Bracknell Road)

properties.name: Carriage
properties.description: A mother and daughter encountered a carriage being drawn by two white horses. A woman in a crinoline dress sat within the vehicle, while the driver wore an old fashioned cravat.
properties.location_name: Ascot (Berkshire) - Woodlands Ride, heading into the forest

properties.name: Night Coach
properties.description: The rector wrote that he had been awoken by the sounds of galloping and opened his eyes just in time to see a phantom coach and a team of horses pass through his bedroom. It was said that the phantom occurrence had also been experienced by previous rectors, and that the rectory was built on top of an old road.

properties.name: Old Man in Green
properties.description: This figure that appeared in one of the bedrooms looked real, though when a witness tried to grab him, he passed through the figure. Described as short, and looking like a tramp, the ghost had what appeared to be a slashed throat. The nearby fields are haunted by the sounds of galloping horses.

properties.name: Swimming Horseman
properties.description: Riding towards the park house, this phantom horseman veers off at the last minute and enters the nearby lake. A white lady haunts the house itself, seen by porters on the night shift. In the seventeenth century, the Earl of Winchelsea cut down several oak trees, bestowing a curse on his family which took the life of his wife and son shortly after.
properties.location_name: Ashford (Kent) - Eastwell Park and Manor House

properties.name: Lord Lonsdale
properties.description: Driving a phantom coach, the ghost of Lord Lonsdale passes through this location.
properties.location_name: Askham (Cumbria) - Area around St Peter's Church

properties.name: White Army
properties.description: A local vicar, while sitting by the river, watched a line of white troops pass accompanied by white horses - he wrote that it took over an hour for the column to pass.

properties.name: Headless Highwayman
properties.description: This decapitated spectral bandit remains lurking in the shadows of his old ambush area. One version of the story says he remains on his horse.
properties.location_name: Atwick (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads in the area

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A driver was forced to brake hard when a horse and rider emerged from a hedge on one side of the road and crossed in front of him. The apparition then vanished into a wall on the opposite side of the road.

properties.name: Burning Horse
properties.description: A horse and rider, both bathed in flame, are said to haunt this area.
properties.location_name: Aylesford (Kent) - Area around the White Horse Stone

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: Woman on Horseback
properties.description: Riding through the forest, the woman travels either to or from her former lover's home - on her last mortal journey her husband intercepted her mid-way and murdered the adulteress.

properties.name: Pony with Coffin
properties.description: A phantom pony with a coffin strapped to its back is doomed to make the same journey over and over. Both pony and pack were lost during a funeral procession that became caught in a freak snap snowstorm.
properties.location_name: Bakerstead (Cumbria) - Road passing the village from Burnmoor

properties.name: Mrs Boulton
properties.description: A woman who visited the house claimed to have been there before, though only in her dreams. To her surprise, the owner of the property agreed, saying that her visitor resembled a ghost that had been seen there several times! Two more traditional ghosts can be found here; seen approaching the house on horseback, a spectral rider dismounts before vanishing on the driveway, while a scruffy little man has been seen near the front gate.

properties.name: No Ploughing
properties.description: A farmer who tried to plough this sacred land died after falling and banging his head. The two horses drawing the plough died the following day.

properties.name: Hearse
properties.description: A man returning home after visiting the local public house was said to have encountered a phantom horse and cart containing a hearse.
properties.location_name: Ballylusk (County Laois) - Roads around an old burial ground

properties.name: Coachman
properties.description: A phantom coach and four rides around the area. The ghostly driver will ask anyone he spots 'is the bridge at Glarryford still up?' - any answer the witness gives will ensure that they will die within a year (so I suggest looking at your feet and saying nothing).

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: A robber escaping from a house rode into a thin piece of wire tightly pulled between the gateposts here, losing his head in the process. His decapitated shade is either seen or heard at Halloween, still on horseback.
properties.location_name: Ballymena (County Antrim) - Road leading to the White Gates, Crebilly Road area

properties.name: Woman on Horseback
properties.description: A phantom woman on a white horse once rode around this area. She is said to have given local man Owen Kelly a lift after he failed to recognise her as a spirit - after riding around in circles and crossing the river three times she vanished, leaving Owen by the fence of his farmland.
properties.location_name: Bandon (County Cork) - Roads in the area

properties.name: Horse and Carriage
properties.description: Moving towards the hall at midnight, the history of this ghostly horse and carriage is unknown.

properties.name: Headless Man
properties.description: Having hanged himself, this headless gentleman is reported to still drive his horse and trap around the neighbourhood. One witness heard a horse passing close to her late at night, but nothing could be seen.
properties.date: Unknown, but may have been heard 17 October 2009, around 22:30h

properties.name: Lady Fane
properties.description: Local stories said that Lady Fane haunted her former home, though no recent witnesses exist. While a woman staying here did encounter a phantom young girl during the Second World War, the entity wore a raggedy green dress - surely unsuitable attire for a lady? Another legend places the Mistletoe Bough tale as occurring on the site.

properties.name: Eloping Elizabeth
properties.description: The phantom coach and horses reputed to haunt this area contain Elizabeth Linley and Richard Brinsley Sheridan as they elope time after time.

properties.name: Knights
properties.description: A phantom knight on horseback appears on the anniversary of the battle, while other figures have been seen more sporadically. The ground is also said to bleed after a storm.
properties.location_name: Battle (Sussex) - Site of the 1066 Battle of Hastings

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Condemned to drive his coach and four horses along this lane, a wicked landowner haunts this area.
properties.location_name: Beaminster (Dorset) - Lane leading to Stoke Abbott

properties.name: Rider with Large Hat
properties.description: This man on horseback is described as wearing a large hat and possessing silver spurs. Some stories say that various people have engaged the man in conversation, not realising he was a ghost until he vanished.
properties.location_name: Bearsted (Kent) - Road leading to Pilgrim's Way

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: Christmas Eve is marked by the arrival of a coach driven by a headless horseman and pulled by headless horses, which travel down the driveway and vanish as they arrive by the front door. Close by, an oak known as Nelson's Tree is reputed to have been used as gallows and is haunted by a woman in white, although it is not clear whether she was one of the tree's victims or mourns the loss of a loved one. In the hall itself, on a wall within in a bedroom cupboard, there is the imprint of the Devil's hoof branded into solid brick. Finally, another tale says there is a window at the hall which always opens itself, even if locked shut.

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A ghostly figure riding a white horse was spotted galloping across this field.

properties.name: Man in Tricorn
properties.description: A phantom highwayman is reputed to haunt this road. There is also a story that a ghostly horse and coach have been heard along the Beckhampton Road.

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: Galloper Thompson
properties.description: A member of the Thompson family who loved horses died and took to galloping along the Avenue after dark (the horse taken from the family stable and normally found exhausted the following day). The haunting ceased after the ghost, nicknamed 'Galloper Thompson', was caught in a bottle and tossed into the sea.
properties.location_name: Belfast (County Antrim) - Alexandra Park Avenue and Shore Road

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: Three girls unable to sleep in their dorm one night all heard the thunderous sound of horses and the clanking of metal pass through the room. One witness said they were so terrified they did not dare open their eyes.

properties.name: Jocelyn Percy
properties.description: Sir Jocelyn has been reported driving his coach and four headless horses around the streets of this otherwise quiet neighbourhood. Another version of the legend says he flies above the street, pausing briefly over an unnamed house.
properties.location_name: Beverley (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads in the village

properties.name: George Lyon
properties.description: Riding on his horse, this former highwayman passes through the village where he once hid out.

properties.name: Lady
properties.description: A ghostly white lady haunted this house before it burnt down. One room was transported to Pace College, Manhattan, and is said to contain a ghost (whether it is the white lady is unknown). Another piece of local folklore says a hawthorn tree on the grounds blossoms red flowers at midnight on Christmas Eve before quickly reverting to its previous state.
properties.date: Pre-1924 (white lady), hawthorn tree on Christmas Eve (reoccurring)
properties.location_name: Binfield (Berkshire) - Billingbear House, and hawthorn tree (which may no longer exist)

properties.name: Galloping Horses
properties.description: A woman waiting for the bus watched a herd of horses stampede close by, but no one else had seen them. A few years later she discovered someone else had also, more recently, seen the herd in the same area.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This lane is a favoured haunt for the black shuck. The same lane is reportedly haunted by a ghostly horse and carriage.

properties.name: Doggett
properties.description: A corrupt manservant who stole thousands of pounds from his employer, Doggett finally killed himself, and now drives his phantom horse and carriage along this area. One local story says he returned as a vampire; after his body was exhumed many years after his death (from St Mary's Church in Tarrant Gunville) it was found to be uncorrupted, with a rosy tint to the cheeks.
properties.location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Area near Eastbury Park (no longer standing)

properties.name: Anne Boleyn
properties.description: To mark the anniversary of her death, a coach pulled by four headless horses pulls up on the driveway, with a decapitated Anne on a seat, her head in her lap. After Anne's headless white ghost arrives, it is said then to climb out, before inspecting each room of the Hall (the place of her birth and childhood). On the same date, Sir Thomas Boleyn is said to drive a team of headless horses in this area, cursed to cross twelve Norfolk bridges (including those at Aylsham, Coltishall and Wroxham).

properties.name: Duelling Knights with Fiery Swords
properties.description: Returning to this realm once a year, the knights are said to be Sir Thomas Boleyn (father of Anne) and Sir Thomas Paston. Their shades battle with burning swords, and Lady Anne watches from a nearby carriage, pulled by headless horses.
properties.location_name: Blofield (Norfolk) - Path leading from Blofield church to St Michael's at Braydeston

properties.name: Stomping Horse
properties.description: The building was once haunted by the sounds of a horse stamping its feet and whinnying loudly. When an equine skeleton was found under the house during extension work, it was reburied in a nice area of the grounds; the ghost no longer troubled the household.

properties.name: Female in Long Dress with Highwayman
properties.description: Sometimes appearing with a horse, the unknown woman and highwayman have been reported a few times over the past two hundred years.
properties.location_name: Blythburgh (Suffolk) - A12 from Ipswich, towards Kessingland, near Blythburgh common

properties.name: Black Toby
properties.description: A drummer in the army, one night Toby became so drunk he left the local public house to rape and murder Anne Blakemore. Or so the story goes. In reality, Anne's body displayed no indication of foul play, but the mob wanted justice, so Toby was hanged. It was said that Toby's ghost drove a coach pulled by four black horses through the area, with other people stating that he just walked along banging his drum in regret. It is most likely the ghost of Toby was created by local smugglers to deter others from investigating their activities.

properties.name: Cursed Stone
properties.description: When the waters are low, an inscribed stone can be seen in the river - it is believed cursed, and anyone who meddles with it is doomed. Adding to the treachery of the waters, a white horse is said to patrol the river, looking for people to drown.

properties.name: The Devil and the Cobbler
properties.description: Built by a cobbler who made a deal with Old Nick and went back on his word, the house became haunted by the man and the devil. A miller who lived nearby was losing money due to his proximity to the location and asked Parson Corker to banish both entities. The parson brought some friends, set up his magic circle and after a mighty battle (involving a storm which levelled most trees in the area) banished the Devil. The ghost proved more difficult to banish, and in the end the house was demolished.
properties.location_name: Bosava (?) (Cornwall) - House no longer standing, location not known

properties.name: First World War Soldier
properties.description: This incorporeal soldier of Indian heritage appears here once a year to help himself to a drink of water. There are also a few reports of phantom horses and carriages being seen outside the building, while a ghostly cat is also said to haunt several of the town hall rooms.

properties.name: Roman Battle
properties.description: Headless horses can be seen, and fierce fighting can be heard - the area was the site of a battle between Roman legionnaires and the Britons.

properties.name: Crusader
properties.description: Buried at the nearby church, this knight materialises at the hall (by a portrait) and rides a spectral mare to the graveyard to ensure his body has not been disturbed. He also sometimes appears in the church itself.

properties.name: Horse and Rider
properties.description: A father and daughter driving home from school watched as an image of a horse and rider emerged from a hedge and crossed the road before disappearing.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: A coach that crashed into the river, killing the horse and driver, is said to periodically repeat the accident. Another report says that the coach overturned into a boggy area.
properties.location_name: Bradford Peverell (Dorset) - Road west of village, near River Frome, leading towards Muckleford

properties.name: Margaret Vernon
properties.description: Margaret lost her mind after her lover married another woman - her shade is seen leaving the hall on horseback, riding off to the church where her boyfriend wed.

properties.name: The 'Galloping Ghost'
properties.description: Several people have heard horse's hooves pass close by to them in this area but have seen nothing. This could be the same entity as a roman soldier seen riding a horse near the site of a long gone roman castle.
properties.location_name: Bradwell on Sea (Essex) - St Peter's on the Wall church area

properties.name: Galloping Figure
properties.description: The cottage was once home to a phantom figure and another ghostly entity which galloped on horseback through locked gates and headed towards the church.

properties.name: Gypsy and Horse
properties.description: A crossroads near to the woods is haunted by an old female traveller and her aging mare.

properties.name: Funeral
properties.description: A phantom horse drawn funeral procession occasionally passes by the area.
properties.location_name: Bratton (Wiltshire) - Unknown road in the area

properties.name: Sir Thomas Boleyn
properties.description: This bridge is said to be one of several which Sir Thomas passes over in his coach on the anniversary of his daughter's death.

properties.name: Coach and Six
properties.description: James Carnegie, a former laird at the hall, was well versed in the black arts - this has resulted in his soul being doomed to travel in a black coach pulled by dark horses for the rest of eternity.
properties.location_name: Brechin (Angus) - Kinnaird Castle and surrounding area

properties.name: Sir Thomas Boleyn Drives Headless Horses
properties.description: The father of Anne Boleyn is said to be cursed with driving his coach over forty (or eleven) Norfolk bridges. One of the passing points is Breckles Hall, where the spectre once scared a poacher to death (another version states that the poacher was taken by the Devil because of his constant law breaking). Locals say that anyone who views the coach is dragged down to hell.

properties.name: Grey Horse
properties.description: Home of a Welsh water horse, this creature who tempt people to sit on it before terrifying the rider by dragging them over rough land and through the air.

properties.name: Huntsman
properties.description: Riding across the moorland with a pack of hounds, this phantom huntsman once ensured no one strayed onto his lands at night. The apparition was last heard by two friend who were unaware of the legend.

properties.name: Young Man
properties.description: This lone figure on horseback slowly heads towards the pond where he vanishes. He was a local man thought to have drowned in the pond's waters, though his body never found.

properties.name: Pocock
properties.description: Pocock, a highwayman, was badly injured in a fight. Pocock managed to escape and hide, only to die of his wounds in a cave which has never been discovered. His ghost has been seen on horseback, curiously galloping on the spot, and has been blamed for several accidents in the area.
properties.location_name: Bridgwater (Somerset) - A39 between Street and Bridgwater

properties.name: Squire Lighte
properties.description: This building was once believed to be haunted by the spirit of a squire who took his own life on the property. His ghost is also seen riding a horse towards the nearby river.

properties.name: Heavy Cart Wheels
properties.description: From their home a witness heard heavy cartwheels and horses' hooves on the road outside, but nothing could be seen. The witness was later told that the road was haunted by a horse and cart which still travelled to the Britannia Brewery, which closed in 1924.

properties.name: Knight in Armour
properties.description: Appearing on horseback, this knight clad in armour appears around the church neighbourhood.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Carrying its occupants to a ball, the coach was caught up in a storm that washed away Able Bridge - the coach plunged into the water, killing all the passengers. The event is heard once a year after sunset, marking the anniversary of the tragedy.
properties.location_name: Brimpton (Berkshire) - Lane running through village, leading to Able Bridge

properties.name: Sounds of Horses
properties.description: The sounds of horse hooves have been heard moving around the house at night - at one time the property was a stable. The sitting room has experienced sharp falls in temperature, accompanied by strange smells.

properties.name: Decapitated Horseman
properties.description: Ashton Court is best known for its ghostly headless horseman, though there are also reports of grey ladies and a phantom hound.

properties.name: Young Girl on Horseback
properties.description: This female jockey broke her neck in a fall while attempting to ride down the hill - her shade, still on horseback, has been seen since her death.
properties.location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - Monument on a grassy hill next to the M32

properties.name: Shocked Lady
properties.description: Killed by a sudden bolt of lightning while out horse riding, this phantom can still be seen galloping by.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: Students have reported seeing a headless horseman patrolling this area late at night.
properties.location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - University of Bristol and Tyndall, Avenue area

properties.name: Funeral Procession
properties.description: A hearse pulled by four black horses, followed by a large crowd of people, is said to head into the village, though the procession suddenly vanishes before reaching the church.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: The sounds of a coach being pulled by horse can be heard in the hall here - the building was moved and rebuilt in the nineteenth century, with the hall being placed where a road once lay. A phantom woman also reportedly roams the building.

properties.name: Dullahan
properties.description: Riding headless on a black horse, the Dullahan are said to be bearers of ill-omen. The entity carries his severed, decaying head in his right hand. Wherever the Dullahan stops, it is said that a mortal will shortly die on that spot.
properties.location_name: Bryansford (County Down) - Hill between Bryansford and Moneyscalp

properties.name: Aunt Charlotte
properties.description: The shade of Aunt Charlotte was so prevalent the first building here was pulled down and rebuilt solely to rid the living of her ghost. Charlotte may never have been seen again, but a phantom horse and carriage now haunt the drive.
properties.location_name: Bryanston (Dorset) - Portman (aka Bryanston) House, currently the school

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: The origins of this phantom coach and team of horses is unclear.
properties.location_name: Buckland Newton (Dorset) - Park Lane, between village and Glanvilles Wootton (aka Wootton Glanville)

properties.name: Lady Bolingbroke
properties.description: After her husband escaped to France with another woman, Lady Bolingbroke (nee Frances Winchcombe) died of a broken heart. Her ghost has been reported at the manor house, while the streets of the village are home to her coach.
properties.location_name: Bucklebury (Berkshire) - Manor House, and general area

properties.name: Riders
properties.description: A policeman returning home after a late shift spotted a small group of horsemen dressed in Cavalier-style clothing riding along this road. He drove past the figures three times to prove to himself that they were not a figment of his imagination.
properties.location_name: Buckstones (West Yorkshire) - A640, near the Nont Sarah public house

properties.name: Grizelda the Highway Woman
properties.description: Dressed as a male highwayman, this girl has been seen still riding her midnight black horse. She reportedly held up a stagecoach carrying an execution order condemning a friend of hers, which bought him enough time to escape the noose.
properties.date: At the end of every month (reoccurring), last seen 1987

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A ghostly man on horseback is said to cross the lane before vanishing.

properties.name: Horses and Coach
properties.description: Reported by at least one driver, a coach with a team of horses haunt the road, seemingly charging at incoming traffic and turning away at the last possible moment. One version of the tale says the coach is either seen or heard, but never both.
properties.location_name: Bungay (Suffolk) - Road between Ditchingham and Bungay at Lions Grave

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: Two witnesses in the back of a camper van heard horse's hooves outside their vehicle (even though they did not hear the creature approach). They heard the creature depart before it returned soon after, this time brushing against the rear window. The witnesses drove away, unable to see anything in the darkness. The driver turned the van around a few hundred metres down the road and returned to see what had been harassing them, but nothing was found.
properties.location_name: Burbage Bridge (Derbyshire) - Layby along Ringinglow Road

properties.name: Sir Thomas
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven (or twelve, or forty) bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: White Horses
properties.description: Galloping around the neighbourhood at night, these creatures are thought to be connected to a battle fought more than a thousand years ago.

properties.name: Headless Horse
properties.description: The result of a Danish skirmish over a thousand years ago, this decapitated horse runs along the lanes surrounding the area.
properties.location_name: Burnham Green (Hertfordshire) - Roads in the area

properties.name: Lady O'Hagan
properties.description: The last owner of nearby Towneley Hall, the woman now drives her horse and trap in the area. It is said you can hear the hooves pounding on the cobbled streets, even though the stones have been replaced with modern materials.
properties.location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - Summit area of Manchester Road

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: King Arthur
properties.description: Escorted by mounted knights carrying lances, King Arthur of the Britons traverses this route back to Camelot (as Cadbury is thought to be) on this day in June. One witness said that on 23 June 1995 they encountered an armoured man (more dark age than medieval) holding a standard on horseback - the figure disappeared around a corner, though there was no place that he could have gone. One legend says a door opens every seven years in the hillside so that Arthur and his company can leave to feed their horses.
properties.location_name: Cadbury (Somerset) - Cadbury Castle, and road between North Barrow and South Barrow villages

properties.name: Coach Circling the Courtyard
properties.description: Warning about the encroaching death of a family member, a horse drawn hearse driven by a headless horseman passes through the gates of the castle and circles the courtyard seven times.
properties.date: Warning of a death in the family
properties.location_name: Caister-On-Sea (Norfolk) - Caister Castle, one mile west of Caister-On-Sea

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This coach, pulled by a team of four horses, crosses a bridge close to Calcutt.

properties.name: Walter Calverley
properties.description: A temporary fit of madness resulted in this Elizabethan gentleman killing two of his children and stabbing his wife (who escaped by feigning death). His regretful spirit rides the countryside on a headless horse, although one source says that it had been laid. Poltergeist-like activities have also been reported at the house.
properties.location_name: Calverley (West Yorkshire) - Calverley House (currently called Old Hall?), surrounding countryside

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: Galloping across the parkland under the light of the moon, this rider could be searching for his missing appendage. Some also say that the area is haunted by a coven of witches.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: Emerging from a deep pit near to Cantley, this headless spirit on horseback rides off towards the nearby village.

properties.name: Horace Wimpole
properties.description: A former owner of the property wrote that he saw the ghost of Horace riding on a black horse several times, always just prior to the death of a loved one.

properties.name: Knight in White
properties.description: The knight has been observed standing by the keep, while his riderless horse trots around nearby. Banging and clanking has been reported in the castle after dark, with searches revealing no apparent source.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A shuck is said to walk the area near the castle, while a phantasmic knight stands motionless on the ramparts. Author Wayne Anthony reports that an old lady and a phantom horse have also been reported in the area.

properties.name: Silent Stagecoach
properties.description: An old fashioned stagecoach pulled by a team of ghostly horses passes along this road and vanishes as it pulls up at the Greyhound Inn.
properties.location_name: Chalfont St Peter (Buckinghamshire) - Amersham Road and Greyhound Inn

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: A man who tried to break into this barrow was driven mad by the sound of horses - each time the robber reached into the hole he had made, the sound of horses could be heard, though the creatures could not be seen. The man eventually lost his sight and hearing, dying three months later.
properties.location_name: Challacombe (Devon) - Broken Barrow (exact location not known)

properties.name: Horse and Cart
properties.description: A phantom horse pulling a cart is said to haunt this road.

properties.name: White Stallion
properties.description: Roaming riderless, this white (or grey, according to other reports) horse jumps large hedges and runs wild in the area.

properties.name: Royalist
properties.description: The ghost of a Royalist on horseback is said to ride silently around the lanes leading to this village.

properties.name: Galloping Horses
properties.description: A pair of galloping horses are said to charge down the hill, vanishing as they approach the bottom.

properties.name: Ann
properties.description: This female ghost is rumoured to ride her horse along the lanes that lead to the castle, recognisable by her tricorne hat.

properties.name: Footsteps
properties.description: People have reported the sounds of footsteps walking on the grass behind them - though no-one is present when the witnesses look around. A spectral man dressed in black rides on horseback between the graves, occasionally dismounting before vanishing.

properties.name: Headless Horse
properties.description: A woman and her daughter reported seeing a headless horse in a field close to Junction 29.

properties.name: Major Smalman
properties.description: A diehard Royalist, Smalman's home was once besieged by Roundheads during the latter stages of the Civil War. The Major managed to escape on horseback, his ghost now seen galloping away from the building. The manor itself is also reported to be haunted by both Smalman and the shade of a young girl who smiles at any witnesses before screaming horribly.
properties.location_name: Church Stretton (Shropshire) - Area around Wilderhope Manor

properties.name: Trotting
properties.description: The clip-clop of phantom hooves has been reported in this area, normally accompanied by a strange pale mist which (it is said) sometimes transforms into a horse with a headless rider.

properties.name: Horsemen
properties.description: While on his way home with his dog, Mr Sampson watched two men on horseback riding around the fort. His dog immediately ran home, leaving Sampson to watch the figures as they dismounted and vanish (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').

properties.name: Riderless Horse
properties.description: Before traffic in the area increased considerably, there were several reports of a ghostly horse without rider which would cross the roundabout before vanishing into a now demolished barn.
properties.location_name: Clacton-on-Sea (Essex) - Junction of London Road and St Johns Road, Great Clacton

properties.name: Horse and Cart
properties.description: Walking his dog one early morning, a man watched a horse and cart appear from nowhere. The man was frozen to the spot. The cart was driven by a weathered man, and appeared quite solid, although old fashioned. After the cart had passed the witness, he composed himself and turned to follow the vehicle, but it had vanished.

properties.name: Royalist
properties.description: This phantom Royalist soldier died on the site from his injuries and has been seen near the bar. A ghostly coach and horses also haunts the area. As a side note, a secret tunnel is said to run from the pub to a nearby house.

properties.name: Puca
properties.description: A dark shadow-like horse was named by some to be a puca, although it would appear no one stayed around long enough to find out for sure.
properties.location_name: Clonlara (County Clare) - Bridge (may no longer be standing), two miles south west of village

properties.name: Sound of a Horse
properties.description: The driver of a horse and cart and a passenger were left shaken after encountering the sounds of a horse which passed them, and although nothing could be seen, the sounds were accompanied by a cold gust of wind. A murder had occurred along the road a few years previous, where a woman on horseback was decapitated by a former lover.

properties.name: Old Man
properties.description: This pathway is reputedly haunted by a little old man dressed in old fashioned clothing.
properties.location_name: Coate (Wiltshire) - Path across field heading towards Pond House

properties.name: Horse in Chains
properties.description: An old work horse still draped in the chains it used to drag a plough around the fields still manifests and scares the unwary.
properties.location_name: Cockpole Green (Berkshire) - Area around Putters Farm

properties.name: Charlotte in White Robe
properties.description: Located in a hidden part of the grounds, Charlotte's Well was built for a woman after which it took its name.
