Charlotte is said to have loved the well so much that she could never leave and is said to have been seen since her death around the bathing pool. She may have last been seen walking around a wooded area in a white dress during the winter of 1977, although there are stories that a phantom woman still appears in the area. A phantom coach is also said to ride around the area.

properties.name: Simon Cunliffe
properties.description: Simon rides the area where he once lived, blowing his horn prior to a tragedy. It is said that he scared his wife to death by riding up the staircase of their home, chasing a fox that had ran inside to escape the hunt (ghost hunters Colin Veacock and Peter Crawley recorded a sound like a riding crop here in 1996). Another tale states that the horseman is not Simon but an assassin, killed by a manservant before he could murder the master of the hall. The young daughter of a family living here during the twentieth century told her mother that a 'white woman' would visit her during the night - the sightings stopped when the child changed bedrooms.
properties.date: Once a year, middle of winter (reoccurring), sound recorded 26/07/1996

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution. A headless Black Shuck is also reputed to cross this bridge once per night.

properties.name: Monks with Body
properties.description: A woman cycling along the lane during the Second World War encountered a procession of cowled monks carrying the body of a young man draped in black. Pedalling away from the scene, the woman was overtaken by a ghostly horseman also galloping from the monks. Man and horse vanished as they reached the railway bridge in the village. Many years later two children walking home encountered a tall, hooded monk along a country lane. They quickly ran past.
properties.location_name: Colwall (Hereford & Worcester) - Road near village green known as 'Haunted Lane'

properties.name: Sir George Sydenham
properties.description: Sir George is reported to ride his headless horse up and down the hills near the village. Combe Sydenham is also a place where the Wild Hunt has been reported, always heard as locals were too afraid to look (and rightly so!).

properties.name: Marbury Lady
properties.description: An Egyptian maid was pushed down the staircase of the old manor house, dying in the fall. Her ghost was said to haunt the old hall, though when it was demolished, the spirit changed location and took up residence in the park. A phantom mare named Marbury Dunne is also said to haunt the park.
properties.date: Exact date unknown, but reported to be around 22:30h (female ghost)
properties.location_name: Comberbatch (Cheshire) - Marbury Park and former hall (no longer standing)

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A man on horseback, considered to be a highwayman, reportedly haunts this building. It may have been this entity which created the loud heavy footsteps from above which followed one witness along the corridor to the back rooms; when asked, the owners said no one else was updates at the time.

properties.name: White Rider
properties.description: This pallid figure on horseback has been reported in the village.

properties.name: Headless Woman
properties.description: The terrifying shade of the headless woman walks the hill near the gateway of where this castle stands. The same ghost may also be responsible for the lights that flit across the ruins at night, or they could be caused by phantasmal Royalists that used the castle in 1643. The sound of a galloping phantom horse has been heard at the bottom of the castle hill. A cloaked figure with a brown floppy hat was observed by a local man in 2009 while he was picking worms for fishing. The figure was in the middle of a field but vanished after the witness took his eyes off the man for a few seconds.
properties.date: Woman and horse unknown, male figure seen 15 May 2009, around 01:30h

properties.name: Peg Sneddle
properties.description: Peg Sneddle, or Elizabeth Sleddall as she was known in life, would manifest prior to a death in the Machell family (she was married to a Machell, but used her maiden name after his premature death). This ceased when her body was hidden under a large rock in the nearby river, though it would reappear every year in September. A phantom coach and four also haunts the same area.

properties.name: Soldiers
properties.description: A phantom army comprising of soldiers and their horses are said to haunt the area round these ancient burial chambers.
properties.location_name: Cregneish (Isle of Man) - Meayll Stone Circle

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: This horse backed rider enters the building though the original gate, that is now walled up. The ghost of Sir William Crichton is also said to haunt the site, walking towards castle tower, manifesting on the date of his death (although that date is not certain, only being recorded as prior to July 1454).

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: A headless horse is reputed to gallop along in this area.

properties.name: Cromwellian Rider
properties.description: Cromwellian troops have been seen near the local church, while another soldier rides a horse along the road; he dismounts and vanishes through the church door.

properties.name: WW2 Soldiers
properties.description: Soldiers dressed in Second World War uniform have been seen around the castle area, and the site is also said to be haunted by a horse and carriage. As a side note, Bram Stoker is said to have been partly inspired to write Dracula after seeing the castle while staying at Cruden Bay.

properties.name: Coach & Horses
properties.description: Once upon a time, the coach and its team of horses arrived too late to save a girl who committed suicide in the local pond - out of guilt it now makes its return journey.

properties.name: Hearse
properties.description: Some accidents occurring along this road have been blamed on the phantom black coach pulled by headless horses that runs wild and driverless.
properties.location_name: Cutcombe (Somerset) - A396, between village and Timberscombe

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: A ghostly figure in robes was seen walking towards the gatehouse before vanishing into a wall. It is said the monk sometimes appears on horseback, and the site is also haunted by a white woman.

properties.name: The Black Prince
properties.description: Dressed in black armour, this ghostly figure on horseback was observed several times walking along the road during the Second World War.
properties.location_name: Dartford (Kent) - A2 road between Dartford and Bexley

properties.name: Sam Hunnaford
properties.description: This local farmer hanged himself out of shame after been conned at a fair - his shade has been seen making his last journey on horseback, though instead of hanging itself, the ghost continues towards his former home.

properties.name: Benjamin Gear
properties.description: A former mayor of Okehampton, the figure is said to haunt the waters of the pool. He also takes the form of a black horse. There are reports of an exorcism attempt which may have failed.

properties.name: Old Crockern
properties.description: Riding on the back of a skeletal horse, Crockern insists on moving down this path waving a sword in his dead hand.

properties.name: Yeth Hounds
properties.description: These variants of the shuck, complete with hunters on horseback, have been heard running in this area after dark. Part of the Tavy is also said to be haunted by the ghost of Sir John Fitz, whose body was found in the river.
properties.location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - General area of the River Tavy

properties.name: Limpety
properties.description: Lost on the moor during a snowstorm, a master of the Dartmoor foxhunt claimed that a petite man on a grey horse came from nowhere and led him to safety. He later saw a painting of a gypsy called Limpety and named him as his saviour, only to be told that he had died on the moor 50 years previous.
properties.location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - Green Hill & Black Lane area

properties.name: People on Horseback
properties.description: Wearing clothing from the middle ages, witnesses observed several horsemen galloping across this Dartmoor area. Another party of witnesses saw what looked like cavemen moving around the stone circle at the Cleave.

properties.name: Military Horseman
properties.description: This figure wearing a grey greatcoat has been seen riding along near the ruins of this building.

properties.name: Coach Arriving
properties.description: Early morning, during the time of golden leaves, the sound of a horse and coach can be heard coming to a stop outside the hotel. There are sounds of someone jumping on board before the hooves can be heard pulling away. One of the bedrooms may be haunted by a robed figure, and the sounds of two people fighting can be heard in the courtyard.
properties.location_name: Dartmouth (Devon) - The Royal Castle Hotel, The Quay

properties.name: Clibbon
properties.description: Found guilty of highway robbery, Clibbon was tied to the back of a horse and dragged around the town until he was dead. His screams are sometimes heard. The same area is also home to a headless horse, though it may not be the same creature associated with the highwayman.

properties.name: Small Carriage
properties.description: Returning from a late shift at work, this witness drove past a horse pulling a small, two wheeled carriage that was dimly lit by lanterns. The driver of the carriage remained motionless as the witness's car went by.
properties.location_name: Delamere Forest (Cheshire) - Road by the Station Cafe

properties.name: Horse and Rider
properties.description: A figure on horseback has been observed in the distance, holding a shield and sword. Perhaps connected to this entity, the sounds of a horse charging through woodland have been reported. There may be an additional relationship to the nearby Denbury Hill Iron Age hill fort which folklore says contains golden treasure.
properties.location_name: Denbury Down (Devon) - Woodland in the area

properties.name: Runaway Horse
properties.description: While driving into Denchworth, two people spotted a panicked horse, which galloped from a side road in front of their car. The driver immediately slammed on the brakes and turned the headlights down so as not to startle the creature further. Not quite believing what they had both seen, they turned the headlights back up only to see the horse galloping in front of the car for a few more seconds before vanishing.

properties.name: Wild Men
properties.description: Dressed in animal skins and on horseback, the dozens of phantom, wild-looking figures are reportedly Viking raiders.

properties.name: Sounds of a Running Battle
properties.description: Three girls reported listening to the sounds of a small battle here in the early twentieth century - gunfire, galloping horses, and men shouting. Four people also heard the sounds while out late at night during the 1980s, and two brothers passing through the area in 2009.
properties.location_name: Derby (Derbyshire) - Alvaston area, near the castle

properties.name: Old Soldiers
properties.description: Squadies tell tales of seeing strange people in the newer buildings who vanish if approached, while outside a phantom horse and cart are occasionally seen.

properties.name: Death Coach
properties.description: As the owner of the house lay dying, the other family members around his bed heard a horse drawn coach pull up outside the house. Believing it to be the doctor, they rushed downstairs, but nothing could be seen on the gravel driveway. The owner died soon after.

properties.name: Darrie Loughlin
properties.description: Around eighteen months after his death, Darrie Loughlin's ghost started to appear in the area, riding a white horse towards the lough.
properties.location_name: Derryveagh Mountains (County Donegal) - Area around Lough Dheerig (possibly Lough Derg?)

properties.name: Runaway Horses
properties.description: It is said eight hundred men died at the Battle of Roundway Hill in 1643, after a battlefield manoeuvre which trapped cavalry in a slopped area. The men and horses were slaughtered, and since the battle ghostly horses have been seen galloping around the area. A headless ghost was once also said to walk the area, possibly linked to a barrow on the site, as is the phantom hound which drags a chain from its neck.
properties.location_name: Devizes (Wiltshire) - Oliver's Castle, aka Roundway Castle, aka Rundaway Castle - Bloody Ditch

properties.name: Grey Pony
properties.description: The churchyard is haunted by a man who rides around on a grey (or white) pony. A local superstition says that anyone who runs backwards around the tomb of Robert Cooke will hear crockery rattling in the grave.

properties.name: Kitchen Horse
properties.description: The kitchen was built over the old stables, and the sound of a horse kicking a stable door is said to be occasionally heard. Horseshoes belonging to Shetland ponies have been found in the garden.

properties.name: Black Coach
properties.description: A farmer returning home encountered a coach pulled by four headless horses, which pulled away from the Court's driveway at breakneck speed. The man later learned that Viscount Doneraile died just before his encounter. The ghost of Lady Casteltown has been reported within the building, as has her husband.
properties.location_name: Doneraile (County Cork) - Area outside Doneraile Court

properties.name: Coach in the House
properties.description: One owner of the property won a large amount of money when he drove a horse and coach up the stairs inside the house - his victorious ghost reportedly re-enacts the antic. A separate entity is that of a headless grey woman, a member of the Trenchard family who slit her own throat, while a priest is said to haunt the gatehouse (where he was held prisoner).

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: The sounds of horses dragging prisoners to their execution site are said to be heard along this road.

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A decapitated rider on horseback follows a road that no longer exists - the figure crosses the gravestones and vanishes through a hedge.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: Police were called after a riderless horse was spotted cantering in the area. No horse could be found, and in one area the creature had been reported, the snow showed no hoof marks.
properties.location_name: Dover (Kent) - Common Lane and another unnamed lane

properties.name: House Party
properties.description: A manor house has been seen in the area, a party in full flow visible through its windows. However, the house was demolished many years previous. Even when not seen, locals have reported hearing music coming from the site. A large black knight on horseback is still occasionally seen, once a year.
properties.date: 31 December knight seen (reoccurring), house reported in the twentieth century
properties.location_name: Downend (Isle of Wight) - Area near Knighton Gorges

properties.name: Coach & Four
properties.description: A phantom coach drawn by a team of four horses travel around this area. They are said to look quite solid, though is illuminated by an unknown source.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This decapitated figure rides along the road once darkness falls, and is thought to be an ill omen for any witness, or one of their family.
properties.location_name: Dublin (County Dublin) - Roper's Rest, leading to Love Lane

properties.name: Buck Jones
properties.description: Riding headless on the back of a white horse, this phantom is named as Buck Jones, former City Sheriff (though the man died in a debtor's prison).

properties.name: Shadowy Coaches
properties.description: Founded in the 1730s, the Hell Fire Club quickly became notorious for its black antics. The former meeting ground is said to be haunted by some of these dark deeds, and by phantom horse and coaches which rush past the building, containing the souls of former Hell Fire Club members.
properties.location_name: Dublin Mountains (County Dublin) - Montpelier Hill - ruins of the Hell Fire Club

properties.name: Headless Rider
properties.description: This decapitated male wearing a flowing cape passes through the village on an off-white horse.

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: This figure rides the area in search of his head that was removed as he hit a low branch while galloping at fantastic speed, escaping from his girlfriend's overprotective brothers. Two more ghosts have been reported in the same area, both running away from a murder site.

properties.name: Squire Fulford
properties.description: Driving a coach pulled by four headless horsemen, the squire passes though the village only at certain times of the year.

properties.name: Squire on Horseback
properties.description: Riding his fastest racehorse, this phantom landowner charges around the heathland worrying the locals.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Seen galloping towards the hall, this coach is pulled by four black, headless horses. One legend says the coach contains a coffin.

properties.name: Phantom Horse
properties.description: This creature, only heard and not seen, would gallop on the road and in the fields in this area.
properties.location_name: Durnford (Wiltshire) - Salisbury Road between Durnford & Amesbury, near Chalk Pit Hill

properties.name: Horse & Cart
properties.description: The phantom horse and cart that haunts this area came into being after falling into the river and vanishing without trace.

properties.name: Man on Horseback
properties.description: The ghostly galloping rider seen crossing this area of land must at times dismount - disembodied human footsteps have been heard on the paths, as have strange voices.
properties.location_name: E4 (Greater London) - Chingford Mount Cemetery, Old Church Road

properties.name: Colt Pixie
properties.description: An equine pixie, this fairy entity would play mild pranks and call out to horses, the distraction causing the creatures to become lost.
properties.location_name: East Boldre (Hampshire) - Cold Pixie's Cave (Bronze Age barrow), Beaulieu Heath

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: The sounds of a horse have been heard as it gallops down the street. Some believe it carries two eloping lovers from the rectory.
properties.location_name: East Chinnock (Somerset) - Main road in the village

properties.name: Taxi Service
properties.description: A phantom horse and carriage arrive in the courtyard, picking up an unidentifiable person before once again vanishing to the ether.

properties.name: Rushing Horseman
properties.description: This figure has been both seen and heard as he darts around trees and over roads. One witness said the entity charged towards their car, disappearing on impact.

properties.name: Duke of Monmouth
properties.description: The Duke is said to return to the place where he rested while escaping to the coast after the Battle of Sedgemoor. The grounds of the inn are also said to be haunted by the disembodied head of a white horse.

properties.name: White Rider
properties.description: Two women out walking their dogs watched a white female figure on horseback in the woods. The entity vanished as it rounded a non-existent corner. A local piece of folklore says the area is haunted by a white horse, although traditionally riderless. In 2022, witnesses spotted a hooded grey lady carrying a basket who disappeared into bushes.
properties.location_name: Eckington (Derbyshire) - Moss valley, between town and Ridgeway

properties.name: Runaway Horses
properties.description: Three farmers walking home from a village meeting heard horses running towards them. The men made ready to stop the runaway but were amazed when the sounds past straight through where they stood, and they could see nothing.
properties.location_name: Edale (Derbyshire) - Crossroads between church and railway station

properties.name: Grizel
properties.description: The face of this lady, heavily scarred by fire, has been seen around the area of the Grassmarket - her and her brother were killed by the local authorities for sex crimes and black magic. Some people have also reported seeing her in a phantom coach being pulled by a team of six horses.

properties.name: Deacon Brodie
properties.description: Brodie is reputed to walk this area, carrying a lantern. A coach pulled by a team of black horses (which sometimes breath fire) s also said to start its journey here before travelling along the royal mile. The coach is filled with people in black garb. Another phantom, General Dalzell, rides the same route, but this time on a white horse.
properties.location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Castle Hill and Royal Mile

properties.name: Riders
properties.description: As Bessie Dunlop tied her horse to the edge of the loch, she heard dozens of people on horseback dash past her into the water, although nothing could be seen.

properties.name: Driverless Coach
properties.description: The driverless phantom coach is said to contain Lady Alice Lisle, one of three locations where her ghost haunts. Though the coach and horses have not been seen for many years, it has been said that the sound of a carriage can still be heard.

properties.name: Alice Lisle
properties.description: For assisting two fugitives, Alice Lisle was hanged in 1685 - though her defence argued that Alice did what any good Christian would do. The rustling of Alice's silken dress and footfalls could be heard echoing down the corridors for many years after her death. Alice also haunted the road outside the hall, appearing in a coach pulled by headless horses.

properties.name: Dark Horseman
properties.description: Possessing a grinning skull barren of flesh, this dark horseman and his black steed gallop furiously down muddy paths towards the nearby waterfalls.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This decapitated phantom on his steed is said to ride along the wall before leaping over the stonework on the stroke of midnight.
properties.location_name: Elsing (Norfolk) - Belaugh Wall, between Elsing and Bawdswell

properties.name: Seventeen Century Boy
properties.description: Two men driving through the village encountered a 'colourless' horse and rider standing in the middle of the road. The rider appeared to be dressed in 17th century clothing. The horse then galloped away across a nearby field.
properties.location_name: Enchmarsh (Shropshire) - Road leading through the village

properties.name: Headless Monk
properties.description: The monk was caught with a woman and killed for his crime. The entity now returns once a year. He may have last been seen in 1981 by a teenager who would search the woods near the abbey for old pots and pipes. After returning home one night from the woods, the teenager reportedly spotted a monk in a brown robe holding a lantern on a hooked pole outside his window. The abbey is also home to phantom horsemen, and Roesia of Dover who is looking for her heart (it was buried here by her father).

properties.name: Lady Charlotte Watson-Taylor
properties.description: Both prison workers and villagers have reported seeing this phantom lady inspecting the prison grounds, and even driving her horse and carriage over a road close by where a bridge once stood.

properties.name: Plague Victims
properties.description: Several graves belonging to plague victims are thought to be haunted by their occupiers.

properties.name: Horse and Coach
properties.description: The sounds of a horse pulling a coach have been heard outside this inn, though never seen.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: The sound of neighing and hoof thuds can be heard in the area, even when there are no wild horses present.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: Thought to be the site of a former stables or old coaching house, the sounds of hooves, whinnying and soft talking have been heard in the area.
properties.location_name: Exmouth (Devon) - Town end of St Andrews Road

properties.name: Drakes
properties.description: While the house is haunted by Sir Francis Drake, the road outside is also populated by the ghosts of a horse and rider. Eliot Drake broke his neck in a fall while racing, but now continues to ride.

properties.name: Old Man
properties.description: Said to haunt the upper area of the building, the old man could be one of the many plague victims from 1665. A white horse also haunts the grounds.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: Riding a white horse and donned in a full suit of armour, his knight can also be recognised by his lack of head.
properties.location_name: Farley (Staffordshire) - Road between Farley and Alton

properties.name: Sounds of a Coach
properties.description: The sounds of a coach pulling into the courtyard of this hotel have been heard, complete with whinnying horses. A ghostly girl in grey has also been seen waiting for the return of a lost lover killed on the outskirts of the town.
properties.location_name: Farnham (Surrey) - The Hop Bag Hotel, Downing Street

properties.name: Horse and Coach
properties.description: An old coaching route, some still say that phantom horses and coaches can still be heard passing along the road.

properties.name: Horses Crossing Drawbridge that No Longer Exists
properties.description: A ghostly coach being pulled by a team of horses is said to cross a ditch next to the fort, where once a drawbridge lay. The fort is also the site of an alleged suicide which is now re-enacted; the ghostly white figure is still seen dangling from the roof beams where he hanged himself.

properties.name: Pale Rider
properties.description: Passing through the castle park, a tall man riding a white horse has been seen. The castle itself is home to a green lady who is said to have committed suicide after her lover ran away.

properties.name: Distressed Horse
properties.description: Said to have been slaughtered in the abattoir, this horse is said to be heard every six months. There are also reports of the creature being heard galloping away from the area.

properties.name: Obrick's Colt
properties.description: Robbed of her jewels after death, a wealthy local woman came back post-mortem, taking the form of a white horse. The phantom horse was reputed to be so bold that it would occasionally manifest during the daytime.
properties.location_name: Fitz (Shropshire) - Area was known as Cuthery or Cutberry Hollow

properties.name: Mad Horse
properties.description: The hall and surrounding area were haunted by a wild mare.

properties.name: Helpful Horse
properties.description: This boggart took the form of a horse. The entity would plough fields and work around the farm in return for a warm place to spend the night (or a pie, depending on the story).

properties.name: Little Folk
properties.description: The area around the lake is reportedly home to boggarts and hobs. There are also stories of ghostly Roundheads moving around the area, some on horseback and others wearily walking holding pikes.

properties.name: The Spittal Hill Tut
properties.description: Nicknamed the Spittal Hill Tut, this ghostly entity would take the form of a small horse. The entity grabbed one man passing through the area on horseback, almost crushing him to death. Some believed the Tut existed to protect some treasure concealed beneath the hill.

properties.name: Horse and Carriage
properties.description: A man out cycling at night reported hearing a horse galloping along - he heard it pass him by, but nothing could be seen. It is reported that a ghostly horse pulling a carriage heads towards the house, but never departs.

properties.name: Horse and Cart
properties.description: Heard near the ford, several people have reported the clip clop of hooves and the grind of an old cart.
properties.location_name: Fulmer (Buckinghamshire) - Ford running through the village

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A coach drawn by four horses and driven by a headless coachman is supposed to travel from Bungay to Geldeston before returning to its departure point.
properties.location_name: Geldeston (Norfolk) - Road to Bungay from Geldeston

properties.name: Armed Rider
properties.description: While walking home from the Bull Hotel, Dave Robson heard the thumping of hooves approach him. Within moments, a dark cloaked figure wearing a big hat and holding a gun dashed by on horseback before vanishing near a group of trees.
properties.location_name: Gerrards Cross (Buckinghamshire) - Area around the Bull Hotel

properties.name: Alexander MacAllister
properties.description: The site of a battle in 1594 between the troops of George Gordon, 1st Marquess of Huntly and Francis Hay, 9th Earl of Erroll, is haunted by the headless ghost of MacAllister riding on horseback - he fought briefly, although was decapitated by the first cannon shot.

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: It was once said that a phantom white horse haunted a spot at the hall where a former owner had concealed a cache of riches.

properties.name: Drowning Lovers
properties.description: A failed escape from Cromwellian soldiers resulted in a young officer and his lover drowning in the river Wye. Their ghosts, mounted on horseback, have been reported struggling in the river, standing in the ruins of the nearby castle, or heard screaming during storms. In addition, an imprisoned Irish chieftain who died trying to escape is said to haunt the Macbeth tower.

properties.name: Civil War Skirmish
properties.description: Six workers claimed they heard charging horses, the clash of swords, and gunfire, though could see nothing strange. A local historian said that a small fight had happened nearby, between Roundheads and Cavaliers.
properties.location_name: Grafton Regis (Northamptonshire) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Captain Blundell
properties.description: Blundell, a British officer, rides decapitated upon horseback in this area. His headless body was found in a nearby barracks, and an investigation ruled that the officer had killed himself. Some people thought this to be unlikely and believed his ghost had returned to prove that a murder had really occurred.

properties.name: Poisoned Horses
properties.description: The sounds of poisoned horses neighing haunts this area - a story tells how thirty men and over a dozen horses died after drinking poisoned water from this river. The river itself is said to reflect the dead soldiers, if one examines it for long enough.
properties.location_name: Great Ayton (North Yorkshire) - Area near the river

properties.name: Kate Slough
properties.description: The apparition of blonde-haired Kate on horseback has been observed near the area where she once drowned. Her white horse has also been observed riderless.

properties.name: Woman in a Crinoline Gown
properties.description: The history of this building goes back to 1138, and several ghosts have attached themselves to it and the surrounding area. The woman dressed in a crinoline gown drifts silently through the house, while a woman with blonde hair sits in front of the fireplace. This may be the same ghost who rides a white horse down the lane outside. A cavalier has been observed in the lounge, and a phantom monk walks a foot above the ground outside in the garden. Finally, just around the corner of the building the spectre of a highwayman still hangs from a tree.
properties.location_name: Great Holland (Essex) - Treasure Holt - farmhouse in marshes

properties.name: Sir John
properties.description: Upon his death, Sir John de Plessis reportedly requested that he be buried upright, sitting on top of his favourite steed. The executors of his will made it so, and Sir John has since been seen riding along the hillside. The same story is attributed to a Captain Backhouse, though his haunting stopped after being dug up and laid out traditionally. A female phantom rider has also been reported haunting the area, her head sitting on her lap.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: The site was said to be haunted by a phantom coach and horses that were heard coming to a halt on the gravel driveway but were never seen.

properties.name: Earl of Shaftesbury
properties.description: This Earl of Shaftesbury, who died in 1683, is said to drive his coach around the lanes in the area, looking for souls to take.
properties.location_name: Gussage All Saints (Dorset) - Lanes in the area

properties.name: Abigail Carstair
properties.description: Carstair was on the run after being accused of witchcraft - she leapt from the cliff rather than being tortured by the religious fanatics. Her shade is sometimes seen trying to hide in the area, while the ghostly horseman also reported in the same place may be one of her hunters.

properties.name: Coach Sounds
properties.description: An invisible, phantom coach is driven through the area by a team of horses.

properties.name: Waiting on the Platform
properties.description: When overgrown and unloved, the station was said by some to be haunted by a figure. Some assumed the ghost was a woman thrown from her horse and trap into the path of an oncoming train. The area was also said to be home to a phantom carriage.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: The coach, pulled by a team of horses, belts down the driveway, enters the house, and disappears while ascending the staircase. In 2017 a visitor to the house spotted a man in Stuart attire reading a book while sitting by a window. The witness, believing the figure to be a reenactor, pulled out their phone to take a photograph, but the figure had vanished. The witness asked around and staff said there were no costumed actors in that day (nor any scheduled for the near future).

properties.name: Pale Horse
properties.description: A phantom white horse is said to roam the area but only visible to those with second sight.
properties.location_name: Hathersage (Derbyshire) - Sicklehome Hollow, between Hathersage and Bamford

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: A phantom man upon a shaggy horse was once said to haunt this hill, manifesting to other riders and causing their mounts to bolt.

properties.name: Sir Thomas
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: Cavalier
properties.description: A fisherman encountered a spectral Cavalier passing over the bridge on horseback. The entity faded away.

properties.name: Men and Horses
properties.description: The vicar called the police after hearing many men and horses traversing the nearby downs. Police found nothing.

properties.name: Hedley Kow
properties.description: A spirit that plagued many households in the village, the Hedley Kow would take the form of human or beast, sometimes luring the living into marshy areas. The entity finally disappeared after it manifested as a horse and ran off, escaping the fury of a victim of one of its 'jokes'.

properties.name: White Horse of Clumly
properties.description: Both horse and rider were killed in an accident as they galloped back home, the rider having just disposed of a body. Now the pair only appear when the environment is right.

properties.name: Headless Highwayman
properties.description: This ghostly figure still rides his horse, still seeking his head that was taken from him as punishment for his crimes.
properties.location_name: Hickleton (South Yorkshire) - Area near the church

properties.name: Lady on Horseback
properties.description: This ghostly woman is reported to ride her mare along the lane where she fell from her horse and died.

properties.name: Old Coker
properties.description: Even though he died in 1730, Henry Coker is said to still ride his horse, follow his hounds, rattle his chains and blow his hunting horn in the area.
properties.location_name: Hill Deverill (Wiltshire) - Gun's Church (or Gunschurch) barrow

properties.name: Horse Drawn Coach
properties.description: This ghoulish coach is drawn by a team of four grey spectral horses.
properties.location_name: Hindon (Wiltshire) - Junction of the A303 and the A350

properties.name: Waving White Lady in a Coach
properties.description: After losing control of the horses, a coach driver and his female passenger died. Now, the phantom vehicle is seen outside the church; the white woman in the carriage waves at witnesses before the coach pulls away and the accident is repeated.

properties.name: Horses' Hooves
properties.description: The hill is apparently home to the sound of horses moving around, the noise providing an uneasy feeling to any witness.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Appearing on the stroke of midnight, this coach pulled by a team of black horses circles the driveway before mysteriously vanishing again.

properties.name: Galloping
properties.description: Heard only at night (and it is said seen only during the day) a ghostly horseman haunts the north part of the village, near where he failed to jump a gate, breaking his neck in the process.
properties.location_name: Hollingbourne (Kent) - Hollingbourne House, north of village

properties.name: Sounds of Fighting
properties.description: A couple of witnesses reported hearing horses and fighting which moved through the village street and over a field in the early hours of the morning.
properties.location_name: Holme Hale (Norfolk) - High Street, near the public house

properties.name: Grey Woman
properties.description: Seen carrying a small baby, this woman dressed in grey was often heard walking about just prior to manifesting. The sounds of a horse passing outside have also been reported.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven (or twelve) bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A wild horseman travels the desolate moors, looking for souls to take.

properties.name: Lady in Brown Dress
properties.description: Local legend says this ghost is a serving girl whose child was killed when the lord of the manor threw it from an attic window. A pair of disembodied hands have been reported floating around the building, also said to belong to the former servant, while on the odd occasion, a male figure in spurs has been observed. A visitor to the manor in 1995 felt a strong tap on her shoulder. Expecting it to be her boyfriend, she turned, but was alone in the room. There are several other local legends about the manor, including hauntings by a horse and carriage, a phantom woman, and ghostly birds.
properties.date: Mostly unknown, tap felt in October 1995

properties.name: Lady on White Stallion
properties.description: Appearing during the day, this female rider has been observed galloping across the road only to disappear once reaching the other side. The same area is haunted by a spectral coach being pulled at breakneck speed by four horses, and by a hooded man riding a bicycle.

properties.name: Horse Worrier
properties.description: The ghost of a man and his horse, supposedly buried under the hill, were blamed for worrying other horses after dark, preventing them from using tracks through the area.
properties.location_name: Icklingham (Suffolk) - Hill near Deadman's Grave (now an SSSI)

properties.name: Headless Shape on Horseback
properties.description: The Archbishop of Sudbury (or another senior religious figure) was decapitated near to this point by his enemies, the man now returns on dark nights to retrace the route of his death. Another story names the headless horseman as a highwayman, or one of three rebels executed nearby for the murder of the Archbishop.

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A phantom horseman is said to appear and charge at pedestrians before vanishing.
properties.location_name: Ightfield (Shropshire) - Path between Ightfield Hall and village

properties.name: Screaming
properties.description: The cries for help heard only on Halloween emerge from the staircase. They may belong to a maid once caught in a fire which engulfed the building - she fell to her death trying to escape. A ghostly carriage pulled by a single horse haunts the driveway, while another spook has been reported in the library.

properties.name: Pale Figure
properties.description: This pallid person spotted standing around the area could be the phantom of one of many suicides which have occurred on the nearby Orwell Bridge. At least one witness has heard an invisible horse.
properties.location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Pipers Vale, aka The Lairs

properties.name: Ewen of the Small Head
properties.description: This decapitated figure on horseback is seen as a member of the family who killed him lies dying.
properties.date: Prior to a death in the MacLean family
properties.location_name: Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Duart Castle

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: While travelling along this road during heavy rain at night, a taxi driver encountered someone riding a white horse. The driver had to stop abruptly to avoid the rider, and when the driver looked up, man and horse had vanished. When the witness later mentioned the encounter to other people, they said it was not the first time the entity had been seen.
properties.location_name: Ivinghoe (Buckinghamshire) - Ivinghoe Aston to Slapton Road

properties.name: James II
properties.description: James II was killed storming the castle, and it is reportedly his ghost on horseback that still charges around the area.

properties.name: Sound of Hooves & Screams
properties.description: Once a family home, local legend has it that a child ran out of the manor to meet his mother and father who were returning for Christmas in a horse-drawn coach. The child was hit by the horses and died of his injuries. The anniversary of the event is marked by the reoccurring sounds of the tragedy.

properties.name: Farmer
properties.description: This entity rides on horseback pulling a roller across the fields.

properties.name: Sounds of a Coach
properties.description: A cyclist who paused on the road to relight his lamp heard the thump of hooves and the wheels of a coach pass him, even though nothing was visible. A local legend says that the phantom sounds belong to a couple who ran away to wed, but one angry parent followed and shot them both.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This area was the haunt of a headless horseman who died while racing the country roads. His ghost was put to rest after a local man was brave enough to let the decapitated figure ride by his side.
properties.location_name: Kilcummer (County Cork) - Hill in the area

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A grisly headless horseman rides around this area of the village.

properties.name: Prince Rupert's Horse
properties.description: Two sentries reported seeing something white, resembling a horse, jump a hedge and disappear close to their camp. A police officer who patrolled the area encountered the ghost so frequently that he ignored the entity. Local folklore claimed a phantom horse foaming at the mouth, thought to have belonged to Prince Rupert, haunted the area.
properties.location_name: Kineton (Warwickshire) - Marlborough Farm Camp (Second World War PoW camp, exact location not known), and Graveyard Spinney

properties.name: Squire Surtees
properties.description: Riding a mottled grey horse, the squire rides circles on the driveway, rattling a handful of chains.
properties.location_name: Kingston St Mary (Somerset) - Tainfield House (no longer a single house)

properties.name: Sir George Blount
properties.description: Angered to such a degree by his daughter eloping with a man he hated, Sir George Blount gained his revenge by coming back from the dead and terrorising this village. He was seen on horseback and would scare locals washing their clothes in a long gone pond. A group of clergymen finally bottled the ghost and cast it into the sea (or buried it in the local churchyard).

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: Tourists staying in this cottage were said to have been awaken as the building shook due to a stampede of phantom horses. Footsteps have also been heard outside, and the face of a quickly vanishing sailor observed looking in through windows.

properties.name: Grey Horses
properties.description: A man walking his dog reported seeing a grey coach being pulled by two grey horses. Two grey men sat driving the ghostly vehicle.

properties.name: Copeman
properties.description: A man named Copeman murdered along the road may still haunt the area on horseback. Two men cycling back from Kirton spotted the ghostly horseman.
properties.location_name: Kirton in Lindsey (Lincolnshire) - Road between Kirton and Grayingham

properties.name: Edward Higgins
properties.description: A former robber and burglar, Higgins was caught, killed, and now haunts the hotel where he made his base of operations. A phantom coach has been seen and heard moving over cobbles outside the building.

properties.name: Horse & Cart
properties.description: A motorcyclist encountered a horse pulling a cart with a high back and sides, being led by a man holding a large whip. As the cyclist slowed to overtake the cart, he was momentarily blinded by an approaching car. When his vision had returned to normal after a few seconds, the cart had vanished.

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: A legend says that when evil or misfortune is en route to villages alongside the lake, a white horse appears and crosses the water.

properties.name: Glowing Horseman
properties.description: Terrorising all who travelled the road between dusk and dawn, it took the combined efforts of all the local priests and their staff to exorcise this ghost.
properties.location_name: Lamarsh (Essex) - Road between Lamarsh Hills and Bures St Mary

properties.name: Horse & Rider
properties.description: A horse and rider were killed by the Wild Hunt when they failed to hide themselves in time from the rabid hounds of hell. Their souls remain trapped in the area.
properties.location_name: Langford Budville (Somerset) - Area near the army base

properties.name: Raided Building
properties.description: Over the past two hundred years, there have been several reports of this building seen to be on fire with soldiers running around its base - the vision vanishes if investigated. Another tale of the area tells of a headless coachman who drives a phantom coach.

properties.name: Funeral Coach
properties.description: A funeral coach pulled by headless horses reputedly terrified people in the area. The locals summoned Rev Richard Dodge to banish the entity. Searching for the apparition, he found the coach parked on the moor, a phantom coachman standing beside it, and by the phantom's feet, the local vicar lay unconscious. Dodge said a prayer, the coachman fled in his vehicle and the vicar recovered (after a few days in bed). The coach never returned to Lanreath.

properties.name: Charging Horses
properties.description: A Civil War battle site, the sounds of horses have been reported charging around the vicinity, though the creatures cannot be seen. Old fashioned figures, two women and a man, are also said to haunt the area.

properties.name: St Thomas of Canterbury
properties.description: The ghost of Thomas Beckett appears on the anniversary of his death, passing through the village on horseback.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Two women returning home to Kingstanding heard the approach of a fast moving horse-drawn coach behind them so climbed up a bank to let the vehicle pass. The coach, too wide for the road on which it travelled, passed at speed and left the road, falling into a ditch. The coach then promptly vanished.

properties.name: Headless Coachman
properties.description: A groom and his assistant heard galloping approaching Leam Hall. Expecting the lord and lady of the hall, the pair stood outside awaiting the coach. However, instead of the master's coach, the approaching vehicle was of a 'strange design' and was driven by a headless man. The phantom coach was driven straight past the witnesses and vanished into a closed gate.

properties.name: Carriage
properties.description: Not reported for years, the ghostly carriage was supposed to be pulled by a team of four horses.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: A phantom coach can be heard travelling the area.

properties.name: Headless Women
properties.description: Having drowned in the pool when their horse and coach fell in, these four headless women (still in their coach) rise from the water at midnight and travel around the area.
properties.location_name: Leigh (Dorset) - Pool in the area, exact location unknown

properties.name: Danes
properties.description: The site of a battle where the Saxons engaged the Danes is said to be haunted by matching soldiers from both camps.
properties.location_name: Leith Hill (Surrey) - Area around Anstiebury hillfort

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This phantom coach was driven along the dark lane by a ghostly woman.
properties.location_name: Lelant (Cornwall) - Lane off the road between village and Chy-An-Gweal (Carbis Bay)

properties.name: Dench Family Coach
properties.description: Belonging to the Dench family, a phantom coach is said to run along this stretch of road.
properties.location_name: Lenchwick (Hereford & Worcester) - Road between Lenchwick and Norton

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: A female witness recalled seeing a phantom coach being pulled by invisible horses along this road, so bright it lit the night up as if day.
properties.location_name: Letton (Norfolk) - Road through village (now the road between Letton hall and Cranworth, the Shipdham Road)

properties.name: Open the Gates!
properties.description: A night worker returning home early in the morning was unnerved as a man riding a black horse charged by him. As the rider approached the locked castle gates, he screamed 'Open the gates! Open the gates!' before vanishing near the still closed entrance. Another witness reported feeling breathing on the back of the neck, hearing strange footsteps and seeing shadows.
properties.location_name: Lincoln (Lincolnshire) - Flat area at top of Steep Hill, outside castle gate

properties.name: General Dalyell
properties.description: A one-time owner of the building, General Dalyell has been seen on horseback on the estate. The seventeenth century commander may return to gloat over his constant poker victories, in which he even beat the Devil himself! A phantom monk has also been reported on site.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: Holding his head under one arm, this horseman was said to have manifested when a small group of men attempted to dig up the barrow while looking for treasure (reputed to be a golden image).
properties.location_name: Litchfield (Hampshire) - Angledown Barrow (aka Angle Down Barrow)

properties.name: Lady Lightfoot
properties.description: This ghostly woman drove her coach and horses around inside the courthouse, screaming in protest of her murder while in prison.

properties.name: Solid Gold
properties.description: Hidden under the Grey Mare is reputedly a coffin made from solid gold. There is also a local legend that connects the barrow to a phantom horse that runs around the area.
properties.location_name: Littlebredy (aka Little Bredy) (Dorset) - The Grey Mare & Her Colts (long barrow)

properties.name: White Hound of Feens
properties.description: A legend says this hound, which can be heard howling in the night, is sometimes accompanied by a young lady in grey on horseback. She, in turn, has been known to ride with a wild hunt called the Dorcas Noble, while trying to win the heart of a former boyfriend. A large white hound was seen in the area during the 1980s - the size of an Irish Wolfhound, during one encounter the creature vanished as if running behind a black sheet.

properties.name: Horseless Horse Rider
properties.description: Thrown off her mount and killed, this shade now stands around the place where she broke her neck.
properties.location_name: Llanerchymedd (Llannerch-y-medd) (Gwynedd) - Road between Llangefni and Benllech, near the California Inn

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: Even the most fearless residents on the village felt terror when they encountered this phantom, snow-white mare along the roads at night.
properties.location_name: Llangynwyd (Mid Glamorgan) - Roads in the area

properties.name: Fantastic Horse
properties.description: This loch monster will disguise itself as a radiant white horse, enabling it to walk up to victims before pulling them into the loch's murky waters.
properties.location_name: Loch Borralan (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Kelpie
properties.description: This lake monster would take the form of a beautiful horse which would try to drown anyone who tried to mount it.
properties.location_name: Loch Coruisk (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: Witness Mr Morrison stopped his car suddenly when he encountered a white horse ridden by a Victorian man in the middle of the road. Both entities vanished quickly into the distance.
properties.location_name: Loch Dornoch (Highland) - Straight piece of road lined with trees on both sides

properties.name: Water Horse
properties.description: This creature was friendlier than many of the other Scottish water horses and assisted in the construction of a local church. It may have been yellow in colour.
properties.location_name: Loch Shin (Highland) - Waters of the loch

properties.name: Alex Skene
properties.description: A local villain, Alex was drowned by Old Nick as he tried to cross the waters of the loch in his horse-drawn carriage. The same coach still travels the surrounding countryside, driven by its dead master. The same area is also 'haunted' by a large hairy monster, apparently once photographed from a distance.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This decapitated ghostly figure was once Ewen of the Little Head. Ewen was killed in battle with a rogue laird and now appears in the area around the house to warn the Ewen family of impending doom. Another animal, a large black dog, is reported to patrol the grounds.
properties.date: Prior to tragedy in the Ewen family

properties.name: Bean-nighe
properties.description: The Bean-nighe is a Scottish spirit, an ill bringer who would be seen washing the clothes of those soon to die. This creature has long breasts, slung over her shoulders, and if she were grabbed from behind, the Bean-nighe would say whom the clothes belonged; if yours, she could change fate. The owner of the castle, upon seeing the creature, tried to grab her but failed - he died in battle the following day. As if this entity were not enough to contend with, the area is haunted by a headless horseman.
properties.location_name: Lochbuie, Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - River by Moy Castle

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: A white horse once haunted the area around the house. Another animal ghost was reputedly a mole - a former resident declared that she would haunt the garden she loved in the form of the furry mammal, and so she did.
properties.location_name: Lochgelly (Fife) - Old house (may no longer stand) on the banks of Loch Gelly, Powguild

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: The horseman, holding his head under one arm, gallops at breakneck speed down the hill, with anyone who witnesses him doomed to suffer a family bereavement.
properties.location_name: Loddington (Leicestershire) - Road on a steep hill in the area

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: The driveway of the house is said to be haunted by a coach containing a headless footman and four headless passengers, driven by a headless coachman, pulled by a team of four headless horses.

properties.name: Headless Horses
properties.description: A coach pulled by six headless horses is said to travel up the hill. There are also vague reports of a shuck-like dog that runs in the same area. Another ghost, that of a scary old woman with matted black hair, was also reported here in 1973.

properties.name: Horse & Cart
properties.description: While overtaking a slow moving lorry, a driver spotted a grey horse and cart to the side of the bridge ahead of him. When the driver reached the bridge, the horse had vanished.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: The site of a Cromwellian defeat, the anniversary of which is marked by phantom hoof falls.

properties.name: Coach & Horses
properties.description: The evening prior to a death in the owner's family a team of four horses would pull a coach up the driveway before fading from view. The nearby Barton Street is also haunted by a phantom horse that throws its rider into a ditch.
properties.location_name: Louth (Lincolnshire) - Cadeby Hall and Barton Street

properties.name: Galloping Horse
properties.description: Demolished in 1934, the surrounding land of the cottage is haunted by a horse which could be heard quite distinctly, though never seen.
properties.location_name: Lower Bebington (Lancashire) - Orchard Cottage (no longer standing)

properties.name: Tommy
properties.description: Seen on marsh fields with another horse at night, Tommy died several years previous, though still enjoys grazing alongside his former friends.

properties.name: Headless Man
properties.description: One folk story says this road is home to several entities that appear to travellers, including a headless man, a white horse, a black dog, and a sad woman with flowing hair who wears a white robe.
properties.location_name: Lulsley (Hereford & Worcester) - Sandy Lane (between Osebury Rock and the river)

properties.name: Duke of Monmouth
properties.description: The ghostly figure of the Duke of Monmouth, upon a white horse, is said to ride inland.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: This piece of road is haunted by a coach that vanished without trace many years ago.
properties.location_name: Lyme Regis (Dorset) - Road near Trent Manor House

properties.name: Coach and Four
properties.description: This team of horses and the coach all fell into the pit and drowned. Their ghosts run along the fateful route and the accident is periodically replayed.

properties.name: Winner
properties.description: A group of miners playing cards invited a passing stranger to join their game. He sat with them, quickly winning all hands, although the game quickly came to an end when the miners fled, having realised their guest had cloven feet.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: A legend once said that horses were driven around the area of the church by an invisible entity. Fire emerging from the horses' nostrils would be blamed for darkened grass and the flaking paint on fencing. In the same area, a phantom donkey was said to have been banished after a man kicked the creature.

properties.name: Sounds of Battle
properties.description: One would have hoped that this Jacobite battle would have finished by now, but it is not the case. The occasional headless horseman is reported in the area. Image (c) Peter Crawley.
properties.location_name: Maghull (Lancashire) - Exact place not known, but south of the area

properties.name: Headless Coachman
properties.description: A headless coachman, his missing cranium in a box besides him, drives his coach and four along this lane.

properties.name: Blazing Eyed Dog
properties.description: A large black hound with burning red eyes was said to lurk in this area. A phantom grey horse is also reported, as is an old woman laden down with a bundle of faggots, drifting towards the Holy Well.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: Walking her dogs, this witness heard and felt a large horse trotting towards her. She called the dogs to one side, to let the rider pass, but upon turning around, she could she nothing there.

properties.name: Pounding Hooves
properties.description: The local stories state that many years ago, a coach returning guests to the hall crashed along the road, killing all on board. The horses (and sometimes the wheels of the coach) can still be heard on occasion, thundering along the road.
properties.location_name: Manifold Valley (Staffordshire) - Road leading to Throwley Hall (no longer standing)

properties.name: Golden Coach
properties.description: Pulled by a team of four headless horses, this golden coach appears at The Hatches.

properties.name: Georgian Coach
properties.description: This Georgian-era coach pulled by four ghostly horses is supposed to be connected to a well-known house in the area. One folktale says a young person mistook the phantom coach as 'real' and asked the driver for directions; coach and horses vanished, leaving only the smell of brimstone.
properties.location_name: Market Harborough (Leicestershire) - Unidentified long road in the area

properties.name: Roman on Horseback
properties.description: This Roman centurion passes through the area on horseback.

properties.name: Catherine Ferrers
properties.description: Seen riding her horse topless, and swinging around tree branches without a stitch on, the ghost of the highwaywoman Ferrers continues to create the same amount of havoc that she did whilst living.
properties.location_name: Markyate Cell (Hertfordshire) - Area around the priory

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This coach, driven by a decapitated horseman, manifests on the stroke of midnight and travels through a tiny gap in a hedge.
properties.date: Last sighting unknown, but said to appear at midnight

properties.name: Racing Driver
properties.description: A phantom coach driver takes is said to dash through the wood, whipping his horses to breakneck speed.

properties.name: Lord on Horseback
properties.description: A former owner of the hall is now said to ride around the area on horseback. A lion was seen by several people just after the Second World War, though no tangible evidence was ever found - it is unclear whether is qualifies as an ABC or a feline phantom.

properties.name: Funeral Procession
properties.description: This line of mourners following a black horse drawn carriage has been reported travelling the roads in Marnhull. Another report states that there are only two pallbearers, and they are more likely to be seen along Sackmore Lane.
properties.date: Always at midnight, thought the date changes
properties.location_name: Marnhull (Dorset) - Area near quarry, and/or Sackmore Lane

properties.name: Phantom Coach and Horses
properties.description: Witnessed by the wife of a landlord, a phantom coach pulled by a team of horses entered the courtyard, quickly fading from view.

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: One woman being driven along this street reported seeing a dark man on horseback. He wore a long cape and a large floppy hat partly covered his face.
properties.location_name: Matlock (Derbyshire) - Bakewell Road, outside of the Railway Hotel

properties.name: Laird on Horseback
properties.description: Killed by a local witch, this former owner of the tower can be seen riding his mare, lost on the barren land. Another ghost story from the area says a woman returned from the grave to ensure that her hidden treasure was given fairly to the landowner and a kind serving girl.

properties.name: Woman riding Horse towards Clouds
properties.description: This sight could only be seen while looking through the branches of a certain tree in the area at night. However, the tree has since been cut down...

properties.name: Mr Baker
properties.description: Mr Baker was a farmer murdered on the hill in 1694. His ghost returns on the anniversary of the crime, possibly driving a horse and carriage.
properties.location_name: Melbury Bubb (Dorset) - Dirt track along Bubdown Hill, known as Murderers Lane?

properties.name: Arthur Bryant Haig
properties.description: Former owner of the house, Arthur Bryant Haig, is still said to mount his (now phantom) chestnut horse and ride through his lands towards a nearby river.

properties.name: Coach and Four
properties.description: This team of horses, together with their ghostly coach, have been seen approaching the front door to this building.

properties.name: Danish Brothers
properties.description: Many moons ago, two Danish brothers fought to discover who should marry a beautiful Saxon girl. They managed to kill each other, and their ghosts continue to fight to this day. Other stories say the island is haunted by the sounds of a horse, the shouts of an abusive man who drowned chasing his wife and daughter, and a Roman centurion.

properties.name: Red Stagecoach
properties.description: A driver along this road closed his eyes and prepared for a crash when this eighteenth century coach driven by four horses appeared in front of him - but the accident never happened, as the coach vanished into thin air. The witness later said he had also seen the driver and a guard on the coach.
properties.location_name: Mevagissey (Cornwall) - Road between Mevagissey and Truro

properties.name: Invisible Carriage
properties.description: Heard but not seen, a horse drawn carriage moves between these two buildings, driven by Lord Ogle.
properties.location_name: Michelmersh (Hampshire) - Between Manor Farm & Dower House

properties.name: Gentle Music
properties.description: Three children reported hearing a battle coming from outside the castle and seeing a knight on horseback who charged at them. Inside the castle, muffled sixteenth century music has been heard, while there are persistent rumours of buried treasure somewhere in the grounds (which can only be found after running nonstop around the fort nine time and quickly entering the mysterious door which magically opens!).

properties.name: Grey Horse
properties.description: Author Alasdair Alpin MacGregor reported encountering the phantom of a grey horse at an overgrown water trough near Kirkandrews upon Eden. MacGregor relayed his sighting to a local postmaster, who told him reports of the apparition were not uncommon.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This phantom rider is named as local highwayman Galloping Dick.

properties.name: Heavy Horse
properties.description: A hunter with a torch looking for rabbits heard and felt a heavy horse running toward and then past him, but nothing could be seen in any direction.
properties.location_name: Milton Ernest (Bedfordshire) - Field along Thurleigh Road

properties.name: Black Bess
properties.description: A former hideout of the highwayman Dick Turpin, both he and his dark horse were once seen in the area - even after his death.
properties.location_name: Milton Keynes (Buckinghamshire) - Woughton on the Green, general area

properties.name: Grey Lady
properties.description: This phantom woman is sometimes seen along with her young child. The road outside the pub is haunted by a racehorse which occasionally gallops by, more often heard than seen.
properties.location_name: Moneyrow Green (Berkshire) - White Hart public house

properties.name: Horse & Cart
properties.description: Patrons of this pub reported seeing a ghostly cart being pulled by a horse, in turn being led by a farmer. The apparition would disappear at a nearby pond.
properties.location_name: Mosbrough (South Yorkshire) - The Crofts public house, road leading to

properties.name: Lucky Venture?
properties.description: The stadium was briefly haunted by a horse named Lucky Venture which died on the track. The animal's shade was exorcised by a couple of local university students.

properties.name: Faceless Woman in Carriage
properties.description: While moth spotting, a woman watched as three men on horseback pulled up beside her, escorting a coach. The witness reports seeing a woman inside, wearing a cloak, and with feathers in her hair, but her face consisted of smooth flesh - no eyes, nose or mouth were visible. After the witness screamed, the coach and horses rode off.

properties.name: Juan White
properties.description: A local witch, Juan White is thought to now haunt this location. She was seen in 2004 by a couple of people who also heard galloping horses just prior to their sighting, though they could not see any of the creatures.

properties.name: Smith on Horseback
properties.description: An owner of a horse once hanged a blacksmith for failing to complete a piece of work within a tight time limit - the date of the death is marked by the appearance of a large white horse carrying the dead smith (or the phantom smith opening the gates, depending on the source). Another report says that the ghost of the smith does not actually appear but unlocks and opens the gates. A spirit in the house itself, that of a grey lady, has been seen several times, and there are unsubstantiated reports that it has been exorcised.

properties.name: Phantom Horse and Rider
properties.description: Fred Archer, a famed jockey from the late nineteenth century, has been blamed for accidents on the Newmarket Course. He has also been seen riding on the back of his favourite mount along Hamilton Stud Lane and the heath.
properties.location_name: Newmarket (Suffolk) - Hamilton Stud Lane, and Newmarket Racecourse

properties.name: Flying Horse
properties.description: This barrow is home to a headless horseman who travels several metres above the ground.

properties.name: Lady Jane Grey
properties.description: Raised here as a child, Lady Jane's tormented shade now haunts both the mansion and the grounds surrounding the building. One ghost story says that she arrives in a coach pulled by four black headless horses at the ruins of Bradgate on Christmas Eve.

properties.name: Horses & Coach
properties.description: A team of four horses pulling a spectral coach reportedly passes the house.
properties.location_name: North Craven (North Yorkshire) - Road outside Brunton House

properties.name: Roundheads
properties.description: At least one witness has reported seeing spectral Roundheads on horseback ride through this area.

properties.name: Children
properties.description: The ghosts of a white woman and of two children have been reported in this wooded area. While the woman in white has been named as Mary Chaworth Musters, the children are thought to have been murdered by their father William Saville, hanged for the crime in 1844. The sound of horses has also been reported.

properties.name: Trotting
properties.description: Traders have reported the sounds of horses trotting on the cobbles, and occasionally cries for help from long forgotten accidents.
properties.location_name: NW1 (Greater London) - Camden Market (Stables Market section)

properties.name: Trotting
properties.description: Sounds of a horse moving around this area were frequently reported prior to the Second World War. More recently, one witness reported her dog taking bites at an invisible presence within the public house.
properties.date: Horses reported in the 1930s, other events in 2004

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: A coach pulled by two headless horses was spotted by a boy transporting wheat during the early hours of the morning.
properties.location_name: Oakley Down (Dorset) - Area near the barrow

properties.name: Headless Driver
properties.description: A coach and four driven by a headless figure is said to travel along this road.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: A woman in the sitting in the passenger side of a car opened her window as the vehicle left the church. Suddenly both the woman and driver heard a horse trotting alongside the vehicle, even though no animal could be seen. As the vehicle reached the main road, the trotting ceased.
properties.location_name: Odstock (Wiltshire) - Lane adjoining St Marys Church

properties.name: Floating Lights
properties.description: Paranormal occurrences have happened here, including lights that have appeared and floated around the house, sounds of horses trotting up to the back door (no such creature being found on investigation), and the roof glowing so violently, witnesses outside thought the building was on fire.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This headless horseman is said to either be a murdered pedlar or a knight killed in combat with the Scots. Either way, the figure is said to be unlucky (with witnesses either dying or losing livestock) and an exorcism once performed on the phantom horseman by seven preachers failed.
properties.location_name: Onecote (Staffordshire) - Road from Leek to Onecote, and Butterton Moor

properties.name: Sir Geoffrey de Mandeville
properties.description: Sir Geoffrey, complete with red cloak and jangling spurs, appears here on horseback and heads towards the church to disappear through its wall. Even though the ghost is said to appear on Christmas Eve, the last verified sighting of Sir Geoffrey was on 17 December 1932.
properties.date: 24 December 2026, 23:30h or 00:00h next due, then every sixth year (reoccurring)
properties.location_name: Outer London: East Barnet (Greater London) - Oak Hill Park

properties.name: Turpin and Bess
properties.description: Reportedly once owned by Dick Turpin's grandfather, the highwayman would visit the family when he needed to hide out. His ghostly form has been seen both inside and out, sometimes accompanied by his horse.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Enfield (Greater London) - Rose & Crown public house, Clay Hill

properties.name: Heavy Breathing
properties.description: This ghost was heard several times, breathing heavily around female members of staff, but never being seen. A second entity wears a bowler hat and has been known to ask about the whereabouts of his briefcase - both case and man were in an aircraft that crashed in the late 1940s, killing all on board. A phantom Dick Turpin rides a brown horse across the runways (or cargo sheds), while cleaning staff during the 1970s reported a presence which held them down, applying pressure around their throats. Finally, prior to the airport being built, a phantom Saxon prince reputedly haunted the area close to a pond where he drowned.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Heathrow Airport (Greater London) - General area

properties.name: Invisible Mare
properties.description: Heard but never seen, a horse would gallop up to the gates of the house before stopping.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Lower Bedington (Greater London) - Orchard House (no longer standing)

properties.name: Queen Elizabeth
properties.description: As she lay dying elsewhere, Elizabeth was observed standing at a window of the palace. A few reports say she still haunts the building. The palace may have been the location where Edward Seymour, Lord Protector of England, and Anne Seymour witnessed a 'hand with a bloodied sword' emerge from a wall. Edward was beheaded a short time later. There are also more recent reports of phantom horses' hooves heard clopping around the courtyard.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Richmond (Greater London) - Richmond Palace

properties.name: Horse Drawn Carriage
properties.description: While sitting in bed in his maisonette on the fourth floor, this witness was startled to see the tail end of a black liveried horse drawn carriage fade through his room and wall. He later learnt that a neighbour had died at exactly the time of his sighting. Soon after this, the now empty flat in which the neighbour had died became the scene of strong paranormal disturbances. Though the windows and doors sealed, items in the building were smashed and thrown around, and it was not until an exorcism was carried out that the apartment fell silent.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Slade Green (Greater London) - Maisonettes, Slade Green

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: White Horse Upright
properties.description: This ghostly creature walks around on hind legs, ending the jaunt as it vanishes down an ancient well.

properties.name: Bobby Bannister the Bastard Landlord
properties.description: Being dragged by a ghostly horse, this tragic figure bounces around the roads, his neck broken whilst hunting. One report says that the ghost was laid by a team of holy men who banished it to the bottom of the millstream. Screaming sometimes comes from the millstream, the sounds belonging to a girl who fell and was crushed by the wheel.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: The ghostly team of horses pulling a coach travel aimlessly in the neighbourhood.

properties.name: Young Horseman
properties.description: Often heard and sometimes even seen, the horseman is thought to have been a young lad who stole a horse from his master to speed up the journey to meet his secret lover. In his haste, he galloped past the scene of an accident involving a cart - he could have saved the life of the driver who was trapped underneath, but lust took priority and the rider left the site without offering assistance. The victim of the accident later died but managed to curse the lad who rode on by; hence, the boy's ghost now roams.

properties.name: Headless Horses
properties.description: Pulling a coach, this team of headless horses manifests around the season of goodwill. Legend states if stared at, it will whisk the witness away to the nether realm.

properties.name: Horse & Dog
properties.description: A man who wagered that he could cross the track in record time fell from his horse and snapped his neck. The man on horseback, now minus a head, with a hound in tow, tries again and again to make the journey once a year.
properties.location_name: Penselwood (Wiltshire) - Sloane Track - between Penselwood and Stourton

properties.name: Smoking Hooves
properties.description: After an earthquake here, an area of the beach was discovered to be covered in small hoof prints, some smoking, and others spouting water. Some blamed the Devil, others a phantom horse and carriage that travels through the town.

properties.name: Horse-Drawn Coach
properties.description: A woman heard a horse drawn coach outside her place of employment, but when she looked out of the window, nothing could be seen.

properties.name: Hounds All Black and Wide-Eyed
properties.description: These woods were the scene of a wild hunt, which included large black hounds and loathsome riders on the backs of mares and goats.
properties.date: Between Lent and Easter (reoccurring), but last reported 1100s
properties.location_name: Peterborough (Cambridgeshire) - In all the woods between Stamford and Peterborough

properties.name: Headless Horseman
