properties.description: Witnessed by at least two different people since 1990, a headless horseman has been seen trotting down the high street during the early hours.

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: One man was told by a gardener that a phantom monk could be heard ascending the staircase in the tower. The man went on to spend three years living in the tower and never heard a thing. Another local story states that the driveway of the building is haunted by a phantom coach and four.
properties.date: Monk in mid twentieth century, coach unknown
properties.location_name: Pewsey (Wiltshire) - Fyfield Manor, the Monk's Tower

properties.name: Horses & Carriage
properties.description: Seen trotting down the main street, the history of the carriage pulled by two horses (or four, depending on the source) is unknown.

properties.name: Horse and Coach
properties.description: Mrs Hackford was driving her car at around 4pm when a horse and carriage carrying an old lady and two men in Victorian clothing rode past her in the opposite direction. Because of the weirdness of the situation, the witness looked behind her, only to realise the coach was no longer there.

properties.name: Hanging Judge Jeffreys
properties.description: As well as the notorious judge's ghost drifting around the building, one witness has also reported seeing the shade of a young blonde girl. She also haunts the road outside the building, causing passing motorists to stop sharply. The road in turn is also haunted by three horsemen.

properties.name: Bloody Horse
properties.description: Driving past the mine in bad weather, a driver and his passenger felt a strange negative sensation wash over them. The driver slammed on the brakes and as the car stopped, they spotted a bloody horse standing in the lights. The driver started to climb out of his car to move the horse out of the road, but the creature had gone - there was a steep drop to one side of the road and a wire fence to the other side.
properties.location_name: Pool (Cornwall) - Road and tunnel close to South Crofty mine

properties.name: Runaway Horses
properties.description: These horses were all killed when they broke away from their coach on the hill and lost control on a tight bend. They are heard re-enacting their final moments.
properties.location_name: Porlock (Somerset) - Road leading downhill to the town

properties.name: Tall, White Figure
properties.description: The pale figure most commonly seen is thought to be Charlotte (see Portchester church entry), though there are other reports of a monk who walks about outside the front of the castle, a Roman soldier, and a lady on the back of a white horse.

properties.name: Lord Rokeby
properties.description: Lord Rokeby races around the area, minus his head, being drawn by a team of four horses.

properties.name: Lady Carew in a Coach Pulled by Four Black Horses
properties.description: Occurring on the stroke of midnight, the phantom coach driven by a skeleton is seen to crash into the side of the bridge and fall into the river below. After selling her soul to the Devil, Lady Carew was taken away by two skeletons driving a coach pulled by four black horses on her wedding day. The coach disappeared on the bridge over the River Thurne, and the scene is repeated every 31 May. Another version of the story says the phantom is Lady Evelyn, who died when her coach crashed in 1741.

properties.name: Charging Horseman
properties.description: Killed by a Lancastrian arrow in the 1400's, this glowing messenger is doomed to carry his cargo forevermore. Another story says he was decapitated by a thin wire that was placed across the road.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: A phantom coach and horses is driven down this lane by a headless horseman. The horseman's whip sparks at each crack.

properties.name: Horse & Chain
properties.description: An invisible team of horses pull an equally invisible coach along this lane. The only indication of their passing is the noise of the horses and the jangling of a chain. The sounds are said to reach the top of Stourton Caundle before ceasing.
properties.location_name: Purse Caundle (Dorset) - Lane between village and Stourton Caundle

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A ghostly horseman has been heard and occasionally observed heading out of the village.

properties.name: Tom King
properties.description: The sound of hooves heard several times from outside the public house are thought to belong to the horse of Tom King, a local highwayman who once frequented the bar.

properties.name: Christopher Bloor
properties.description: Christopher Bloor had a reputation for being a seducer of women. This proved to be Christopher's undoing - he was decapitated by a posse of local men with wives with whom he had slept. The lane was haunted by the headless Bloor, who reputedly drove his coach pulled by headless horses, and ghostly hoofbeats heard around the church.
properties.location_name: Rainham (Kent) - Lane leading to Bloor Place (no longer standing) and near church

properties.name: Man on Black Horse
properties.description: Locals tell of an unknown man on a black horse that is believed to haunt the area. The devil is seen riding across the broad every 31 December; under his arm is the soul of a Colonel who had dared hunt on the Sabbath.

properties.name: Earl Gerald
properties.description: The earl is said to walk under Lough Gur, emerging on the back of a black horse every seven years to visit his former home. Another former earl, James, is also said to haunt the castle - he was murdered by his servants.

properties.name: Something
properties.description: A vague entry in the Laois Burial Grounds Survey 2011 says that the word Dullow is used to describe a phantom, although details of the haunting are not given.

properties.name: Fast Moving Procession
properties.description: A large group of people following a horse drawn hearse have been seen on several occasions, moving much more swiftly than one should expect.
properties.location_name: Ratlinghope (Shropshire) - Road towards village, along the Long Mynd

properties.name: Sound of Horses
properties.description: A local legend says that the phantom horses heard in this area belonged to Roman riders who fell into a large pit trap dug by their enemies.
properties.location_name: Redditch (Hereford & Worcester) - The Lane, Church Hill South

properties.name: Horse with Trap
properties.description: The ghost haunting this road is said to be that of a farmer riding his horse and cart.
properties.location_name: Rettendon (Essex) - Road leading to East Hannington

properties.name: Dog under Horses' Hooves
properties.description: A couple in a trap encountered the Shuck, and it ran under their horses. The driver tried to hit the black dog with his whip, but it passed straight through the creature. The same area is also haunted by a phantom coach, being driven at breakneck speed.

properties.name: Coach & Horses
properties.description: The ghost of a smuggler driving his coach runs around the sandy area of this building, while on some nights a macabre entity is seen climbing out of the sea and enters the rectory. This may be the same entity which asks (in a gruff voice) 'why don't you turn around and look at me?' - those who do so have never seen anything.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This coach pulled by a team of four horses is driven over a piece of private land, where a county lane once ran.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: A farmer heard a galloping horse rapidly approaching him, even though he could not see the creature. Moments later, there was a crashing sound, and a large gust of wind passed the man, even though the creature remained invisible.

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: Together with a fairy, this pale horse rises from the dangerous waters once a year. It has been known to drown people who come too close.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: This lane is reputed to be haunted by the phantom sounds of horses.

properties.name: Coach Crash
properties.description: A phantom coach and horses is seen to crash before vanishing into a wall somewhere in this area.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: A coach pulled by a team of frantic horses reputedly vanishes into a field after crashing through a wall on the roadside.
properties.location_name: Rockfield (Gwent) - Road between Rockfield and Monmouth (Ancre Lane?)

properties.name: Alice Lisle
properties.description: Alice Lisle was the last woman to be executed by decapitation, at the hands of Judge Jeffreys. Her ghost rides around the area in a driverless coach, pulled by headless horses.
properties.location_name: Rockford (Hampshire) - Area around Alice Lisle public house

properties.name: Headless Horsemen
properties.description: This church was said to have been relocated due to the intervention of headless horsemen.

properties.name: White Woman & Coach
properties.description: It is said that a phantom white woman haunts this area, sometimes seen climbing into a ghostly horse-drawn coach which then drives off.

properties.name: Mary Jenning
properties.description: Mary Jenning, a rather large drunken woman, is said to haunt this area, and was observed by two women sheltering under a tree from a sudden downpour. In addition, a phantom female voice can be heard calling out the name 'Daniel'; the woman is never seen but is thought to be a lady who fell to her death from the nearby Wellington Rocks. Finally, a horseman dressed in mauve on the back of a black horse also travels the area.
properties.location_name: Royal Tunbridge Wells (Kent) - Common land opposite The Pantiles

properties.name: Guarded Hoard
properties.description: Magically concealed, the treasure hidden here only stands a chance of discovery when the moon is full. However, if anyone comes to close to discovering the hoard, a phantom horse appears and charges the treasure hunter.

properties.name: Bottled Squire
properties.description: This ghost of a squire driving his coach and horses raised hell in the neighbourhood until exorcised; trapped in a bottle now hidden within the walls of the hotel, he now waits until released again. A recent email received suggests that late night drivers in the area still encounter the phantom coach along Leicester Road.

properties.name: Sister Lucy
properties.description: Sister Lucy ran away from her chapel, but realising she had made a mistake, tried to return. She slipped as she passed the well and drowned in its waters. Her ghost is now said to haunt the area. The same location is also said to be home to a phantom horse.

properties.name: Thundering Hooves
properties.description: Three witnesses standing outside watching the lunar eclipse heard horses loudly galloping across the field ahead of them, although the field was empty. One of the witnesses kept horses of their own and was familiar with the sound.

properties.name: Hunting Party
properties.description: A rider on horseback and his hunting dog are reported to haunt the area around the hall, where their bodies were once discovered while the house was extended. Thomas Tresham, who constructed the building, wrote circa 1584 that he and two servants had heard three loud knocks (as if made from a hammer) emerge from a table.

properties.name: White Horses
properties.description: A pair of white horses would occasionally appear along this road.

properties.name: Misty Horseman
properties.description: Sometimes, around midnight, a thick mist rolls into the churchyard. Soon after, a headless horseman rides in, vanishing into the mist, heading towards an old castle keep.

properties.name: Heavy Breathing
properties.description: A witness travelling this road reported hearing the heavy breathing of galloping horses along this road, but not accompanied by hoof falls. The hedge from which the sound could be heard moving past glowed briefly as the eerie sound climaxed.
properties.location_name: Salcombe (Devon) - B3181 Salcombe to Malborough road

properties.name: Black Hounds
properties.description: The forest is reported to be home of several black dogs which bring ill fortune to any witnesses. There are also stories of a ghostly white deer which darts between the trees, a headless woman riding on horseback, and a coach and horses which leaves the forest and heads towards a nearby farm.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: A phantom horseman can be heard laughing as he drives his team of horses around this area. He was blamed when a local man drowned in a lake that no longer exists. Another phantom male was said to haunt this lake, but what has become of him is not known.
properties.location_name: Scawby (Lincolnshire) - Road north of village, just before the crossroads (geography may have changed since story first published)

properties.name: Lord John Angerstein
properties.description: Lord John's coach, pulled by four headless horses and driven by the lord himself, is said to leave Trafalgar Road and return to his former home on Vanbrugh Road.
properties.location_name: SE10 (Greater London) - Trafalgar Road to Vanbrugh Hill

properties.name: Fox Hunt
properties.description: A local legend says that the five trees are where an old witch coven would meet. One of the witches took the form of a fox and lead the local hunt over the edge of a cliff - the scene is repeated if the weather conditions are right.

properties.name: Two Men with Pack Horses
properties.description: Two men leading pack horses escort the body of Edward the Martyr Prince to his final resting place.

properties.name: Cavalier on Horseback
properties.description: The family which once lived in this house were haunted by a Cavalier who would appear galloping towards the building just prior to a death or bad news.
properties.date: Prior to tragedy in the family

properties.name: Uther Pendragon
properties.description: A large man on horseback seen galloping around on winter nights is said to be Uther Pendragon, though his motivation for being here is unclear.

properties.name: Arthur's Victory
properties.description: This hillfort has been named by some as the location where King Arthur defeated the Saxons. Several phantasmic events also surround the Rings, including the sighting of a ghostly warrior on horseback, a spectral woman wearing a black dress, and an ugly dwarf-like entity which peers into the windows of parked cars. Finally, there are stories of a golden coffin buried in the area.
properties.date: Battle in sixth century(?), entities in late twentieth century

properties.name: Horse and Cart
properties.description: This phantom horse and cart would be seen in the car park and were frequently mistaken as being 'real'. When a pond on the grounds was filled in, the apparition never returned.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - The Crofts public house, Mosborough

properties.name: Coach and Four
properties.description: Appearing at night, this phantom coach pulled by four horses would move at breakneck speed along the roads in and around the village.
properties.location_name: Shepley (South Yorkshire) - Roads around the Shepley War Memorial

properties.name: Hooves on Gravel
properties.description: While walking home through the nearby countryside, a woman reported hearing the overworked horses and the sound of crunching gravel. The site of the occurrence, upon investigation, proved once to be a brick yard.

properties.name: Shadows
properties.description: A former occupier of this property moved out after his dog chased a shadow out of the living room and up the stairs. They also reported hearing horse's hooves and experiencing poltergeist-like behaviour.

properties.name: Peasant Girl
properties.description: A knight took his own life after having his love rejected by a peasant girl. Lying in his own blood, the knight cursed her, and she died soon after. The peasant's ghost remains in the forest. Another entity reported to haunt the area is the wild hunt, complete with headless horseman (thought by some to be the aforementioned knight).
properties.location_name: Silverstone (Northamptonshire) - Whittlebury Forest (and site of the former woodland)

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: This figure on horseback is supposed to date from the late Bronze Age, which probably makes him the UK's oldest ghost. When a nearby barrow was excavated, it revealed the bones of a man and horse. The area is also said to be home to fairies.

properties.name: Headless on Headless
properties.description: The headless highwayman seen around this area rides a headless horse. A road on a hill south of the town is haunted by a woman (who may also be headless) wearing black, who quickly vanishes just before reaching a hill.
properties.location_name: Skipsea (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Roads around town

properties.name: Clifford's Coach
properties.description: The appearance of Lady Ann Clifford in her horse drawn coach was said to herald the death of the owner of the local castle.
properties.date: Midnight, prior to a death in the family (reoccurring)

properties.name: Young Girl on Horseback
properties.description: Legend says that the girl and her horse were both drowned when the mill stream flooded; the sound of the horse's hooves can still be heard. One driver reported seeing a well-dressed person upon a white horse as they travelled between Ivinghoe Aston and Slapton. They feared that they had hit the horse, so stopped, only to find no one there.
properties.location_name: Slapton (Buckinghamshire) - Road from Slaptonbury Mill (no longer standing) to farm on other side of the village

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: Often seen early in the morning, this riderless phantom steed moves towards witnesses and dissolves away seconds before reaching them. The entity may be related to the ghostly carriage pulled by a team of horses which was said to pass through the village along the lane close to the old butcher's shop.
properties.location_name: Slindon (Sussex) - Area around village and Fontwell Park

properties.name: One Man and his Dog
properties.description: Two men witnessed a man with a hound, pursuing several horses along a steep rocky ridge. Man and beasts suddenly vanished - the witnesses were at first concerned that they had all fallen over the edge of a cliff, though were unable to find any bodies.

properties.name: Army Marching On
properties.description: Over two dozen witnesses saw a mass of troops ascending the mountain on horseback, though there were no real soldiers in the area. The ghostly army had been seen twice before, always on the same date.
properties.location_name: Souter Fell (Cumbria) - Side of Mountain visible from Wilton Hall

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: Ghostly horses and their riders are reported to haunt this region.

properties.name: Judge Peck
properties.description: Perhaps connected to the Spixworth Manor ghost, a local tale warns of the phantom Judge Peck who travels the area in a coach.

properties.name: Horse Drawn Hearse
properties.description: Several spooky manifestations were claimed by the manor house, included a spectral hearse driven up the driveway, a piano which played itself, a man bearing a lantern who resembled a night watchman, and a ghostly woman holding a candle.

properties.name: Sobbing
properties.description: The sound of a lady's gentle sobs has been heard diffusing through the fabric of the pub. The common outside the pub is home to a highway woman on horseback, after whom the building is named.
properties.location_name: St Albans (Hertfordshire) - Wicked Lady public house

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This spectral coach and four is seen passing through the area, vanishing in a puff of blue smoke and leaving only the reek of sulphur.
properties.location_name: St Andrews Bay (Fife) - West Sands Road, and general area

properties.name: Galloping
properties.description: The sounds of horses pulling a carriage have been reported coming from this stretch of road during stormy nights.
properties.location_name: St Just (Cornwall) - Road between St Just & Tregeseal

properties.name: Three Horsemen
properties.description: Three figures riding white horses were said to gallop loudly down the hill.

properties.name: Lady Matthias
properties.description: A headless coachman driving headless horses pulling a headless lady in a coach would travel around the former court building. The woman in the coach, named as Lady Matthias, was exorcised many years prior to the demolition of the building.
properties.location_name: Stackpole Elidor (Dyfed) - Stackpole Court (no longer standing)

properties.name: Bloody Horseman
properties.description: This phantom rider on a white horse has his face covered in blood. The entities are said to slowly descend into the ground.
properties.location_name: Staindrop (Durham) - A688 between town and Raby Castle

properties.name: The Baron on Horseback
properties.description: A baron who took over the land on which a monastery stood had the building dismantled and the stones used to construct his manor house. He was cursed by monk, however, and fell off his horse and broke his neck before the house was finished. His shade still rides around the neighbourhood.
properties.date: Winter nights (reoccurring), but last seen nineteenth century?

properties.name: Turpin
properties.description: It was once believed that the ghost of Dick Turpin passed by this inn on Wednesday nights - the thud of horse's hooves could be heard, and some people reported seeing a man on horseback just outside of the building.

properties.name: Scarlet Rider
properties.description: This figure on horseback, dressed in an unmistakable red, appears once a year - in 1630 he is said to have taken the life of the owner of the hall.
properties.location_name: Stockport (Greater Manchester) - Bramall (also known as Bramhall) Hall

properties.name: Wild Hunt
properties.description: Hounds, horses and hunters all pass through the village while chasing the souls of the damned. It is not known for sure whether the hunt last passed through this way in 1960 as no one dared peek out from their windows.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Driving a phantom coach over road and field, an angry ghost of a former squire is doomed to relive his journey over and over.
properties.location_name: Stoke Abbott (Dorset) - Between Stoke Abbott and Beaminster

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A horseman with his caparisoned horse was said to travel over this bridge, the sound of his jangling armour causing unease to anyone unfortunate enough to be nearby.

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A driver reported glancing at her rear view mirror and seeing a horseman wearing a long black cape leap out into the road. The horseman was so close, the driver thought the rider would hit the car, but he vanished.
properties.location_name: Stow on the Wold (Gloucestershire) - A429 Fosse Way, heading towards Warwick

properties.name: Outline of Coach
properties.description: A couple out walking in the 1970s watched in amazement as the ghostly outline of a horseless coach passed them. In 2013, a group of friends watched as an old fashioned car silently moved by. No driver could be seen and as they tried to follow, it vanished. Other witnesses have seen the phantom, and others have reported headless horsemen or of hearing galloping.
properties.location_name: Stroud (Gloucestershire) - Road running alongside Rodborough Common, and apartments built near Rodborough Manor

properties.name: Coach & Horses
properties.description: A local legend says a phantom coach haunts this area.

properties.name: Danny Grimshaw
properties.description: This ghostly figure, charging around the roads on the back of a white horse whilst waving a bloody, straight edged razor, has survived at least one exorcism attempt.
properties.location_name: Sutton Courtenay (Oxfordshire) - Hobbyhorse Road and near the local power plant

properties.name: Jeremiah Abershaw
properties.description: No wombles here, only a ghostly padfoot looking for victims after being hanged for his crimes two hundred years ago. On two occasions the ghostly entity has been seen wearing convicts clothing. Abershaw is sometimes heard on horseback, hooves heard thumping on the ground, yet the animal never appears.

properties.name: Brown Bear
properties.description: Bears, brown, white and black in colour, have all been reported in this area - a once popular place for bear baiting. A man on horseback has also been sited, riding in the area, and an unidentified home along the street was reputedly haunted by the sounds of choking.
properties.location_name: SW3 (Greater London) - Glebe Place, Chelsea (aka Henry VIII hunting lodge)

properties.name: Highwayman with Horse
properties.description: Appearing under the light of the full moon, the ghostly highwayman on horseback is reported to say, 'Your money or your life', and other such cliche expressions.

properties.name: Running Rein
properties.description: Racehorse Running Rein (or Running Rain) was put down on the property when he became part of a legal dispute (possibly over the horse's true age) and his owner became unwilling to hand the mare to another trainer. The creature could be heard at night, galloping around the site.

properties.name: Battle of Bramham
properties.description: The sound of horses galloping on the field is thought to be those forever fleeing the nearby site of battle, which took several hundred lives.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: Drivers reported hearing the sound like a hammer striking an anvil before seeing a phantom horse being led across the road, both man and beast cut off at the knees as if walking a much older track since built upon.
properties.location_name: Talke (Staffordshire) - Road just outside the village

properties.name: Black Coach
properties.description: This area of moorland was haunted by a black carriage pulled by two black, headless mares. The entity was never seen again after the Reverend Doidge exorcised the entity.

properties.name: William Doggett
properties.description: Having borrowed large amounts of money to build the house, William Doggett committed suicide when he realised he could never pay off the loan. Doggett's ghost was heard walking around the house and blamed for opening and closing doors. It was also seen, headless, driving a coach in the grounds.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: There is said to be a secret passage connected this site and Dally Castle, with phantom horses heard running the route at midnight.

properties.name: Plotter
properties.description: A horseman who rides through the village on this date was considered to be a member of the Gunpowder Plot escaping from London.

properties.name: Drake
properties.description: Pulled by black headless horses, Sir Frances Drake drives a dark hearse down these roads whenever his drum is heard to beat (see Yelverton (Devon) entry).
properties.location_name: Tavistock (Devon) - Road between town and Plymouth

properties.name: Headless Group
properties.description: A headless team of horses driven by a headless coachman carries a headless lady in a coach along this road every night. As they reach the crossroads known as Sampson Cross, some ten miles from their starting point, they all disappear in a large explosion. Some people believe the entities have been laid, and now rest in a nearby lake.
properties.location_name: Tenby (Dyfed) - Between Tenby and Sampson Cross

properties.name: Heinous Horse
properties.description: Two young adults driving along this road witnessed an otherworldly horse with the face of a man, the creature's eyes bulging from their sockets. The manifestation stood in the road, blocking their path for two minutes before vanishing.
properties.location_name: Termonfeckin (County Louth) - Road passing Rath House

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: A local farmer told his friends that he watched a headless horseman sitting upon a white steed pass by this crossroads.

properties.name: Funeral Procession
properties.description: A wooden wagon bearing a coffin, pulled by jet black horses, has been spotted along this old dyke. The spectacle vanishes if witnesses become too close.
properties.location_name: The Wansdyke (Wiltshire) - Various places along the post Roman dyke

properties.name: Little George
properties.description: The seven year old George fell from a wooden horse in May 1559, cracking his skull on the wall of the bridge and dying from his injury. The young ghost haunted the area for many years (riding around on a headless wooded horse) before being exorcised.
properties.location_name: Thetford (Norfolk) - St George's Nunnery - Nun's bridges

properties.name: Mustard Coloured Coach
properties.description: On the stroke of midnight, a mustard coloured coach is believed to drive down the driveway of the ruined building. Its owner is thought to be Duke de Morrow, who lived in the building at the beginning of the twentieth century.

properties.name: Radiant Boy
properties.description: Lighting up the night with his brilliance, this lad rode past the witness on horseback, disappearing across a field.
properties.location_name: Thirsk (North Yorkshire) - Road to the town from Topcliffe, exact location unknown

properties.name: Horse & Cart on Driveway
properties.description: The present Lord's father told stories of hearing horses and wheels on the gravel driveway; on investigation, there was nothing ever seen.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: One unnamed lane in the village is the haunt of a headless horse. The village must attract decapitated entities, as a headless pig is also said to haunt the general area.

properties.name: Merlin
properties.description: A place featuring in Arthurian legend (Arthur is said to have been born here), the cave under the castle is said to be the one time home of Merlin. His spirit now shows a reluctance to leave the place. A ghostly horseman was reported by one witness, who promptly named the spirit as King Arthur.

properties.name: Galloping Horses
properties.description: A ghostly herd of horses is said to haunt this area.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pale figure is said to be one of a handful of ghosts reported here over the years, normally seen in the garden. A woman who starved to death in a bedroom and an old nurse also continue to reside here, while a headless horseman has been reported just outside the grounds of the building. In 1995 a member of staff spotted a glowing man wearing a cloak who faded away, remarkably like an event in 1976 where a young man watched a cloaked figure wearing a tricorn hat glide silently across the road outside the hall before passing effortlessly over the hedgerow.
properties.date: Mostly unknown, but cloaked figure October 1976 and circa March 1995

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: A phantom coach pulled by ghostly horses was spotted as it passed through the village.

properties.name: Trotting Horse
properties.description: A newspaper article from 1956 suggested that the phantom house which would trot across the mountain was only the sound of distant (real) horses that drifted in on the wind.

properties.name: Galloping Horsemen
properties.description: A group of four or six horsemen wearing brown leather were seen charging across this bypass.
properties.location_name: Tremadog (Gwynedd) - Bypass close to the Tremadog roundabout

properties.name: Bolting Horses
properties.description: The pool was once the scene of a tragedy when a horse drawn carriage crashed into it, drowning all on board. This mishap can still occasionally be heard. The pool was once believed to be so deep that it could never be measured.

properties.name: Red Coach
properties.description: This large red coach, drawn by four horses, charged at a motorist driving the opposite way down the narrow road - the apparition vanished just before impact.
properties.location_name: Truro (Cornwall) - Road between Truro and Mevagissey

properties.name: Headless Rider
properties.description: A beautiful white horse carrying a headless rider gallops across the valley when the night sky is right. The phantom is either a peddler or a soldier, depending on the legend.

properties.name: Horses
properties.description: While picnicking with her daughter, this witness heard a stampede of horses close by, but no animals could be seen.
properties.location_name: Twechar (Dumbartonshire) - Antonine Wall - Bar Hill Fort

properties.name: Young Male Rider
properties.description: The area around the castle is haunted by a decapitated horseman. The ghost is thought to be a man who tried to marry a girl residing at the castle, but her brothers chased him away. During the pursuit, the young man fell from his horse and lost his head.

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: The famous white horse seen on the hill from the village is reported to leave once a year (or every 100 years, depending on the storyteller) to have its feet shod at the nearby Wayland's Smithy. Another local legend states that the horse is not really a horse, but the dragon slain by St George on that very hill.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Heard but not seen, a phantom coach occasionally crosses the bridge.

properties.name: Stagecoach
properties.description: This black and red coach is still drawn along the roads, pulled by four phantom horses.

properties.name: Horse and Pedestrian
properties.description: One local reported witnessing a phantom horse pass by, closely followed by the sounds of footsteps in pursuit of the creature. During the 1920s, two skeletons were discovered close to site of the haunting.
properties.location_name: Ventnor (Isle of Wight) - Old Park Road

properties.name: Carriage
properties.description: A nineteenth century coach and horses are said to still run along this stretch of road. Another ghost reported was a shadowy woman in shabby clothing with a face which resembled death incarnate.
properties.date: Coach unknown, woman possibly early twentieth century
properties.location_name: W2 (Greater London) - Bayswater Road, area near Hyde Park

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: Moving at breakneck speed, a phantom team of horses pulling a coach race across the bridge before vanishing on the other side.
properties.date: Once per year, on a night of a full moon

properties.name: Fallen Horses
properties.description: Walking her dog across the bridge, Amanda West was frozen with fear when the sound of horses passed her - with no horses to be seen. Without warning, she then heard a loud splash. Looking into the river below, the water was calm, but the sound of panicking horses could now be heard coming from below. She later found out that the original bridge had collapsed in 1836 and a team of horses pulling a cart had drowned.
properties.location_name: Wakefield (West Yorkshire) - Denby Dale Road bridge, and river below

properties.name: Equine Entities
properties.description: Said to be heard only during the day, phantom horses gallop across the common. In addition, reports of a phantom man have been frequent since the 1940s, appearing both on the common and in the church ruins.

properties.name: White Horse
properties.description: This pale riderless horse gallops over the gardens, twice a decade. The hotel itself is haunted by a young man, who some speculate to be the former rider of the horse.

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A coach pulled by a headless horse runs down Quay Lane on dark nights, believed to be driven by a former owner of Raydon Hall.
properties.location_name: Wangford - SW of Thetford (Suffolk) - Raydon Hall, Quay Lane

properties.name: Rider
properties.description: The threat of spotting a phantom woman on horseback ensured locals would not use this route after dark.

properties.name: Phantom Horseman
properties.description: A phantom horseman, perhaps connected to the nearby battle site of Edge Hill, visits this area twice a year. One local man claimed to have seen a lantern being swung along a road, although no one held it.

properties.name: Caesar
properties.description: Several ghosts have been reported here, including Caesar (and an accompanying army). Other people have reported an elderly man with a long white beard who moves between the trees, and the pounding hooves of invisible horses. Phantom druids (or the Devil himself, depending on the source) appear if you run around the fort seven times at midnight.

properties.name: Force
properties.description: A witness walking home in the early hours of the morning was pushed two metres by an unseen force. No one else could be seen. The witness said the sensation resembled a push from a horse and counted it as one of their worst experiences.
properties.location_name: Waterford (County Waterford) - By the old infirmary, towards John's Park / Polberry Cross Road

properties.name: Headless Horse
properties.description: The headless horse which runs along this road was said to have been a casualty during the civil war.

properties.name: Horse and Half A Rider
properties.description: Recorded by the local press, a man reported that his wife had seen a horse pass in front of the car in which she was travelling, and upon the creature was the bottom half of a rider - the figure was not visible above legs. The driver of the car did not spot anything.
properties.location_name: Wendover (Buckinghamshire) - Steep road off the M40, though Wendover Woods

properties.name: Speeding Horseman
properties.description: Seen galloping at the speed of an arrow, this horseman vanishes once he enters the dense woodland.
properties.location_name: West Auckland (Durham) - Route between town and Hamsterley Forest

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: Pulled by a team of four horses, a spectral coach haunts the area.
properties.location_name: West Kennett (Wiltshire) - Road, exact area unknown

properties.name: Headless Driver
properties.description: A ghostly coach pulled by horses and driven by a headless coachman reportedly travels along this lane. The coachman was said to have been decapitated during a highway robbery.
properties.location_name: West Lulworth (Dorset) - Lane along Coach Lane Gates (gates may no longer stand)

properties.name: Pitch Forked Dog
properties.description: A farmhand discovered a dog laying in the hay - he poked it with his pitchfork, causing the entity to vanish. The sound of a coach has also been heard as it stops by the front of the house, though nothing is seen.

properties.name: Mad Maude
properties.description: Burnt at the stake for having an affair with a monk, Maude wanders the hotel, which was once a convent. The eerie sounds of an invisible horse and coach moving around the courtyard have been reported by more than one visitor.
properties.location_name: Weston on the Green (Oxfordshire) - Weston Manor Hotel

properties.name: Old Soldiers
properties.description: The spirits here reportedly include voices that call out to witnesses over the nearby River Carey, and horsemen who gallop silently along. People have also reported pockets of cold air, and watching a white woman glide over the marsh, near where her lover was murdered by Royalists. On the anniversary of the battle, sounds of fighting have been heard.

properties.name: Coach and Four
properties.description: It was once said that a ghostly coach pulled by four horses and driven by a headless figure would pass through the village and vanish into the church wall.

properties.name: Officer on Horseback
properties.description: Two hunters reported seeing a locally known Captain riding his horse over the warren, even though several months had passed since the officer's death.

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This ghostly coach is pulled by a team of six grey horses.

properties.name: Horse?
properties.description: While staying in a house along this road, a witness heard a horse walking outside on the gravel - when they looked from the window, nothing could be seen.

properties.name: Sir Laurence Tanfield
properties.description: A former landowner in the area, Sir Laurence returned after dying and can be seen driving a coach pulled by four black horses. It is said that bad luck falls on any witnesses.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: After a spectral horse and carriage stops at the top of the driveway, a woman climbs out and walks to the hall's door to be met by a long dead butler. A similar story says that Anne Boleyn travels in a coach along the roads surrounding the Hall around the same time of year.

properties.name: Horse
properties.description: A driver spotted a white horse crossing the road ahead and commented to her passenger, who said she could not see anything. As the driver reached the spot the horse crossed, she realised there was nowhere the horse could have gone.

properties.name: Coffin
properties.description: A coffin has been spotted on the roadside here, though it disappears if looked at closely. Some believe it belongs to someone who committed suicide, denying them the right to be buried in a churchyard, being buried close to this spot instead. A phantom black horse is also said to haunt this area.
properties.location_name: Wimborne (Dorset) - B3078, close to Clapgate Junction

properties.name: Sounds of Assault
properties.description: The sounds of several miners attacking and murdering Clara and Allan, a young eloping couple, can be heard in this Derbyshire area. One version of the story the miners died or went mad one-by-one, with the final miner confessing to the crime and claiming the ghost of Clara followed him everywhere. Clara may also be seen riding her horse through the area

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: It was said that horse and carriage could sometimes be heard outside this coach house, even though they could never be seen.
properties.location_name: Winsley (Wiltshire) - Converted coach house at Winsley Manor

properties.name: Horse and Cart
properties.description: This spectral horse and cart continue to retrace the final journey which abruptly ended when the creature fell into the pond and drowned.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: Appearing at midnight on Christmas Eve, a phantom coach pulled by four ghostly horses gallop along this road. Another story (or a variant of the first) states a spectral horse would charge along the road dragging chains which bounced along the ground.

properties.name: Headless Horseman
properties.description: This decapitated figure on the back of a stallion still rides around the area where Crowden Hall once stood.
properties.location_name: Woodhead (Derbyshire) - Area where Crowden Hall once stood (demolished 1937)

properties.name: Headless Horses
properties.description: Rumoured to be a battleground between Britons and Romans, the sounds of battle, accompanied by the site of headless horses, have been reported. The battle was said to be so intense, the valley filled with blood.
properties.location_name: Woodmanton (Wiltshire) - Location known as Patty's Bottom

properties.name: Coach
properties.description: This horse drawn coach drawn by four horses drives by the house, heading towards the River Frome (or Bindon Abbey passing over the fifteenth century stone bridge over the river. Only those who have Turbeville blood can view the phantom.
properties.location_name: Wool (Dorset) - Hethfelton House and/or stone bridge close to Bindon Abbey

properties.name: Woman
properties.description: This phantom woman wearing a flowing grey dress is said to be quite beautiful and walks from the ruins to the nearby River Frome.

properties.name: Guarded Secret Tunnel
properties.description: Leading to an untold amount of wealth, the tunnel hidden in this hillfort is guarded by a pair of snake-like dragons. The ghost of a druid is reported to haunt the site looking for this treasure, and the sound of phantom galloping horses have also been reported. Finally, another legend says the structure was formed by earth falling off the Devil's shovel as he tried to dig a hole in the South Downs.

properties.name: Galloping
properties.description: Reports slightly differed regarding this phantom horse. Although never seen, the creature would either be heard galloping, or a slower steady clop like a shire horse pulling a heavy load.
properties.location_name: Wrington (Somerset) - Lane through woodland between village and Cleeve

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: Horsemen
properties.description: Both ghostly horsemen and a phantom car have reported around this junction. The area is considered an accident black spot, begging the question whether ghosts cause the accidents or are they the products?
properties.location_name: Wyke (West Yorkshire) - A58/A641 junction, also known as Hell Fire Corner

properties.name: Screaming Horses
properties.description: Locals say that the horses belonged to a Roman chariot; the driver lost control in the ice and snow, and the entire team fell into a deep ditch and died.

properties.name: Nobleman
properties.description: Having lost an illegal duel, this figure died; his ghost now staggers around the area, fooling people into believing he is still alive prior to vanishing. A man on horseback also appears in the park and is named as Dick Turpin.

properties.name: Bathing Mermaid
properties.description: Twelve people watched a beautiful pale woman washing herself in the sea, with what appeared to be a black tail splashing around behind her.

properties.name: Shape Changers
properties.description: The mermaids said to live in this area would be capable of changing their shape as they came on to land. They would also be seen sitting on the rocks playing harps.

properties.name: Women with Child
properties.description: Crofter Colin Campbell raised his rifle to fire at what he thought was an otter eating a fish but stopped when he realised the creature was a mermaid holding a child. The creature dived into the sea after Campbell made a noise.
properties.location_name: Barra (Outer Hebrides) - Reef in Caolas Cumhan

properties.name: Swan Eater
properties.description: An unknown large creature was seen to attack several swans from beneath the water of the lake - it is believed to be a type of large catfish, which can grow up to sixteen feet in length. There has also been at least one mermaid sighting in the lake, and Sir Lancelot's body is supposed to be buried nearby.

properties.name: Mer Children
properties.description: Three men from Douglas found two merchildren on the rocks. One was already dead, but they saved the second child and took it to their town. The creature was described as around 60.3 centimetres in length and brown in colour, although the scales around its tail were slightly violet, and the hair on its head light green.
properties.location_name: Calf of Man (Isle of Man) - Rocky area, exact location not known

properties.name: Shorted Armed Mermaid
properties.description: Watched by a farmer for two hours, this mermaid had a pale upper body and a red/grey tail covered in hair. Its face was human, with deep-set eyes and short neck. The creature combed the hair on its head with what looked like short, stubby arms before diving into the water and washing its torso.

properties.name: Teenager
properties.description: Henry Reynolds spotted a merman off the coast which he initially believed to be a pale skinned teenager before noticing its brown tail which waved about. While the body and arms looked human, the arms and hands looked too short and thick, while the creature's nose was long and sharp. Reynolds went to find other witnesses, but by the time he returned, the merman had gone.
properties.location_name: Castlemartin (Dyfed) - Off the coast in the area

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: This mermaid was so upset at the way she was treated by onlookers, she drove to the bottom of the pool and never returned. It was thought that she guarded a mass of treasure.

properties.name: Mermaid Curse
properties.description: The mistress of the castle ordered the destruction of a black rock which stood just a short distance away, to prevent a mermaid sitting upon it and singing. As revenge, the mermaid cursed the family, ensuring they all died without leaving an heir for the estate.

properties.name: Macphie's Captor
properties.description: A mermaid kidnapped a man by the name of Macphie and held him in a cave along the island's shore. Macphie was given any item he wished for but remained unhappy until he managed to escape. The mermaid gave chase, but Macphie tossed his dog into the sea. Dog and mermaid fought until they both died from their injuries.
properties.location_name: Colonsay (Argyll and Bute) - Unknown cave along the coast

properties.name: Llys Helig
properties.description: Llys Helig was a mythical palace, which was sunk to punish its evil owner. The ruins of the walls can sometimes be seen at low tide, though in recent years it has been discovered that the rocks are a natural formation. Mermaids have also been seen in these waters, and one was said to have cursed the town of Conway after she was washed up on the beach and no one offered to help her back into the sea.

properties.name: Mermaids
properties.description: These women of the water were seen dozens of times during the last twenty years of the nineteenth century. One was said to have been shot, but the body was not recovered.

properties.name: Treasure Guardians
properties.description: A team of mermaids and mermen are reported to guard a hidden cave full of treasure.
properties.location_name: Duncansby Head (Highland) - Cave system off coast

properties.name: Captured Fisherman
properties.description: A good looking fisherman once caught the eye of a mermaid who fell in love with him - she kidnapped the fellow and held him in a secret cave under the sea, where he remains to this day...

properties.name: Wish Granter
properties.description: A local man caught a mermaid on the rocks, only releasing the creature after she agreed to grant three wishes. One of the wishes ensured the man became a skilled doctor, another granted the gift of prophecy, but the third wish, to become a great singer, apparently failed.

properties.name: Vanishing Twitchers
properties.description: Two birdwatchers that vanished without trace on the island after arriving with 86 other people were thought by locals to have been taken by merfolk. An extensive search by the coastguard could find no trace of the missing people.

properties.name: Well Dweller
properties.description: An old story, most likely told to prevent children playing too close to the well, said a mermaid waited in the water at the bottom. She was ready to drown children who touched the water's edge.
properties.location_name: Fornham All Saints (Suffolk) - Well in the village (exact location unknown)

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: Anyone who sees the creature is said to gain immortality. One man who came here once a year lived to be 104 (so the story goes)!
properties.location_name: Hayfield (Derbyshire) - Mermaid's Pool, near Kinder Reservoir

properties.name: Sea Folk
properties.description: The area around the island was said to be home to many mermaids and kelpies.

properties.name: White Figure
properties.description: Neil McIntosh, steering a boat between the two islands in stormy weather, glimpsed a white human figure in the sea. McIntosh thought the creature to be a mermaid, although admitted he did not have much of an opportunity to examine it.
properties.location_name: Isle of Canna (Highland) - Sea, about quarter of the way between island and Skye

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: A mermaid perched on a rock threw herself into the sea when approached by two local men walking along the beach. After the mermaid entered the sea, the witnesses could see the creature's upper body resembled a woman.
properties.location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - Beach on north end of island

properties.name: Hybrid
properties.description: Folklorist Walter Traill Dennison recorded in 1893 that there were decedents of a union between a male selkie and a woman living on the island - you could tell who the offspring were because they had webbed hands and feet.
properties.location_name: Isle of Sanday (Highland) - Exact area not known

properties.name: Halibut-Tailed creature
properties.description: Three (or six) fishermen claimed to have caught a ninety centimetre long mermaid while at sea. The creature had arms around 23 centimetres in length with webbed fingers, and while it had blue eyes and nostrils, no ears or chin could be seen. It possessed a tail similar to a halibut and had two fins on its shoulders. After listening to the creature wail for three hours, the fishermen threw it back into the sea.
properties.location_name: Isle of Yell (Shetland) - Thirty miles off the coast

properties.name: Mermaid?
properties.description: A mermaid which was spotted several times in this area was described as a 'normal' woman, leading one to conclude that maybe she was..?

properties.name: Bloodied mermaid
properties.description: A mermaid who frequented the lake would often steal wine from the crypt beneath the church (or the cellar of Newhall, depending on the storyteller). She was stabbed (or shot) when caught but managed to drag herself back to the lake before dying. The waters are said to turn red with her blood every forty years.
properties.date: Every forty years, exact date unknown
properties.location_name: Killone Lake (County Clare) - Waters of the lake

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: A report exists of a seven foot long mer-creature being seen here, bathing on the rocks.

properties.name: Sailor
properties.description: Leaving no footprints as he crosses the sand, this bearded sailor is one of the many who lost their lives in the sea nearby. He is particularly noted for the large brass buttons on his jacket and for shouting 'All on this beach is mine!' at some visitors during the 1940s. A red-headed mermaid has also been reported in the area.

properties.name: Killer Mermaid
properties.description: The fish woman surfaces to comb her hair at midnight - anyone approaching too close are dragged down into the deep waters with her. One legend says that the mermaid last appeared in the nineteenth century to prevent her pool being drained, threatening to flood the nearby town of Leek if people proceeded to dig away at her home.

properties.name: Singing
properties.description: Although this creature has not been seen, it can be heard singing - a ship will sink nearby exactly seven days later.

properties.name: Seaton
properties.description: Upset with the residents of the village Seaton, a mermaid caused all the buildings to be buried by sand.

properties.name: Angry Mermaid
properties.description: These waters were once the swimming area of a mermaid renowned for her bad temperament.
properties.location_name: Lornty (Perth and Kinross) - Waters between town and Loch Benachally

properties.name: Abducted Woman
properties.description: A farmer spotted a beautiful woman leave the lough and forced her to return to his farmhouse. Eventually she agreed to stay with him, on the condition that she never glazed upon the lake again. Twenty years passed and the farmer and the mermaid had several children. While waiting for the farmer to return from the fields on a clear day, the mermaid spotted the lough in the distance and fled towards it, never to be seen again.
properties.location_name: Lough Sheelin (County Meath) - Waters of the lough

properties.name: Thieving Mermaid
properties.description: An accident sent a church bell into the waters of the local river - a passing mermaid took the opportunity to steal the bell and can sometimes be heard ringing it. As a sidenote, a bell was recovered from the water in 1848.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: A sailor once fell in love with a mermaid here. The woman gave him a golden ring, informing the sailor that she would be back in five days to meet him. Five days later, the man died.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: This fishy female comes to the surface of the pool at night, ready to pull any witnesses down to the dark waters where they remain forever.
properties.location_name: Morridge Moor (Staffordshire) - The Black Mere, also known as the Blake Mere

properties.name: Upset Mermaid
properties.description: The last time this area of water was cleaned, a mermaid popped up out of the water and threatened to destroy Newport and Meretown if the pool was emptied.

properties.name: Therianthropy
properties.description: The Goodman of Wastness, a farmer by trade, stole a selkie's seal skin and hid it, forcing the woman to become his wife. After many years of marriage and a house full of children the selkie found her seal skin and ran off back to the sea, never to be seen again.
properties.location_name: North Ronaldsay (Orkney) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Mermaid's Grave
properties.description: The area reportedly contains the grave of a mermaid killed in the early nineteenth century. The mer-creature had been seen for a couple of days, before a teenage boy threw a rock at it, killing the entity. Accounts stated that the upper part of the creature was the size of an infant, while the bottom resembled a salmon.
properties.location_name: Nunton, Benbecula (Outer Hebrides) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Mermaid's Stone
properties.description: The concave nature of a stone on the road leading to the church was attributed to the foot of a mermaid (an untraditional untailed one) escaping from her jailor.
properties.location_name: Old Cambus (Borders) - Road leading to Church of St Helen

properties.name: Merman
properties.description: Fishermen caught a naked, hairy man just off the coast. The man did not speak, even when hung upside down and tortured. His diet considered of fish, which if given to him raw, he squeezed with his hands and drank only the liquid. He was held at the castle for a while (some sources say six months) before being allowed to swim in the sea, albeit guarded in a netted enclosure. The wild man managed to escape the enclosure and played in the waves, occasionally disappearing under the sea. Just as the guards had given up of hope of recapturing him, the wild man returned and was taken back to the castle. As he had returned voluntarily, the wild man was not guarded as intensely as previous and stayed at the castle for another couple of months before once again escaping into the sea, but this time never returning.
properties.location_name: Orford (Suffolk) - Coast near to Orford Castle

properties.name: Angry Mermaid
properties.description: A local tale states that the harbour here was silted up by a mermaid, more than a little upset after she was shot at by a fisherman. The area has also been home to sea serpents.

properties.name: Selkie Prince
properties.description: A group of hunters who arrived at the island killed and skinned many seals before a violent storm began to batter the island - unbeknown to the hunters, a selkie prince was amongst the seals. The hunters finally returned the skins to the remaining seals, after which the storm ceased.
properties.location_name: Papa Stour (Shetland) - Exact location not known

properties.name: No Mermaid Here
properties.description: A Canadian newspaper reported that the Mayor of Peel and others had seen a mermaid off the coast and were offering a reward for the creature's capture. The story turned out to be a fake.
properties.location_name: Peel (Isle of Man) - Sea off Peel

properties.name: Mermaids
properties.description: This area is another haunt for the Cornish merfolk.
properties.location_name: Perranuthnoe (Cornwall) - Cudden Point, off the coast

properties.name: Merman
properties.description: This figure normally warned of ill fortune at sea - if the crew of a fishing vessel spotted him, they would return to port for the day. Two fishermen spotted two of the creatures in 1814 - one they described as black in colour, with curly hair and long arms, while the other was female with long hair.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: One story says that a mermaid came ashore here but died soon after being discovered by local people heading to church.

properties.name: Rake
properties.description: This freshwater mermaid was reputed to be armed with a rake, ready to drag in any children who strayed too close.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: There are several entities said to haunt this small island, including a mermaid, the sound of rattling chains, and water horses. A woman supervising cattle on the island was attacked by something tall and hairy, with no skin upon its face. She managed to survive by throwing boiling water over the entity and creating a magic circle, remaining inside it.
properties.location_name: Rinns of Islay (Argyll and Bute) - Small island off Rinns of Islay

properties.name: Gipping Girl
properties.description: The mermaid that frequented the Gipping would prey on children who played near the deeper waters of the river.

properties.name: Mermaid
properties.description: Her reasons unknown, this mermaid returns once a year to ring a bell hidden deep underwater and sing.

properties.name: Rothesay
properties.description: Three men spotted a mermaid carefully combing her long black hair along the shoreline. One of the witnesses also reported a large sea monster nearby, which had the body of a man 'but with the head of a brute'.
properties.location_name: Rothesay (Argyll and Bute) - Shoreline within a mile of town

properties.name: Bench End
properties.description: A carved wooden bench arm is in the shape of a mermaid. A notice in the church said that, in days of olde, a mermaid tried to enter but was turned away by a mortified crowd. She later snuck in undetected and remains to this day.

properties.name: Cursed
properties.description: A fisherman who caught a mermaid while out at sea killed the creature with a knife. It was said that from that moment, he was cursed.

properties.name: Noggle
properties.description: The Noggle was a type of water horse that could be found stopping water wheels. When a miller appeared to chase the beast off, he would often be tricked into mounting it - being taken to deep water and his doom.

properties.name: A Sea Man
properties.description: A man was caught in the sea by fishermen and kept for many weeks in a cottage where he lived on a diet of fish. After a while, the 'sea man' returned to the sea, waving goodbye to people standing on the coast before diving down and never returning.

properties.name: Dark Haired Mermaid
properties.description: Little is known about the sighting of a mermaid with long dark hair once spotted here.

properties.name: Glassy Eyed Mermaid
properties.description: Becalmed in their skiff after setting out from the Sound of Mull, two men spotted a creature in the water which had the head, neck and upper torso of a woman, although the hair was coarser and the eyes glassy.
properties.location_name: Sound of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Sea

properties.name: Blocked Door
properties.description: Having just dropped anchor in the bay, the captain of a ship was surprised when a mermaid stuck her head out of the sea and asked if the anchor could be moved, as it had landed on the entrance to her home. The captain was happy to comply.

properties.name: Naked Mermaid?
properties.description: William Munro, a schoolmaster from Thurso, watched a naked woman combing her hair on a rock in the bay. After a few minutes she dived under the water and vanished.

properties.name: Woman in the Sea
properties.description: Caught in a storm at sea, Lachlan McArthur watched a creature resembling 'a woman in upper parts' rise from the water, leaving him terrified.
properties.location_name: Uist (Outer Hebrides) - Sea south of islands, heading towards Skye

properties.name: Saxi and Herman
properties.description: Saxi and Herman, two giants who were brothers, once lived on the island. They both fell in love with a mermaid who said she would marry whoever would swim to the North Pole with her. Both brothers began the trip, but neither returned.

properties.name: Mathew Trewhella
properties.description: A son of a church warden, Mathew Trewhella one day disappeared without trace. A sea captain later told the village folk that Mathew was with a mermaid, living under the sea.

properties.name: Bushy Bearded Man
properties.description: This cloaked, bearded gentleman was seen lurking around the attic and one of the bathrooms. A few unearthly notes from a non-existent music box have been heard in the bar area.

properties.name: Blind George
properties.description: Said to still be playing his fiddle in tunnels under the village, Blind George ventured into the Devil's Hole cave with his trusted dog - the hound ran out, and was found with its hair burnt away, and George was never seen again.
properties.location_name: Anstey (Hertfordshire) - Fields around the village, and Devil's Hole cave

properties.name: Lost Piper
properties.description: Emanating from deep underneath the farmhouse, piping can be heard - a musician named Tam once hid in a nearby cave complex, found himself lost, and is still looking for a way out.

properties.name: Piper
properties.description: What is it with inquisitive pipers and tunnels? Playing his pipe while investigating this secret passageway, a man vanished without trace (though, of course, he is now heard).

properties.name: Loud Heart Beats
properties.description: A family fled the building after being subjected to strange sounds, including deafening heart beats and mournful music played by an unplugged organ, and furniture which would move itself around. When a priest tried to perform an exorcism, his holy water threw itself on him. After the family left the property, they asked the council to rehouse them and were refused.

properties.name: Bagpipes
properties.description: Played by the fairy folk, the sound of bagpipes could once be heard emerging from the stones. A tree in the centre of the stone circle is considered to belong to the fairies.
properties.location_name: Athgreany (County Wicklow) - Stone circle, aka Piper's Stones

properties.name: Organ Plays
properties.description: Several times, late at night, the church organ was heard to burst into tune, stopping as investigators reach the churchyard.

properties.name: Piper Piping
properties.description: The sound of pipes echoes throughout this cave structure on certain nights, as the ghost of a piper tries to find his way out.
properties.location_name: Ballintra (County Donegal) - Cave system known as Pullans

properties.name: Band
properties.description: On two different occasions, villagers heard drums, brass, and a cornet being played in the village hall at midnight, although the building would be locked and unlit.

properties.name: Scottish Piper
properties.description: The ghostly Scot plays his pipes while pacing up and down one of the ruined battlements.

properties.name: Talking
properties.description: While checking out this site, investigators heard a piano and children playing in an empty area.

properties.name: Pianist
properties.description: The owners of this pub heard the piano playing in the back room as they were tidying up. When investigated, no one could be found, and the piano keyboard remained covered. The event occurred a day after two other nearby pubs reported poltergeist-like incidents.

properties.name: Lady in Red
properties.description: This spectre stands on the top of a tower, illuminated by bright moonlight, as if waiting for someone. A child haunts the bridge - the young boy disappears when he reaches the halfway point. More stories tell of phantom singing, music, and words spoken in an unknown tongue.
properties.date: Unknown, singing heard on Easter Sunday (reoccurring)

properties.name: Moving Coffins
properties.description: Music, crashes, thumps, bell ringing and footsteps have all been reported emerging from the church after it has been locked up for the night. A local tale involves the coffins in the Waldegrave family crypt mysteriously moving on their own accord between the times the tomb was opened.

properties.name: Phantom Organ Music
properties.description: A witness reported hearing the organ playing, though they were alone in the church at the time.

properties.name: Woman's Voice
properties.description: A couple awoke to hear their baby gripe in the adjoining room. Before they could climb out of bed, they heard a strange woman's voice say 'hello' in a maternal fashion, followed by a 'Shhh...'. Bounding into the baby's room to challenge the woman, all that could be seen was the sleeping child. A couple of days later, toys could be heard playing tunes in the loft, although the music promptly ceased when the loft hatch was opened.

properties.name: Tinkling the Ivory
properties.description: The piano in the restaurant is said to occasionally play a note or two under its own accord, while cutlery is moved, and doors close by unseen hands.
properties.location_name: Bradford on Avon (Wiltshire) - Fat Fowl restaurant

properties.name: Secret Door
properties.description: Once a year, a secret door opens here which will take mortals to the fairy realm. The door was once always open, but after a man stole a fairy flower, access was restricted. The area is also home to an old woman who uses music to lure the weak willed into the lake waters where they drown - once she has killed nine hundred people, she will regain her youth and become mortal.
properties.location_name: Brecon Beacons (Powys) - Llyn Cwm Llwch (somewhere around the area)

properties.name: Music
properties.description: A couple sitting close to the church heard loud music, sounding like an ice cream van tune, although nothing was in sight. When the music started a second time, they left the area.
properties.location_name: Breedon on the Hill (Leicestershire) - Near the church

properties.name: Music in the Air
properties.description: Fairy music was once not uncommon in this area. While last heard in 1939 by a group of school children, one hundred years previous it was said a young girl danced herself to death to the tunes.

properties.name: Victorians
properties.description: While walking along the seafront, two sisters watched Victorian figures walking around and listened to the sound of music and laughter. The seafront was lit up as if daytime, and a young man and a boy walked out to sea along a rope bridge. As the sisters walked away, they realised the scene they had watched was impossible, and turning back they saw only the darkness over the sea. One sister later discovered that there was a chain bridge on the spot which led to the pier, but it was destroyed in 1896.

properties.name: Piano
properties.description: A former member of staff reported that they and other staff have heard a piano being played when the club is quiet and no one else around.

properties.name: Piano
properties.description: A friend of the owner was awoken to see the owner's deceased husband standing over the bed. The site was also home to phantom piano music and poltergeist activity in a bedroom.

properties.name: Fairground Organ
properties.description: A few spooky events were documented in the local press, including the music from a fairground organ, a disembodied male voice, and the fleeting sighting of a Victorian woman dressed in white. A poltergeist was blamed for moving pots of paint around the site.
properties.location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - Gardiner Haskins store (former soap factory)

properties.name: Piano Man
properties.description: A piano in a lecture theatre here was seen to play itself and has been also heard several times since.

properties.name: Pam the Fiddler
properties.description: Pam, the local schoolmaster, was killed by the village vicar after refusing to stop fiddling around the area after dark. The vicar covered up the crime by secretly burying Pam's body; within a few weeks villagers began to report hearing Pam playing the fiddle, and some claimed to have seen him, though his head had changed into that of a goat.

properties.name: Pharmacist
properties.description: Phantom music could be heard coming from the basement where a pharmacist had taken his own life in 1913. A broom had also been spotted moving unaided. The showroom briefly opened the basement to the public in 1993 to raise money for Comic Relief.
properties.location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - British Gas Showroom (was next door to Cupola House)

properties.name: Music
properties.description: Fairies could once be heard making music under this artificial hill.

properties.name: Famh
properties.description: This author is not sure if this is a joke, but... the Famh is said to be a strange dog/mole hybrid (Dole? Mog?), which possesses a vicious set of teeth and a disproportionately sized mouth. It lurks in the mountain range, shying away from human contact. The area is also haunted by a piper, who is seen standing on the tallest peaks, and is heard playing at night.

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: A young lady dressed completely in white is said to haunt this building. The ghost would descend the main staircase. Phantom music has also been reported in the house.

properties.name: Piping
properties.description: Something strange lured a piper into this cave from which he never returned. Hundreds of years later, the piper could still be heard playing his instrument deep below the earth.

properties.name: Rustling
properties.description: The rustling of a woman's skirt could occasionally be heard behind the walls, as could ghostly music.
properties.location_name: Castle Combe (Wiltshire) - Salutation Inn (was The Gibb Inn)

properties.name: Violinist
properties.description: Wearing an evening dress, sightings of this female violinist have slowly petered out as time has gone by, and the only recent reports of her say she can only be heard playing the instrument she carried.

properties.name: Dancing Folk
properties.description: The round barrow on this site was home to a hundred or so dancing fairies (according to Mary Howitt), with nine harp players to provide music.
properties.location_name: Cauldon Low (Staffordshire) - Ribden Lowe (round barrow on top of a hill)

properties.name: Music
properties.description: On quiet nights, nineteenth century villagers said that fairy music and the patter of little feet could be heard from the surrounding area.

properties.name: Fiddler
properties.description: A former landlord can sometimes be heard playing the violin, deep within the heart of the building. Phantom footfalls have also been reported.
properties.location_name: Chalfont St Peter (Buckinghamshire) - White Hart public house

properties.name: Man in 1940s Suit
properties.description: Walter Charnock, a builder working on the renovation of the theatre, encountered a figure wearing a 1940s suit leaning on a banister. Within thirty seconds, the figure vanished. Music is also said to be heard within the building when none should be playing.

properties.name: Elfin Music
properties.description: This site was said to often be filled with the sound of fairy music and singing.
properties.location_name: Chillingham (Northumberland) - Area around the Hurle Stane (aka Hurl Stone)

properties.name: Singing
properties.description: Sounds of singing, pipes and drums are said to be occasionally heard from the bridge.

properties.name: Piper
properties.description: The sound of the phantom pipe music is said to be heard by fishermen off the coast. One story says that, pre-ghostly form, the piper ventured into the cave with his dog not realising that it was home to fairies - the little people retained the piper but let his dog escape, but not before removing every one of the creature's hairs.
properties.location_name: Clanyard Bay (Dumfries and Galloway) - Cave along the coastline

properties.name: Fairy Music
properties.description: A Bronze Age cemetery, the haunting sound of fiddling heard in this area has led some to say that the music is produced by fairies. A few people who have removed stones from the site are reported have returned them after suffering streaks of bad luck.

properties.name: Miss Holland Playing on the Piano
properties.description: Site of a murder in 1899, the victim, Camille Holland, occasionally returns home and plays on the piano. She has also been blamed for poltergeist activity in the area.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: The outside of this public house is said to be haunted by a ghostly black dog. The inn took the name 'Kremlin' after people reported hearing Russian voices and music emerge from the jukebox during the Cold War.

properties.name: Dancing Trolls
properties.description: Small troll-like creatures are said to party and play jovial music in this wooded area. They welcome anyone who shows no fear of them.

properties.name: Piano
properties.description: A piano was said to play itself, even though most of the keys were broken. The site was also said to be home to an old woman with her hair tied up in a bun.
properties.location_name: Congleton (Cheshire) - Lion and Bell public house (was renamed Bar Twelve, as of 2011 closed)

properties.name: Headless Piper
properties.description: How this phantom headless piper can pipe without a head has yet to be answered.

properties.name: Laughter and Music
properties.description: The basement of this pub has recently been subjected to phantom music, laughter and a dark shadowy stranger.
properties.location_name: Coventry (West Midlands) - Prince William Henry public house, Foleshill Road

properties.name: Piano
properties.description: An occupier of a house along this road reported hearing the twangs of a piano, as if an unseen hand plucked the strings rather than hit keys.
properties.location_name: Coventry (West Midlands) - Private Victorian House, Humber Avenue

properties.name: Music and Chanting
properties.description: Several locals reported hearing the church organ playing during the winter months, while the church was locked and dark.

properties.name: Soft Music
properties.description: Two musicians were tricked into entering the cave by fairies, who promptly took them to their realm. Occasionally the men's music escapes from the mouth of the cave to deter others from making the same mistake.

properties.name: Piper
properties.description: Though only a hundred years old, the castle is said to be haunted by a traditional piper and a lady in white, the latter named as Duchess Blair (or Betty, for short).
properties.location_name: Culrain (Highland) - Carbisdale Castle (aka Castle of Spite)

properties.name: Choir
properties.description: Villagers heard the church organ and a choir singing in the church but found the building empty and quiet when they entered.

properties.name: Piano Playing
