properties.description: This wandering shade, thought to be Princess Nest, known as the Helen of Wales, travels around the castle grounds as if looking for something. Another legend says that the great hall is haunted by a former owner and his pet ape, both of whom were found dead and on fire.

properties.name: Messy Bed
properties.description: The ghost of Charles I is blamed for disrupting the bedding in the room that contains his death mask. John Cockburn, who committed murder here, can be heard dragging the body along various parts of the building. Finally, the castle is also haunted by a beggar, falsely accused of using witchcraft against another former owner.
properties.location_name: Pencaitland (Lothian) - Penkaet (or Pencaet, or Pencait) Castle, also known as Fountainhall House

properties.name: Nell
properties.description: The king's mistress would stay at the inn when the King travelled and resided in a nearby manor house. Her spirit remained until the inn was demolished.
properties.location_name: Pewsey (Wiltshire) - The Phoenix Inn (original building no longer stands)

properties.name: Eleanor of Aquitaine
properties.description: The wife of Henry II is reported to haunt these ruins, appearing as if she regretted plotting against her husband. However, there is no evidence that Eleanor of Aquitaine visited the area in her lifetime.
properties.location_name: Quarr Hill (Isle of Wight) - Quarr Abbey

properties.name: Boadicea
properties.description: This site has been named as another contender for the final resting place of Queen Boudicca.
properties.location_name: Quidenham (Norfolk) - Low mound, around 300 metres from the church, known as Viking's Mound

properties.name: Elizabeth Hesketh
properties.description: The lady now manifests herself as a grey shade, waiting for her husband to return from a war with Scotland. She may have last been seen by a curator as he sat playing the piano. A shade resembling Queen Elizabeth has also been reported, though her link with the hall is unknown. Ghostly music is also reputed to occasionally be heard.

properties.name: Hooded Figure
properties.description: This figure, covered so no flesh is visible, is sometimes seen darting between the trees. It could be a lover of Nell Gwynn, who the king had murdered.

properties.name: Nellie & Charlie
properties.description: The figures of Nell Gwynn and King Charles II have been seen on the property, though never together.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Some people believe that this phantom figure is Margaret Radclyffe, Queen Elizabeth's favourite Maid of Honour. She died of a broken heart in 1559 shortly after the death of her brother.

properties.name: Anne Boleyn
properties.description: The former queen sometimes returns to this church, where local legend says she was laid to rest after her body was clandestinely removed from the Tower of London.

properties.name: Queen Maude
properties.description: The queen is said to walk around the depression in the land that bears her name. Some others name her Queen Mab or Empress Maude.

properties.name: Queen Mary, and Girls Laughter
properties.description: Queen Mary rebuilt the Hall after it was burnt down by troops searching for the monarch. Seen in the room in which she slept, the Queen's ghost appears to always smile, and wears the same dress which appears in her portrait located elsewhere in the building. An explanation for the laughter which sometimes echoes through the Hall has never been discovered, although another royal, Lady Jane Grey, is still seen in her former bedroom and walking down corridors - it is said this was the only place that she felt happy.

properties.name: Footsteps
properties.description: The ghost here is said to constantly walk up and down the passage, always at the same constant speed. One witness reported feeling something hit her arm three times while passing along the corridor.
properties.location_name: Scone (Perth and Kinross) - Scone Palace, South Passage

properties.name: Anne Boleyn
properties.description: Carried in a Thames barge crewed by faded grey figures, the shade of Boleyn is taken to the Tower of London where she met her maker. The palace itself sometimes echoes with Anne's voice, pleading and crying for her life to be saved.
properties.location_name: SE1 (Greater London) - Lambeth Palace, & River Thames near the building

properties.name: Bodiless Hand
properties.description: The phantom hand photographed on the Tulip Staircase is one of the most frequent images to crop up in books about ghosts and hauntings, though recently the effect has been duplicated with a long exposure time. The other ghost in the former palace resembles Queen Elizabeth, complete with period costume and red hair. The Queen Anne block is haunted by Admiral Byng, executed for treason in the 1700s.
properties.location_name: SE10 (Greater London) - Greenwich Palace (currently Royal Naval property), & the Queen's House - Tulip Staircase

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: When this man died, he was the only one to know the true location of the Abbey's hidden treasure. He now returns to ensure it remains hidden. It is worth noting that the ghost 'walks' on his knees - one assumes this is because of a change in ground level. Several people claimed to have seen a phantom man running around the ruins at night; some say that this was the ghost of King Edward.

properties.name: Mary Queen of Scots
properties.description: Folklore says that Mary, wearing a long black dress, appears in the Turret House and glides along corridors and passes through walls. In 2015, a visitor to the site watched a glowing white figure of a woman wearing a long dress run towards and vanish behind a building.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Manor Lodge (aka Manor Castle)

properties.name: Mary with her Jailor
properties.description: Mary Queen of Scots was held here for many years, and on occasion her ghost is seen looking out from a window. Her male jailor is sometimes seen standing guard on the stairwell.

properties.name: Isabella of Valois
properties.description: Wife of Richard II, Queen Isabella' drifts around the field where the palace once stood. As the phantom grey woman moves, ghostly music emerges from the ground. George III's organist lived nearby and may have been the last person to witness the phantom.
properties.date: Summer evenings (last seen late eighteenth/early nineteen century?)
properties.location_name: Sonning (Berkshire) - Site of Bishop's Palace (no longer standing)

properties.name: King Charles I
properties.description: King Charles I is said to have used this building as a hideout whilst on the run, and his phantom now appears in the King Charles Suite. Another man, sporting a powdered wig, also haunts the building, as does a woman in eighteenth century dress.

properties.name: Mary
properties.description: The Queen of Scots has put in at least one appearance here, praying at the altar.

properties.name: King Harold's Army
properties.description: The re-enactment of Harold's 1066 battle has been observed in the skies over the field where much blood was shed.
properties.location_name: Stamford Bridge (North Yorkshire) - Area over battlefield

properties.name: Princess Cicely
properties.description: Never seen, but her presence is announced at night when her invisible form is felt sitting on the end of a bed.
properties.location_name: Standen (Isle of Wight) - Great East Standen Lodge

properties.name: Man in Highland Dress
properties.description: A photograph taken in the 1970s shows what could be a spectral figure walking along the cobbled path. Reports of phantom footsteps echoing down corridors have also been reported at the castle, and a green lady has been observed. Not to be forgotten, some also say a woman in a pink silken dress stalks the area outside the fort, and she is named as Mary Queen of Scots.

properties.name: Chained Monk
properties.description: Said to predate the construction of the palace, this monk appears bound in chains once a year on Christmas Day. The sound of a suicide (the single shot of a pistol) is occasionally heard on the first floor - the private secretary to King Edward VII ended his life here.

properties.name: Misty Shape
properties.description: A misty shape scared a worker in the building, causing her to flee. The visitation had been preceded by much banging and crashing.

properties.name: Animated Queen Anne
properties.description: Once a year the statue of the Queen becomes animated and walks around the neighbourhood. Until recently, the statue was believed to be Bloody Mary, until a clean-up operation revealed an inscription pointing out the error.
properties.location_name: SW1 (Greater London) - Queen Anne's Gate, Westminster

properties.name: Madame de Beauclair
properties.description: Madame de Beauclair was a good friend of the Duchess of Mazarine, and both lived in the palace grounds. They made a pact in which the first to die would appear to the other. The Duchess was first to die, and months passed without her ghost appearing. Suddenly one day the ghost appeared to Madame de Beauclair with the warning that the Madame would be dead by the following day. Sure enough, at one in the morning, Madame de Beauclair died. Both women are said to have been seen since, the Duchess of Mazarine by a servant.

properties.name: Queen Elizabeth
properties.description: Said to be home to several shades, one of the figures that haunts the fort is reportedly Elizabeth I.

properties.name: Ghost Hunt
properties.description: This building broke the record for having the world's largest ghost hunt, with 700 people looking for the phantom monk, a grey lady, a drummer boy and Mary Queen of Scots, amongst other shades...

properties.name: Eleanor of Castille
properties.description: The ruined building houses this former Queen's heart, and it is she who is said to now walk the area.
properties.location_name: W4 (Greater London) - Blackfriar's Priory, Queen Victoria Street

properties.name: Barbara Villiers
properties.description: Barbara, mistress to King Charles II, was once a beautiful young lady, until she contracted a disease that swelled her body to a huge size - her bitter, misty shade walks the house, acting threateningly towards any who see her.
properties.location_name: W5 (Greater London) - Walpole House, Chiswick Mall

properties.name: George II
properties.description: George II has been seen looking out of a palace window at the weathervane, waiting for a message from Hanover. Another ghost, thought to be Sophia (aunt of Queen Victoria), can be heard operating an old spinning wheel late at night and the final entity takes the form of a man wearing breeches and walks around the courtyard.

properties.name: Blue Lady
properties.description: Her origin unknown, one story says that the phantom blue lady walked straight through a van while crossing the bridge. The driver was convinced he had hit someone until he spotted the figure continuing to walk unimpeded across the bridge.

properties.name: King's Mistress
properties.description: Room 3 was reportedly the home of a ghostly mistress of Charles I, who cried herself to death on hearing of his execution. Room 5 appears to be the haunt of two children, who stand by the wash basin.

properties.name: Mary Queen of Scots
properties.description: Mary was held here briefly en route to Fotheringhay castle, and after her execution, it is said her shade returned here (as well as many other places throughout the UK).

properties.name: Man in Grey
properties.description: Seeing this ghost, dressed in a long grey coat with a Tricorn hat, is considered a good omen; if it appears either in the morning or afternoon during rehearsals, the performance will go on to be a great success. King Charles II is also supposed to haunt the building (he was a great theatre lover), as does the shade of Joe Grimaldi who helps anyone struggling with their lines. The backstage corridors are home to the wraith of Charles Macklin, a murderer never found guilty, and now walks the scene of his crime. Finally, actors have been known to experience a chill and simultaneously catching a whiff of lavender while on stage, the sensations blamed on a ghost.
properties.date: Prior to a long running show (grey man)
properties.location_name: WC2 (Greater London) - Drury Lane Theatre Royal

properties.name: Anne Boleyn
properties.description: This building held Anne during her last days, and while the house no longer stands, its basement remains, and it is here the ghost still resides.
properties.location_name: WC2 (Greater London) - Durham House (no longer standing) Durham House Street

properties.name: Jailed Mary
properties.description: Imprisoned by the English, Mary Queen of Scots spent some time at this castle - one story has it that her shade walks around the courtyard. One person walking through the main hall spotted, out of the corner of their eye, a woman in Tudor clothing, sitting and watching the outside world through a bay window.

properties.name: Mary Rose Tudor
properties.description: This building may contain the ghost of Henry VIII's sister.

properties.name: Coach and Horses
properties.description: After a spectral horse and carriage stops at the top of the driveway, a woman climbs out and walks to the hall's door to be met by a long dead butler. A similar story says that Anne Boleyn travels in a coach along the roads surrounding the Hall around the same time of year.

properties.name: Anne Boleyn
properties.description: It was claimed that the hall was both the birthplace of Anne Boleyn and where her ghost returned.
properties.location_name: Wilmslow (Cheshire) - Bollin Hall (demolished nineteenth century, likely a railway viaduct now stands on the site)

properties.name: King Alfred
properties.description: King Alfred's remains were originally buried at the Old Minster, but the monks claimed Alfred's ghost would walk around at night - the King was moved to the New Minster.

properties.name: Four Royals and a Hunt
properties.description: Henry VIII, Queen Elizabeth, Charles I & George III have all reportedly been seen here after their natural lives ended. Elizabeth frequents the library, Charles the Canon's House (traditionally walking on the night of 26 June), while George haunts the rooms in which he was bound after being declared mad. Henry VIII can only be heard, moving slowly down the corridors, dragging his bad leg. The great park attached to the castle is home to Herne the Hunter, who together with his devil dogs, stalk the woodlands. In 1906 a guard was investigated after he fired five rounds at a white figure - he claimed the bullets passed straight through the figure. Finally (for the time being), the park is also haunted by the ghost of a Grenadier Guard who killed himself while on duty.

properties.name: Child in Yellow Dress
properties.description: This young girl with raven hair is reported to lead anyone who follows her to a mass grave of soldiers killed in the Civil War.
properties.location_name: Windsor Forest (Berkshire) - Callins Bridge, near Shurlock Row

properties.name: Mary's Lights
properties.description: The spirit of Mary Queen of Scots is reported to have taken a liking to the ballroom, while it is in debate whether the spheres of light seen floating around the site after dark are part of the same entity.

properties.name: Phantom Coach
properties.description: A ghostly coach and four passes over this bridge once a year. It is said to be driven by a headless Sir Thomas Boleyn and is one of eleven bridges that he passes over on the night of his daughter Anne's execution.

properties.name: Thomas Percy
properties.description: Local folklore says that a decapitated Thomas Percy, the Seventh Earl of Northumberland, fumbles around the churchyard searching for his severed head. Thomas and head separated after upsetting Elizabeth I; the earl attempted and failed to raise an army against his queen and was executed for this error of judgement. More recently, a ghostly figure in a long gown (possibly a nun) has been observed on the site.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Holy Trinity Church, Goodramgate

properties.name: Lady in Green
properties.description: The female ghost which haunts the Principal's House is said to be either Anne Boleyn or Catherine Howard, dressed in a green dress and carrying roses. The ghost of a monk frequents the King's Manor. The sounds of wounded men can sometimes be heard here - the building was used as a makeshift hospital during the Civil War.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Principal's House and the King's Manor, Exhibition Square

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: This headless dog was said to have once belonged to a young giant who ran so fast that he decapitated the dog by pulling too sharply on the leash.

properties.name: Red Eyed Dog
properties.description: This huge hound possesses eyes that burn as bright as the sun, making the creature easy to spot if he tries to sneak up on you.

properties.name: Dreadful Eyes
properties.description: A few entities were said to have made their homes here, including a black dog with dreadful eyes, a ghostly horseman, and a wagon containing a coffin pulled by four phantom horses.

properties.name: Running Hound
properties.description: A large black dog is said to run from the Downs to Town Fields - here it stares over a flint wall before retreating the way it came.
properties.location_name: Alfriston (Sussex) - Between the Downs and Town Fields

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: This creature is reportedly the dog of a young man murdered while out walking during the 1700s - because the pitiful hound sat by the victim's shallow grave, the criminals killed it also. The haunting is said to have ceased after the bodies of dog and owner were discovered when the road was widened.
properties.location_name: Alfriston (Sussex) - The White Way, road leading from the town towards Seaford

properties.name: Long Necked Dog
properties.description: This black dog was described by a witness as being tall and lean, with a long neck and a protruding muzzle. The hound is not regarded here as an ill omen.
properties.location_name: Algarkirk (Lincolnshire) - Area around the village church

properties.name: Dog in Trees
properties.description: This large black hound was seen between three trees that grow close to the religious house.

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: The story of the black dog was used to frighten children in this area. One man may have been chased by the creature (though it was dark and stated it might have been a pony), while another couple who spotted the hound said it stood almost as tall as their pony.
properties.location_name: Allington (Wiltshire) - Area between village and All Cannings

properties.name: Grey Greyhound
properties.description: After seeing a creature in his chicken coop, a farmer fired a shotgun at the creature - it ran through the wire netting and escaped. Examining the scene in the morning, the farmer could find no holes in the netting or footprints of the dog.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: Charles Walton (the man murdered in Lower Quinton (see Quinton database entry for further information)) claimed to have seen a phantom black dog for several successive nights when he was younger - on the last night, instead of the dog, he saw a ghostly headless woman. He soon heard that his sister had died at roughly the same time as the last sighting.

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: Murdered alongside his peddler master, this glowing white hound was said to haunt the lanes close to Alveston.

properties.name: Greyhound
properties.description: A phantom hound was said to haunt this prehistoric site - it beat up a local preacher a couple of times before moving on to a further plane of existence.
properties.location_name: Amlwch (Gwynedd) - Stone circle between village & St Ellian Church

properties.name: Barguest
properties.description: Possessing glowing red eyes and a yellow tint to its fur, the dog-like barguest is a harbinger of doom.

properties.name: Death Prediction
properties.description: The black dog around this area would warn of impending death. One version of the story says that dog carried a puppy on the back of its head. In 1909, Dr MacDonald spotted the dog while treating chief Murdoch Gillian MacLaine - his patient died soon after.
properties.location_name: Ardura, Isle of Mull (Argyll and Bute) - Area around Lochbuie House

properties.name: Shaggy Dog
properties.description: It has been reported that this house is haunted by a sheepdog-looking entity, which has also been heard barking. Folklore said a man in a red coat also haunted the site.

properties.name: Vanishing Hound
properties.description: For several nights, a man walking to milk his cows in the early hours of the morning encountered a large black dog which blocked his path. He finally grew tired of having to walk around the hound and, while walking with a friend, struck the creature - the dog vanished but the man fell down paralysed, never to be able to move or speak again.
properties.location_name: Aylesbury (Buckinghamshire) - Unnamed field in a village close to the area

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: Mistakenly blamed for killing a child, it was later discovered that the dog had tried to defend the infant from a wolf (a la Gelert). However, this hound was less forgiving for its former master's murderous action and now runs around the dense woodland.

properties.name: Webbed Footed Hound
properties.description: A large dog with eyes the size of saucers once haunted this area.

properties.name: Yellow Eyed Dog
properties.description: A witness watched a large black dog with yellow eyes cover eighteen metres in just three strides. Over the following two months the witness lost two members of their family.

properties.name: Lofo
properties.description: Nicknamed Lofo by the local population, this black dog would grow to several times its original size if challenged or acknowledged. A group of men returning from a wake fled from the hound in terror after one man tried to kick the creature, only for his foot to pass through the dog.

properties.name: Moddra-na-Craeuv
properties.description: The phantom woman who once appeared here was said to be a Creagh girl who died shortly after her lover was killed by her father. The Moddra-na-Craeuv, also known as the dog of the Creaghs, also haunts the area.
properties.location_name: Ballyandrew (County Cork) - Glen of Croke, which stretched from Saffron Hill to Pinegrove

properties.name: Trotting Dog
properties.description: Potentially last seen by a cyclist, the black dog which haunted this area would vanish into thin air as witnesses looked on.
properties.location_name: Ballygar (County Galway) - Lane in the area

properties.name: Shug Monkey
properties.description: For reasons unknown, the shuck which has been observed in this area is believed to have a bald head and wide eyes, like that of a monkey, while the body is that of a large black shaggy dog. It leaps out in front of cars travelling along this road, only to dart out of the way at the last possible second, either on all four legs or standing upright.

properties.name: Black Collie
properties.description: This phantom dog stands as tall as a very large collie and is said to belong to the fairies.

properties.name: Rough Coated Dog with Large Yellow Eyes
properties.description: The dog harassed a couple of men walking back from work - one of them reported trying to hit the creature with a stick, but the piece of wood passed straight through the dog's body. Another version of the story states the dog disappears into a solid wall.
properties.location_name: Barham (Suffolk) - Along the main Norwich Road, Barham Church Lane, just past Barham Hall gates.

properties.name: Shuck Haunt
properties.description: Legend states the stream to be the haunt of a Shuck, though no further information could be found.

properties.name: Shag Foal
properties.description: A black dog the size of a donkey, this entity was said to let loose the most unearthly cries.
properties.location_name: Barnoldby le Beck (Lincolnshire) - Church of St Helen's

properties.name: Chained Hound
properties.description: Buried in nearby woodland by an owner, this phantom hound is now reputed to roam the area, a large chain around its neck.

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: A woman passing through the cemetery watched a large white dog as it faded away in front of her.

properties.name: Red Eyed Hound
properties.description: This large black dog with glowing eyes is said to have driven a local farmer mad after the man tried to block the creature's path. The farmer was also left almost completely paralysed. The hound is said to walk from the churchyard to nearby marshland before fading from view.

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: It is said that if this large creature follows you, death is not far behind.
properties.location_name: Beetham (Cumbria) - Between village and Milnthrope, and surrounding countryside

properties.name: Capelthwaite
properties.description: Also known as a Cappel, this spirit is said to be able to transform into any four legged creature, though it prefers the form of a dog. A local vicar is said to have banished the Capelthwaite into the River Bela, even though it would help some locals drive their sheep.
properties.location_name: Beetham (Cumbria) - General area near the river Bela

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A particular gate in the area was said to be home to a black dog once the sun went down.
properties.location_name: Belchalwell (Dorset) - Unnamed gate in the area (still standing?)

properties.name: Sluagh
properties.description: The Sluagh are a fairy host that travel across the sky to the land of the ever young, Tir-nan-Og. A couple of hunting hounds belonging to the Sluagh are said to have landed in Benbecula briefly before heading off on their travels.

properties.name: Glowing Mouthed Hounds
properties.description: The locals here say their devil dogs, known as Shag Dogs, have a glow that emanates from the inside of their mouths. A pit, long since filled in, was said to be the creatures' home.
properties.location_name: Birstall (Leicestershire) - Pit (no longer exists) near Kilby Avenue, and general area

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: Dragging a chain, this decapitated dog crosses the churchyard.

properties.name: Large Hound
properties.description: This phantom dog, said to be huge, patrols the road. Its presence makes normal dogs nervous and horses shy. One story says to stare into the creature's eyes three times will result in death.
properties.location_name: Blairgowrie (Perth and Kinross) - A93 north of the town

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This lane is a favoured haunt for the black shuck. The same lane is reportedly haunted by a ghostly horse and carriage.

properties.name: Old Shuck
properties.description: This lane is another site where the black shuck was said to wander after dark.

properties.name: Hunt
properties.description: Thought to be part of a phantom hunt, these ghostly dogs have been heard running and yelping through woodland. It is also thought that a headless shuck haunts the area, though it is less sure whether the entity is connected to the hunt pack.
properties.location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Woods in the area

properties.name: Following Dog
properties.description: As this creature followed a woman, she turned and lashed out with her umbrella - only for it to pass straight through the creature.
properties.location_name: Blyborough (Lincolnshire) - Road by village pond, leading to Grayingham

properties.name: Shuck
properties.description: During a church service in 1577, a large black dog burst in and created havoc. Blackened scorch marks said to be caused by the creature still exist today on the church door. Many years later, in 1973, a man working in the marshes near the church reporting hearing a panting dog standing beside him, though nothing could be seen.

properties.name: Watcher Shuck
properties.description: The large black dog here would observe people while they were in the wood, though the creature would never leave the protective tree line. The site may also be the haunt of a grey woman and a grey man.

properties.name: Shadows
properties.description: A shadowy hound, said to be slightly larger than a Saint Bernard dog, was reported to haunt this area. The creature has never seen directly, but only from the corner of the eye. Scraping sounds resembling claws on concrete can also be heard, always behind the witness.

properties.name: Country Dog
properties.description: The area between these two villages is reportedly patrolled by a shuck.

properties.name: Grave Hound
properties.description: The site where a Jacobite was murdered by a companion is now haunted by a phantom black dog.

properties.name: Invisible to Some
properties.description: Only one of the two lead miners playing cards at the gates to the town could see a large black dog as it approached them. The man tried to point the creature out to his co-worker, but his friend was completely obvious to the phantom hound which then disappeared. Shaken, the witness refused to go to work the following day, and later heard his co-worker and several others had been killed when a shaft collapsed.

properties.name: Bedroom Shuck
properties.description: Appearing in a bedroom, this dog had glowing red eyes and warm breath - it disappeared as it approached the bedroom door to leave.
properties.location_name: Bredon (Hereford & Worcester) - Cottage, exact location not known

properties.name: Hound with Pointed Ears
properties.description: This large black dog, with pointed ears and glowing eyes, is said to haunt the area. In 1972, one witness watched a large ball of light crash into the ground, and the hound appeared soon after.
properties.location_name: Brereton (Staffordshire) - Coal Pit Lane, and roads leading to Brereton

properties.name: Trotting Dog
properties.description: The phantom hound haunting this crossroads would appear nightly and trot off along a road before vanishing. It was said several people had tried to follow the creature but would always lose sight of it.
properties.date: Said to manifest every night at midnight (reoccurring)
properties.location_name: Bridgend (Mid Glamorgan) - Crossroads between Bridgend and Laleston

properties.name: Sitting Shuck
properties.description: This shuck is said to sit inside the church, and to see it will quickly bring ill-fortune.

properties.name: One of Three Shucks
properties.description: One of three bridges in Lincolnshire that is said to be a haunt of the black shuck, this one normally manifesting on a Saturday night.

properties.name: Willie Sled's Dog
properties.description: The crossroads was home to a phantom white hound known as Willie Sled's dog, named after a local sandpit worker.
properties.location_name: Brigham (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Brigham Lane, at a crossroads

properties.name: Large White Dog
properties.description: Reportedly the size of a small horse, this phantom dog will follow lonely walkers on the beach for short distances before vanishing. A witness reported seeing a tall man in old fashioned clothing and a tall hat walking along the sidewalk during the night in 1977 - the figure vanished into the mist.
properties.date: Last dog sighting unknown, tall man seen in Spring 1977

properties.name: Morphing Shuck
properties.description: The large black dog seen here in 1908 was seen to climb out from a hedge, before transforming into a donkey and standing on its hind legs. A very similar creature was seen in 2004, as it passed through a car, shaking the occupant.
properties.location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - Dead end lane in Hallen Bristol, next to the M49 bridge

properties.name: Shaggy Dog
properties.description: This road is another haunt of the black shuck in Dorset.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A gentleman who committed suicide in the village is said to have returned in the form of a large black dog.

properties.name: Cwn Annwn
properties.description: A driver passing through Buckholt in 1961 reported seeing a large black hound, believed to be Cwn Annwn, a spectral hound of Annwn, although some people believe it to be a ghostly donkey. More recently, although no date has been mentioned, the hound has been seen in a nearby cottage which is also haunted by two phantom people (one short, one headless). Finally, a witness encountered the face of a beautiful woman along the road. The witness apologised and stepped to one side before realising that there was no one else present.
properties.date: 1961 onwards (hound), face sighting possibly mid twentieth century

properties.name: Leaping Dog
properties.description: After scaring three teenagers walking through the graveyard, this shuck went on to unnerve a passing motorist as the creature leapt over the churchyard wall and landed on the bonnet of a car.
properties.location_name: Buckhurst Hill (Essex) - Area around local graveyard

properties.name: Horseman
properties.description: A ghostly man on horseback is said to cross the lane before vanishing.

properties.name: Strange Hound
properties.description: A phantom dog has been spotted in an area near the school.

properties.name: Shuck
properties.description: Believed by some to be the headquarters of Old Shuck, the ruined castle is still avoided at night.

properties.name: Lightning Dog
properties.description: Appearing on 4 August 1577, the same date as the Blythburgh report, a black dog entered the church during a storm and killed several people. Some believe the dog still patrols the area.

properties.name: White Shuck
properties.description: A white dog the size of a bullock frequented this area.
properties.location_name: Burgh (Suffolk) - Bath Slough, a boggy pool near the town

properties.name: Old Scarfe
properties.description: The shuck is known locally as Old Scarfe, named after the Baron Rudolf Scarfe who was a thirteenth century villain who lived near the castle. Old Scarfe haunts the castle grounds and the Yarmouth Road, his single eye glowing red and yellow.

properties.name: Trash
properties.description: A local name for their version of the shuck, Trash was said to be very hairy, with feet that created splashing noises no matter what surface it stepped on. Death would follow a sighting.
properties.location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - General area, though near the church a favourite haunt

properties.name: Ghostly Black Dog
properties.description: Another shuck legend, though details are sketchy.
properties.location_name: Burwell (Cambridgeshire) - Burwell to Snailwell - path southeast, by a belt of trees leading to the A11

properties.name: Patting the Fading Dog
properties.description: While passing the church, a man saw a large black dog. He reached out to pat the hound's head, but the creature disappeared into thin air. The man later learnt that his brother had died at the exact moment he saw the dog.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A black dog may haunt this area.
properties.location_name: Cadley (Wiltshire) - Fields between village and Marlborough

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: Very little is known about the hound that is said to have frequented this area, other than appearing black and large.

properties.name: Unlucky Shuck
properties.description: This road, and area, are said to be frequented by a shuck that brings ill luck to all that see it.

properties.name: Gabriel Hounds
properties.description: A pack of demonic dogs glide (or fly) across the lands, their eyes burning with fire and mouths covered in blood, with their howls carried on the wind.
properties.location_name: Cannock Chase (Staffordshire) - The Chase and surrounding moorlands

properties.name: Robin
properties.description: The phantom hound which haunts the road connecting these two locations was thought to be the ghost of Robin of Ross in animal form.
properties.location_name: Carrigaholt (County Clare) - Road between Carrigaholt and Ross (R487?)

properties.name: Powerful Dog
properties.description: Said to have made its home in the ruins of a big house, this hound left its dwelling at midnight. Most witnesses were said to have been drunk men, although on one occasion several sober gentlemen chased the creature and had it cornered - the dog vanished in front of their eyes.
properties.location_name: Carrigaline (County Cork) - Lanes in the area

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A shuck is said to walk the area near the castle, while a phantasmic knight stands motionless on the ramparts. Author Wayne Anthony reports that an old lady and a phantom horse have also been reported in the area.

properties.name: Sitting Hound
properties.description: A phantom dog was once a common sight here, as it sat on the corner of the road near a railway bridge waiting for an owner which never came.

properties.name: Fiery Eyed Hound
properties.description: As the name of the location suggests, this wooded area is home to a black dog whose eyes glow as red as hot coals. To see him means death within the year. One story says the hound once belonged to a man killed in a duel; the dog tore out the throat of the surviving fighter.

properties.name: Great Dane
properties.description: This dog leapt out in front of an incoming car - the driver thought he hit the creature but could not find any evidence of an accident when he stopped.

properties.name: Grave Dog
properties.description: This creature followed a couple of witnesses as they walked home from a nearby village. The creature turned away near the graveyard, and they watched it disappear next to a gravestone. A phantom priest is also said to haunt the same area.
properties.location_name: Chideock (Dorset) - Road from Morcombelake, & local graveyard

properties.name: Barrow Dog
properties.description: A witness watched this large black creature bounding towards the barrow, where it promptly disappeared.
properties.location_name: Chilham (Kent) - Pilgrim's Way, opposite the Woolpack Inn

properties.name: Wild Edric
properties.description: The large hound with eyes that glow red like hot coals is said to be a manifestation of Wild Edric, local hero and protector of Britain.
properties.location_name: Church Stretton (Shropshire) - Hills around the town

properties.name: Mean Old Dog
properties.description: This phantom hound is said to harass those using the road late at night. It can easily be recognised by its pair of horns.

properties.name: Big Eyed Dog
properties.description: The phantom hound with glowing eyes which walked the corridors at night was said by students to be the manifestation of a hanged murderer. The building was also once witness to a Crisis Manifestation, when the ghost of a Marshall appeared to two sisters - they identified him as their brother, who, it was later discovered, was killed in action at the time of the visitation.

properties.name: Earless Dog
properties.description: The ghostly, earless dog that haunted this area was supposed to emerge from the castle's moat at night before wandering off to hunt rabbits.
properties.location_name: Clashmealcon (County Kerry) - Area around Brown's Castle

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: The outside of this public house is said to be haunted by a ghostly black dog. The inn took the name 'Kremlin' after people reported hearing Russian voices and music emerge from the jukebox during the Cold War.

properties.name: Snapping Dog
properties.description: Three men travelling along this road found themselves followed by a hound which stayed a reasonable distance before vanishing at a roadside cross. When one man headed away from the other two, the hound appeared again, chasing and nipping at the man. After sharing their experience with a priest, the area was blessed and the dog never returned.
properties.location_name: Clonakilty (County Cork) - Road east of town

properties.name: Tchen Bodu
properties.description: This black hound, known locally as tchen Bodu, is seen as a death omen for either the witness or someone close to them.

properties.name: Wide-Eyed Dog
properties.description: A large dog with saucer sized eyes would sit on the roadside. No one would see it move (it would just vanish from view), although if stones were thrown at the creature, the eyes would grow even larger. It once appeared to one man who discovered the time of the sighting matched the time that his father died. The same area is also haunted by a phantom white donkey.

properties.name: Barghest
properties.description: A hound-like creature with eyes as large as saucers was once said to lurk in this area, which was once considered a place of gambling and other morally dubious activities.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This witness, who would often sit on the pebbles with their two dogs, watched another dog, black in colour, move silently across the beach some fifty metres away. The witness's curiosity was raised by the fact that despite the rocks and stones underfoot, the hound moved smoothly, and their own dogs remained oblivious to the strange canine. Sixteen years later the same witness reported their second paranormal event on the beach - a nebulous black cloud which formed around their feet. After tolerating the cloud for several minutes, the witness left the area.
properties.location_name: Cold Knap (South Glamorgan) - Beach, between cave and mini golf course

properties.name: Dog of Justice
properties.description: This shuck appeared to two men who had just committed a murder, chasing them back to the village from where they were trying to escape. The creature vanished once they were arrested.

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: The black shuck which walks this area is instantly recognisable as the creature is missing a head.

properties.name: Screaming
properties.description: Cries and screams can be heard from a man torn into small pieces by hell hounds. The echoes can be heard once a year.
properties.date: August, first Monday of the month (reoccurring)

properties.name: Small Black Dog
properties.description: Smaller than the average shuck, this phantom dog was reported to silently follow people down a hill before vanishing.
properties.location_name: Combs (Derbyshire) - Road between Combs and Cockyard

properties.name: Pongay
properties.description: Part dog and part ghost, Pongay was once often blamed for accidents and mishaps in the area.
properties.location_name: Coppull (Lancashire) - Darkland Bridge and surrounding area

properties.name: Escort
properties.description: This shuck was regarded as the traveller's friend, walking alongside anyone traversing the road on their lonesome. It would vanish into thin air if touched.

properties.name: Great Shaggy Beast
properties.description: Standing almost six foot tall, this black dog made itself known to a couple of water workers along Watery Lane (who wisely ran away). Prior to this, the creature had appeared in the middle of the eighteenth century and was said to denote a death in the witness's family.

properties.name: Portent of Doom
properties.description: To be followed or escorted by this black hound would result in misfortune soon after the encounter.

properties.name: Human-faced Dog
properties.description: On closer examination, this shuck has the horrible face of a person, with little devil-like horns. If it barks more than two times, disaster will follow shortly.
properties.location_name: Creag an Ordain (Highland) - B869, between Stoer and Lochinver

properties.name: Phantom Dog
properties.description: A Woman's Institute member encountered a ghost dog here, terrifying her.

properties.name: Fire Breather
properties.description: This ghostly hound was said to be able to blow fire from its nose.
properties.location_name: Crockerton (Wiltshire) - Unnamed road in the area

properties.name: Shuck
properties.description: Many people have reported seeing the black shuck in this area of Norfolk, with the local populace claiming the creature is the ghost of a dog whose master drowned just off the Cromer coast.

properties.name: Black Retriever Drowning Child
properties.description: A local child befriended a 'stray' black dog and took the creature swimming in the ocean - while out over deep water the dog changed into a human and tried to drown the youngster. Another version of the tale states the dog does not transform, by merely barks and growls, preventing the child from swimming back to land. In both versions, the child is saved by the intervention of sailors.
properties.location_name: Cromer (Norfolk) - Off the coast of Cromer

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: A local legend says that this area was home to a large black dog which hunted souls. A local minister faced the hound and banished it after smacking the creature between the eyes with a bible. The story found renewed interest in 2022 after the chapel sold at auctions for fifty-three thousand pounds.

properties.name: Changing Entity
properties.description: The large black dog once said to haunt this area could also take the form of a flaming headless man, a headless woman, a white cat, or a rabbit.
properties.location_name: Darlington (Durham) - Glassensikes (stream or river) and Harewood Hill area

properties.name: Dewer
properties.description: A Wisht huntsman, Dewer would chase people up the highest crag in the area and leave them to fall to their deaths - if the drop did not kill them, the unfortunates would be consumed by hell hounds waiting at the bottom.

properties.name: Large Black Hound
properties.description: Said to have inspired the famous 'Hound of the Baskervilles', several phantoms are held to blame for this creature (including Drake (for his dealings with the devil) and Richard Cabell (a man so evil that he now hunts children with his devil dogs!)).

properties.name: Yeth Hounds
properties.description: These variants of the shuck, complete with hunters on horseback, have been heard running in this area after dark. Part of the Tavy is also said to be haunted by the ghost of Sir John Fitz, whose body was found in the river.
properties.location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - General area of the River Tavy

properties.name: Wish Pack
properties.description: Led by Drake, this pack of hounds patrol the area.

properties.name: Wish Hounds
properties.description: Large black dogs are rumoured to hunt in this area of the moors - they once killed the son of a farmer. In the same area, a procession of white clad figures could be seen walking through the wood, following the path of what was once a well-used funeral route.
properties.location_name: Dartmoor (Devon) - Wistman's Wood, one mile north of Two Bridges

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: The ghost of a local weaver metamorphosed into a great black dog when the village vicar threw consecrated earth into his face - the hound was then taken to a pool by the waterfall and instructed to only harass people once it had emptied the water using a small, cracked shell.

properties.name: Huge Hound
properties.description: The phantom black dog that haunted this area was believed by some to be a former murderer, or the Devil. The dog was said to have been banished in 1931, after a local Reverend buried some bones in the churchyard which had been discovered in a cottage, although it has been seen since, most recently in 2008 by a witness who described it as having an abnormally shiny coat and a spiked collar.

properties.name: Weaver
properties.description: A weaver returned to continue work after he died, refusing to leave until a vicar convinced the weaver he was dead. The weaver transformed into a hound and was taken to a pool of water by the vicar, who issued the dog with a broken shell and told the creature he could only leave when the pool was emptied.
properties.date: Said to briefly appear at midday and midnight (reoccurring)

properties.name: White Spotted Dog
properties.description: A secret room in the moat was said to contain nine kegs of gold protected by a large black dog with a white spot. The only way to escape with the gold was to stab the dog three times with a dagger to the white spot.

properties.name: Dog with Long Shaggy Coat
properties.description: While returning home late one night, a male witness saw a large black dog walking along the same path towards him. He stepped into the middle of the road to let it pass, only to see it disappear once close to him.
properties.location_name: Ditchingham (Norfolk) - Halfway between Ditchingham and the Maltings

properties.name: Wish Hounds
properties.description: The local shucks, known as wish hounds, reportedly stalk these roads. One of them is said to be headless.
properties.location_name: Ditchling (Sussex) - Road between town & Westmeston, and nearby downs

properties.name: Pack of Hounds
properties.description: The lands around Doneraile are home to a phantom pack of hounds, sometimes heard but not seen and at other times observed in silence. On one occasion, a witness spotted a rider leading the pack, leading to speculation that a former lord of the manor had not yet realised he should have passed over.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: The hound that haunted this road was not thought to be harmful, though the locals were said to be afraid of travelling here at night.

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A black dog haunted this stretch of road, although there have been no sightings for at least one hundred years.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: This dark hound reportedly patrols the village from dusk until dawn.

properties.name: Pack of Hounds
properties.description: A hunting pack of black dogs are reported to chase the unwary in this area of Lancashire.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: Once thought to haunt this area, the phantom black hound would appear to be mostly forgotten about.
properties.location_name: Drumcliff (aka Drumcliffe) (County Clare) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Chained Hound
properties.description: The large black canine which walked his area created the sound of jangling chains with each step it took.
properties.location_name: Dublin (County Dublin) - Pussy's Leap, Templeogue area

properties.name: Lord Norbury
properties.description: The otherworldly black hound which stalks the area is said to be the ghost of Lord Norbury, a harsh judge renowned for his overuse of the hangman's noose.

properties.name: Devil in Disguise
properties.description: The devil would take the form of a black dog and stalk the area around the bridge.
properties.location_name: Dublin (County Dublin) - Grand canal near Harcourt Bridge (aka Rialto Bridge)

properties.name: Glowing Eyes
properties.description: A witness recounted how friends were terrified by a strange black dog with glowing red eyes which appeared and disappeared around them. The sightings were accompanied by high pitched screeching.

properties.name: Stalking Hound
properties.description: Cycling from his home towards the RAF base, John Harries was stalked by a black dog. The hound stopped when Harries stopped, although it vanished when Harries reached the base.
properties.location_name: East Dereham (Norfolk) - Between village and (former) RAF Swanton Morley

properties.name: Headless Shuck
properties.description: Manifesting on dark stormy nights, this shuck brings death to those who see him within a year.
properties.location_name: East Runton (Norfolk) - Runton Heath, between East Runton and Aylmerton

properties.name: Three Hounds
properties.description: Seeing one shuck is often thought of as an ill omen - the three which seem to haunt this area in a pack are a guaranteed bad portent.

properties.name: Big Animal
properties.description: A large, black ghostly dog is reputed to walk around The Tips area.

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: The large black dog which brings death and misfortune in this neighbourhood is reportedly headless.

properties.name: Barguest
properties.description: This northern variant of the shuck is seen just prior to the death of a local man or woman.
properties.location_name: Egton (North Yorkshire) - Area around the Esk Valley

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: The grounds around this house were said to be home to a black dog, which despite its size, was quite harmless.
properties.location_name: Ennistymon (aka Ennistimon) (County Clare) - Ennistymon House (now Falls Hotel)

properties.name: Postman's Dog
properties.description: A postman reported that he would see a large phantom black dog every night, watching the creature move silently as if on a mission.
properties.location_name: Ewenny (Mid Glamorgan) - Crossroads where one road leads to Ogmore

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This port town is rumoured to be patrolled by the black shuck, though there are no dated reports of the entity being seen here.

properties.name: Black Animal
properties.description: A large black dog-like animal was once said to stand under a tree along this road, snarling at travellers.
properties.location_name: Fingest (Buckinghamshire) - Road between Fingest and Skirmett

properties.name: Dog with Glowing Eyes
properties.description: Locally known as 'Old Trash', this black dog bounds along the dunes leaving no prints or marks in the soft sand. He was last seen by two journalists and a photographer, though it is unclear whether an image was caught of the entity.

properties.name: Labrador
properties.description: Author Hugh Brawn encountered a black Labrador-like shuck here. The thin creature crossed the road in front of him but vanished before reaching the other side.

properties.name: Devil & Shuck
properties.description: Believed to have occurred in 1602, a miller was harassed by the Devil. The Fallen Angel tried to talk to the miller, who refused to stop working. The Devil gave up, turning into a large black dog before running away.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This phantom hound is thought to relate to a murder which once took place close to the church, although further details are fleeting.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: One witness claimed to have encountered a black dog while walking along the road. The witness tried to stroke it, but his hand passed through the creature. The dog then walked alongside the man until he reached Coombs Cross, when it jumped eight foot (2.4 metres) into the air and disappeared into the cemetery.

properties.name: Odin
properties.description: The large phantom dog reputed to haunt this royal household is named the 'Hound of Odin'.

properties.name: Running Dog
properties.description: The small lane leading to the local churchyard is reputedly home to a phantom hound - the creature has been spotted running along the lane. It is thought that the location that the hound appears will soon be the site of a crime. A ghostly donkey seen here may be the same entity. The same area is also haunted by a haunted coach as it travels towards Bungay Castle.
properties.location_name: Geldeston (Norfolk) - Lover's Lane, leading to the churchyard

properties.name: Wicked Parson
properties.description: Last seen sitting on a verge along Dogs Lane with glowing eyes, this phantom hound is thought to be a holy man with less than holy tendencies. The entity was also reported outside the church in 1913.
properties.location_name: Gillingham (Norfolk) - Dogs Lane and All Saints church

properties.name: Brown Dog
properties.description: The size of a cow, this large dog was mistaken for an escaped lion until it vanished into thin air.

properties.name: Dog with Long Legs
properties.description: A woman reported seeing a strange dog-like creature with long legs on two different occasions around Gorleston. When she spotted the creature the second time running down the High Street, it vanished without trace.
properties.location_name: Gorleston (Norfolk) - Suffield Road and High Street

properties.name: Large Running Dog
properties.description: While on duty at the HQ, Graham Grant watched a large black dog running in the sand, pausing every now and then to look around. It vanished without warning, in the middle of the beach.
properties.location_name: Gorleston-on-Sea (Norfolk) - Along beach, half a mile from the Gorleston Rescue Headquarters

properties.name: Pony Sized Dog
properties.description: This strange hound stands as tall as a pony, has green tinted fur, and either has a very small head or no head at all.
properties.location_name: Grantown (Moray) - A939, normally around the Glenlivet forest area

properties.name: Rainbow Eyed Barguest
properties.description: This shuck had rings of colour in its eyes and would rattle before making itself visible.
properties.location_name: Grassington (North Yorkshire) - Area near the village

properties.name: Hairy Jack
properties.description: This large black dog was said to have a particularly shaggy coat.

properties.name: Shaggy Brown Creature
properties.description: While walking her dog with her father, this witness observed a large dog-shaped figure in the middle of the track about 20 metres away. It was around a metre high, very shaggy looking and had brown fur. It was also 'blurry'. The entity vanished as the witness approached, although dematerialised as the witness walked away.
properties.location_name: Great Bookham (Surrey) - Between Polesden Lane and Admiral Walk (before the crossroads with Crabtree Lane)

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: A local man tried to hit this phantom dog with a stick while returning from the pub. The stick passed straight through the creature.
properties.location_name: Great Durnford (Wiltshire) - Chalk pit between village and Netton

properties.name: Phantom Dog
properties.description: The shucks around this area bring misfortune to any who see them, one witness having a serious road accident exactly one year after seeing a ghostly dog. Reports of a grey phantom dog have also been made, quite rare in East Anglian Shuck mythology.
properties.location_name: Great Massingham (Norfolk) - Peddar's Way & Massingham Heath

properties.name: Phantom White Dog
properties.description: Just prior to the Second World War, a farmer reported driving straight through a phantom white dog. He was so shocked he stopped his car and ran. Many cyclists in the area had also reported the creature.
properties.location_name: Great Snoring (Norfolk) - Road South of Great Snoring

properties.name: Large Black Dog
properties.description: This area lays claim to be one of the many places in East Anglia where the shuck has visited.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: This stretch of road is said to be home to Old Scarf, a black dog that haunts the area.

properties.name: Running Dog
properties.description: An animal resembling a dog with long legs was seen running along the path, vanishing within seconds of being spotted. The same entity was also observed along the High Street by the same witness, though years previous.
properties.location_name: Great Yarmouth (Norfolk) - Suffield Road, near the police station

properties.name: Bear Sized Hound
properties.description: This creature was described as white and the size of a polar bear by two witnesses as they watched it leap over engines.
properties.location_name: Great Yarmouth (Norfolk) - Yarmouth South Station (no longer standing)

properties.name: Scared Shuck
properties.description: While this shuck is said to be just as terrifying as his brethren in other parts of the country, a good loud shout is reportedly enough to drive him away with his tail between his legs.

properties.name: Thomas Colley
properties.description: The shuck spoken about around these parts is thought to be the spirit of a particularly nasty man who drowned two people thought to be witches - he was executed for his crime and his body left on a gibbet. The large black dog with red eyes was last seen sinking into the earth by the village headmaster.

properties.name: Large Black Dog
properties.description: A local legend says that the area where the Saxon church once stood is home to a large black dog which glides around. Its howls are prevalent throughout August.

properties.name: Two Dogs
properties.description: Twice over a period of two years one witness watched two phantom dogs run along this lane. A black hound is chased by another dog - both a slightly out of focus and move thirty centimetres above the ground.

properties.name: Gabriel Hounds
properties.description: To hear the howl of this demonic dog indicated the imminent death of a family member, although on one occasion, the howling came from a farmer's son who then eloped with a woman in a Scooby-Doo-que ruse that is too convoluted to summarise here.

properties.name: Snarling unlike any Dog
properties.description: When walking back from the arcades, a couple and their nineteen year old daughter witnessed a large dog with glowing red eyes, growling 'unlike any dog... ever heard before'. The family reported a run of bad luck ever since.
properties.location_name: Hemby (Norfolk) - Lane leading to a Caravan Park, behind a fence in the garden of a house

properties.name: Vanishing Dog
properties.description: The dog in question ran in front of a cyclist - the man braced for impact, only to see it vanish. A quick search revealed no sign of the creature.

properties.name: Black Dogs
properties.description: These calf-sized dogs patrol the roads connecting the two villages. A woman who encountered one of the hounds is said to have lashed out at it with her umbrella, only for the object to pass straight through the creature.
properties.location_name: Hemswell (Lincolnshire) - Area between village and Grayingham

properties.name: Fiery Eyed
properties.description: The large demonic dog of this area is said to be the size of a calf with the fires of hell burning within its eyes.

properties.name: Devil Hound
properties.description: This hound would appear to members of the Vaughan family before one of their number died. It is said that when Black Vaughan was beheaded in 1483, his faithful hound seized and ran off with the head. The creature is said to have inspired Sir Arthur Conan Doyle to write 'Hound of the Baskervilles'.
properties.location_name: Hergest (Hereford & Worcester) - Hergust Court, now a farmhouse

properties.name: Faines
properties.description: Faines were creatures that stood as tall as a calf, with large eyes. One account has the entity possess the ability to blow anyone nearby away which a large gust of wind.
properties.location_name: Hethersett (Norfolk) - Mill Road and Gravel Pit Lane

properties.name: Vanishing Hound
properties.description: A policeman reported an encounter with a black dog which suddenly vanished without trace along this road.

properties.name: Loping Phantom Dog
properties.description: Appearing on misty nights, those who see the dog are said to die before the end of the year. One witness walking home on a dark night fainted after a large black dog appeared by her side, disappearing into thin air several minutes later. It is not known if she suffered the fate that legend dictates.
properties.location_name: Hockley (Essex) - Road between Hockley and parish church

properties.name: Invisible to Some
properties.description: The barghast, a demonic dog with saucer-sized eyes, was considered a death omen. The creature which lurked here was spotted by a woman, but remained invisible to her sister who walked besides her. The sister died a month later.
properties.location_name: Holmesfield (Derbyshire) - Bury Hill ('three lane ends'), although cannot find on modern maps

properties.name: Padfoot
properties.description: A man returning home spotted a white dog in the hedgerow and hit out at it with a stick. The stick passed straight through the creature, which then turned and stared at its attacker. The man ran home, fell ill and died soon after. The shuck is said to sometimes run around on two legs and is considered a harbinger of death.

properties.name: Red Eyed Dog
properties.description: This lane is home to a local shuck; with red eyes and fur black as coal, his passing is to be taken as an ill-omen.
properties.location_name: Horton (Dorset) - Bagman's Lane and Pot Lane

properties.name: Subduing Hound
properties.description: This black dog attacked one man walking by the hall, pushing him to the ground but not causing any harm. Coincidently, at the same time, the Hall's owner died.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: This devil dog is more likely to be heard baying at the moon, its howl freezing the blood of witnesses.

properties.name: Dog of Ill Omen
properties.description: Banished by an unknown holy man, this creature would once walk through the town warning the inhabitants of misfortune.

properties.name: Rooftop Hounds
properties.description: This old fort was used by two sons of a local farmer to watch over the family cattle overnight, as the pasture was far from the family home. One evening, the children's mother heard a voice warning her that her sons were in danger. The mother ran to the fort and found her sons terrified, claiming that they had heard fairy hounds stomping and howling on the roof of the fort.
properties.location_name: Hynish (Argyll and Bute) - Fort at foot of hill in the area

properties.name: Barguest
properties.description: The large black hound was believed to lurk on the moors in this area.
properties.location_name: Ilkley (West Yorkshire) - Moorland to the north of the village, and of Otley

properties.name: White Hound
properties.description: While walking to Imogane from Liscarroll, a white dog appeared next to Matt MacMahon and accompanied him home. When Matt opened the gate leading to his house, the hound vanished (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').
properties.location_name: Imogane (County Cork) - Jer. Sullivan's Cross, travelling from Liscarroll

properties.name: The Devil Dog
properties.description: Although initially sounding like a shuck, the Devil Dog in this area is said to be the spirit of a canine that died around 1914. Small and black, the hound was reputedly seen several times in the twentieth century, trying to block pedestrians walking along the lane.
properties.location_name: Ingatestone (Essex) - Area around the Star Inn

properties.name: Big Black Dog Saves Bishop Petre
properties.description: A mugging was foiled when Bishop Petre's attackers were scared off by a large black hound which came from nowhere. As he brushed himself down, Petre realised the dog had disappeared as quickly as it had appeared.

properties.name: Farm Dog
properties.description: The lane outside of the farm was once the haunt of a phantom black hound. The same dog (or maybe another) also haunted the area around Indefont Well.
properties.location_name: Ipstones (Staffordshire) - Area around Hermitage Farm, and Indefont Well

properties.name: Burning Dog
properties.description: While visiting the island with his hunting dog, Macphie came across a mountainside bothy and decided to rest. A dark entity appeared in the doorway and the dog attacked it. Macphie noticed his dog's fur was smouldering, so fled back to his boat. The dog followed but had changing - the creature was now mad and breathed green flame. Macphie killed the dog with two sixpences fired from a shotgun.
properties.location_name: Islay (Argyll and Bute) - Bothy on Beinn na Caillich

properties.name: Potato Eater
properties.description: A crofter noticed his potato store was raided each night. One morning he discovered the tooth of a Cu Sith stuck in a potato - looks like this Cu Sith liked a meal of spuds!

properties.name: Tow Dog
properties.description: This shaggy dog that stands six foot tall has an unusual trait for a shuck - it only blocks people from passing, never injuring them. The hound is also known as the Row Dog.
properties.location_name: Isle of Portland (Dorset) - All over Island

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This black dog vanishes just as quickly as it appears.

properties.name: Tail Touch
properties.description: Mr Foley was said to have encountered a phantom hound while passing through this area. The dog's tail brushed against him, and later that night Foley was unable to sleep. He died a few days later.

properties.name: Elfin Hound
properties.description: A large black fairy hound would walk the sandbanks during storms, the creature's howl carrying in the wind. The same area was also home to bean-nighe, who could be heard scrubbing the clothing of those soon to die.
properties.location_name: Kennavarra, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - General area

properties.name: Paws the Size of Hands
properties.description: A man walking along the links changed direction after spotting a huge dog asleep on a sand dune. Returning to the place the following day, the man found the dog's pawprints in the sand, each the size of his hand.
properties.location_name: Kennavarra, Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Grassy sandy area close to a hill

properties.name: One Eye
properties.description: The phantom hound that haunts this area is said to be large, black, and in possession of only one eye.
properties.location_name: Kenworth (Bedfordshire) - Footpath between Common Road and Hollicks Lane

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: In 1949 a water worker walking along the lane early one morning encountered a large black dog with glowing eyes. The creature sat on its hind legs and watched as the worker crept around it. Other encounters are not dated. One states that a horse rider struck the demonic hound with his crop after the creature blocked his path. The dog exploded, throwing the rider from his horse.
properties.location_name: Keresley (Warwickshire) - Watery Lane and area around Hound's Hill

properties.name: Angry Dog
properties.description: Three men heading towards Kilbrittain from Clonakilty encountered a large black dog which followed them until they reached a cross in the road, where the hound disappeared. The group split up at this point, but one man reported that the dog reappeared behind him, snapping at him until he arrived home at Ballinspittle. A priest blessed the area where the creature first appeared, and there were no more reported sightings.
properties.location_name: Kilbrittain (County Cork) - Road two miles west from the village, and continuing east of village

properties.name: Two Headed Dog
properties.description: A pool in the area is said to contain treasure, though unfortunately it is guarded by a large hound with two heads. A man who managed to drain the pool of all its contents was visited by the hound, resulting in him reversing his actions and replacing the water.

properties.name: Large Dog
properties.description: Pushing their bike up the field, this witness paused to take in the view. Looking back the way they were walking, they spotted a horse-sized black dog ahead. The creature ran along a hedge at the top of the hill. The witness took their eyes from the dog for a moment, during which it vanished.
properties.location_name: Kimberley (Nottinghamshire) - Field close to the water tower

properties.name: Shuck Home
properties.description: A shuck is said to have set up home in a hole in the stream bank near this farmhouse. The farmer's wife reported seeing the creature enter the kitchen - she dared not move until the shuck left on its own accord.
properties.location_name: Kirton (Lincolnshire) - Belle Hole Farm, and nearby stream

properties.name: Lady-faced Dog
properties.description: A story here tells of shuck that possessed the face of a woman, or sometimes a woman who had the face of a dog. The hound would emerge from the hills close to where the lady lived. The entity is thought to have once been a normal woman who underwent the transformation after being murdered.
properties.location_name: Knaith (Lincolnshire) - Area around cottage, no longer standing

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A phantom hound is said to lurk around a long barrow in this area.
properties.location_name: Knighton Down (Wiltshire) - Barrow in the area

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: This dark dog is said to be extremely unlucky for all those who see it, for the creature's temper is reportedly quite heinous.

properties.name: Retriever
properties.description: This black hound is said to resemble a retriever. The creature is said to 'accompany' anyone who walks through the area. Another phantom (or perhaps the same entity in different form) is that of a figure who materialises in the back seat of cars passing through the village, visible only in the rear view mirror.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: Fortunately for any witnesses, this member of the Cwn Annwn is reported to be harmless as it runs between castle and town, although on one occasion it screamed so loudly that a witness thought the ground was moving beneath her feet.

properties.name: Padfoot
properties.description: This creature, a midnight black dog the size of a donkey, would bring bad luck and misfortune to all who saw it. It was also always heard when a local person of some importance died - his howls and barks would cause all the mortal dogs in the neighbourhood to also howl.
properties.location_name: Leeds (West Yorkshire) - Area between Wreghorn (?) and Headingly Hill

properties.name: Gabble Retchets
properties.description: These local devil dogs were believed to be the souls of those who were not baptised as children and have returned to haunt their parents.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: This park, like many in Yorkshire, is reputedly home to a large demonic black hound. A photographed was published by the local press in 2019 which claimed to show the entity but was more likely to be a dog being walked by its owner.

properties.name: Shag Dog
properties.description: Black lane was patrolled by a large black dog with bright eyes and a large, fanged mouth which glowed like burning coals. It once saved a local girl from being attacked by a robber.
properties.location_name: Leicester (Leicestershire) - Kilby Road (was once Black Lane)

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: This is another site in Dorset reported to be home to the large, black devil dog.

properties.name: Shuck Skeleton?
properties.description: The remains of a hound seven foot (2 metres) long were unearthed on the abbey grounds. Because of the proximity to the village of Bungay, some media outlets claimed the remains belonged to the Black Shuck. An old local legend says the area was home to the large black dog.

properties.name: Gallytrot Leaping over Wall
properties.description: Seen by Lady Rendlesham and Lady Walsingham, the shuck appeared at midnight, leapt over the low churchyard wall, and disappeared towards the sand hills.

properties.name: Calf Sized Dog
properties.description: An early twentieth century witness reported seeing this creature several times over the years, always when cycling at night between the two villages. A more recent witness heard howling and spotted two red eyes - after the witness's uncle died a short time later, they believed what they had encountered had been a shuck.
properties.location_name: Leverton (Lincolnshire) - Road between village and Wrangle

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: A phantom dog with no head is reputed to cross a country lane close to the farm.
properties.location_name: Leweston (Dorset) - Junction north of Stokebridge Farm

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: This large hound is said to jump from the castle ruins at people, before slinking back from where it came.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: A man cutting turf along this road just after dusk was approached by a large phantom hound. The presence worried the man, so he quickly left. It was speculated he was digging close to buried treasure, and phantom animal protecting the hoard.
properties.location_name: Lismire (County Cork) - Road between village and Bawnmore

properties.name: White Hound of Feens
properties.description: A legend says this hound, which can be heard howling in the night, is sometimes accompanied by a young lady in grey on horseback. She, in turn, has been known to ride with a wild hunt called the Dorcas Noble, while trying to win the heart of a former boyfriend. A large white hound was seen in the area during the 1980s - the size of an Irish Wolfhound, during one encounter the creature vanished as if running behind a black sheet.

properties.name: Cwn Annwn
properties.description: The Cwn Annwn, a pack of hell hounds, were heard as they passed a local man as he walked home. The invisible dogs were heading towards the nearby river.
properties.location_name: Llanhilleth (Gwent) - Unidentified road in the area

properties.name: Grey Hound
properties.description: Said to be of immense size, a large grey hound was believed to haunt the area after dark.

properties.name: Coach Crash
properties.description: A coach crash which occurred here killing the driver was reported to have been caused when a phantom dog appeared; its presence made the horses bolt which upset the carriage.
properties.location_name: Long Newnton? (Gloucestershire) - Jackament's Bottom - was a farm between Kemble and Badminton

properties.name: Devil Dog
properties.description: This hound, believed by some to be the Devil, appeared during one annual festival. The terrified attendees fled the scene, and the party was never held again.

properties.name: Fire Breathing Hound
properties.description: This abomination with cloven hooves protects a hidden hoard of treasure somewhere near this house.

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A phantom dog is said to haunt this wooded area, though no witnesses exist.

properties.name: Dark Hound
properties.description: A phantom black dog, possibly a shuck, is said to haunt this area.

properties.name: Faery Dog
properties.description: This hound has been known to leave oversized paw prints on the damp sand which vanish suddenly halfway across the beach.

properties.name: Dog on Fire
properties.description: During a storm, the gallows that once stood here were hit by lightning - a large fire quickly took hold around the area, and the witnesses who quickly arrived on the scene reported seeing a large black dog in the middle of the blaze running around the flames. The entity suddenly vanished.

properties.name: Missing Corner
properties.description: The shuck which lives here is reported to have knocked off the corner of the pub while passing by one night. The shuck was last seen in 1959 and was described as the size of a calf with glowing red eyes.
properties.date: Corner incident unknown, phantom last seen 1959
properties.location_name: Lyme Regis (Dorset) - Old Black Dog Hotel (was the Black Dog public house)

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: Departing the station, this train driver reported seeing a white misty dog move quickly across the track from left to right.
