One side of the railway stands a six metre high wall, while on the other a 3.5 metre high mesh fence - nowhere where the dog could have come from or disappeared to.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This shuck is only an ill omen for those who see him and live at the castle.
properties.date: Prior to a death or grave misfortune

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A black dog which was said to haunt this area was once heard invisibly passing by three women, although one of them speculated the sound may have been a peacock. A woman in white was also seen by a doctor using the road.
properties.location_name: Mainsriddle (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road between village and Prestonmill, area once known as Dairymaid's Hollow

properties.name: Blazing Eyed Dog
properties.description: A large black hound with burning red eyes was said to lurk in this area. A phantom grey horse is also reported, as is an old woman laden down with a bundle of faggots, drifting towards the Holy Well.

properties.name: Headless Hound
properties.description: A tradesman spotted the spectral dog outside the cathedral and the creature jumped up at him. The phantom creature was finally exorcised under the bridge over the Irwell.
properties.location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Cathedral area, & bridge over the Irwell towards Salford

properties.name: Yellow Dog
properties.description: This large hound of ill fortune is yellow in colour - one story states it was once mistaken for a lion.

properties.name: One of Three Shucks
properties.description: One of three bridges in Lincolnshire that is said to be a haunt of the black shuck.

properties.name: Headless Dog
properties.description: This headless dog would have been silent if not for the chain dragging on the floor which dangled from its neck. The owner of the property was never afraid of the entity.
properties.location_name: Manton (Wiltshire) - House (since demolished) along Preshute Lane

properties.name: Happy Puppy
properties.description: This shuck is most likely the friendliest on record - it would run around the children's bedroom, hide under beds, and was also once found in the attic.

properties.name: Not Boxer
properties.description: Mick Donovan spotted this phantom hound and mistook it for his own dog 'Boxer', calling out to the creature until he realised that the entity was a ghost.
properties.location_name: Mawbeg (County Cork) - Bridge over the River Bandon

properties.name: White Dogs
properties.description: An area around here was known as 'grave of the white dogs' and thought to be haunted by the creatures.
properties.location_name: Meenogahane (County Kerry) - Area once known as Laghtnegunbane

properties.name: Donkey Faced Dog
properties.description: The shuck that was once seen around Melton possessed a donkey's head and a smooth coat. The creature bit one man on the head as he tried to capture it, before vanishing.

properties.name: Sometimes Headless
properties.description: The shuck which haunted this churchyard sometimes appeared headless.

properties.name: Large Dog
properties.description: This dog was thought to contain the soul of a man named Blomeley, who shot several people on the site. Accompanying poltergeist like activities made it difficult for anyone to live in the house.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This dark hound is believed to be connected to the Roman fort.
properties.location_name: Mildenhall (Wiltshire) - Earthworks around Folly Farm (a former Roman camp), and surrounding villages

properties.name: Scared to Death
properties.description: One report of a shuck in this village states that the female witness who encountered the demonic dog with eyes like red hot coals died of shock three days later.

properties.name: Bulging Eyes
properties.description: A large black dog with bulging eyes, moving faster than a normal hound, was spotted by a local farmer. The farmer followed the dog as it crossed a field and reached the farmhouse of a neighbour. As the dog entered the farmhouse a firearm was heard to discharge - upon investigation the body of the neighbour was found shot dead.
properties.location_name: Milton (Ayrshire) - Unnamed fields in the area

properties.name: Transparent Hound
properties.description: The phantom hound which stalks the road here is said to be semi-transparent. Phantom horsemen are also said to ride through the area.
properties.location_name: Minchinhampton (Gloucestershire) - Area of Woefuldane Bottom, aka Woeful Danes' Bottom, aka Woeful Dane Bottom

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: Folklore says the white dog's appearance is a prelude to a death in the Norman family.

properties.name: Vanishing Dane
properties.description: While heading towards the village, a driver was forced to break when a Great Dane ran into the road. As the car stopped, the hound walked up to the car and then vanished.
properties.location_name: Molland (Devon) - Road leading into the village

properties.name: Large Dog
properties.description: The large hound which was reputed to haunt the area may have been mistaken for a ghostly donkey which quickly dissipated into mist when kicked by a passerby.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This large hound was seen several times on the road, always vanishing at the same place. Other animals also felt the presence of the creature, with one pony refusing to travel down the road - the owner had to go the rest of the journey on foot. The same area was also haunted by a woman in a white apron.
properties.location_name: Moortown (Lincolnshire) - Road leading to Moortown House

properties.name: Dark Hounds
properties.description: These relatives of the shuck stalk the shady zones of the mountainside, while water horses patrol nearby lochs. Other pieces of folklore have Schiehallion the home of fairies and strange unearthly creatures.
properties.location_name: Mount Schiehallion (Perth and Kinross) - General area

properties.name: Gwyllgi
properties.description: This Welsh shuck, with foul breath and a coat as dark as midnight, is said to haunt this piece of road. A servant who encountered the creature had to be helped home as the shock left him unable to walk. Another witness described the dog as having a human-shape, although the body and limbs were that of a large spotted hound.
properties.location_name: Mousiad (South Glamorgan) - Path between village and Lisworney Crossways

properties.name: White Hound
properties.description: The white phantom dog which lurked in this area was harmless unless provoked.

properties.name: Death Harbinger
properties.description: This black dog of ill fortune can be fended off with a crucifix, though it is unsure whether this will stop the witness of dying shortly after viewing the hound.

properties.name: Incorporeal Dog
properties.description: While walking home, a man named Finch spotted what he thought was a fellow villager's dog in the road. The dog snarled at Finch, so he tried to kick it away. His foot passed straight through the creature.
properties.location_name: Neatishead (Norfolk) - Unnamed road in the area

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: To see this huge black dog is normally a sign of ill fortune.

properties.name: A Hound Follows
properties.description: It was believed that a phantom dog would follow midwives as they travelled around the city. If the dog laughed as the midwife reached her destination, all would be well, but if the hound howled, then complications would follow. One report described the hound as Mastiff-like.
properties.location_name: Newcastle upon Tyne (Tyne and Wear) - General area

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: The area near the docks is haunted by the ghost of a sailor murdered by his parents; they failed to recognise their own son after his many years at sea and killed him for his money. He returns in the form of a large black dog, patrolling the area where the crime was committed.
properties.location_name: North Shields (Northumberland) - Milbourne Place, now Smith's Dockyards

properties.name: Large Hound
properties.description: A large black dog ran in front of two women travelling to Leeming Bar by car. The driver closed her eyes and braked hard, expecting to hit the creature. The passenger watched the hound pass through the bonnet, and noticed the creature had no facial features, floppy ears, and was shadow-like. A man who the women spoke to once they reached their destination later killed himself - was the hound a portent?
properties.location_name: Northallerton (North Yorkshire) - A684, between Northallerton and Leeming Bar

properties.name: Large Dog
properties.description: A driver and his passenger spotted a large black shaggy dog with red eyes standing in the road ahead of their car. They drove slowly past the creature, which was tall enough to look down at the occupants through the driver side window. The driver then gazed into his mirror to see the dog vanish.

properties.name: Lady Howard
properties.description: This large black dog is reportedly the ghost of Lady Howard, who walks from here to Tavistock. Some say it is a separate entity, and Lady Howard really travels by a coach made from the bones of her dead husbands.

properties.name: Large Black Dog
properties.description: A strange black dog was reported in this area in 2005.
properties.location_name: Old Costessey (Norfolk) - Area around The Heart of Costessy Pub

properties.name: Shrieking Dog
properties.description: Shuck sightings were so abundant here that the parish named a lane after the beast - local legend also states when the creature disappears, it leaves the area charred and smelling of brimstone. The path leading to Cromer lighthouse is also rumoured to be popular with the creature.

properties.name: Creature with One Yellow Eye
properties.description: A woman with her son reported seeing a creature with a single yellow eye that watched them from behind a bush. When the creature ran off, she could see that it was the size of a calf.

properties.name: Vanishing Black Dog
properties.description: A jogger spotted a black dog just in front of him while moving down the steps. Concerned for the creature's welfare, as the owner could not be seen, the jogger followed the dog. No more than a couple of seconds behind the creature, it vanished - there was no sign of the dog in the sea, and nowhere else for it to have gone. Around a year later, the jogger was talking to another person who said that they had been followed by a black dog in the same area.
properties.location_name: Peacehaven (Sussex) - Steps on cliffs leading down to beach

properties.name: Moddey Dhoo
properties.description: The local shuck, the Moddey Dhoo, haunted the local guardroom - appearing at night to sleep by their fire. One guard grew tired of hiding away from it and went to face the creature alone; he returned soon after, silent and dying, but the creature was never seen again.

properties.name: Black Dog with Drooping Ears
properties.description: While with a friend driving a horse and trap, a man reported being followed by a dog the size of a calf, with drooping ears and eyes that glowed like bicycle lamps.
properties.location_name: Peldon (Essex) - Wigborough Road between Guisnes Court and Sacott crossroads

properties.name: Phantom Hunt
properties.description: Phantom hounds and hunters were once said to make an occasional appearance here.
properties.location_name: Penbryn (Dyfed) - Unidentified farm in the parish

properties.name: Cwn Annwn
properties.description: This Welsh demonic hound has been reported in the area.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This hound makes itself known only to those who will soon die.

properties.name: Gytrack
properties.description: This phantom black hound once kept the local populace away from the area at night.
properties.location_name: Philpots Camp (Dorset) - The Big-Upon-Little Stone (aka Great-Upon-Little stone)

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: This area is reputedly home to a black spectral hound. The ghostly creature is said to cause fear in living dogs.
properties.location_name: Pontoon (County Mayo) - Crossing between Lough Conn and Lough Cullin

properties.name: Bad Omen
properties.description: A phantom hound is reputed to haunt the area around the monument, bringing bad luck or death to any witness.

properties.name: Dog and Dragon
properties.description: A fearful dragon once made its lair near here but was driven off by a mysterious white shuck-like creature. The dog is still said to haunt the area.

properties.name: Drowning
properties.description: The large, black dog that lives in an unknown cave grabs the unwary and drowns them.
properties.location_name: Portland (Dorset) - Cave Hole (or alternatively an unknown cave), close to the lighthouse

properties.name: Large Black Dog
properties.description: A black hound haunted the area where a man was once murdered.
properties.location_name: Portlaoise (County Laois) - Exact area unknown, possibly Ridge Road

properties.name: Slender White Hound
properties.description: This phantom dog is said to resemble an albino wolfhound, though some say the entity is more likely to be a phantom deer.

properties.name: Soul Gatherers
properties.description: The phantom dogs that frequent this area are reportedly sent to collect the souls of the recently dead, who in turn can sometimes be heard screaming and shouting for help. In 1953, a newspaper reported that a man was chased over the hill by what he claimed was the Devil, while the area also became a mini UFO hotspot after blue lights were seen and car engines cut out.

properties.name: Headless Dog
properties.description: It is said that the town was once haunted by a headless black hound, appearing when danger threatened the town. Another ghost said to wander the general area of Preston was that of a white woman.

properties.name: Black Dog Bounding
properties.description: The sight of a large ghostly black dog running in this area, heading towards Ely, is said to have quite common.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This black shuck would only to appear at midnight.
properties.location_name: Princethorpe (Warwickshire) - Road known as Banbury Gap, between Princethorpe and Marton

properties.name: Searcher
properties.description: This hound was once the pet of a traveller murdered in the area, and now continues to look for its owner. One man who reached out and tried to stroke the hound was struck by lightning.
properties.location_name: Princetown (Devon) - Road between town and Plymouth

properties.name: Rattling Hound
properties.description: This large demonic dog is reputed to jump around, rattling a chain behind it.
properties.location_name: Puncknowle (Dorset) - Road between village and Swyre, and area near the Old Rectory

properties.name: King John's Hounds
properties.description: Twice a year, this pack of devil dogs is let loose in the neighbourhood. Sounds of ghostly plainchant have been heard coming from the manor house.
properties.date: Varies: 20 June or 23 June and 31 December (reoccurring)

properties.name: Chained Dog
properties.description: One witness at Pound's Mill spotted a black dog with a chain hanging around its neck. The hound vanished through a closed door. The same creature is also thought to haunt other areas in the hamlet.
properties.location_name: Quemerford (Wiltshire) - Pound's Mill (no longer present), Marlborough Road, and fields in the area

properties.name: Dark Hound
properties.description: A hound of hell was once said to lurk around the tower after dark. Some say it was the reincarnation of a murdered girl fed to her unsuspecting family by her stepmother.

properties.name: Searching Dog
properties.description: This phantom hound is said to be looking for its master who was killed while cutting down a tree. The hound's presence is denoted by the dragging of its chain.
properties.location_name: Ramsbury (Wiltshire) - Area between village and Axford

properties.name: Red Eyed Dog
properties.description: The large black dog reported here has glowing red eyes. It has walked past two witnesses without interfering with them, suggesting a benign entity.
properties.location_name: Ramsey (Isle of Man) - Area around Milntown

properties.name: Haunt of Black Dog
properties.description: One of the areas that the Black Shuck is said to run along, to see this creature will result in either the witness becoming insane within two months or indicate their death within a year.
properties.location_name: Reach (Cambridgeshire) - The Devil's Ditch: defensive earthwork that ends close to Reach, crosses Newmarket Health, via A11 and A14

properties.name: Incorporeal Hound
properties.description: A woman walking home tried to pat a black dog which appeared and padded along beside her. Her hand passed straight through the creature, and it vanished a few moments later.
properties.location_name: Redcross (County Wicklow) - Country lane in the area

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: This location may have once been home to a ghostly hound.

properties.name: Dog under Horses' Hooves
properties.description: A couple in a trap encountered the Shuck, and it ran under their horses. The driver tried to hit the black dog with his whip, but it passed straight through the creature. The same area is also haunted by a phantom coach, being driven at breakneck speed.

properties.name: Combusting Dog
properties.description: A shuck which lurked here transformed itself into a raging fire, the size of a field, to scare a local man into respecting the Sabbath.

properties.name: Skriker
properties.description: A man who encountered Skriker, the fiery-eyed black dog of doom, standing on the bridge expected his own life to shortly end; unfortunately, it was his eldest son and his wife who died within a few weeks of the incident.

properties.name: Pack of Hounds
properties.description: This vicious pack of dogs belonged to a rogue priest, who allowed the hounds to freely hunt and kill whatever they found. Another legend says the Devil did not dare cross this river into Cornwall, as he feared the Cornish would turn him into a pie. Finally, a myth states that the river was a nymph named Tamara who was turned into a spring by her father, angered by her flirting with a couple of local giants.

properties.name: Lightning Dog
properties.description: Walking home one night, a man encountered a large black dog. He tried to pat the creature, but his hand passed straight through the dog's head. The dog then yawned, and after smelling the sulphur-like breath, the witness fled, pursued by the dog. After a while, the man was thrown to the ground by a blinding flash, where he was found unconscious the next day. A legend says the phantom hound belonged to a murder victim, and now it tries to kill every man it encounters.
properties.date: December evening, possibly nineteenth century (if not, earlier)

properties.name: Large Hound
properties.description: This area is reputedly haunted by a huge black hound which protects treasure buried somewhere under the structure. Another version of the legend says a swarm of rats and not a dog protect the treasure.
properties.location_name: Rock of Dunamase (County Laois) - General area

properties.name: The Hunt
properties.description: A wild hunt, complete with hell hounds, was once believed to be at home in this woodland.

properties.name: Bright White Dog
properties.description: The witness to this entity crashed his car after swerving to avoid the partially transparent dog which ran out into the road.

properties.name: Padfeet
properties.description: A gorge or hillside chasm was home to the neighbourhood's demonic hounds.
properties.location_name: Saddleworth (Greater London) - Gorge, was known as Hell Mouth

properties.name: Large Wolfhound
properties.description: Belonging to a drowned skipper, the dog failed to save his master's life, and now runs along the beach. His glowing eyes and black coat firmly place him in the shuck category. The creature was last seen by a man cycling in the area - he reported spotting a large shaggy dog which passed through a solid gate.
properties.date: Seventeenth century onwards, last confirmed sighting early twentieth century

properties.name: Trotting Dog
properties.description: Only appearing at night, a strange hound would trot alongside anyone walking between the two areas.
properties.location_name: Sandwick (Orkney) - Road between Sandwick and Birsay

properties.name: Black Hounds
properties.description: The forest is reported to be home of several black dogs which bring ill fortune to any witnesses. There are also stories of a ghostly white deer which darts between the trees, a headless woman riding on horseback, and a coach and horses which leaves the forest and heads towards a nearby farm.

properties.name: Protective Shuck
properties.description: This guardian dog appeared by the side of a woman, preventing a gang of labourers from attacking her. It vanished as she reached home.
properties.location_name: Scunthorpe (Lincolnshire) - Road between Scunthorpe and Crosby

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A large black hound is said to haunt this churchyard and has been seen vanishing into a large headstone.
properties.location_name: Seatown (Dorset) - Old graveyard, where the A35 intersects the road leading away from village

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This dog, known as the Capelthwaite, is the harbinger of death, and anyone who spots the creature will either lose their own life or that of someone close to them.
properties.location_name: Sedbergh (Cumbria) - Area around Capelthwaite farm (no longer present or renamed)

properties.name: Shaggy Dog
properties.description: This shuck comes across as quite harmless and seems to appreciate the company of living animals.

properties.name: Grey Dog
properties.description: This phantom dog, slightly larger than a collie and in dire need of a groom, walks silently along this road at dawn and dusk.
properties.location_name: Selkirk (Borders) - B7009 between town and Langholm

properties.name: Suicide Shuck
properties.description: This shuck is thought to contain the soul of a local man who committed suicide.

properties.name: Expected Accident
properties.description: This large black dog would run across the road before leaping over a cliff that dropped several hundred feet. A few days later a serious accident would occur at that spot.

properties.name: Gabriel Hounds
properties.description: These hounds are said to have the faces of men, and have been heard passing the church on dark, still nights.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Area around parish church

properties.name: Saucer Eyed Dog
properties.description: This creature, with its unusually large eyes, has been known to turn itself into mist if threatened. A ghost hunter in the area reported to have spoken to a couple who witnessed a large black dog appear out of nowhere as they travelled down the lane.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Bunting Nook, Graves Park

properties.name: Scratching Hound
properties.description: A phantom dog attacked one witness walking along this road - it bit his calf and clawed his back, leaving red weals. The man developed a high fever but later recovered after a spirit exorcism was performed.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Road outside the Botanical Gardens

properties.name: Shock
properties.description: The black dog known as Shock to residents of the town would emerge from the sea and run up the hills. The entity was said to be headless, with white cloth covering the stump.

properties.name: Vanishing Hound
properties.description: A dog was encountered by a local man as he walked home late one night. Wanting to move the creature from his path, the man threw a stick at it - the piece of wood passed straight through the hound, causing it to vanish.

properties.name: Black Hound
properties.description: A priest was called in to rid a building of a large black dog that had taken up residence - the creature was banished to under the river Wyre, allowed only to come back when the river dried up.

properties.name: The Padfoot
properties.description: This black, horse-sized hound unset the villagers when it paid them a brief visit.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: An unnaturally large dark hound ran across the lawn.
properties.location_name: Snitterfield (Warwickshire) - Brook House (AKA Bell Brook Inn)

properties.name: Disappearing Dog
properties.description: A large black dog here often escorted a local farmer as he drove his cart to market. When a girl tried to pat its head, it vanished.

properties.name: Doggy Escort
properties.description: A black dog would often appear along this road, trotting by the side of horse riders and motorcyclists.
properties.location_name: South Muskham (Nottinghamshire) - Crow Lane, between village and Bathley

properties.name: Cu Sith
properties.description: A woman walking with two friends in the pitch dark watched as a self-illuminating dog, the size of a collie but with a small head and no eyes, ran towards her. The creature vanished as it bounded past, but the two men with her saw nothing at all. Once she reached home and described what had happened to her aunt, the older woman told her it was a Cu Sith, a fairy dog.
properties.location_name: South Uist (Outer Hebrides) - Road alongside Lochnan Eilean?

properties.name: Calf Sized Dog
properties.description: A calf-sized dog walking on the other side of the road was spotted by the passenger in a car (although not by the driver), unnerving the witness considerably.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: If it was not bad enough that this stretch of road was haunted by a shuck, some also say the area is also home to a phantom coffin that lies ominously in the middle of the lane and a grey misty shade of indeterminate origin.
properties.location_name: St Audries (Somerset) - Road between village and Holford

properties.name: Noisy Dog
properties.description: This large black dog (some say it is a bear) is said to have sounded like a horse trotting, startling the witnesses who had expected to see a pony.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A large black dog was said to haunt the lanes and the farms in this area, as far down as Copplestone.
properties.location_name: St Giles In the Wood (Devon) - Lanes in the area

properties.name: Headless Dog
properties.description: A large black headless dog was said to patrol this road at night. Those brave enough to strike out at the hound hit nothing but air.

properties.name: Calf Sized Hound
properties.description: This large black dog was said to have caused one man to die of shock after it sat in his cart.
properties.location_name: St Pierre du Bois (Guernsey) - Road leading to St Saviours from L'Eree

properties.name: Shuck
properties.description: Large black dogs run about this area, a witness from the 1930s claiming to have seen one the size of a pony. A later witness said they had spotted the entity on more than one occasion during the early 2000s, and that the large hound was non-threatening.

properties.name: Pack
properties.description: This ancient site is patrolled by a pack of large black shucks. The barrows are said to have been constructed by the devil.

properties.name: Harriet
properties.description: The shuck which appears here is believed to contain the spirit of Harriet, once a local witch who remains much feared.

properties.name: Phantom Pooch
properties.description: This ancient burial mound is reportedly haunted by a dog, which conceals itself beneath the hill.

properties.name: Shuck with a Monk's Body and Dog's Head
properties.description: This strange entry in the 'shuck' category stands guard over a hidden hoard of gold. A legend states that St Felix instructed a monk and a dog to guard the buried treasure, and over the years the entities merged into a single presence.

properties.name: Cat or Dog?
properties.description: Reports vary on whether the creature that haunts this area is a large dog or very large cat. Either way, the entity was said to have large, saucer shaped eyes, and people went out of their way to avoid the area.
properties.location_name: Sturminster Newton (Dorset) - Castle ruins and Newton Hill

properties.name: Headless Dog
properties.description: The appearance of this headless hound denoted a forthcoming tragedy.
properties.location_name: Swaledale (North Yorkshire) - Humpbacked bridge leading to Ivelet

properties.name: Cat Dog
properties.description: A large black dog was seen by two witnesses as it ran across the road ahead of them. It moved more like a cat than dog but did not look feline. One of the witnesses, a vet, was not able to identify it.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This demonic hound once haunted the area before the area became a suburb of Swindon.
properties.location_name: Swindon (Wiltshire) - Hill in Toothill area (was known as The Toot)

properties.name: Large Dog
properties.description: It was reported that a large dog with red eyes was seen by three people over a two day period. The hound appeared unable to cross an old metal bridge.
properties.location_name: Swindon (Wiltshire) - Old canal bridge between Shrivenham Road and Drakes Way

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: The road between these two locations was once believed to be the haunt of a shuck.
properties.location_name: Swindon (Wiltshire) - Road between Moredon and Haydon Wick

properties.name: Guard Dog
properties.description: The site where three Jacobites are buried is haunted by a large black hound that ensures their graves are not disturbed.

properties.name: Velvet Chain
properties.description: An invisible dog runs around this area, with a chain as soft as velvet. One would imagine this makes it one of the hardest entities to detect in the whole of the UK. A legend says that a clump of yew trees in the region of the village conceal a silver table.

properties.name: Lady Howard
properties.description: Lady Howard is said to have poisoned two of her four husbands, and now takes the form of a large black dog with a single eye which departs the driveway of Fitzford House, followed by a carriage pulled by headless horses, heading towards Okehampton Castle. Once they reach their destination, Lady Howard plucks a single blade of grass and then returns home, doomed to repeat the action until there is no grass left and she is released.
properties.location_name: Tavistock (Devon) - Fitzford House driveway, heading towards Okehampton

properties.name: Black Dog Lies near Bed
properties.description: Never returning since the destruction of the building, a large black dog was seen on several occasions, lying across the beds.

properties.name: Banishing the Dog
properties.description: The ancient custom of 'clipping the church' once preceded the chasing of the black dog, which was thought to be Satan, out of the village's boundary.

properties.name: Horse sized Dog
properties.description: This great dog is said to be the size of a small horse, also sharing a few other equestrian characteristics.

properties.name: Bloody Tongue
properties.description: Though the exact location of the well has now been lost, it was guarded by a large black hound with glowing red eyes, known as 'Bloody Tongue'. The beast was sometimes accompanied by the ghost of an old hag with a storm lantern.
properties.location_name: Thornton, Bradford (West Yorkshire) - Jim Craven Well

properties.name: Barguest
properties.description: Little is known about the Throstlenest shuck, which patrols the area between Darlington and Houghton.

properties.name: Vanishing Shuck
properties.description: While cycling home, a witness reported that a large black dog had leapt out in front of him, before disappearing through a solid wall.
properties.location_name: Thurton (Norfolk) - Near the village, precise details unknown

properties.name: Barking Hound
properties.description: This harbinger of doom, known locally as a Cusith, was renowned for its barking - if it yapped once or twice, it was a sign everything was okay, but three times warned of incoming danger for the listener or their family. Some people believe that the creature can become semi-human in appearance.
properties.location_name: Tiree (Argyll and Bute) - Cladach a Chrogain beach

properties.name: Cu Sith
properties.description: Two boys spending the night in a shieling hut were awoken by the sound of a large creature on the roof howling. When dawn came and they ventured out, they discovered the large prints of a Cu Sith on the turf roof.

properties.name: Pack of Hounds
properties.description: Wailing at the moon, this pack of devil dogs, known locally as Gabriel Ratchets' run over both land and water looking for people to terrify.
properties.location_name: Todmorden (West Yorkshire) - Area between Todmorden and Mankinholes

properties.name: Dog as Large as a Bike
properties.description: Shuck sightings appear quite common in this area. This encounter involved a woman coming face to face with a black dog with a bright red tongue, which was as large as her bicycle. It chased her (or ran alongside her, according to other stories) as she peddled down the road, disappearing soon after.
properties.location_name: Tollesbury (Essex) - On the route to Jordan's Green, along the lane that leads up to Gorwell Hall.

properties.name: Snapping and Snarling Black Dog
properties.description: A cyclist reported being attacked by a large black dog while travelling down the coast road. Leaping off his bike to scare the animal, it disappeared before his eyes.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This wish hound has been seen running along this stretch of road.

properties.name: Lean Hound
properties.description: A black, lean dog with fiery red eyes would stand in the middle of the road in his village, sinking into the ground if approached. It was thought to be the spirit of a chimneysweep executed for murder.
properties.location_name: Tring (Hertfordshire) - Area where gibbet once stood

properties.name: Le Tchan de Bouole
properties.description: This dog, sometimes dragging a large chain, is said to appear before the area is hit by bad weather. Writer Linda Corby claimed that three of her friends had seen this large black hound a few hours before the Great Storm of 1987 (although there may be a more mundane explanation), while in 2008 a car full of people were chased by a black shadow accompanied by the sound of dragging chains.
properties.date: Weather Dependent: Prior to a storm (last seen 2008)

properties.name: Huge Dog
properties.description: A very large black hound is said to stalk the village, and may have attacked two locals in 1745, killing one and severely injuring the other.

properties.name: Large Dog
properties.description: During a thunderstorm, a driver spotted a large black dog cross the road ahead and move down a residential lane. The driver stopped to look for the hound, but it had vanished. A phantom black dog is said to haunt the nearby Carsington Water.
properties.location_name: Turnditch (Derbyshire) - Unnamed lane, and Carsington Water

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: A demonic black dog once haunted this area.

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: This small piece of woodland was the home of a phantom dog that would physically prevent anyone from walking between the two villages.
properties.location_name: Tyneham (Dorset) - Boatswain's Coppice, between village and Lulworth

properties.name: Growing Hound
properties.description: The black dog here increases in size until it matches the height of the local trees, after which it turns into vapour and vanishes. A local tale reports it haunted a farmhouse that once stood nearby - the farmer once chased the dog away, and it vanished into the ceiling of one of the rooms; on examination, he found much gold hidden in the roof where it disappeared.
properties.location_name: Uplyme (Devon) - Lane near Black Dog public house

properties.name: Intangible Hound
properties.description: Out walking during the evening, a girl watched a phantom black dog pass through a wire fence.
properties.location_name: Upper Booth (Derbyshire) - Exact area not known

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: The creature known as the Upware Hound would walk the lanes at night.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This hound is thought to be a harbinger of misfortune.
properties.location_name: Urchfont (Wiltshire) - Unspecified footpath that crosses fields and allotment

properties.name: Phantom Dog, Calf Size
properties.description: For four hundred years, a black shuck has been reported in the area, local legend stating that it is the Devil incarnate. The creature has most been recently witnessed by two women during the 1980s; they reported seeing a large phantom dog, black in colour and the size of a calf.
properties.location_name: Walberswick (Suffolk) - Road between the Bell Hotel and the Vicarage, and the beach leading to Dunwich

properties.name: Cursed Castle
properties.description: After being caught stealing from her customers, an old servant at the castle lost her job. In revenge, she bewitched the place - a large black dog appeared and created havoc. The creature was defeated by quotes from the bible read aloud by a local holy man.

properties.name: Hunting Pack
properties.description: These Yeth hounds stalk as a pack after dark on the hill side.

properties.name: Gurt Dog
properties.description: This shuck has proved quite helpful when encountered, appearing to lost walkers and leading them to safety. However, another entity in the same area known as the Wild Rider is considered fatal if met.

properties.name: Gabriel Hounds
properties.description: Gabriel Hounds, demonic dogs that could fly, would often be heard in this region. Other witnesses thought they were geese.

properties.name: Fire Breathing Hounds
properties.description: This pack of albino dogs run while breathing flame from their mouths.

properties.name: Shuck Attack
properties.description: Local legend says a man was attacked by a shuck down a lane between Wells and Stiffkey. Author Christopher Marlowe was allegedly chased by the same creature a little short time later, while out looking for the beast.
properties.location_name: Wells-next-the-Sea (Norfolk) - Lane leading to marshes between Wells & Stiffkey

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: Appearing to mark the start of the longest day of the year, this pale hound has been observed at sunrise. Some reports say the dog is accompanied by a ghostly druid. In 1995 a witness heard an almost unbearable high-pitched sound before observing a Neolithic man emerge from the barrow and walk away. The witness was terrified and fled. An experience in 1996 resulted in a witness feeling as if they had hit an invisible wall and something was trying to push them out, and they later read of someone four years earlier who had been grabbed by invisible hands.
properties.date: 23 June (or Midsummer Eve), sunrise (reoccurring). Encounters in 1995 and 1996

properties.name: Dog with Large Eyes
properties.description: This canine entity with eyes as large as saucers is said to roam the fields around the lane.

properties.name: Pitch Forked Dog
properties.description: A farmhand discovered a dog laying in the hay - he poked it with his pitchfork, causing the entity to vanish. The sound of a coach has also been heard as it stops by the front of the house, though nothing is seen.

properties.name: Cerberus
properties.description: This three-headed hound stands guard over a hoard of hidden treasure somewhere on this site.

properties.name: Barghest
properties.description: This demonic dog is the size of a small bear, with yellow eyes as large as saucers. To stare the beast in one of these eyes will result in the death of the onlooker within days.

properties.name: Former Knight
properties.description: One local legend says that a knight was driven to madness and suicide after having his love rejected by a ranger's daughter. After she died, the knight's ghost returned in the form of the shuck, to hunt and chase the spirit of the woman. Other local legends say that the shuck is just part of a larger phantom hunt.

properties.name: White Dog
properties.description: A young man driving his car was said to have been chased by a large white dog which reached up to speeds of thirty miles per hour for prolonged periods.
properties.location_name: Wick St Lawrence (Somerset) - Back lanes around the village

properties.name: Shuck
properties.description: A calf-sized dog bringing death to all that see it runs this area of the Fen, which is currently a nature reserve.

properties.name: One of Three Shucks
properties.description: One of three bridges in Lincolnshire that is said to be a haunt of the black shuck.

properties.name: Hairy Jack
properties.description: Known locally as Hairy Jack, the black dog which haunts this area is said to be covered in pigs' hair. A local man was said to have been pushed into a gatepost by an invisible dog after it placed its paws against his shoulders.

properties.name: Panting Dog
properties.description: The phantom hound found here would be completely silent, though the dog's panting gives away its location. The Roman road is also haunted by a woman in black, said to move so quickly that she is impossible to catch.
properties.location_name: Wilton (Wiltshire) - Field known locally as Severals (path of old Roman road)

properties.name: Dog with Glowing Eyes
properties.description: The devil dog found in Exmoor has been known to dematerialise slowly, leaving just a pair of red glowing eyes behind.
properties.location_name: Winsford (Somerset) - Winsford Hill, and surrounding Exmoor area

properties.name: Bridge Dogs
properties.description: Two bridges along this stretch of road are the haunt of the black shuck.

properties.name: Long Legged Dog
properties.description: A dark dog with long legs and drop ears suddenly appeared in front of a car. As the driver passed, the dog vanished as quickly as it had arrived.

properties.name: Galley Trot
properties.description: The large demonic dog once reported around Woodbridge was white and as large as a cow. The creature was said to give chase if people fled from it.
properties.date: Unknown, but prior to twentieth century

properties.name: Ghostly Dog
properties.description: A large black hound has been observed here several times, recognisable from its lack of ears and saucer-sized eyes.

properties.name: Talking Shuck
properties.description: The shuck in this yarn caught a man and spoke, informing the unfortunate gentleman that he would be dead within a week. Not surprisingly, he died the following night.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: Unsurprisingly, it is a black dog that haunts the road which has taken its name. It is believed to only reveal itself to those soon to die.

properties.name: Huge Dog
properties.description: A black dog with glowing red eyes standing taller than a Great Dane was encountered by a woman driving home. The creature appeared to be dragging something large across the road, although the witness was not sure what it was. Distracted by something else for a few moments, the woman turned back to look at the hound, only to find it had gone.
properties.location_name: Worksop (Nottinghamshire) - Blyth Road, near Hodsock Priory

properties.name: Headless Dog
properties.description: The site of a murder which took place many years ago was said to have become home to a headless black dog.
properties.location_name: Yeaton (Shropshire) - Road between village and Baschurch

properties.name: Barghest
properties.description: This large black hound with red glowing eyes, known as Barghest, Barguist or Barguest, is said to prey on humans alone along the snickleways in the city. The creature has also been spotted in the old castle area.
properties.location_name: York (North Yorkshire) - Side roads and snickleways in city

properties.name: Pensioner
properties.description: The remains of an aging soldier were discovered in a barn along this road; much of the body had been reduced to ash, though the face could be recognised. The bales of straw surrounding him failed to catch fire.

properties.name: Thomas Williams
properties.description: Human combustion, but in this case not so spontaneous. A sailor who liked his rum, Thomas Williams fell unconscious after a drinking session. His friend summoned a doctor who arrived after dark. The attending physician held a candle close to Thomas, who promptly caught light, bathed in blue flame. A bucket of water thrown over the fire only made the flames larger. The witnesses all left (for reasons not fully explained) and returned the following morning to find Thomas's torso completely consumed, but his shirt only charred. Thomas's friends said that he had been wicked and that the devil had come for him.

properties.name: First Irish Case?
properties.description: The BBC reported that Dr Ciaran McLoughlin, West Galway coroner, had recorded a verdict of spontaneous combustion in a case he had investigated. The body of Michael Faherty had been found in his living room, totally burnt, although damage to the rest of the room was minimal.
properties.location_name: Ballybane (County Galway) - Private residence, Clareview Park

properties.name: Fire Dancer
properties.description: As Phyllis Newcombe (22) left the dance floor, her dress burst into flame, and she died a few hours later in hospital. Tests showed that her clothing could not be ignited with a cigarette.

properties.name: Soldier
properties.description: The body of a soldier was discovered in a hay loft, much of his corpse charred by fire, but the surrounding area untouched by the flames. His legs and boots were also undamaged.

properties.name: Mrs Stout
properties.description: Mrs Stout was seen going to bed, apparently fine, but found dead the following morning. Her body was heavily burnt, with parts not altered by fire crumbling when touched. Her nightdress remained undamaged by the fire. An inquest determined she had 'died by the visitation of God'.

properties.name: Untouched Armchair
properties.description: The body of a seventy two year old was found reduced to ash, leaving only a pair of feet in socks, a soot covered skull, and an undamaged armchair in which most of the remains sat...
properties.location_name: Ebbw Vale (Gwent) - Council house, exact location unknown

properties.name: Barry Soudaine
properties.description: Barry Soudaine was found his kitchen, only a foot and a trainer remaining amongst a pile of ash. Some plastic fittings in the kitchen had melted, but there was not serious fire damage. The coroner recorded an open verdict regarding the death.

properties.name: Elizabeth Clark
properties.description: Elizabeth Clark spent the night here and was discovered in the morning covered in heinous burns which later claimed her life. No one heard any noises during the night, and although covered in the ash from her nightdress, the bed itself was not scorched.
properties.location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Trinity Almshouse

properties.name: Torso Reduced to Ash
properties.description: Grace Pett's burnt body was discovered in her house; other people living there were not aware of the fire that had killed her during the night.

properties.name: Blackened Remains
properties.description: The charred remains of John Anderson could only be recognised by the horses and cart found by his body. One witness said that John had fallen from his cart while burning, his horses remaining still, and that the fire had been extinguish with buckets of water within fifteen minutes of starting. The doctor who performed the autopsy believed the fire to be 'no ordinary combustion'.
properties.location_name: Nairn (Highland) - Area close to Hardmuir Wood

properties.name: Annie Webb
properties.description: When Annie was found, only a pair of legs, piece of arm and skull remained. A white tablecloth lay untouched nearby.
properties.location_name: Newport (Gwent) - Private house along Corporation Street

properties.name: Mrs Carpenter Bursts into Flame
properties.description: While enjoying a boating holiday, Mrs Carpenter burst into flame and was reduced to a charred corpse.

properties.name: Combusting Cat
properties.description: Peppi the cat enjoyed life at the nursing home, before exploding into blue flame with a bang and a flash. If the story is true, then Peppi could be a rare example of Spontaneous Feline Combustion.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Stanmore (Greater London) - Anmer Lodge nursing home

properties.name: Body in the Basement
properties.description: A young daughter found her mother burnt to death in the basement - some witnesses described the body as charred, while the room itself was barely touched.
properties.location_name: Reading (Berkshire) - Private house along Baker Street

properties.name: Robert Francis Bailey
properties.description: This homeless man's burning body was found in an empty building, blue flame still coming from his charred body. It was though Robert was still alive when he combusted, as his teeth were dug deep into a wooden stair banister pole.

properties.name: Human Fireball
properties.description: A girl walking down the stairs with a group of friends suddenly burst into flame. They quickly beat the flames out, but she later died of her injuries.

properties.name: Woman's Skull
properties.description: This item is said to be impossible to remove from the building - it always teleports back!

properties.name: Chatty Fellows
properties.description: A local legend tells of talking skulls found in this graveyard, though what became of them is not known.

properties.name: Bony Guardian
properties.description: Home to the skull of a slave, the object is said to scream if removed from the house. One former owner threw the skull into a pond, only to fish it out two days later after being kept awake at night by supernatural 'sounds'. The site is also home to phantom coach and horses, and/or a phantom funeral procession.

properties.name: Woman in Black
properties.description: When the skull of a local girl was displayed here, several people reported seeing a spectral female figure standing by it. The skull's owner was convinced the item was cursed and died a week after finally giving it away.

properties.name: Footfalls
properties.description: Loud footsteps can be heard traversing empty parts of the building - it may be the last remnants of the haunted skulls that once were on display here, since relocated to Turton Tower.

properties.name: Cursed Cranium
properties.description: The cursed skull created havoc whenever removed from the property, so a solution-focused individual finally walled it up in a room of the hall.

properties.name: Cursed Skull
properties.description: When the skull was last removed from the hall (as a joke), it was blamed for spontaneous fires and family deaths. The skull was quickly returned and has not left since.
properties.location_name: Browsholme (Lancashire) - Browsholme Hall (referred to as Bowland Hall by Eric Maple)

properties.name: Anne
properties.description: Falling in love with the hall, Anne decreed that upon death her head should be severed and stored within the house. Immediately after she was murdered, the house was plagued by crashes and bangs, which ceased when her head was removed from the coffin and brought inside. Her skull has since been bricked up to ensure it never accidentally leaves the homestead.
properties.location_name: Burton Agnes (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Burton Agnes Hall

properties.name: George Whewell
properties.description: If removed from the public house, this discoloured skull which once belonged to executioner George Whewell is said to scream until returned.
properties.location_name: Bury (Greater Manchester) - The Pack Horse Inn, Affetside

properties.name: Screaming Skull
properties.description: The skull of William Corder, infamous for the Red Barn Murder, was stored in the prison - it was thought to be possessed by evil, and strange sounds and spectral figures were seen around it. The skull was finally removed and buried in an unknown local country graveyard.
properties.location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Old Bury Gaol (possibly)

properties.name: Duellists
properties.description: Two skulls are currently stored in the Towers, that when stored in previous places, have been blamed for causing two ghostly men to appear and begin fighting. Poltergeist activity is also attached to the stories, and a woman apparently dressed in mourning costume haunts the staircase of the tower (and possibly an old path outside the tower). The area outside the tower is haunted by a phantom coach.

properties.name: Theophilu Brome
properties.description: Local legend says that when the skull of Theophilu Brome (or Broome) has been removed in the past, the house has been besieged by howls and wails. When the tomb of Theophilus Brome was opened in the local church, the skull was found to be missing.

properties.name: Drink Obsessed
properties.description: This entity, which enjoys switching the barrels off and causing general mischief, could be related to the thousand year old skull discovered in the basement.
properties.location_name: Derby (Derbyshire) - O'lafferty's public house (once known as The George), Sadlergate

properties.name: Drifting Nun
properties.description: Appearing at dusk, the figure of a nun has been reported floating down this lane. Her skull is housed at Pennis Farm and it is said if the object is ever removed a great curse will befall the valley.

properties.name: Livvy
properties.description: The bar manager watched a faceless girl in a white nightgown walk through the closed front door. The ghost is thought to be Livvy, who drowned in the Urswick Tarn. A skull nicknamed 'Jimmy', believed to be bad luck if touched, is kept in a cupboard that sometimes opens on its own.
properties.location_name: Great Urswick (Cumbria) - General Burgoyne public house

properties.name: Dafydd
properties.description: The skull of Dafydd, a Welsh Prince, can be found here. The last time it was removed from the building, the person responsible was plagued by shadowy figures and screams - these stopped once the skull was returned.

properties.name: Unhappy Skull
properties.description: The skull (or skulls - one version of the story has two) causes trouble whenever removed from the site - it is said that to prevent 'accidental removal', the skull is now part of the brickwork.

properties.name: Dafydd
properties.description: The skull of Dafydd Prince of Wales is housed in a box at the hall. A curse is said to befall the owners of the building if it is ever removed from the property.

properties.name: Freda
properties.description: It is said that if the Freda's skull located behind the bar of this pub is moved, the former owner returns and haunts the building until the cranium is replaced.
properties.location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Ye Old White Hart public house

properties.name: Reappearing Skulls
properties.description: Belonging to a married farming couple, the skulls came back to haunt the person who framed them for a crime that resulted in their execution. No matter how they were destroyed, the grinning skulls always returned to the hall, until the death of the man they cursed.

properties.name: Talking Skulls
properties.description: A local legend speaks of a pair of skulls which once resided in the churchyard which would chatter away after dark.

properties.name: Walled Up Skull
properties.description: Not much is known about the skull and its surrounding legend, other than to prevent the removal of the object, it was bricked up in the attic.
properties.location_name: Lund (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Manor house

properties.name: Francis Downes
properties.description: This building is reported to have had a skull bricked up within its walls, to prevent it from accidentally being removed and condemning the house's occupiers to misfortune or death. The macabre relic is thought to belong to Edward Barlow, a Catholic executed by Protestants in 1640.

properties.name: Sister
properties.description: Two sisters once lived here and fell in love with the same man. One sister murdered the other, but before dying, she said that she would never rest in any grave. The dead sister's bones were stored in a container on the property and if moved, strange noises could be heard at night and the farm's cattle would die.

properties.name: Headless Sister
properties.description: The house connected to the park is home to a skull of a woman - it is believed to belong to the ghost of a young girl who carries her head under her arm, killed by her own sister. Another legend connected to the park is the 'Elm-Ash' tree, a single tree comprising of both types of wood given in its name - it was said to have grown from the grave of a male suicide into which two wooden stakes were thrust to ensure he did not return.

properties.name: Teleporting Relic
properties.description: The home of a skull with history unknown, the relic returned home despite several attempts by the property owners to dispose of it. They finally stopped trying and bricked up the skull behind a wall.

properties.name: Homely Skull
properties.description: The home of another screaming skull, the building would echo with the sounds of cries and moans if the morbid item were removed.

properties.name: Dicky of Tunstead
properties.description: Dicky is the name given to the skull that resided in the farmhouse (now thought to be buried on the grounds). The ghost of a girl that appeared in the 1880's was blamed on Dicky, as was the large black dog that followed locals around the farmland after dark.
properties.location_name: Whaley Bridge (Derbyshire) - Tunstead Farm, located at Combs Edge, between Whaley Bridge and Chapel-en-le-Frith

properties.name: Skeleton
properties.description: These prehistoric barrows were destroyed to make room for farmland, though one story states that one farmhand found and removed a human skull from the site, only to have the rest of the skeleton visit him during the following night, demanding the return of its head.

properties.name: Orange Lights
properties.description: A handful of reports concerning strange lights were recorded during 2006. One family observed six orange lights hovering in the sky before appearing to disintegrate.

properties.name: Green Light
properties.description: A witness spotted a green glowing bell-shaped object hovering above the square. The witness followed the object as it moved towards the pier and finally watched as the green light vanished out to sea. The witness later discovered the same object had been seen by a policeman who had been ridiculed at work after reporting the UFO.
properties.location_name: Aberystwyth (Dyfed) - Sky above St James Square

properties.name: Landing Craft
properties.description: This ufo report was based on a story made up by two young people returning late from a leave pass. As time went on, the story grew, and it eventually became that an UFO had touched down near a hangar on the base, and was recovered and removed for reverse engineering.

properties.name: Round Platform with Handrail around Edge
properties.description: A female witness watched for 5 minutes as this disk carrying a dozen men dressed like sailors flew overhead, at a height of about 30 feet.

properties.name: Little Green Men
properties.description: An elderly man who set up here for a spot of night fishing was invited into a UFO by two little men in tight green outfits, with visors covering their faces. They shone a light at him before saying that he was 'too old', promptly returning him to his fishing rod.

properties.name: Golden Men
properties.description: A youngster playing in the grounds of the school watched a round craft land and three figures dressed in golden clothing climb out. The child ran off to find friends to tell, and when he returned with someone the craft was already back in the sky - it quickly flew off.
properties.location_name: Anlaby (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Anlaby school, playing ground

properties.name: Man with No Face
properties.description: Thought to be an early alien encounter (though it can also be interpreted as ghostly in nature), a woman playing in the fields came across a figure without a face dressed in black. Behind him, she could see a circular 'hut'. By the time she awoke her father, who was sleeping in a nearby field, both hut and man had gone.
properties.location_name: Anstey (Leicestershire) - Fields along Anstey Road, leading to Leicester

properties.name: Floating Lights
properties.description: Admiralty Intelligence Officers investigated a series of strange lights which were seen in the skies over the area by multiple witnesses. The investigating officers also watched the lights rise into the air and vanish. The officers' report suggested that the lights may have been attached to a balloon used to lift wireless equipment to enable German spies to contact the mainland, although no evidence could be found.
properties.location_name: Ashburton (Devon) - Skies north of the village

properties.name: Triangle
properties.description: Three large orange lights which formed triangular patterns were observed in the sky to the north-east.

properties.name: Mystery Lights
properties.description: Some say that UFO sightings here go back at least three hundred years, though only more recently have crop circles and photographs of glowing balls of light been documented.

properties.name: Hairy Creatures with Large Pointed Ears
properties.description: While driving home, a family of four were abducted by a blue UFO; the occupants described as four foot high hairy beings, with large eyes and pointed ears. After 3 hours, the family were returned to their car.
properties.location_name: Aveley (Essex) - Road in the area, exact location not known

properties.name: Saucer
properties.description: Watched for between five and eight minutes, this saucer shaped craft had 'fifty pence piece type edges'. White lights could be seen at the front of the object, and blue lights at the rear.
properties.location_name: Bagshot (Surrey) - Two miles past Junction 3, heading towards London

properties.name: Orange Globes
properties.description: Several media companies and air traffic control were contacted after witnesses watched three orange globes fly across the night sky. The objects were visible for around five minutes before disappearing.
properties.location_name: Bangor (County Down) - Skies around the area

properties.name: Purple Object
properties.description: A purple pear-shaped object was seen by Kris Reed, from his home. The ufo hovered before moving away, creating a noise like a washing machine. On the same night, another witness reported seeing a large red ball moving slowly across the sky.

properties.name: UFO Technology under R&D
properties.description: A relatively new myth, this location is said to house downed UFO technology, under reverse engineering by UK scientists.

properties.name: Green Triangle
properties.description: Shoppers at the centre reported seeing a green triangular ufo cross the sky above them.
properties.location_name: Belfast (County Antrim) - Above Yorkgate shopping centre

properties.name: Inverted Mushroom
properties.description: This strange flying machine passed overhead here.
properties.location_name: Bircham Newton (Norfolk) - Skies over Bircham Newton

properties.name: Mystery Rockets
properties.description: Fast moving objects seen during the day at low altitude and moving from east to west leaving a trail of sparks were identified as 'probably' shooting stars by the Edgbaston Observatory. One witness said that he thought the object he witnessed resembled a rocket.
properties.location_name: Birmingham (West Midlands) - Skies above the area

properties.name: Silver Saucer
properties.description: Four witnesses watched a silver object in the sky during a storm. One of the witnesses claimed the object would periodically pause in its movements before continuing.
properties.location_name: Birmingham (West Midlands) - Skies over the area

properties.name: Fifty Pence
properties.description: A nightclub manager reported watching a heptagon shaped object hovering before moving away at speed. The ufo projected two beams of light from the front and two weaker beams from the rear, and had clusters of red, yellow and green lights on the underside.

properties.name: Men with Light Bulb Heads
properties.description: A local lad escaped one night from his house to play hide and seek with his friends. While searching for them, he observed three figures in grey rubber suits and fishbowl helmets in a back yard; they also possessed pallid faces shaped like upside-down pears, with slits for eyes and mouth. When they moved towards the boy, he ran home.
properties.location_name: Bolton (Greater Manchester) - Alleyway in town, exact location unknown

properties.name: Murphy Incident
properties.description: After spotting a ufo hovering over his field, a farmer reported the sighting to the Manchester Aerial Phenomena Investigation Team. The team reported by were followed by an unidentified man, while the farmer was warned to stop talking about the incident by the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food, although the government agency denied they had spoken to the farmer.

properties.name: Mysterious Lights
properties.description: Sightings began at this UFO hotspot around 1977, though it was not until the 1990s that the popular media began to take an interest. Though media coverage has died down as of late, it has been reported that many locals still witness strange lights but are now less likely to talk about their encounters.

properties.name: Almost a Disc
properties.description: A UFO videotaped over the village was circular with a semicircle missing from an edge, rather like a digestive biscuit with a bite taken from it.

properties.name: Missing Time
properties.description: Two friends driving from the hospital to one of their parent's homes, a two minute trip, lost one hour forty minutes of their lives. Neither was aware of any periods of unconsciousness or 'jumps' in time. Two years later, another report came in of a missing time episode in the area; this time the witness lost three hours during a five minute walk.
properties.location_name: Bordon (Hampshire) - Forest Road / Hendon Road area

properties.name: Round Object
properties.description: A brother and sister watched a large round metal looking object with blue and red lights around the sides hovering over the field opposite their home. It was stationery for around three minutes, before disappearing in a flash.

properties.name: Red and Orange Spheres
properties.description: Witness David Hinde reported two spherical lights in the sky, glowing red and orange. He watched them for ten minutes while they darted around, defying the movements of conventional aircraft.
properties.location_name: Bridlington (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Dotterel junction area

properties.name: Drone-Like UFO
properties.description: A witness watched a small drone-like UFO above the ocean before it vanished without trace.
properties.location_name: Bridlington (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Off South Beach

properties.name: Domed Craft
properties.description: This craft hovered over the town for around an hour before disappearing.

properties.name: Saucer Occupants
properties.description: A large disc was seen landing near the school by fourteen of its pupils, some of whom also said men with pointed ears and dressed in silver clothing had climbed out. For several months after the event, sightings continued in the area.
properties.location_name: Broad Haven (Dyfed) - Broad Haven County Primary School

properties.name: Silver Suited Figure
properties.description: A seven foot (2.13 metres) tall silver clad entity was seen by Billy and Susan Coombs from the window of their farm. The family were plagued by other ufo related experiences around this time, although in 1996 businessman Glyn Edwards claimed he had wandered the area in a silver suit as a joke.

properties.name: Flashing Orange
properties.description: A couple in their car thought a falling flashing object was going to hit them. They reported the object as being half the size of their Vauxhall Corsa, flashing orange, and being a dinnerplate shape. The ufo landed in nearby fields, but what became of it is unknown.

properties.name: Plasma Ball
properties.description: Six young people watched a glowing ball hovering above the park. They originally believed the light to be from a police helicopter's searchlight before realising there was no rotor blade sounds. The ball remained for five minutes before shooting upwards and disappearing.

properties.name: Snaking Lights
properties.description: A married couple, along with other witnesses, observed five circular lights in the sky. The lights were different colours and 'snaked' for a few minutes before all turning white and shooting off in different directions. Twenty-two years later a witness heard a 'TV white noise' sound emerging from the sky which lasted around 20 minutes.

properties.name: Boomerang
properties.description: While outside, a male witness heard aircraft engines and looked to the sky. He watched a 737 appear briefly in the gap of the clouds, closely followed by another craft which was twice the size of the recognisable airplane. The latter craft was boomerang shaped with squared wing tips and dark grey or black in colour. After disappearing into the clouds, the witness did not see either craft again.

properties.name: Flying Arrow
properties.description: A night fisherman spotted an arrow shaped object travelling at a fantastic speed above him. The craft was silent, had a light at each corner and left no contrails. The witness believed it could have been manmade. And possibly connected to a nearby military base.
properties.location_name: Burnley (Lancashire) - Over Rowley lake, Brunshaw road

properties.name: Ball Lightning?
properties.description: A van driver reported an extremely bright ball of light the size of a steering wheel hovering above the road. After around three seconds it darted past the van, the driver estimating it moving between 80 and 90 mph.

properties.name: Four Spheres
properties.description: The Ministry of Defence confirmed that Suffolk Police had handed over a report of four black spheres which one person had seen moving across the sky in formation.
properties.location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Howard estate, heading towards Fornham St Martin

properties.name: Revolving Lights
properties.description: Several people, including a member of staff at the Cathedral, reported observing a luminous grey UFO over the area. One witness contacted the police.
properties.location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Sky over the Cathedral

properties.name: Helicopter
properties.description: A phantom helicopter was reported flying around the area at night several times over a two year period. Press and police were unable to discover who or what was behind the mysterious craft.
properties.location_name: Buxton (Derbyshire) - Skies above Harpur Hill area

properties.name: Flying Wedge
properties.description: Two pilots in a 737 reported narrowly avoiding a large black wedge shaped UFO while flying into the airport from Milan. The object passed so close to the passenger aircraft that one of the pilots ducked.

properties.name: Silver Disc
properties.description: The occupants of the abbey were said to have been terrorised when a large radiant silver object flew overhead just as they sat down to eat. Many years later, a passing farmer listened to phantom plain chant while watching a ghostly form move around the gravestones.

properties.name: Dark Disc
properties.description: A dark disc-like object was filmed flying over the castle.

properties.name: Airship
properties.description: Two men speaking in a strange tongue were seen by a witness here - they made their escape in an airship. Though it is thought they were German, no country in the world had an airship capable of making that kind of flight at the time.

properties.name: Rugby Ball
properties.description: While stopping at the club to pick up a coat, Stanley Taylor watched an object hovering in the sky. He said it was the shape of a rugby ball and around the size of two double-decker buses. The dull silver object gave off a hum and, with a sudden 'whoosh' flew off, quickly becoming a dot in the clouds. Another person nearby was said to have heard the sound but did not witness the object.
properties.location_name: Caistor St Edmund (Norfolk) - Caistor Hall Country Club

properties.name: Spinning Balls
properties.description: Two witnesses watched three large white spinning balls holding equal distance from each other for around twelve minutes. The balls eventually moved at speed; one flew left, one right, and the third straight up, all vanishing from sight within a couple of seconds.

properties.name: The British Roswell?
properties.description: A disc shaped alien spacecraft crash landed in this area. Three people witnessed the event; a farmer and son who were held by military personnel for two weeks, and a poacher who claimed to have seen two bodies, with 'faces like the Devil's' (the poacher had a breakdown soon after). The RAF (?) removed all evidence of the incident from the area.
properties.location_name: Caldbeck (Cumbria) - Cumbrian fells - exact area not known

properties.name: Circle of White Light
properties.description: Travelling along a busy road, one witness watched a circle of white light slowly cross the rooftops of four houses, as if a huge torch had been shone on the surfaces, before disappearing. Nothing could be seen in the sky, and there were no vehicles on the road that could have caused the light.

properties.name: Solway Spaceman
properties.description: Jim Templeton, while taking family photographs, caught on film a strange white figure wearing what appears to be a spacesuit floating behind his daughter. Jim stated that the figure was not visible at the time of taking the photograph. Author David Clarke wrote that the figure is likely to have been Templeton's wife Annie, the 'spaceman' illusion created by overexposure.
properties.location_name: Carlisle (Cumbria) - Coastal marshland NW of town

properties.name: Bright Light
properties.description: Initially thought to be a star, a small bright ball of light started to follow this witness in her car as she returned from Christmas shopping. As the car reached the edge of Carnwath, the ball suddenly shot into the sky and disappeared.

properties.name: Streaming Light
properties.description: Now thought likely to be Aurora Borealis, parish records mention a bright light over the skies of the village, changing from white to the colours of the rainbow. Occasionally flashes of light and smoke could be seen.

properties.name: White Shape
properties.description: Filmed by a witness for several minutes, a round ball of white light darted around the sky. The light changed shape and size several times before finally disappearing behind a dark cloud.

properties.name: Unidentified Snowy Object
properties.description: A witness here watched a bright object for around twenty seconds before it disappeared into a dark and heavy snow cloud.

properties.name: Dumbbell Shaped Craft
properties.description: Elsie Oakensen saw this craft as she drove through the village, returning from work. Passing underneath, her car lost power, and she experienced fifteen minutes of 'missing time' - later hypnotic regression revealed she was taken on board the craft.

properties.name: Downed Cigar
properties.description: A rumour floating around on the internet said that a cigar-shaped ufo, around six metres in length, was forced down by two RAF fighters. The crashed craft deeply embedded itself in mud, and rather move it straight away, authorities buried the ufo with the intent to move it once interest died down. Whether this happened is unknown.
properties.location_name: Cleveland (North Yorkshire) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Anchor
properties.description: During Mass, the congregation at this church were interrupted as an anchor dropped from the sky and caught on the church door. The people investigated and spotted a ship floating above them; one of the crew diving overboard and swimming towards them through the air towards the anchor. The congregation grabbed the sailor, but the bishop demanded that they let him go. As the group released the man, he floated upwards and once on board the ship, someone cut the rope attached to the anchor - the ship sailed off into the sky. The anchor was said to have been on display at the church.

properties.name: Darting Light
properties.description: A group of children playing football watched a light dart around the sky at incredible speed. It momentarily stopped moving before darting off once again in the direction of the A12 road. The light later returned to the area and was seen by other witnesses.

properties.name: Three Discs
properties.description: The famous black and white photograph of three discs flying over the town taken by a 15 year old schoolboy remains a mystery to this day.

properties.name: White Light
properties.description: After a wind turbine was found damaged (one blade bent, another laying on the ground), several theories emerged to explain why the incident had happened. County councillor Robert Palmer reported seeing a round white light which hovered near the area, while the media reported that John Harrison had seen a massive ball of light with something akin to tentacles reaching for the ground. While the 'ufo hit our turbine' stories were popular with the media, there is a high chance that the blade just fell off, hitting the second blade as it fell.

properties.name: Fog Bank with Flag
properties.description: In what could be an early UFO sighting, a man walking along the beach watched a localised 'fog bank' sitting on the sand, with a huge flagpole sticking out. After ten minutes, the object rose and faded from view.

properties.name: Saucer
properties.description: While staying with relatives, a witness watched a saucer shaped object with blue lights. The saucer was bright metal, and the object hovered for a while before moving off at high speed. The witness ran downstairs to tell someone but was so shocked that they were unable to speak and soon realised that even although the sighting only appeared to last a few minutes, three hours had gone by.

properties.name: Rockets
properties.description: Witnesses outside Crawley railway station watched two rockets heading west, with other reports coming from across Sussex during the same week.

properties.name: Flying Saucer with Lights
properties.description: A mother thought her daughter had been attacked after hearing her screaming along the road. As she ran inside, the mother received a call from the mother of her daughter's friend who said that they had seen a flying saucer with coloured lights around it. The mother ran outside and spotted the object in the sky.

properties.name: Fifty Pence
