properties.description: A witness walking to the nearby college suddenly realised there was no traffic on the roads. Something made him look up only to see a large a fifty pence (heptagon) shaped craft hovering silently in the sky. It had a red underbelly, bright silver sides and moved from left to right. The witness looked down briefly, during which time the craft started to move away at incredible speed. The witness chased it across the road (traffic had appeared again) but it had disappeared. The witness later found out that he had lost an hour of time.

properties.name: Silver Ball
properties.description: From the passenger seat, a witness watched a silver ball bearing-like object hovering in the sky. The large, stationary object hung quite low and did not move for two minutes, despite the windy conditions.

properties.name: Four Balls
properties.description: Four orange balls of light were spotted silently floating in the sky, forming a pyramid. They remained for quite some time.

properties.name: Glowing Saucer
properties.description: Driving home from his shop, as Robin Peck approached the village his van's engine cut out. He climbed out of the vehicle and as he lifted the car's bonnet, Robin realised that this hair was standing up. It was then Robin realised that a glowing, saucer shaped object was hovering around ninety metres away. After a short while, the object darted away, and the van started working again.

properties.name: Orange Triangle
properties.description: Two children reported seeing a large orange triangular shaped object above them, making a funny noise.

properties.name: Silver Cigar
properties.description: A silver cigar shaped aircraft was spotted flying high over East Dean. It left no vapour trail and moved steadily before darting off at an incredible speed.

properties.name: Football Shaped Object
properties.description: Just down the road from the famous Bentwaters UFO incident, several villagers have reported other strange craft in the skies above the area.

properties.name: Black Shape
properties.description: A group of eight people watched a perfectly round, dark object silently traverse the sky. No lights were visible from the object. A strange fiery UFO has also been spotted off the coast, though the date of this sighting is unknown.

properties.name: Orange Orbs
properties.description: On a very hot summer evening two orange orbs were seen falling from the sky. Moving extremely fast, they appeared to chase each other. The objects were watched for around ten seconds before dropping and disappearing behind the horizon.
properties.location_name: Felixstowe (Suffolk) - Skies northwards at Old Felixstowe

properties.name: 2004 Hotspot
properties.description: Filey was named as UFO hotspot of the year, after local UFO groups received 48 reports of unidentified flying objects over a twelve month period.

properties.name: Orange Energy
properties.description: One witness spotted two red lights hovering silently hovering over the cliff before they descended and vanished. Sheep in a nearby field appeared destressed. The following night, the same witness spotted a small drone-like craft which transformed into a rectangular orange object, like a ball of energy, which crackled and buzzed. The object remained visible for a few minutes. Two people nearby were able to hear the crackling of the object but could not see it.
properties.location_name: Flamborough (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Area near the lighthouse

properties.name: Rectangular Block
properties.description: One witness heard a quiet mechanical sound and could make out the faint shape of dark rectangular shape pass their caravan.
properties.location_name: Flamborough (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Thornwick Bay

properties.name: Dead Alien
properties.description: Five-year-old Robert Hall, together with other children, encountered several strange figures shortly after observing an egg-shaped flying object. One of the figures resembled a short bigfoot, while another had a skeletal body and bat wings. After taking a sample of Robert's blood, the figures left. The following day, a short 'grey'-like alien appeared to Robert. The boy's screams brought his uncle to the scene, who killed the alien with a shovel. A local story says the alien's body is buried in a nearby churchyard (or concealed within a secret room).
properties.location_name: Gateshead (Tyne and Wear) - Hedley Street and St Cuthbert's Church

properties.name: Washing Machine
properties.description: During an overnight camping and fishing trip, Philip Cotton and Jean Clark watched a rotating orange and blue object that made a sound like a washing machine. They retreated to their car because the noise grew too loud, but reported the light was 'friendly' and they did not feel afraid of it.
properties.location_name: Gilling West (North Yorkshire) - Area around Crabtree Farm

properties.name: Three Orange Globes
properties.description: An apprentice joiner spotted three bright orange globe-shaped lights in the sky while walking from work. He rushed home and told his parents, who laughed at his tale until a local radio broadcast mentioned several people had reported orange lights over the city.

properties.name: Bright Ball
properties.description: Three men watched a bright glowing ball, between four to five feet in diameter pass a few feet over a house and descend into a field where it disappeared in a small flash of light. The following day they went to check where the ball had vanished but found nothing. One witness said the ball had a white centre but glowed different colours at the edges.
properties.location_name: Glendale, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Sharp Turner
properties.description: Two men walking dogs along the shore at dusk spotted a bright star-like light at high altitude. One witness said that he would have passed it off as a military jet, but it made an instant 140 degree turn before disappearing into clouds.
properties.location_name: Glendale, Isle of Skye (Highland) - Skies in the area

properties.name: Flying Square
properties.description: Teacher Michael Valance encountered a flying box hovering over the moors. He reported the UFO as glowing and having four bright windows. The craft vanished after several minutes.
properties.location_name: Glossop (Derbyshire) - Road between Glossop and Chapel-en-le-Frith

properties.name: Flying Wheelie Bin
properties.description: Several people spotted a strange wheelie bin-shaped object flying silently across the sky, fire seemingly coming from the bottom of the craft. One man photographed the object, and although the images were reasonably clear, the object remained unidentifiable until it was reported in the local press the 'craft' was a home-made hot air balloon.
properties.location_name: Gorleston (Norfolk) - Skies over Brasenose Avenue area

properties.name: Huge Shape
properties.description: A family driving home spotted a huge object in the sky, so large that it appeared to blot out the sky as it moved slowly across. When the family arrived home, one phoned the local radio station to report it and was told 'many' others had also reported it, and that the RAF had told the station that it was a crashing Russian spy satellite.

properties.name: Circular Lights
properties.description: At least three witnesses observed a very large circle of flashing lights for ten minutes as it crossed the sky. The lights moved both clockwise and anticlockwise, while another set pulsated.
properties.location_name: Great Ryburgh (Norfolk) - Skies in the area

properties.name: Green Light
properties.description: This witness spotted a solid green glowing ufo with an orange trail as it crossed the sky heading east. The object generated no sound.

properties.name: Abduction
properties.description: This female abductee reported watching a male human vivisected while she was on board the alien craft. Later the same day, several witnesses reported lights and strange shapes in the sky.

properties.name: Triangular Craft
properties.description: Two police officers spotted a bright white light in the distance while driving along the A3. As they drove under the light at the flyover, they could make out three white lights on a black triangle shape with a dim pulsating red light in the centre. Other drivers had pulled over and watched the shape. Eight more lights were spotted drifting over Guildford.

properties.name: Red Light & Blue Man
properties.description: Two police officers watched a large red light moving by the lake - as they approached it vanished suddenly. The officers then spotted a strange white object; this also vanished. Three days later, two men walking their dogs encountered a blue figure that stood around 2.5 metres tall and 1.25 metres wide. Like the UFOs, this figure also vanished.
properties.location_name: Hainault Forest Country Park (Essex) - Lake and Cabin Hill

properties.name: Red Lights
properties.description: Seven red lights were seen bobbing up and down as they slowly moved across the sky before fading away. The witness was told the lights were actually 'light reflecting off migrating birds', although they had not seen this effect prior to the sighting or since. The village was considered a hotspot for UFO sightings during the early 2000s.

properties.name: CE III
properties.description: An alien contactee claimed to have witnessed a ufo landing on the mountain, where the craft left imprints. The witness was later contacted by a strange being with short curly hair who wore a blue jumpsuit.
properties.location_name: Halkyn Mountain (Clwyd) - Exact location not known

properties.name: Downed Craft Hoax
properties.description: A witness wrote to the RAF claiming he had seen a large silver disc crash and that he had investigated inside the craft before it had (mostly) sunk into the marsh. The RAF asked the police to investigate; the witness admitted that while he had seen the craft and thought it was going to land, he had fabricated the rest of the tale.
properties.location_name: Haltwhistle (Northumberland) - Ten miles north of town in a marshy area

properties.name: Hanbury Orbs
properties.description: A BBC cameraman caught three bright lights hovering over the Malverns on video. A local UFO group named them as the 'Hanbury Orbs', a group of lights commonly seen above the landscape in this region.

properties.name: Black Cylinder
properties.description: Four people watched a black cylindrical object, possibly rotating, for ten minutes as it crossed the sky. The object changed direction once, moving north to south before turning west.
properties.location_name: Harlow Hill (Northumberland) - West of Harlow Hill

properties.name: Blue Spinner
properties.description: A witness watched a bright blue object 'like a spinning top' cross the sky and quickly fade into space.

properties.name: Yellow Oval
properties.description: Two yellow, oval bright lights were seen silently moving over the moors from Haytor by people standing in a carpark. The lights moved in a swooping motion and would loop the loop.

properties.name: Flashing Lights
properties.description: Five people out metal detecting all watched the sky light up with flashing red, blue, purple and green lights which illuminated the field which they stood on. The lights stopped suddenly.

properties.name: Pill-Shaped Craft
properties.description: One witness recalled hearing a strange noise from the sky and, looking up, spotted three metallic, pill-shaped containers flying through the air attached by a strong chord.

properties.name: William Loosley's Abduction
properties.description: A hoax penned by David Langford, the premise being that the great, great granddaughter of William Loosley discovered a hidden account of his UFO sighting on the 4 October 1871, one day before William mysteriously vanished forever.
properties.location_name: High Wycombe (Buckinghamshire) - Woodland near the town, but exact location non-existent

properties.name: Flying Cross
properties.description: Two police officers chased a large cross-shaped UFO in this area as it flew low overhead. Another witness observed the craft later in the evening hovering and spinning on the spot.

properties.name: Bright Lights
properties.description: While walking their dog, a witness watched several fast moving bright lights high in the sky performing irregular manoeuvres. The witness later checked for aircraft in the area, but the UFOs could not be explained by the few aircraft which were out on the night in question.

properties.name: Fast Ball
properties.description: At least four people watched a fast moving ball, around the size of a car, pass overhead. After the object moved away, the witnesses were left with an odd static feeling.
properties.location_name: Hull (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Noddle Hill Playing Fields

properties.name: Small or Far Away?
properties.description: A pilot and another witness stopped their car to have a better look at an orange oval ufo. The pilot did not recognise the oval as any aircraft that she was aware off, and because of the lack of clouds and landmarks could not determine whether the object was large and very far away or smaller and closer.

properties.name: Abduction Photographed
properties.description: A former policeman photographed a strange creature running away from him on the moor - he gave chase, only to see a large silver disc take off and fly away at fantastic speed. The man soon realised that he had 'lost' almost two hours of the day; he was later hypnotically regressed and recalled being abducted. The area has a history of fairy sightings, which may be connected to this abduction story.

properties.name: Orange Lights
properties.description: A witness photographs six strange orange lights as they passed over the town at night.

properties.name: Nocturnal Light
properties.description: A family driving home one night watched as a bright light manoeuvred around the sky, before disappearing vertically into the clouds at a fantastic speed.
properties.location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - A12 towards Woodbridge, after Orwell Bridge

properties.name: Spinning Lights
properties.description: Two dull, spinning lights followed a teenager driving across the bridge for several miles. The lights always remained in front of the car, turning at the same time as the driver. Several other witnesses reported seeing similar lights around the Nacton Road area at roughly the same time.
properties.location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Orwell Bridge and the A12 towards Felixstowe, and Nacton Road

properties.name: Mushroom
properties.description: High in the sky, this mushroom shaped object was said by the witness to be bright blue underneath. The ufo was also seen be the witness's wife and two daughters.
properties.location_name: Isle of Arran (Ayrshire) - Skies over area

properties.name: Figure in Silver Suit
properties.description: A figure in a baggy silver suit climbed down a grassy verge by the roadside, watched by several motorists. It is thought this has a connection with a UFO seen a few hours earlier in the area.
properties.location_name: Isle of Sheppey (Kent) - Roadside of Sheppey Way

properties.name: Triangle
properties.description: A large triangular craft was watched as it moved irregularly across the sky. It made no sound and would occasionally disappear and reappear.

properties.name: Triangle
properties.description: Two witnesses in a car spotted a triangular craft, around the size of a house, hovering above a field. Soft lights were visible under the ufo. The witnesses also noticed other road vehicles were slowing down to look at the object.
properties.location_name: Ixworth (Suffolk) - Road between Diss and Bury St Edmunds

properties.name: HALO
properties.description: RAF Wharton was believed to be the home of the UK stealth aircraft project, HALO (High Altitude, Low Observable) being the project name. Some believe this to be the result of back engineering a downed UFO.
properties.location_name: Kendal (Lancashire) - RAF Wharton, outside of the town

properties.name: Triangle
properties.description: A witness looked out the window at night and spotted a large triangular object, at least the size of a bus, hovering over the house and street. It had numerous lights around the edge, but the lights did not appear to cast any shadows. The object silently flew off in the direction of Holme Moss.
properties.location_name: Kirkheaton (West Yorkshire) - Skies above the village

properties.name: Tailed Sphere
properties.description: The Kirkwall coastguard reported a very bright spherical object with a tail, moving north west to the south east. The object then split in two. The Aberdeen coastguard reported seeing the same or similar two objects a little later.

properties.name: Domed Craft with Flashing Lights
properties.description: During this sighting, the witness claims that the flashing lights became painful to look at, and he felt his body tingle, as if hit by a bolt of electricity.

properties.name: Robot Attack
properties.description: A woman driving near the village was attacked and abducted by a large robot after her car broke down - it took her to a domed craft where she was experimented on by three creatures.
properties.location_name: Langford Budville (Somerset) - Lane near the village, exact location unknown

properties.name: Flying Loaf
properties.description: Several witnesses reported seeing a UFO hovering in the sky, resembling a glowing loaf of bread.

properties.name: Balls of Fire
properties.description: These balls of flame where seen many times during the winter months, spinning like wheels.

properties.name: Giant Pumpkin Smelling of Acid Drops
properties.description: This strange UFO was reported over the sea.
properties.location_name: Leiston (Suffolk) - Beach north of Leiston, in the skies

properties.name: Large Yellowish & Greenish Ball
properties.description: Observing the power station here (or maybe formed by it?), the unnatural ball only hung around briefly before leaving the scene.

properties.name: Abductions
properties.description: Jenny Randles claims that there were two missing time episodes during these decades.

properties.name: Mile Long Craft
properties.description: Lee Jones, his wife and her mother watched a huge saucer shaped craft hovering in the sky, clouds occasionally covering the object. A passenger jet that passed near the craft enabled Jones to estimate the ufo was about a mile long. Other witnesses reported seeing the craft throughout June and earlier in the year.

properties.name: Anti-Ship Mine
properties.description: A forestry worker was attacked by 2 robotic creatures that resembled mines, with six protruding legs. They gave off a smell that knocked him out. Later, when the site was examined by police, they found evidence of a fight, and marks from the robot's 'legs'.
properties.location_name: Livingston (Lothian) - Dechmont Woods (a monument now marks the area)

properties.name: Cube With Arms
properties.description: A UAP was spotted by multiple witnesses. The object was difficult to make out, due to the piercing light it emitted, but was thought to be similar to a cube with 'arm light' appendages. A witness estimated the object to be between four and six times the size of an aircraft. The UAP was initially stationary but moved north over the Edinburgh flight path.

properties.name: Downed Craft
properties.description: Some researchers believe that a UFO crashed on the mountain, the event covered up by the UK Government.

properties.name: Downed Craft
properties.description: A loud crashing sound was heard by many locals, coming from the nearby hill. They thought it was a light aircraft or military training jet, but RAF personal reported finding nothing, though they kept the area closed to the public for several days afterwards. Some now report that a small alien craft crashed there, and the wreckage recovered by the Government.
properties.location_name: Llandrillo (Clwyd) - Cader Bronwen hill, east of village

properties.name: Alien Chase
properties.description: A couple in a car were reportedly pursued by a UFO. Both people claimed to have seen the occupant of the craft and described the figure as large with frizzy hair.

properties.name: Figures in Jumpsuits
properties.description: Walking home with her two daughters, Lynda Jones and the children watched a twenty metre long orange/pink oval craft drop into a field. When they approached to take a better look, the ufo had changed to a grey colour and was hovering sixty centimetres above the ground. They all ran away after one daughter started screaming, and in later hypnotherapy Lynda recalled being abducted by almost human figures in jumpsuits.
properties.location_name: Manchester (Greater Manchester) - Banks of the River Mersey, south Manchester

properties.name: Darting Lights
properties.description: Several circular bright lights were observed by a man walking his dog in the night sky. The balls were all different sizes and travelled very fast in a zigzag motion, creating no sound. The man was left shaken after watching the display but did not contact the authorities fearing they would think he was mad.

properties.name: Dark Torpedo
properties.description: Reported as being over thirty metres long, this long, thin scareship of unknown origin passed over the town late at night.

properties.name: Fast Moving Light
properties.description: Two people in a garden watched a light in the sky as it moved fast, suddenly stop, and move back on itself before disappearing.

properties.name: Domed Object
properties.description: While out walking, Cathie Connelly encountered an illuminated domed object surrounded by tall men wearing one-piece overalls. The men had strangely tall foreheads, and one man cleaned his hands using a duster smiled at the witness. As Connelly left the area, she turned back and realised they had gone.
properties.location_name: Meriden (West Midlands) - Footpath in area, exact location not known

properties.name: Orange Lights
properties.description: It was reported that traffic was brought to a standstill when between 10 - 15 orange lights were seen moving in a southerly direction. The UFOs all moved at the same speed, before stopping and moving upwards. The sighting lasted for about ten minutes.

properties.name: Triangular Object
properties.description: Two friends sitting in this car park watched as a large, brightly illuminated triangular craft pass overhead. After the sighting, as they tried to drive away, the car froze, as if held in place by an unseen force.

properties.name: Aurora?
properties.description: The strange doughnut-shaped exhaust trail left across the sky is said by some to have been created by the top secret US hypersonic Aurora spy plane.
properties.location_name: Ness (Outer Hebrides) - Skies over the area

properties.name: Soldier Shot by UFO
properties.description: A soldier once based here alleged, in later life, to have been abducted while on guard duty at the base. Remembering the abduction from his dreams, the man claimed he fell unconscious after being hit by a thin beam of light.
properties.location_name: Newbiggon on Sea (Northumberland) - Old RAF base

properties.name: Orange Ball
properties.description: A police officer reported watching a spherical orange object pass directly overhead, moving from north to south. Three other witnesses were with the officer at the time.
properties.location_name: Newcastle Under Lyme (Staffordshire) - Over Wolstanton Marsh

properties.name: Orange Lights
properties.description: A witness reported watching dozens of bright orange lights high up in the air. The lights were visible for several minutes before fading out and were compared to missile countermeasures used by military aircraft.
properties.location_name: Northrepps (Norfolk) - Sky above North Walsham Road

properties.name: Banana
properties.description: A flying banana shaped object was seen in the sky over the stadium a couple of times. It was described as bright and made up of several different colours.

properties.name: Scareship
properties.description: A possible early ufo encounter. A married couple spotted two men wearing caps working on a cylinder-shaped craft perched on the ground. As the married couple approached for a better look, the men disappeared, and the craft took off.

properties.name: Bus Sized UFO
properties.description: Saddleworth Moor has been home to many strange and curious sightings of UFOs over the years, the largest of which was the size of a British double decker bus seen in 1975. More recently, six lights spread out in a hexagon pattern which vanished after several seconds were seen in the sky, quickly followed by a sighting of a strange van which also vanished.
properties.location_name: Oldham (Greater Manchester) - Saddleworth Moor, near Dovestones Reservoir

properties.name: Pair of Lights
properties.description: A couple of men watched two lights hovering quite close to the water, which darted off at incredible speed as they approached. The area was home to Project Cobra Mist, which involved radar waves that followed the earth's curvature.

properties.name: Power Cut
properties.description: A witness recalled how bright orange lights had moved across this area and cars stopped, their power drained. Fazakerley Hospital windows were damaged on the same night.

properties.name: V
properties.description: Larger than a jumbo jet, this V shaped craft was only spotted for a few seconds before disappearing.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Uxbridge (Greater London) - Skies over Hillingdon

properties.name: Light Show
properties.description: Several witnesses in different areas of Paignton reported seeing up to one hundred orange, green and red lights in the sky. Witness accounts varied - one person said they moved in formation, while another reported some were moving to the left while others to the right. One person reported a blue light that the other ufos moved towards.

properties.name: Yellow Lights
properties.description: A driver stopped his car to watch five large yellow lights hanging in the sky above the village. The balls disappeared after thirty seconds.

properties.name: Green Flash
properties.description: One witness reported seeing a green flash in the sky, followed by a green light 'swirling' before vanishing.
properties.location_name: Pershore (Hereford & Worcester) - Skies above the area

properties.name: Scareship
properties.description: Seen by two police officers, this UFO resembled a large airship, travelling at a then unheard of speed.

properties.name: Orange Lights
properties.description: Four orange lights were seen moving across the sky at night, their speed said to be several times quicker than other aircraft.

properties.name: Ship
properties.description: A strange ship appeared in the skies over Portland for seven or eight minutes. Sails, rigging, and men armed with pikes could be observed on board, and the vessel was accompanied by stormy weather.
properties.location_name: Portland (Dorset) - Exact location not known, was observed from Lyme

properties.name: Silent Vulcan
properties.description: Christened the 'Silent Vulcan' by students because of its similarity to a Vulcan bomber (but without any sound), this large aircraft was seen by a student in the skies of Preston. He stated the object shot off towards the dock area, moving at incredible speed.
properties.location_name: Preston (Lancashire) - Sky near to the university campus

properties.name: Space Man
properties.description: Mark Taylor photographed a strange man floating high in the sky. After local press picked up the story it was revealed the sighting occurred on the same day a child had their life-sized stormtrooper balloon swept away by the wind.

properties.name: Rugby Ball
properties.description: A zigzagging rugby ball shaped object, larger than an aircraft, was reported by a witness. The ufo was surrounded by red and orange lights.
properties.location_name: Ramsden Heath (Essex) - Skies over Hanningfield Reservoir

properties.name: Red Ball
properties.description: Three teenagers spotted a strange red glowing orb flying overhead as they were out walking.
properties.location_name: Reading (Berkshire) - Skies above Basingstoke Road, Whitley

properties.name: Spinning Saucer
properties.description: A schoolteacher and several pupils watched a white spinning disc with pulsating lights pass overhead.

properties.name: Radar/Visual Sighting
properties.description: Several UFOs were detected by the base's radar, crossing the North Sea at speeds unmatched by any contemporary aircraft. One UFO was intercepted by a US fighter, but was soon lost.

properties.name: CEII
properties.description: Once only famous in ufological circles, the story of the landing of an unknown craft at Rendlesham during the early 1980s still causes controversy today. Unrelated to the UFO sighting, the Big Cats of Suffolk website reported there had been a string of big cat sightings in the forest prior to 1995, with occasional sightings still reported.
properties.location_name: Rendlesham (Suffolk) - Rendlesham Forest - near former USAF Base

properties.name: Green Light
properties.description: Two police officers watched a bright green light quickly pass over the town and disappear over the horizon.

properties.name: Diamond Formation
properties.description: Andrew Warren and his son watched a diamond formation of lights as they passed overhead. Though the source of the lights appeared close, there was no sound, and the pattern appeared to hover before moving away. They also saw two other patterns of light, one of which was a 'V' shape.
properties.location_name: Ring's End (Cambridgeshire) - Skies above March Road

properties.name: Black Oval
properties.description: One witness watched as a black oval shape unnervingly blocked out the night sky as it passed by.

properties.name: Orange Jellyfish
properties.description: A family of four observed a set of three orange lights, 'undulating like a jellyfish', drifting over the town and heading upwards before they disappeared amongst the stars. The UFOs were also witnessed by two people on the outskirts of Romford.

properties.name: Spaceship Alley
properties.description: This part of the county has so many reports of strange nocturnal lights that it justifies the dramatic nickname. However, cynics name British Aerospace as a possible source for the displays.

properties.name: Alien Fairy
properties.description: Three figures, all less than four foot tall with tissue paper wings, drifted into a house in the town and interrogated a housewife for an hour. She said they looked like fairies, though they had laser beams on top of their heads and left via an eight foot long UFO.

properties.name: Circular Object
properties.description: A witness pulled their car over to have a better look at a circular object hovering about twenty metres above the road, as did another car behind them. The object did not make a sound and the witness estimated it was around five metres in diameter. The witness then lost their nerve and drove off, as did the occupant in the other car.
properties.location_name: Saxlingham (Norfolk) - Holt Road heading towards B1156

properties.name: Glowing Light
properties.description: A forty-six centimetre disc covered with strange hieroglyphics was found by three men who were investigating the sighting of a ufo which glowed eerily in the night sky.
properties.location_name: Scarborough (North Yorkshire) - Reasty Hill, Silpho Moor

properties.name: Spherical Object
properties.area:normalized: Sea Between Isle Of Man And Belfast
properties.description: A captain, first officer and flight engineer of an aircraft flying from Belfast to the Isle of Man reported a bright white spherical object that became an orange beam.
properties.location_name: Sea between Isle of Man and Belfast (Isle of Man) - Sky

properties.name: Black Object
properties.description: A large dark object was seen passing over the town, buzzing loudly.

properties.name: Red Cloud
properties.description: A red cloud which covered the roof of a house was seen by three people. Different coloured beams of light, mainly green, pink and blue, emerged from the cloud for around fifteen minutes before vanishing.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Skies about Woodthorpe area

properties.name: Dark Red Cloud
properties.description: This 'cloud' which shot beams of light, heating up the cold winter night, hovered over the town before heading off towards Ireland.

properties.name: Hotspot
properties.description: This area was claimed to be a UFO hotspot by enthusiasts.

properties.name: Stretched Plectrum
properties.description: While watching some F15s over the Wash, a witness spotted a dark grey craft, the shape of a stretched plectrum, emerge from a cloud behind them. The ufo shimmered before entering a small cloud from which it did not reappear.

properties.name: Low Flying Egg
properties.description: Two separate groups of witnesses watched as this craft, around 25 meters long, passed low over the road, melting the tarmac surface. Vehicles and lights nearby were disrupted.
properties.location_name: Sopley (Hampshire) - A338 between Avon and Sopley, near bridge over the river Avon

properties.name: Flying Disc
properties.description: A fighter pilot, while out on routine patrol, reported a near miss with a silver domed disc - the UFO travelled towards him at fantastic speed, veering off at the last possible second.

properties.name: Radiant Ship
properties.description: A brightly coloured ship passed overhead, on an otherwise clear night.

properties.name: Landing UFO
properties.description: Robert Bennett told the St Helens Star newspaper how he had worked with police and radio enthusiasts in chasing a strange craft across the St Helens sky. The pursuit ended after three police officers watched the ufo take off after landing in a field.
properties.location_name: St Helens (Merseyside) - Field in Rainhill area

properties.name: Bright Flashes
properties.description: A witness spotted two objects the shape of spinning tops which flashed brightly as they followed each other across the sky.

properties.name: Scareship
properties.description: A dark cigar shaped object with a light was spotted moving above the grounds for several minutes before vanishing into cloud.

properties.name: Flashing Lights
properties.description: A low flying circular object was spotted by two friends as it moved across the sky towards them. As it approached, they noticed two small flashing lights on the side of the craft that grew in intensity until the glare became blinding. The witnesses shielded their eyes to avoid the light; as they did so the object made a strange sound and disappeared - no trace could be seen in the sky.

properties.name: Halo
properties.description: Driving back from work in the early hours of the morning, this witness spotted a halo shaped object hovering and darting around the sky before vanishing.

properties.name: Flying Mushroom
properties.description: A woman, her husband and his mother watched glowing orange, mushroom shaped object for an hour as it hovered overhead, before moving away quite quickly towards the Stoke area.

properties.name: Saucer Landing
properties.description: A saucer that changed colour from red to green to blue landed in an empty field on this estate, glowing brightly and seen by at least six people. The object took off, spinning, and headed off over the horizon.

properties.name: Large Saucer
properties.description: With lights on either side and a flashing one on top, this large saucer shaped object was reported moving slowly across the sky.
properties.location_name: Stoney Stanton (Leicestershire) - Skies over the village

properties.name: Five Fliers
properties.description: Over 100 people watched five objects moving over the town centre for a period of around thirty minutes. One witness said three of the lights formed a triangle, another hovered to one side, while the fifth light darted around. Another witness claimed they looked like little balls of flame.
properties.location_name: Stratford-upon-Avon (Warwickshire) - Skies over the town centre

properties.name: Blond Haired Alien
properties.description: Rosalind Reynolds was abducted from her car by a tall alien with messy blond hair accompanied by several classic greys. She claims that after the event she developed a psychic talent for foreseeing disasters.
properties.location_name: Sudbury (Suffolk) - A1092 between Long Melford and Haverhill

properties.name: Hovering Craft
properties.description: Seen hovering silently in the sky, this UFO had white, red and green lights and remained visible for around 90 minutes.
properties.location_name: SW1 (Greater London) - Sky over Palace of Westminster

properties.name: Flashing Light
properties.description: A pilot waiting for a train at Clapham Junction observed a flashing light which would periodically disappear and reappear elsewhere in the sky in an instant. The object was still visible when the pilot reached his home in Wimbledon. Now a retired training captain, the man has never been able to explain the object.
properties.location_name: SW11 (Greater London) - Sky above Clapham Junction (also Wimbledon)

properties.name: Sky Ship
properties.description: A group here for Sunday service watched a strange ship drop anchor from the heavens, which became entangled around a gravestone. One of the crew jumped overboard and headed towards the anchor, moving as if swimming. Witnesses grabbed the stranger as he reached the ground; he struggled briefly before drowning, even though on dry land. The remaining crew in the ship severed the rope of the anchor and sailed away; the strange artefact was melted down and used to make the hinges on the local church doors.
properties.location_name: Swanscombe (Kent) - Church and nearby Blue Anchor public house

properties.name: Sky Ship
properties.description: Conghalach Cnoghbha, High King of Ireland, and his men were said to have spotted a sailing ship in the sky here. A crew member of this ship threw a spear at a salmon, also in the sky, which missed and landed close to Conghalach. The crew member leapt from the ship and, swimming in the sky, came down to the ground to retrieve the spear. One of the King's men grabbed the swimmer, who screamed out 'I'm drowning!'. Conghalach told his man to release the crew member, who 'swam' back to the ship which then disappeared into the clouds.
properties.location_name: Tailltenn (now known as Teltown) (County Meath) - Exact area not known

properties.name: Large Eyed Aliens
properties.description: Ambulance mechanic Gary Wood and his friend Andrew encountered a black shiny UFO along the A70 - it had no lights and was silent. After realising that they had lost one and a half hours, they underwent hypnosis where they discovered they had been abducted by frail grey aliens with large eyes.
properties.location_name: Tarbrax (Lanarkshire) - A70 between Tarbrax and Balerno

properties.name: Ruggers Ball
properties.description: A group of school children reported seeing a flat-bottomed UFO shaped like a ruggers ball hovering above woodland. The object had three bands of lights, hummed, and appears to disturb the tree line.
properties.location_name: Taverham (Norfolk) - Skies south of Red Woods

properties.name: Flash
properties.description: A disc-like craft was observed by one witness. It had two lights which looked like windows, and a small red light towards the rear. The craft vanished in a flash of white light.
properties.location_name: Thorpe Market (Norfolk) - South off the B1436

properties.name: Abducted Policeman
properties.description: Alan Godfrey, a policeman on the Yorkshire force, reported seeing a large UFO along the road, and experiencing missing time. He was later hypnotised and 'discovered' that he had been taken inside the alien craft and subjected to experiments.

properties.name: Flying Lights
properties.description: The skies between Todmorden and Bacup have been home to frequent reports of unidentified lights which have alarmed both people and animals alike.
properties.location_name: Todmorden (West Yorkshire) - Skies in the area

properties.name: Orange Lights
properties.description: A group of ten orange lights flying in a straight line were spotted by fishermen, a few forming triangular shapes. Changing position slightly, the witnesses could see around twenty more lights flying low behind the tree line, all moving in the same direction.

properties.name: Flying Cigar
properties.description: Dozens of people contacted the local media with reports of a strange flying cigar which passed over the town. One witness managed to take a reasonably clear photograph of the UFO with his mobile phone.

properties.name: Sixty Red Lights
properties.description: Sixty or more red lights were seen by several people over the skies of Uckfield. The lights moved in close formation and eventually moved away.

properties.name: Brighter than Daylight
properties.description: Driving through the area, two people suddenly became aware of an unusual light coming from within a nearby barn. The people initially thought the light belonged to a man who would collect milk from farms in the area. As the people drove closer, three beams of brilliant light shone down from a disc shaped craft hovering above the field to the right of the car. As they passed the lane, the lights shut off and the craft flew northwest, leaving the area in complete darkness. The following day, the people returned to Uppertown and visited a farm. They noticed that all the produce had been dumped in the yard, as though the milk collector had quickly fled from something.

properties.name: Moped
properties.description: On a dense, foggy night, author Andrew Collins (pictured) and a team of four other paranormal researchers encountered a small light that moved a few feet off the ground, rather like the headlamp of a moped. The light vanished into an area blocked by low hedgerow, and when they examined the ground the following morning, there was no evidence of tread prints.
properties.location_name: Upton Scudamore (Wiltshire) - Farm track, opposite a lane leading down to Upton Scudamore

properties.name: Burning Triangle
properties.description: Five office workers watched a triangular object with burning corners move across the sky from north to south. They only watched the ufo for a few seconds before it faded into the distance, moving at high speed.

properties.name: UFO Capital
properties.description: To celebrate the countless UFO sightings over the town, the Marquis of Bath and the local tourist board officially called the town the UFO Capital of Britain.
properties.location_name: Warminster (Wiltshire) - Skies above town, normally near Cradle Hill

properties.name: Fireball
properties.description: A fireball with three points was seen speeding across the sky. The witness said that the object was like an aircraft.

properties.name: Figure with Laser Eyes
properties.description: A driver pulled over to watch a two metre tall creature, dressed in silver and with thin beams of light coming from its large eyes, as it moved along the embankment by the side of the road. When the witness arrived home, he discovered that he had 'lost' almost an hour. Just prior to the driver's sighting, a local woman spotted the figure from her garden as it walked through the former munitions factory on Warrington Road.
properties.location_name: Warrington (Cheshire) - Drayton Road, Risley exit from the motorway, and Warrington Road

properties.name: Heptagon
properties.description: From their back garden, a witness watched a dark grey heptagon shape silently fly over their house.

properties.name: Hovering Triangle
properties.description: Two friends spotted a triangular shaped ufo hovering over a lake. The craft tilted upwards before simply vanishing, resulting in the friends running home without looking back.
properties.location_name: Welwyn Garden City (Hertfordshire) - Sky over city

properties.name: Zigzagging Lights
properties.description: This witness observed between ten and twenty slow moving lights zigzagging across the sky in formation. Each light was semi-transparent, yellow with a red centre and moved silently from the south east, heading towards Walsall.
properties.location_name: West Bromwich (West Midlands) - Sky over Hateley Heath

properties.name: Giant Meteorite
properties.description: A large fireball was seen passing overhead. Several hours later there were a series of shockwaves that were attributed to an earth tremor.

properties.name: Green Disk
properties.description: Several observers watched the fluorescent green ufo as it hovered over the area. A driver on a nearby road managed to photograph the object.

properties.name: Greenish Grey Craft
properties.description: While patrolling the town centre, PC Colin Perks spotted a hovering craft that glowed a greenish grey colour. Colin said the UFO was around thirty foot long and that he could see portholes along the side. After several seconds it flew off.

properties.name: Man in Silver Suit
properties.description: A couple out driving had to pull over as their car it began to shake out of control. They noticed a large cigar shaped object with three faces peering out nearby, and a bearded man dressed in a silver suit moving towards them. The strange entities then vanished, and the couple were able to drive away normally.
properties.location_name: Winchester (Hampshire) - Chilcomb Road, near the A272

properties.name: Ballerina Alien
properties.description: Councillor Adrian Hicks recalled in 2009 his encounter with an alien entity along a busy street. He described the creature as having large oval eyes, looking almost human, and wearing a tutu. The alien shuffled along the street like a penguin.

properties.name: CE I
properties.description: A triangular UFO was seen slowly floating towards the ground, several independent witnesses reporting it as a crashed aircraft. No aeroplanes were reported missing, nor any wreckage found.
properties.location_name: Woodbridge (Suffolk) - Road out to Martlesham from Woodbridge

properties.name: Zig Zagging Light
properties.description: On two occasions, a witness travelling through the area observed a bright light flying low in a zig zag motion. During the second sighting, the witness checked their flight tracker app, but the craft did not appear. On both occasions, the witness found online comments that other people had seen the lights.

properties.name: Foaming Wolf
properties.description: A driver on the way back from the cinema in Norwich encountered a large black wolf eating a carcass along this road. The creature was described as standing around a metre at the withers, with yellow eyes and black matted hair. The driver slowed and the wolf briefly looked up before continuing to eat. The witness continued home, shaken.
properties.location_name: A1067 (Norfolk) - Road between Fakenham and Norwich

properties.name: Creature from the Tomb
properties.description: The vampire that once frequented this castle, a former lord of the estate, lived under the site and would emerge at night to attack the local villagers. An outbreak of plague was also attributed to the unholy creature, and this resulted in the villagers digging the monster up from its shallow grave and burning it.

properties.name: Female Spectre
properties.description: A local tale says that this old manor house is home to a female vampire, though many believe the entity to be ghostly in nature.

properties.name: Walking Dead
properties.description: A former wealthy man who died was said to have returned from the grave and walked the streets at night. A pack of dogs were said to escort him. Finally, a group of local men dug up the grave and incinerated the body, though most of the men are said to have died soon after from an unknown disease.
properties.location_name: Berwick on Tweed (Northumberland) - Exact area(s) unknown

properties.name: Vampire Attacks
properties.description: A gentleman, described as black and in his twenties, bit another man walking along the street before pouncing on neighbours who came to the victim's aid. One woman present was said to have had a chunk bitten out of her hand. Police, however, stated that they had received no reports of such an incident and dismissed the story as an urban myth.
properties.location_name: Birmingham (West Midlands) - Glen Park Road (Ward End) and Saltley, Small Heath and Alum Rock areas

properties.name: Blood Sucker
properties.description: Two poachers shacking up in a bothy reported being attacked by a creature that drank blood from one of them. The pair managed to fight the creature off, and it flew away. The story is very similar to the vampire tale that happened at Fealaar, Aberdeenshire.
properties.location_name: Blair Atholl (Perth and Kinross) - Glen Tilt, north of the town

properties.name: Doggett
properties.description: A corrupt manservant who stole thousands of pounds from his employer, Doggett finally killed himself, and now drives his phantom horse and carriage along this area. One local story says he returned as a vampire; after his body was exhumed many years after his death (from St Mary's Church in Tarrant Gunville) it was found to be uncorrupted, with a rosy tint to the cheeks.
properties.location_name: Blandford Forum (Dorset) - Area near Eastbury Park (no longer standing)

properties.name: Speed Wolf
properties.description: Although this creature is reported to resemble a normal wolf, it moves at fantastic speeds and covers great distances in a single bound. It is unclear whether the wolf is a physical entity, but the nearby village of Wormhill claims to be the location where the last wolf in England was killed in the sixteenth century.

properties.name: Vampire
properties.description: One of the few vampire reports in the UK, this creature attacked a young girl in this building. One of her brothers witnessed the attack and shot the monster in the leg. The blood trail enabled them to track it to the village graveyard, where it was dug up and burnt.

properties.name: Welsh Wolf
properties.description: Reported to be the size of a horse, this huge wolf would create mayhem in the region, feeding on livestock, dogs and the flesh of men.
properties.location_name: Denbigh (Clwyd) - Area between Denbigh and Wrexham

properties.name: Wolf-headed Skeleton
properties.description: A local archaeologist digging in the peat discovered a human skeleton but with a wolf's head. The man took his discovery home, but during the night he found his house besieged by a werewolf. The archaeologist spent the night barricaded in the kitchen as the beast tried to gain entrance to his house, and as the sun came up and the werewolf left, the man took the skeleton and reburied the bones where they were found.
properties.location_name: Dogdyke (Lincolnshire) - Langrick Fen, but exact location unknown

properties.name: Coffin Carrying Returners
properties.description: Several servants fell ill and died after taking seed corn from the Abbey barns. The servants soon returned, their coffins carried on their backs while running across fields and knocking on doors. More villagers fell sick, and the pestilence only ceased when the dead were dug up and burned. The survivors fled the village and left it to fall into ruin.

properties.name: Suicidal Lycanthrope
properties.description: Andrew, a teenager from the village, was found dead at the crossroads having killed himself with a penknife. One of his friends reported that Andrew had called him just before midnight, saying that his skin was changing and that he was turning into a wolf.

properties.name: Howl like a Foghorn
properties.description: A large black creature of unknown origin caused havoc here in the 1920s when it killed dozens of sheep. Though rarely seen, the creature was said to have a howl like a foghorn. Locals allocated the blame on a lycanthrope.

properties.name: Werecat
properties.description: Sir Roger Smith and his two wives have their tomb within this church, with the effigy of Lady Ann possessing a dark mark on one whist. A local legend says that Ann was a witch who could change into a cat - the mark on her statue shows where a butler hit Ann in cat form with a small axe.

properties.name: Wolf Man
properties.description: A woman walking home after dark reported seeing a grey man with a wolf's head, apparently stalking a large rabbit. The werewolf vanished when disturbed by a stag that ran out from a nearby wooded area.

properties.name: Vampire
properties.description: Two hunters were said to have been attacked by a vampire while spending the night in the bothy. This story is very similar to the vampire tale that happened at Blair Atholl, Lowlands, and is likely to be derived from it.
properties.location_name: Fealaar (Aberdeenshire) - Hut hidden away in a wooded area

properties.name: Stinker
properties.description: There are stories that the area around Flixton is home to a werewolf, which has glowing red eyes and a particularly bad body odour.
properties.location_name: Flixton (East Riding of Yorkshire) - General area

properties.name: Gorbals Vampire
properties.description: An urban myth circled the area during the 1950s that the graveyard was home to a vampire with iron teeth. At the story's height, it is said that hundreds of children patrolled the site, looking for the monster which, they thought, had kidnapped and eaten two local children. Another story to emerge from the Necropolis is that of the white woman - a feminine statue that sits on top of the grave of three people killed in a tramcar accident is said to turns its head and watch visitors as they pass.

properties.name: Abhartach
properties.description: In a battle for the throne, the warrior Cathrain killed his rival Abhartach. Abhartach, however, returned from the grave two days later and preyed on the locals. Cathrain once again killed the undead fiend, but two days later, Abhartach came back and continued his attacks. Cathrain discussed the problem with druids, who told him to stab his adversary through the heart with a weapon made of yew. This he did, and Abhartach never returned.
properties.location_name: Glenullen (County Derry / County Londonderry) - General area, Abhartach's final resting place marked by a large stone with a thorn tree growing up from the side

properties.name: Witch
properties.description: A witch who could change into a wolf was unable to return to human form after the Abbot of Basingwerk asked heaven to stop the witch from killing innocents. The witch/wolf ran into King Henry II's hunting party and after a vicious fight, the party slayed the creature.
properties.location_name: Holywell (Clwyd) - Basingwerk Abbey, and forests of Longdendale

properties.name: Wolf Men
properties.description: It was said that men from this kingdom could transform into wolves whenever they pleased. However, while in wolf form their human bodies would be left at home. If moved, the person would be trapped as a wolf for the rest of their life.
properties.location_name: Kingdom of Ossory (County Kilkenny) - Ossory was a sub-kingdom, most of which was in County Kilkenny

properties.name: Werewolves
properties.description: The island was once home to a colony of wolfmen - even though they are extinct, disturbing their graves is reported to release their spirits.
properties.location_name: Loch Langavat, Isle of Lewis (Outer Hebrides) - Exact area unknown

properties.name: Hooded Dead Figure
properties.description: Tom Robertson investigated the woods after hearing stories that animals had been found drained of their blood. He encountered a tall figure dressed in sacking with a hood over its head, which black eyes and grey face. The creature leapt into a tree and swung away. Eight years later Robertson went looking for the creature again, finding it and taking a couple of photographs.
properties.location_name: Lochmaben (Dumfries and Galloway) - Woodland around Lochmaben Castle

properties.name: Strange Wolf
properties.description: This area was reportedly the stomping ground for a werewolf - sightings were reported up until the 1990s.

properties.name: Cormac Tadhg McCarthy
properties.description: Charged with tracking down Irish rebels, Cormac once ate the flesh of one man he caught. This may have damned him, and as such a few believe he still lurks the ruins of the castle. The site is also home to a Pooca, a ghastly grim entity which reputedly attacks visitors if the opportunity arises.
properties.location_name: Macroom (County Cork) - Carrigaphoca (aka Carrigaphouka, aka Carrigaphooca) Castle

properties.name: Evil Monk
properties.description: An evil monk returned from the dead as a vampire, leaving his grave at Melrose Abbey to feed at a local nunnery. When the other monks discovered this, the bravest stayed up one night with a large axe, and when the vampire climbed out of its tomb, it was beheaded (although the vampire's evil presence remains in the abbey). The building is also the final resting place of Michael Scot, a great wizard who discovered the secret of flying, and was locally named as building Hadrian's Wall single handed.

properties.name: The Damned
properties.description: A sighting of a cloaked, vampire-like entity seen standing next to the cemetery in 1980 was explained 38 years later when Dave Vanian of The Damned admitted he went for a late night walk after recording at Rockfield Studios. As he walked through the cemetery dressed in a black cloak, he was spotted by the driver of a mini who almost crashed. The story of the cloaked figure appeared in the press a few days later, and Vanian decided not to say anything.

properties.name: Smoky Vampire
properties.description: A plethora of sightings of a red eyed creature that could turn to smoke resulted in the cemetery becoming a second home to all manner of researchers, reporters, and fame seekers. The sightings died down after legal action against one of the vampire hunters. More recently, North London Paranormal Investigators encountered a pale figure in a suit, resembling an undertaker, while touring the area.

properties.name: Church Dog
properties.description: This churchyard is rumoured to be the haunt of the black shuck, which may have been a shape-changing local wizard. Another tale says the dog's remains were bricked up in the church walls and he is nothing more than a folk memory of the interment.
properties.date: Circa early to mid nineteenth century

properties.name: Fox or Wolf?
properties.description: The area was once said to be the home to large grey creatures; thought by some to be wolf/fox hybrids, others believed them to be werewolves.

properties.name: Modern Myth?
properties.description: First-hand accounts appear to be rare, but one story says a nurse and her taxi driver both spotted a werewolf in this area.
properties.location_name: Sefton (Merseyside) - Church and roads in the area

properties.name: Slain Dwarf
properties.description: When a tyrannical dwarf was murdered by a local chieftain, his body was placed in the earth here. Less than a day later the dwarf returned, more vicious than before. He was killed again and reburied but came back and tormented the entire country. The chieftain consulted a druid, and following the wise man's instructions, slayed the dwarf for the final time, placing the body into the ground head first, robbing him of his magical powers.
properties.location_name: Slaghtaverty (County Derry / County Londonderry) - Abhartach's grave, aka Slaghtaverty Dolmen

properties.name: Wolf on Hind Legs
properties.description: In two separate sightings, two men reported a large hairy creature standing on its hind legs, between 180 - 200 centimetres in height. One of the men approached it, but the creature ran off.
properties.location_name: Stafford (Staffordshire) - German War Cemetery, Camp Road, between Stafford and Cannock

properties.name: Hiding from Vampires
properties.description: A body found in the building was surrounded by garlic, salt, crucifixes, and other items reported to scare off vampires - tragically, the dead man was found to have choked to death on a clove of garlic that lodged in his throat as he slept.
properties.location_name: Stoke on Trent (Staffordshire) - Number 3, The Villas

properties.name: Vampire
properties.description: One piece of source material states that at three separate times over three months in 1938, a woman reported being attacked by a winged creature which bit her neck and drew blood. However, it is likely that the 'vampire' attacks occurred during the 1970s, and the 1938 incidents were more poltergeist is nature (contemporary newspaper reports support this). Even so, the 1970s incidents also involved poltergeist activity, and the woman at the centre of the claims alleged she endured a tiger attack.
properties.location_name: Thornton Heath (Surrey) - Private house, Beverstone Road

properties.name: Box Carriage
properties.description: Travelling home in 2007, a driver watched a horse drawn box carriage cross the road around 100 metres in front of him. The witness reported that he could not make out too many details as it was raining hard, though he was able to see a dim lantern towards the front of the carriage. As the driver reached the spot where he had seen the vehicle pass, he realised there was no side road where the coach and horses could have crossed. Another witness in the 1960s spotted a female figure with long hair, wearing a white dress, standing by the roadside. The witness stopped to see if she was lost or needed a lift, but she had vanished. Mentioning the encounter to a work colleague the following day, the colleague described the figure which was encountered and said the area was haunted.
properties.location_name: A31 (Surrey) - Hogs Back, Guildford bound. 1 mile Northeast of Tongham junction

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The daughter of a previous owner, this shade is thought to have returned after the girl's suicide. She is said to walk down the staircase dressed in nightwear.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pallid woman is said to leave the house at midnight and walk into a nearby wood. The White Room is said to be haunted by a little man with green eyes. It is not known for sure whether these ghosts are connected to a series of dark stains on the staircases of the building.

properties.name: Coloured Women
properties.description: This castle is haunted by both a white woman and a lady in red, the latter thought to have been killed during a fox hunt, while the former returned after being buried in unconsecrated ground. Another story says that the white woman was the former owner of the castle (Winifred Cochrane, Countess of Dundonald) who returned after her bitter husband, written out of her will, purchased the site and destroyed the contents.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The phantom white woman is said to make her way from the nearby church to a door on the east side of the hall.

properties.name: Girl in White Lace
properties.description: A ghostly girl dressed in white lace is said to haunt this ruined site. A student managed to photograph a strange misty 'face' in 2004 and reported hearing scratching noises. (Image copyright 2006 Lawson Clout)

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: A woman in white is said to appear at midnight, sit for a while, and move off to a nearby stream to drink.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: Seen by two lay preachers driving a horse and trap, this phantom was mistaken for a real woman as she walked towards them in the rain. They could see she had auburn hair and a beautiful face, before she vanished without trace.
properties.location_name: Allington (Wiltshire) - Road to Horton, near Tan Hill

properties.name: Burnt Dog
properties.description: A phantom dog reportedly haunts the hotel, where it was kicked to death by a drunken customer. Some say that a white (or grey) woman also glides around inside the building; the girl was a kitchen maid murdered in the hotel. Finally, the ghost of an old man haunts room number three.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This flitting entity was seen several times in a particular room of the hall, her large black eyes staring into the empty space ahead. The room was sealed up as the haunting presence was so strong.
properties.location_name: Altrincham (Greater Manchester) - Ashley Hall (no longer standing)

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: While camping close to this property, one traveller awoke to see a white female form standing at a gate. He woke one of his friends who also observed the ghostly figure. The following day the travellers moved on and never returned (special thanks to the Churchtown Village Renewal Trust, from their 'The Annals of Churchtown').

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Dying in childbirth, this mistress of a former owner still walks the walls. The ghost, or perhaps another, was seen in 1970 as it crossed the road outside the hall; the driver of a car drove straight through the figure. In October 2012, one team of ghost hunters recorded laughter in the stable when the area was empty - the area is reputed to be the location where a serving girl hanged herself - while another team in September spotted a black dog.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: During a fashion shoot in this former prison, studio photographer Alan Wells caught a strange white female apparition in the background of one of his images.

properties.name: Swimming Horseman
properties.description: Riding towards the park house, this phantom horseman veers off at the last minute and enters the nearby lake. A white lady haunts the house itself, seen by porters on the night shift. In the seventeenth century, the Earl of Winchelsea cut down several oak trees, bestowing a curse on his family which took the life of his wife and son shortly after.
properties.location_name: Ashford (Kent) - Eastwell Park and Manor House

properties.name: Gliding Lady
properties.description: The phantom white lady here was reportedly seen by soldiers using the building during the Second World War and was thought to be the murdered wife of a former owner. Within the manor she would ascend the staircase holding a candle, while one person who observed her outside said she had no feet and had drifted across a nearby field. The kitchen area was also reputedly haunted by a ghostly monk.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A local story says that a woman hanged over the pit was saved by a washer woman. This did not prevent the woman's ghost from returning to the area when she later died.
properties.location_name: Ashmore (Dorset) - Ashmore - area between Washer's Pit and Spinney's Pond

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A white lady drifts around the grounds of the manor and sometimes the nearby henge and stone circles. The phantom woman's face is covered by a large hood. The inside of the manor house is said to be haunted by several other entities. One spirit would scatter rose petals across the floor of the Crimson Room, and a phantom monk was once spotted in the library. A ghostly Cavalier occasionally makes an appearance, and in 2014, a visitor was left with a sinking feeling after seeing (but not physically) two Victorian-era children swinging from the bannisters.

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: A government worker walking from Avebury Manor reported encountering a ghostly woman in white lace and a white hood at an iron gate close to Truslow. The witness said the woman appeared from nowhere, took his shoulders, and spun him around before pushing him away.

properties.name: Tabby Cat
properties.description: This feline apparition has been observed padding around on the first floor, vanishing when it sits down. A white Elizabethan lady is also reported to haunt the same floor, seen sitting in a chair.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: While the clothing of the woman varies (from wearing a raincoat to a wedding dress), the figure is always white. In true 'phantom hitchhiker' style, the woman had been picked up by passing drivers, only to vanish a few miles down the road.
properties.date: Late twentieth century, though sightings may go back to eighteenth century

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The White Lady has been observed looking into each pit, crying out in pity. Her origin is unknown, but she could just be a story concocted by local smugglers. The sound of the screams heard in the pits, which local folklore attributes to the woman's phantom, is more likely to be the wind.
properties.location_name: Aylmerton (Norfolk) - Circular Hollows (local name Shrieking Pits)

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A husband and wife encountered a white female phantom standing by the kitchen door, after which they had their bed covers pulled from them by an unseen hand on three consecutive nights.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A small bridge over a stream near Ballygarvan was home to a white woman. After appearing to one man who fell critically ill the following day, a priest went to the site and banished her.
properties.location_name: Ballygarvan (County Cork) - Small bridge over a stream

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The area around the site thought to be Robin Hood's final resting place (one of them!) is said to be haunted by a white lady who drifts around the area.
properties.location_name: Barnsdale Forest (South Yorkshire) - Robin Hood's Grave

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A lady in white haunts this field, while the road nearby is home to a phantom dog.

properties.name: Woman with Bright Pink Eyes
properties.description: The witness, a farmer, reported seeing two figures, one a young boy and the other a woman wearing a long white dress, standing in a field by his livestock. When he shone his flashlight at the people, he could see that the woman had eerie pink eyes. Filled with fear the farmer did not move until the figures disappeared towards the local graveyard, after which the witness ran home.

properties.name: Gibbet and Those Hanged
properties.description: An oak tree standing in the park is said to be haunted by those once hanged there (including a woman in white), and that the Devil can be summoned by walking around it six times.

properties.name: Water Fairies
properties.description: A witness watched the little people playing merrily in the water at the pass. Nearby is the grave of Gelert (the dog that was mistakenly killed by its master after saving a child from a wolf) where the hound's ghost has been reported. Finally, the ghostly woman in white who haunts the area is an ill omen, with death said to follow a sighting.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A woman left traumatised after being attacked along the road later committed suicide. Her phantom is said to stand by the roadside, waiting for a lift. One witness who spotted her said she wore a long white dress, possibly a nightdress, and had long black matted hair. Another driver heading towards Astley in May 2012 said she spotted a figure on the roadside with her hands against her face which was holding a dreadful expression. Two more witnesses in December 2012 described a figure standing by the road with long black matted hair, although this time the figure was dressed in black.
properties.location_name: Bedworth (Warwickshire) - Bedworth Road, aka Rabbit Lane

properties.name: The Running Lady
properties.description: This woman in white is said to run along the road between the two villages, close to the North Frodingham junction.
properties.location_name: Beeford (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Road between Beeford and Brandesburton

properties.name: Waiting Woman
properties.description: A woman in a white dress is said to wait for her Viking lover by the riverside.

properties.name: Mrs Waggs
properties.description: A former housekeeper, Mrs Waggs is said to haunt this location. Another phantom, a woman in white still loiters in the garden, and finally an unseen coach pulls into the driveway.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A phantom woman in white would drift around lanes near the grange.

properties.name: Anne Bruce
properties.description: Anne has been named as the white woman who walks along the path which bears her name. She spent many months walking this path after discovering her husband's infidelity after his death at sea.
properties.location_name: Biel (Lothian) - Lady's Walk, in grounds of the mansion

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: Prior to becoming a public house, the staircase of the manor house was haunted by a woman in a white gown. While it is not clear whether the ghostly woman remains, more recently a baby has been heard crying and mild poltergeist activity reported.
properties.location_name: Bilston (Staffordshire) - Greyhound and Punchbowl public house, was once Stow Heath Manor House

properties.name: Lady
properties.description: A ghostly white lady haunted this house before it burnt down. One room was transported to Pace College, Manhattan, and is said to contain a ghost (whether it is the white lady is unknown). Another piece of local folklore says a hawthorn tree on the grounds blossoms red flowers at midnight on Christmas Eve before quickly reverting to its previous state.
properties.date: Pre-1924 (white lady), hawthorn tree on Christmas Eve (reoccurring)
properties.location_name: Binfield (Berkshire) - Billingbear House, and hawthorn tree (which may no longer exist)

properties.name: Woman with Long Blonde Hair
properties.description: Said to be quite pleasant to watch, this beautiful wraith in her white dress disappears into a bedroom wall. The sounds of phantom footsteps have also been reported.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Though seen wearing several different sets of clothing over the past fifty years, this young girl with blonde hair is thought to be Victorian and has a habit of passing through parked cars.

properties.name: Loud Lady
properties.description: A white lady that manifested at this location would be accompanied by loud crashing sounds. Poltergeist activity was also reported. Twelve clerics finally exorcised the spirit.

properties.name: Raped Nurse
properties.description: This figure dressed in white is reportedly a nurse raped and killed near the barracks - her shade has been observed floating around the area, her facial features almost blank.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A child told her mother that she had seen a white woman drifting towards the house. Her mother dismissed the claim, until they received word that her father had drowned around the same time as the sighting.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This phantom lady is said to haunt the area surrounding the bar, although her presence is more likely to felt then seen.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: One witness in a car reported seeing a white woman wearing an old fashioned party dress, although the woman's legs disappeared below the knee and the face was blurred. The witness said it was like watching a 'replay', and although the witness's car headlights did not illuminate the figure, the woman was visible against the darkness of the environment.
properties.location_name: Boltby (North Yorkshire) - Road leading into village

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A phantom white lady is said to haunt the house, throwing herself from the roof and occasionally heard stomping on the staircase. She has been variously named as Lady Alice or Ailie, Lilyburn, Lilburne or Lillieburn.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The area around the A348 bridge is reportedly haunted by a ghostly white (or grey) woman, killed when hit by a horse and trap along the road. One story says she tries to entice men to jump off the bridge.
properties.date: February (reoccurring); last confirmed in 1970s, but legends say she continues
properties.location_name: Bournemouth (Dorset) - Millhams Lane, and surrounding Longham area

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A lady dressed in a white gown floated across the road before disappearing through a wall which led to a garden.
properties.location_name: Bournemouth (Dorset) - Outside West Howe Baptist Church, on the corner of Holloway Avenue and Anstey Road

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This incorporeal woman has been reported in various parts of the inn.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A ghostly, upset white lady is said to enter the house from the front door, drift up the stairs to the east window in the attic and throw herself out. The building is also reputedly haunted by an Elizabethan gentleman.
properties.date: Said to occur every seven years, but exact date unknown

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: This woman in white was said to have been a princess - anyone who carried the ghost to a nearby churchyard could put her soul to rest.

properties.name: Lady with Dog
properties.description: A male witness at the gate of this building spotted a lady in a white dress, walking her dog in the distance. The witness turned momentarily and when he looked back, the figure and dog had vanished - there was nowhere for them to have disappeared to. The same witness reported that several years previous he had heard someone whisper his name at the hall, even though the nearest person was some one hundred metres away.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Seen by actors as they rehearse, the phantom lady is described as wearing a long white dress wearing bouffant style hair. A soldier dressed in a red tunic, the sound of disembodied footsteps and the rattling of chains also haunt the building.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Nothing is really known about this phantom white lady, and it is questionable whether she still exists.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A certain part of the road is said to be covered in skid marks where various cars have had to brake to avoid hitting a phantom woman dressed in a white coat. The figure always vanishes immediately afterwards.
properties.location_name: Bristol (Somerset) - A38 near Barrow Gurney Reservoir

properties.name: Modern Man
properties.description: The shade of a man dressed in contemporary clothing was seen in the cellar before quickly vanishing, while the ghost of a woman with long dark hair and dressed in white has been reported in the bar.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A local legend claimed a white lady haunted the hotel grounds.
properties.location_name: Broad Haven (Dyfed) - Haven Fort Hotel (aka Havens Hotel)

properties.name: White Nurse?
properties.description: A photograph taken by artist Malcolm Baker appears to show the upper body of a white figure moving through foliage just outside the castle. Baker believes the figure to be a nurse in 1950s uniform, carrying a tray in front of her. A few months later musician Dan Smith reported spotting a nun dressed in white on the beach just after dawn, the figure quickly vanishing.
