properties.location_name: Broadstairs (Kent) - Kingsgate Castle and beach below

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A lady in white is said to drift along this stretch of road.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Differing stories for the same myth - the first states that the White Lady was the wife of a former owner, murdered by her husband. She floats around the moat, the place she was drowned. The second tale places the murder in the hall, this time by persons unknown. The body was then thrown into the moat from the battlements.
properties.location_name: Bury St Edmunds (Suffolk) - Rushbrooke Hall (3.5 miles from Bury)

properties.name: Black Monk
properties.description: Standing taller than six foot, the monk dressed in black was said to rise from the graveyard at the old priory before drifting off. Though reported on the land immediately around the priory, some islanders encountered him further away, in their homes or on the roads. The ghosts of a white lady and of a madman were also said to haunt the island at some point in the dim and distant past.
properties.location_name: Caldey Island (Dyfed) - Priory and all over Island

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: A young lady dressed completely in white is said to haunt this building. The ghost would descend the main staircase. Phantom music has also been reported in the house.

properties.name: Ginger Bearded Man
properties.description: The main road leading through Calverton is said to be haunted by two ghosts - a man wearing a tweed suit and sporting a ginger beard walks along the path, while a phantom white lady waits patiently at a bus stop.

properties.name: A Nun and Brown Dog-Thing
properties.description: It is claimed that Abbey House is, or at least was, the most haunted house in Cambridge. While I'm not a fan of 'most haunted' titles, when you look at the number and type of the ghosts supposed to haunt the site (a poltergeist, ghostly echoes of chains, a butler, a woman in white, a grey lady, a squirrel, a dog, and a hare all amongst the entities which have popped up), one can understand why the title has been applied here. An exorcism was performed in the 1980s, which may have laid the entities.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Once said to be the oldest bookshop in the UK, the building claims to have a white lady who walks silently down the aisles. Her history is unknown. There are also reports of a man in Victorian evening dress.
properties.location_name: Cambridge (Cambridgeshire) - Sherratt & Hughes, Trinity Street (closed in 1991)

properties.name: The White Lady & The Black Man
properties.description: No recent sightings of these figures have been reported, and locals accept them as being little more than smuggler's stories told to inspire fear. The white lady is said to offer to dance with anyone she came upon during her walks, while the black man offered to buy souls in return for a song. The White Lady was seen around 1917 by a group of men out partying - one of them chased after the figure with a large stick, but the apparition disappeared before he reached it.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A 'new' building on the site of thirteenth century ruins, the White Lady once haunted the older area, and now has set up shop in the replacement castle. The woman is said to have died of a broken heart after her son drowned in a local pond. There is also reported to be a buried hoard of treasure here, zealously protected by either three eagles or an entity who confuses any potential gold thieves, while an ethereal cavalier walks the castle grounds.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A soldier on guard duty challenged this fabled phantom woman; she faded in front of him and he died of shock several hours later. It is believed the ghost may have belonged to a body found in 1835, bricked up in a castle wall.

properties.name: Stone of Dismissal
properties.description: In a reversal of the normal tale associated with these stones, if a witness spotted the Devil nearby, she or he had to run around the stone to dismiss Old Nick. The stone was also said to be haunted by a woman in white who had been sacrificed on it.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This small pool of water is haunted by the pale ghost of a woman who is said to have drowned in it many years ago.
properties.location_name: Castle Gresley (Derbyshire) - White Lady Springs, wooded area just outside the village

properties.name: Glowing Woman
properties.description: A glowing woman dressed in white was observed by four people here. The radiant figure walked towards them before turning towards the sea and vanishing. A local legend says the woman had been spurned by her lover and walked into the sea (which before then was further inland) to drown herself.
properties.location_name: Chapel St Leonards (Lincolnshire) - Seafront between Trunch Lane and Vickers Point Ingoldmells

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The white lady who haunted this building was said to have been murdered and disposed of down a well that is now located under the dining room. The lodge is also haunted by a monk in a brown habit, who may be the cause of the disembodied footsteps occasionally heard travelling around the building.

properties.name: Drifting Woman
properties.description: A driver and her passenger both watched a woman in white sweep across the road as they drove towards Bridport. They both reported that the car suddenly became cold at the time of the sighting.
properties.location_name: Charmouth (Dorset) - Road between Charmouth and Bridport

properties.name: Lady of the Lake
properties.description: A radiant white female form has been seen a couple of times in the lake within the Hall grounds - it is believed to be a woman who drowned herself after falling in love with a monk. His ghost in turn may haunt the hall.
properties.location_name: Charnock Richard (Lancashire) - The Dam (lake near Park Hall)

properties.name: Bess of Hardwick
properties.description: Wayne Anthony, in Haunted Derbyshire and the Peak District, writes that Bess, who finished constructing her home around 1560, is one of several shades to haunt this house. Mary Queen of Scots is rumoured to walk where she was once held captive, while a grey lady and a white woman haunt other parts of the building. The library is haunted by a transparent figure which drifts from shelf to shelf. The bridge on the grounds is haunted by a screaming woman who was forced to watch her baby drowned after it was thrown into the river by her partner.

properties.name: Silky
properties.description: A former mistress of a Duke of Argyll, this white woman has yet to leave the bedroom of this house.

properties.name: Madam Pigott
properties.description: Having died in childbirth, Madam Pigott was seen dozens of times carrying her baby and combing its hair. Some also reported the lady also having a cat with her. She was eventually exorcised by twelve parsons, though in 1969 two motorists separately reported a white figure on the road near the church.
properties.location_name: Chetwynd Aston (Shropshire) - A41, and Chetwynd Hall & surrounding area

properties.name: Edwardian Gentleman
properties.description: Said to resemble Stan Laurel, this ghost was seen in Friday Hill House by a former occupier. A crying woman in a white veil has also been seen on the site.
properties.location_name: Chingford (Essex) - Friday Hill House, Simmons Lane

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Hanged during the Civil War by a traitorous priest, this female member of the Mundesley family still returns home. The beam of wood from which her body dangled is considered lucky if you touch it.

properties.name: Cloaked Man
properties.description: This figure was seen running up the staircase of the building - when the landlord gave chase, the man vanished into thin air. A white woman has also been seen on site, and at one time a group of bar dwellers watched as a pint of beer was pulled by a phantom hand!
properties.location_name: Chudleigh (Devon) - Bishop Lacy Inn, Fore Street

properties.name: Lady Banks
properties.description: Lady Banks held nearby Corfe Castle against the Roundheads during the English Civil War. The defences were only broken after a treacherous Royalist officer opened the gates. Lady Banks has been named as the lady in a white flowing dress who haunts the bridge.
properties.location_name: Church Knowle (Dorset) - Stone Bridge along Knowle Hill

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A white female phantom is said to walk this area. It is unclear whether the ghost is that of an old woman who once lived in a nearby cave and watched over a hoard of treasure.
properties.location_name: Churchill (Oxfordshire) - Crossroads close to Lyneham Longbarrow

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Last seen by a newspaper editor and a friend, this phantom lady darts between the trees on a hillside. Some say she is accompanied by another white figure in a cloak.
properties.location_name: Cilfynydd (Mid Glamorgan) - Woods in the area

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The white lady was said to have been seen by several schoolgirls when this building was a dorm. Sounds of heavy foots could also been heard walking along the corridors late at night.
properties.location_name: Clacton-on-Sea (Essex) - Wilson House, Leas Road (no longer standing)

properties.name: Quake
properties.description: The rector wrote of a deep rumbling which manifested in the kitchen, like an earthquake, although no furnishings moved. The noise suddenly stopped and was replaced by smashing crockery in the pantry. Together with his servants, the rector approached the pantry, but before reaching the door, it opened and a white woman emerged. Pursued by the rector, the white woman moved upstairs and vanished in front of the rector's son. The woman appeared several more times afterwards, but never so dramatically.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Walking to a friend's house late at night, this witness spotted a glowing woman in white with long hair cross a bridge ahead of him. The girl vanished without trace, and upon mentioning it when he arrived at his destination, his friend told him that the same figure had appeared at the foot of his bed a few nights previous.
properties.date: Late nineteenth or early twentieth century
properties.location_name: Clonmel (County Tipperary) - Unnamed road leading southwards, passing over a bridge

properties.name: Moon or Hitchhiker
properties.description: Reports of a white phantom in the form of a woman resulted in an approximately sixty car long traffic jam as people tried to spot the spook. Local Councillor Desmond Donnelly said the 'ghost' was more likely to be the moon reflecting in the nearby river.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A white female form is reputedly one of the phantoms which haunts the island as is a phantom soldier with two black holes instead of eyes. In 1914, a witness fled from a tall, off-yellow glossy figure that stood looking over the sea towards Cobh.
properties.date: Unknown (woman and solider), tall figure in May or June 1914
properties.location_name: Cobh (County Cork) - Spike Island - general area

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A former member of staff reported seeing a woman in a long white dress in the stockroom, hearing footsteps, and witnessing boxes of shoes jump off shelves. The worker eventually refused to work in the room.

properties.name: Faceless White Lady wearing Perfume
properties.description: There is little to suggest who this white lady is, but those who have witnessed her standing on the staircase have been in shock for some time afterwards. The accompanying smell of perfume is said to be very strong.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This pub is rumoured to be the home of a phantom white lady.
properties.location_name: Colne (Lancashire) - Hare and Hounds public house

properties.name: Simon Cunliffe
properties.description: Simon rides the area where he once lived, blowing his horn prior to a tragedy. It is said that he scared his wife to death by riding up the staircase of their home, chasing a fox that had ran inside to escape the hunt (ghost hunters Colin Veacock and Peter Crawley recorded a sound like a riding crop here in 1996). Another tale states that the horseman is not Simon but an assassin, killed by a manservant before he could murder the master of the hall. The young daughter of a family living here during the twentieth century told her mother that a 'white woman' would visit her during the night - the sightings stopped when the child changed bedrooms.
properties.date: Once a year, middle of winter (reoccurring), sound recorded 26/07/1996

properties.name: Bearded Man
properties.description: A small man sporting a long beard is said to haunt this building, as is a white woman in a flowing gown.

properties.name: Grey Monk
properties.description: Sightings of this spectral figure date back to the 1770s, when he was first seen drifting among the ruins. Reports also exist of sightings of a white woman, and the sounds of quiet voices and bumping.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: Occupants in this cottage witnessed three ghosts, one a woman in white who resembled a nun, a woman in black, and a man wearing a nightshirt, handing a candle, descending the staircase Doors would open unaided and footsteps crossed the upstairs landing. The family would also hear rattling chains several times a week; when renovation work took place on the roof, a secret room was discovered that chains hanging from a wall.
properties.location_name: Coventry (West Midlands) - Private cottage, Wall Hill Road, Allesley

properties.name: Lady Mary
properties.description: Polish troops based at this house claimed the tower was haunted by a white woman. The tower was demolished in 1963.
properties.location_name: Crieff (Perth and Kinross) - Ferntower House, tower (no longer standing)

properties.name: Piper
properties.description: Though only a hundred years old, the castle is said to be haunted by a traditional piper and a lady in white, the latter named as Duchess Blair (or Betty, for short).
properties.location_name: Culrain (Highland) - Carbisdale Castle (aka Castle of Spite)

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: A ghostly figure in robes was seen walking towards the gatehouse before vanishing into a wall. It is said the monk sometimes appears on horseback, and the site is also haunted by a white woman.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: The ghostly woman who haunted this guest house is said to have been killed on the road outside many years earlier. She would be seen standing on top of the staircase by the manager. Poltergeist activity was also reported on the site - items in the kitchen would be moved overnight, lights would turn themselves on, and the manager was 'held' at the top of the staircase by an unknown force.
properties.location_name: Darlington (Durham) - Former guest house (no longer operating), Corporation Road

properties.name: Hunting for the Child
properties.description: A woman in white is said to haunt the old cemetery, looking for her child. She either died in childbirth or was murdered by men who then stole her baby. A local myth says that by speaking the words 'white lady, white lady, I stole your black baby' she will appear.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: While walking to Frodsham from the Delamere Forest station, two people encountered a white lady. She stared at them for a couple of minutes before floating upwards into the trees.
properties.location_name: Delamere Forest (Cheshire) - Deep within the forest, between Frodsham and Delamere Forest rail station

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Seen on the steps behind the structure, this white lady is known to make brief appearances. A woman in tears has also been seen near the cathedral, carrying a young child. The same location is home to a man wearing old fashioned clothing, not forgetting the former executioner who killed his father and brother who jointly haunts the area.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A hanging white woman was said to haunt this piece of woodland, her dress always blowing in the wind to her left.

properties.name: Distressed Woman
properties.description: This woman, dressed in white, had been observed crying in the hotel. She last appeared at the end of a four poster bed.

properties.name: Screaming White Lady
properties.description: This sad spirit is said to have been murdered in the 1600's by a servant. The white lady now drifts through the castle corridors, wailing and screaming.

properties.name: Ladies
properties.description: Two women, one in black and the other in white, were said to haunt the land in this area. One version of the story says they are looking for each other to finish a fight they started during life.
properties.location_name: Douglas (Isle of Man) - The Nunnery estate

properties.name: Old Lady
properties.description: A cyclist riding along the canal witnessed a glowing elderly woman in white with long hair dancing with a young man. The man wore a seventeenth or eighteenth century military uniform and looked 'normal', unlike the woman who also hummed a tune. The cyclist did not stop.

properties.name: Lipsticked Lady
properties.description: The Westwood family had lived in the former inn for two years before their ghostly outbreak. A young blonde woman wrapped in white and wearing lipstick was seen in various parts of the house, as was a phantom bald man. Beds were moved, knocking started and footsteps were heard in empty parts of the building. Eventually, as strangely as it had started, the hauntings ceased.
properties.location_name: Dudley (West Midlands) - Jolly Collier, Holly Hall (former inn)

properties.name: Two Horses
properties.description: As well as the phantom horses, the ruined building is also home to a phantom white lady.

properties.name: Lady Waving Handkerchief
properties.description: Appearing upset, this white woman frantically waves from a high window once every year - it is rumoured she was having an affair with the Cardinal Beaton.

properties.name: Lady in Grey
properties.description: The staircase here is reportedly haunted by a fleeting female in grey, while a beautiful woman in white has been seen in other areas of the building.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Shortly after moving in, a family demanded to be relocated after a white lady manifested in front of a four year old boy and ordered the child to leave. The family dog refused to enter the boy's bedroom.
properties.location_name: E17 (Greater London) - Council house, Cooper Avenue, Walthamstow

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The phantom woman or women who haunt this building are not alone - the sound of a wailing hound has been heard, and disembodied voices emanate from empty rooms.

properties.name: White Woman with Pointed Head
properties.description: Observed by a group of bird watchers, a woman in white was seen drifting down an old trail that leads to the ruins of a chapel. It is unclear if her 'pointed head' was a hat or bonnet.
properties.location_name: East Bridge (Suffolk) - Road towards ruined chapel

properties.name: Pale Lady
properties.description: Locals have reported strange banging sounds coming from the castle, as well as the occasional glimpse of a phantom woman in white.

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: Her identity unknown, this ghostly woman's perfume is said to be overpowering, and has made many people gag around St John's Chapel. One visitor felt tapping on her shoulder, although could not see who could have been responsible. When they mentioned it to a guide, the guide said it could have been 'Daniel', who people have seen wearing a black cloak.
properties.location_name: EC3 (Greater London) - Tower of London, the White Tower

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A white female phantom is said to hover over the graves of the fallen knights on the site. Another tale says a Knight Templar also haunts the church.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Appearing soon after the sun goes down, this pale unknown figure drifts around the courtyard.
properties.location_name: EC4 (Greater London) - Wardrobe Place, Carter Lane

properties.name: White Rider
properties.description: Two women out walking their dogs watched a white female figure on horseback in the woods. The entity vanished as it rounded a non-existent corner. A local piece of folklore says the area is haunted by a white horse, although traditionally riderless. In 2022, witnesses spotted a hooded grey lady carrying a basket who disappeared into bushes.
properties.location_name: Eckington (Derbyshire) - Moss valley, between town and Ridgeway

properties.name: Lady Mary Douglas
properties.description: Lady Mary, manifesting in the form of a white lady, is said to wander along the corridors, looking into the original rooms. Her presence is reported to be calming.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This white lady stabbed her lover to death by the tree, while fending off his attack. She was executed for the crime, but her ghost holding a sword would return to the tree where the crime was committed. Another version of the story says that she was murdered here.
properties.location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Area where sycamore tree once stood, Dovecote Road, Corstorphine

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: At least three night porters reported seeing this tall, beautiful woman in white with long black hair move quickly down the corridors. Workers demanded to work in pairs until the ghost had been exorcised.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: This tall phantom female had a preference for the staircase and was blamed for an accident where someone fell (or was pushed?). Another ghost, a male which haunted the dining room, possessed an evil looking face.
properties.location_name: Edinburgh (Lothian) - Unnamed residence along Blackett Place

properties.name: White Lion
properties.description: This large albino cat was seen close to the cottages and was reported to resemble a lion. The same area is reportedly haunted by a headless white woman.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Folklore placed the spirit of a white woman within a house chimney on Oteley Park. Another version of the tale places the woman on the banks of the Mere, shouting at people to stop using her water.

properties.name: Edwardian Lady
properties.description: This white woman has been seen near the railway, melting away if approached.

properties.name: Doppelganger of Mother
properties.description: Twice a woman in white has appeared, witnesses mistaking the figure for Mrs Punter (the owner of the house), though she has been in other places at the time.

properties.name: Two Ladies
properties.description: Two female figures haunt the grounds of this hotel; a lady in grey walks near the staircase where she was killed, and the other (dressed in white) walks the terrace. Two children can be seen and heard crying, standing by the river that runs through the grounds. In March of 2000, a man staying at the hotel reported seeing and feeling a strange concretion of 'baleful' energy in his room, while in a photograph taken in October 2014, an amorphous shape appeared above someone's head. As a side note, the building was used in the original film of 'The Haunting'.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This white woman was said to point towards Ewenny, where treasure was supposed to be hidden. One legend says that a local man offered to help the ghost. The white woman asked the man to hold her hands, which he did until startled by a dog. Scared by the dog, the man snatched his hands back - the ghost screamed 'I'll be bound for another seven years!' before vanishing.
properties.location_name: Ewenny (South Glamorgan) - Areas known as White Lady's meadow and White Lady's Lane

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This ghost is likely to be responsible for the cold spot located in the moat.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A family were terrified by poltergeist activity, which included the entity tightening a crucifix necklace around someone's neck. Unusually, a phantom woman in white was also seen on the site by several people, including a neighbour.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A ghostly white lady haunts this country lane - she is also said to cross a field and vanish into a pond.

properties.name: Knight in Shining Armour
properties.description: Seen in the grounds and along corridors of this former castle (now a hostel) a figure in armour has been known to appear. It is not known if he is responsible for the disembodied footsteps along the walkways. A young child has been heard to cry in the King's Hall (where the bones of a baby were once found walled up), and finally a ghostly woman in white was spotted on the staircase in 1998.
properties.location_name: Forest of Dean (Gloucestershire) - Saint Briavel's castle

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This phantom woman, dressed in a white dress with pink trim and holding a broom, manifested to a witness and floated through a bedroom. Another witness had a similar encounter in an adjoining room.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Though not seen recently, the stories of the white lady still exist.
properties.date: Prior to the outbreak of the Second World War

properties.name: Construction of Gallows
properties.description: Not shy of more than a few hauntings, Glamis can claim the following... The sounds of a set of gallows being constructed, Lady Glamis drifting in mist around the clock tower, the unknown ghost in the 'Haunted Room' which caused a previous owner to faint when he went to investigate the mysterious sounds contained within, a white lady that drifts the corridors, 'Jack the Runner' who's long legs carry him down the same corridors, a black page boy that quickly appears and disappears near a sitting room, a pale girl who is dragged away by an invisible force in the courtyard, a giant in armour that stands over and looks down on people in bed, and a re-enacted murder in the parlour.

properties.name: Woman
properties.description: One story says that this area is haunted by the ghost of a woman in white who accidently flooded the surrounding lands.
properties.location_name: Glasfryn, Lleyn Peninsula (Gwynedd) - House, lake and nearby field

properties.name: Nelly
properties.description: The slim teenager in a white dress which haunts this property on army land had the nickname Nelly. Stories claimed Nelly to be a murdered servant.
properties.location_name: Glen of Imaal (County Wicklow) - Coolmoney House

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: Not a 'normal' post-mortem apparition; a woman returning to see her ill brother watched as a ghostly white woman left the building in which her brother lay. She soon discovered her brother had died seconds before her arrival.
properties.location_name: Godley Green (Buckinghamshire) - Road from Gee Cross

properties.name: Funeral Procession
properties.description: This phantom line of people was reported walking past the building. A white woman, once married to the First Earl of Godolphin, is also said to climb out from a closet and walk along the terrace.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Drifting down the corridors of the upper regions of the building, this figure may be responsible for the poltergeist like actions in one of the bedrooms.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pale woman was seen several times in the house.

properties.name: No Locks
properties.description: Owners of a farmhouse here were said to have once removed all the locks and bars from their doors, as a white lady would open all the doors at night, no matter how securely they were left closed. A workman called in to mend the roof encountered the ghost and refused to finish the job - another builder had to complete the repairs.
properties.location_name: Great Staughton (Cambridgeshire) - Unnamed farm on the moors

properties.name: Dead Woman in Night Gown
properties.description: Two men walking along this dark road at night were scared by an approaching figure dressed in white. As it came ever closer, the men were relieved to recognise it as the wife of one of the men. However, the figure suddenly vanished - the two Welshmen rushed back to the house of the woman, only to find her dead.
properties.location_name: Gresford (Clwyd) - Little Acton Hill (now Bluebell Lane), between Gresford and Wrexham

properties.name: Maid of Rokeby
properties.description: This entity, dressed in white flowing clothes, patrols the banks of the river after dusk. A monk is also reported to do the same, having taken in own life in the river many years previous.
properties.location_name: Greta Bridge (Durham) - Waters where the two rivers meet

properties.name: Smashed Furniture
properties.description: The sounds of furniture being smashed can be heard here, though investigations can never find anything broken. Glimpses of a woman in white have been reported by staff, though she quickly vanishes.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This ghostly pale woman heard screaming when a death is imminent in the family. She has also been spotted running across the grounds.
properties.date: Prior to death in Suffield family
properties.location_name: Gunton (Norfolk) - Gunton Park and House (partly destroyed by fire in 1882)

properties.name: White Lady Who Slaps
properties.description: Legend tells of the story of a milkmaid who met a ghost at the castle. The white lady requested that the milkmaid returned the following night, but the girl stayed away. The next day, the white lady hunted down the girl and slapped her for disobedience! More recently, a jogger spotted a woman in a white dress (possibly a wedding gown) standing in the path ahead him. The woman turned and started running just ahead of the jogger. The woman in white suddenly vanished on a bend on the path, and the jogger felt the hair on the back of his neck stand up.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Seen by local fishermen (even though they cannot remember the exact date they witnessed the phenomenon), the white lady's history is unknown.

properties.name: Nun
properties.description: The roads of this small village are reportedly haunted by the shade of a nun. A white woman also stalks the area, though this may be the same entity.
properties.location_name: Hanham Abbots (Somerset) - General area, but mainly around the church

properties.name: Lady Jane Stanhope
properties.description: This white lady, condemned to haunt the area where she murdered her gardener after he accidentally killed a plant, only appears to those who are due to die within a few months.
properties.location_name: Harrington (Northamptonshire) - Tollemache Arms public house, fields behind the building

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This phantom woman is likely to be the same suicidal figure that haunts elsewhere in the village.
properties.location_name: Harston (Cambridgeshire) - Area between the Queens Head public house and Mill Road

properties.name: Suicidal Leap
properties.description: Here a white lady has been seen throwing herself off the bridge, but like so many other ghost stories, the reason behind it is unclear.
properties.location_name: Harston (Cambridgeshire) - Road bridge en route to Haslingfield

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A farmer reported seeing this pale spectre on several moonlit nights crossing the stable yard - he would always tip his cap to her! The ghost was also observed entering the front door in the hall itself and drifting up the staircase. The rustling of her dress could be heard moving down corridors at night. Another phantom reported her is the ghost of a mason caught playing dice instead of working - he was executed!

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Even though the phantom white lady has not been observed for around one hundred years, she still opens a single door on one night per annum. A 1927 newspaper report claimed the figure to be Charlotte Bronte and that the appearance upset the family's dog, although the vicar denied the story.

properties.name: Dragging
properties.description: A woman was murdered by her lover; her body was dragged down the stairs before being dumped in a pool at Stoney Ford. Many people have reputedly heard the bumps of her body as it descends the staircase. The area around the farm is said to be haunted by a woman in white, most likely to be the murder victim in a state of unrest.
properties.location_name: Hayfield (Derbyshire) - South Head Farm, and surrounding area

properties.name: Jolly Fat Man
properties.description: This jovial chap was seen laughing away as he sat on a bed. A white female form has also been reported on site.
properties.location_name: Hemel Hempstead (Hertfordshire) - Old King's Arms public house

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This small cottage was haunted by a lady in white, who would vanish if anyone came too close.
properties.location_name: Hey Houses (Lancashire) - Fancy Lodge (no longer standing)

properties.name: Woman's Singing
properties.description: Once a year a gentle female voice can be heard singing. This could be the Woman in White, seen punting from one side for the Broad to another, heading towards a mill. Another ghost is associated with the broad; a drummer is said to be a Napoleonic soldier who drowned while skating across the ice, en route for a secret rendezvous with his girlfriend, can sometimes be heard.
properties.location_name: Hickling (Norfolk) - Hickling Broad, south of village

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The area around this farm was said to be haunted by a drifting white woman.

properties.name: Waving White Lady in a Coach
properties.description: After losing control of the horses, a coach driver and his female passenger died. Now, the phantom vehicle is seen outside the church; the white woman in the carriage waves at witnesses before the coach pulls away and the accident is repeated.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The gates to this hall are reportedly haunted by a white lady who has unnerved several drivers through the village.

properties.name: Juliet Tewsley, Her Love Unrequited
properties.description: Juliet took her own life after being jilted by a lover. The pub was later built over her grave, and she is now seen in the form of a white lady, appearing near the Ferry Boat Inn and walking towards the banks of the river on the date of her death.
properties.location_name: Holywell (Cambridgeshire) - Ye Olde Ferryboat Inn, gravestone in bar

properties.name: Rebecca
properties.description: Murdered the day before her wedding by her fiancee, Rebecca now appears in her white dress in protest over her rough treatment.
properties.location_name: Horton (North Yorkshire) - Bracken Hall, village green, and surrounding moor land

properties.name: Party Girl
properties.description: This phantom young woman in a white party dress is reported to be barefoot.

properties.name: Woman in Nightdress
properties.description: Several residents and at least one visitor spotted a phantom woman in a white nightdress during their stay in this house. Footsteps were also heard on the site, doors would slam shut, and one person found themselves locked in a room. One of the children injured themselves trying to float down the stairs like their 'friend'.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This ghostly white lady has been seen by several witnesses in the upper part of the hall. One person reported a strange mist emerging from the chimney, though no fires were burning within.
properties.location_name: Ipswich (Suffolk) - Gippeswyk Hall (currently occupied by Red Rose Chain)

properties.name: Waiting Woman
properties.description: This railway museum is haunted by a woman in white who is said to wait for a lover who never returns home. This Victorian woman later took her own life by throwing herself under a train.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The temperature is said to drop sharply as the phantom white lady materialises. One workman fled the site after encountering her.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This phantom white lady, quite possibly a nun, was said to live in harmony with a former owner (a Doctor Bedford). She was seen on the landing and near the staircase. It may be fair to speculate that a white lady that haunts several other local buildings may be the same entity.

properties.name: Walled Up
properties.description: When they were discovered in each other' arms, the wife of the Hall's owner and her secret lover were forcefully separated - she was locked in her bedroom, and he bricked up behind a wall. They both starved to death. The wife now manifests as the Grey Lady and ascends the staircase, and the lover peers out of a window at passers-by. A white lady also haunts the site, as does a Highlander, and a coachman who walks on the surface of the lake looking for his sunken coach. In 2019, a witness in an empty room watched a child's chair being pulled away from a table and then slide back in, as if someone unseen had sat down.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A female member of the Schivas family was hanged from the bridge after she refused to betray her husband. Her shade was said to haunt the area of her death.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This ghostly figure was seen standing by the bar, as if waiting to be served. Her presence was also reported at a table.
properties.location_name: Kidderminster (Hereford & Worcester) - Ye Olde Seven Stars, Coventry Street

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A gamekeeper who spotted a woman's face at the window of the empty castle quickly gathered assistance to discover who the intruder was. The men found nothing, and so the white lady was blamed for the sighting. The ghostly lady was also seen by others at the chapel; an old blacksmith tried to speak to her, causing the phantom to disappear.

properties.name: Bloodied Woman
properties.description: A young lady of the Cromlix family fell in love with Sir Malise Graham (known as the Black Knight of Kilbryde), who returned the feelings by murdering her and hiding the body. The woman's ghost returned, dressed in white and covered in blood, beckoning any witness who spotted her to approach. Most ran away, until one man did as the ghost requested. The woman then led the man to a nearby glen where he discovered a woman's body; the ghost returned no more.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The wife of a former Langford man, this woman now only appears before a death in the family.
properties.date: Prior to a death in the Langford family
properties.location_name: Kilcosgriff (County Limerick) - Kilcosgriff Castle (no longer standing)

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: This female form is said to occasionally appear on a staircase and in the garden. A legend says this ghost (or perhaps others) ensure that no guest will have a restful night within the castle.

properties.name: White Ladies
properties.description: Two white ladies are said to lurk in the ravine, as are the ghosts of people who found the area so pretty that they were unable to leave.
properties.location_name: Kilmany (Fife) - Area known as Ghouls' Den (aka Gouls Den), near Kilmany Cottage

properties.name: White Woman on Horseback
properties.description: Manifesting when moonlight hit the ground, this white lady is said to have been killed as her horse bolted somewhere north of the village, the panicked creature throwing her into a quarry.
properties.location_name: Kilpatrick (County Cork) - Road near old quarry

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The history of this phantom female is unknown, but speculated to have been murdered, or was a murderer, or could have been a nun. Or all three. Or none.
properties.location_name: Kilspindie (Perth and Kinross) - Lady's Brig, area of land close to Balmyre Farm

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A ghostly woman in white would upset anyone using the lane.
properties.location_name: Kineton (Warwickshire) - Area known as Madam's Lane (no longer present?)

properties.name: Small Lady
properties.description: Shortly after a wedding, the bride was stabbed to death by her new husband in the hotel. Since then, a short woman in a long white dress has been spotted and phantom footsteps heard.
properties.location_name: King's Lynn (Norfolk) - The Tudor Rose Hotel, 11 Nicholas Street

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Her appearance preluded by coughing and shuffling sounds, staff and customers in the building spotted the apparition of an old woman as she passed down the corridors. A later story would emerge that the owner, Jack Bond, created the ghostly tale, asking a friend to wear a white sheet and pass by the building's windows.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This woman leapt to her death from the battlements after discovering that her father had mistakenly shot dead her newlywed husband. Since her death, she has been seen walking through locked doors and was blamed for knocking unconscious at least two witnesses. The site was also haunted by a rabbit which attacked guards asleep on duty.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Julian Hawthorne's minor vampire story Ken's Mystery is said to be based on an encounter with a phantom woman who travelled from the fort to Desmond Castle, where she vanished.
properties.location_name: Kinsale (County Cork) - Road from Fort to Desmond Castle

properties.name: Pale Woman
properties.description: A woman in white was said to haunt this stretch of road, walking along the treetops and occasionally on the road with (mortal) travellers.
properties.location_name: Kirkbean (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road between village and Prestonmill

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: This phantom woman's past is unknown, although her quiet form was said to startle passersby. One local story says that a local farmer's daughter exploited the story; dressing in a white sheet to meet her lover by the roadside ensured no one came to speak to her.
properties.location_name: Kirkbean (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road coming in from the north

properties.name: Dunn
properties.description: The body of a young stableman by the name of Dunn was found here - the law believed he had committed suicide, but others thought he had been murdered by the brother of a woman he was romantically involved with. Dunn's ghost was said to have returned to the spot, and occasionally the area was also haunted by a white lady.
properties.location_name: Kirkbean (Dumfries and Galloway) - Three crossroads, near Arbigland

properties.name: Jenny
properties.description: When a private house, this building was haunted by a white woman in a beautiful flowing dress. There were also reports of a phantom goose, which would peck at the young girl who lived there.

properties.name: Woman with Dog
properties.description: A woman dressed in white escorted by a ghostly dog are said to haunt this inn. Other spectres reported here include a headless lady and a man dressed in black.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This lady's untrusting husband locked her in a room as he went overseas to war - by the time he returned, all that remained was her shade, which now haunts the tower.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Singing has been reported coming from the building when it is empty, and a ghostly woman dressed in white has also been seen. This entity may be the same as the grey lady reportedly seen in the chapel.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: This female form was briefly seen before vanishing into the corner of a bedroom. At least one guest left suddenly after spending the night in the room.

properties.name: Lady of Lawers
properties.description: This long deserted village is home to the phantom white lady of Lawers. Workers at a nearby hotel report the feeling of being watched in the area.
properties.location_name: Lawers (Perth and Kinross) - General area of the ruined village, on banks of Loch Tay

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A woman wearing white who took her own life in the reputedly bottomless pool emerges at night and wanders lanes in the area.
properties.location_name: Leebotwood (Shropshire) - Unnamed pool in the area

properties.name: Abbot
properties.description: The former Abbot of the abbey walks the area that has been converted into a museum. It is more usual to hear the spirit moving around than see him. Traditionally the entity is said to wear white robes, although the sighting in the 1970s reported the ghost's robes as being brown/red. The abbey grounds are also home to a white female entity named Mary who witnessed her lover committing murder and turned him in.

properties.name: May
properties.description: Several phantoms are reported to haunt this area, including a young woman in white who walks the corridors. She is named as May, who died aged eighteen of tuberculosis. Other phantoms are said to be carriages pulled by black horses which vanish into the trees, a groom who hanged himself in a room above the stables, and a sad looking boy who glazes from an upstairs window.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This pale woman has been seen moving around the headstones.

properties.name: Disconcerting Monk
properties.description: There are mixed opinions to whether the library here is haunted by a grey monk or a white lady. A tormented Cavalier has been seen in the Great Hall, a phantom policeman haunts other parts of the building, a ghostly dog has been reported in the courtyard, a black cat in the Great Hall, and at least one witness claims to have been shocked to see a pair of legs manifest from the portrait of Henry Earl of Huntington hanging in the Major's Parlour. Ghost Scene Investigations and Leicester City Museums released video footage in 2006 of what appears to be a ghostly figure crawling across the floor in one room.

properties.name: Slapper
properties.description: It is said the area was once haunted by a headless white woman who would hunt down highwaymen and give them a slap. What became of the entity is not known.
properties.location_name: Leven (East Riding of Yorkshire) - Road near White Cross

properties.name: Lord Byron
properties.description: The story goes Lord Byron once met the ghostly black friar that haunted this building for close to 500 years, though contemporary legends state Bryon himself now sits in the corridor, near his own picture. Also reported in the area is a white lady who smells of rose petals, Bryon's pet dog Boatswain, and a woman in black who is considered unlucky.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: The woman who haunts this building may be connected to the skull which is bricked up within a wall. The skull once stood in a room, but when it was removed from the building it started to scream. The owners decided it would be for the best to brick it up in a wall to prevent future accidental removal.
properties.location_name: Lincoln (Lincolnshire) - Great Hall, near Edward King's House

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This pale woman is thought to have been a former prisoner who died during her stay. Witnesses have also reported the sounds of doors slamming and keys jangling in the area. One witness heard footsteps and the rustling of a dress - when they looked around, no one could be seen.

properties.name: Mary of Guise
properties.description: Accompanied by a slight smell of perfume, this phantom lady in a white dress has on occasion been mistaken for a real person. Mary Queen of Scots is also reported to frequent the chapel, and Margaret Tudor haunts Queen Margaret's Bower.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: The abbey was once haunted by a young woman dressed in white. She was seen and heard moving in the upper part of the building, though it is said the haunting stopped once a Major discovered a heart in a metal box in the attic and gave it a proper burial.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This spook is reportedly a woman whose body was found on the nearby beach. A long dead cat is also reported to play on the beds at night, while a man in 'modern' dress is said to slowly materialise from the feet up.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This female apparition closely follows the same route as she calmly drifts through the area.
properties.location_name: Littlewick Green (Berkshire) - Boundary Elm, Green Lane, Cold Harbour and Knowl Hill

properties.name: Rosina Buckman
properties.description: Rosina's ashes were moved when the power station was constructed on the site of her old house, and her pallid wraith now drifts around the vicinity. During the construction of the power station, several workers are said to have quit their jobs after encountering a phantom woman in white.
properties.location_name: Llanbadrig (Gwynedd) - Area around Wylfa power station

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A motorist reported almost running over (or through) a white lady that stepped out on the road in front of him.
properties.location_name: Llangennith (West Glamorgan) - Main road through village

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This ghostly figure has appeared several times, manifesting in the middle of the road only to pass through incoming traffic. One male driver stated that the female form looked at him with sad eyes as she passed through the bonnet of his car.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This pallid figure moves from the hall and disappears into the nearby wood. There have also been sightings of a short man dressed in green.
properties.location_name: Llanvihangel Crucorney (Gwent) - Between Llanvihangel Court and Lady Wood

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The monastery was thought haunted by a woman whose husband had sought shelter there - he was finally found, had his leg cut off and was finally bricked up alive within the walls. Centuries after the event, a legless skeleton was discovered in the building, although legend does not state whether this prevented the female phantom from returning.
properties.location_name: Llanvithyn (aka Llanveithin and Llanmeuthin) (South Glamorgan) - Former monastery (no longer standing)

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Her white robes flowing as she walks, the white lady is said to leapt from the battlements to escape punishment after being caught with a manservant. A young boy that also haunts the site is thought to be Richard Paterson who drowned in the Tay in 1939.
properties.location_name: Longforgan (Perth and Kinross) - HMP Castle Huntly, open prison

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: A woman in a flowing white dress is said to haunt the banks of the river close to the village.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This white lady drowned in the pool, and now dances around the area. One man who saw the entity thought she was real and tried to dance with her, only to find his arms passed straight through her body.

properties.name: Tied Figure
properties.description: A manor house in Lossiemouth was said to be haunted by a woman draped in white with a heavily scarred throat and her hands tied in chains behind her back. The figure was said to appear every night, and because of the stories, the site remained empty for years before being sold at a highly discounted price.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This figure in white holds a red rose in her mouth and turns and walks into the fogou once spotted. Some say to see her will bring misfortune, and anyone foolish enough to follow her... well, no one as yet has followed her.
properties.location_name: Lower Boscaswell (Cornwall) - Pendeen Vau, aka Pendeen Fogou

properties.name: Headless Man
properties.description: One folk story says this road is home to several entities that appear to travellers, including a headless man, a white horse, a black dog, and a sad woman with flowing hair who wears a white robe.
properties.location_name: Lulsley (Hereford & Worcester) - Sandy Lane (between Osebury Rock and the river)

properties.name: Ann
properties.description: A white figure glimpsed here is thought to be the ghost of Ann, murdered in the pub so long ago that the date has been forgotten. She has been known to talk to visitors who are unaware she is a phantom. The figure may be the same entity which has been seen on the street outside, moving from the pub towards nearby shops.
properties.location_name: Luton (Bedfordshire) - Cock and Bull public house

properties.name: Factory Worker
properties.description: On several occasions the ghost of a white woman managed to unnerve workers at the plant. She was thought to be the spirit of a former worker who died nine years previous.

properties.name: Woman in Gown
properties.description: A phantom white woman in a long white gown is said to haunt the base of the waterfall.
properties.location_name: Lydford Gorge (Devon) - White Lady Falls (aka Whitelady Waterfall)

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pale figure was seen standing over a guest's bed. She has also been observed in the bar.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: One of several ghosts to be found in the hall, this unknown Clifton family member has been seen in various parts of the grounds. One bedroom is said to be particularly haunted, the manifestation complete with clanking chains and stomping feet.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This area is reputed to be haunted by a white woman with no feet.
properties.location_name: Macclesfield (Cheshire) - Kendel Road (area known locally as the Birdy)

properties.name: Young Girl
properties.description: Wearing a long white dress, this young girl barely in her teens has been reported in several parts of the village. It is thought she drowned in a flood which swept through the area in the mid-nineteenth century.

properties.name: Hound
properties.description: A black dog which was said to haunt this area was once heard invisibly passing by three women, although one of them speculated the sound may have been a peacock. A woman in white was also seen by a doctor using the road.
properties.location_name: Mainsriddle (Dumfries and Galloway) - Road between village and Prestonmill, area once known as Dairymaid's Hollow

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This female form causes trouble on the roads as she drifts out in front on oncoming traffic. Some witnesses say the entity is more like a monk than a woman and a cyclist who spotted the figure in the road in 1960 said the phantom figure was twice the height of a normal person.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: This phantom woman in a white dress is thought to have once haunted Hall Place before it burnt down - her ghost has now migrated to the nearby road.
properties.location_name: Mansfield (Nottinghamshire) - A60 and area around Hall Place (no longer standing)

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: The woodland is said to be haunted by a white female form. She is thought to be connected to the ruins of a house on the outskirts of the woodland.
properties.location_name: Mansfield Woodhouse (Nottinghamshire) - The Vale woodland and surrounding area

properties.name: White Rider
properties.description: In the days when horses were the main form of transport, this white woman would leap onto the back of a passing rider and refuse to leave until taken to the Red River, where she would vanish. The last witness, Buck Billy, died in 1840.
properties.location_name: Marazion (Cornwall) - Area once known as Marazion Green

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The building was haunted by a white female spectre murdered in the property.
properties.location_name: Market Lavington (Wiltshire) - Old Rectory (no longer standing)

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Before the mansion burnt down, it stood empty for twenty years. The caretaker reported seeing a phantom grey lady within the building, and even though the mansion was boarded up, furnishings and fittings would be found thrown around within. Some believe the grey lady started the fire which finally reduced the mansion to a shell which was demolished.

properties.name: White Lady Riding
properties.description: A drunk man fell asleep in the barn, waking at midnight to see a woman in white riding around on a buck. She moved so fast that the wind whipped up dust, causing the man to sneeze - the sound causing the lady to vanish.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Two members of staff, on different occasions, witnessed a woman in a long flowing dress pass through a room in this building. There have also been reports of poltergeist activity, which included a large table being overturned.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Sightings of this phantom white lady are said not to be uncommon, though any real details are fleeting.
properties.location_name: Milton Abbas (Dorset) - Road through the village

properties.name: Mary Way
properties.description: This crossroads was said to be haunted by the ghost of Mary Way, murdered on the site. The phantom is reputedly headless and wears a shimmering white dress. A murderer was also said to be buried at the crossroads, although it is not clear whether it was Mary's killer.
properties.location_name: Much Wenlock (Shropshire) - Crossroads two miles southeast of town

properties.name: Monks Cooking
properties.description: Several shades of religious people have been seen here, including monks, a bishop and a woman in white. The smell of fresh meat being cooked sometimes wafts through the house, even when no one is cooking.

properties.name: Woman with Hollow Eyes
properties.description: This ghost, dressed in white, was seen emerging from a wardrobe by several members of the family. The family finally left the building after a mysterious fire broke out.
properties.location_name: N16 (Greater London) - 69 Spencer Grove (no longer standing), Stoke Newington

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: The pallid ghost of a lady was reported here.
properties.location_name: N2 (Greater London) - Strawberry Vale, East Finchley

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: One police officer is said to have lost his mind after seeing this phantom woman and became obsessed with reading everything he could about local ghost stories.
properties.location_name: N3 (Greater London) - Nether Street, West Finchley

properties.name: Man with Hound
properties.description: A phantom man, tall and covered by a cloak, once haunted this area, accompanied by his hunting hound. The area is also home to a shadowy nun and a woman in white who screams as she is chased by an unidentified shadow.
properties.location_name: Navan (County Meath) - Skyrne Castle, Hill of Tara

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Falling in love and becoming pregnant by the lord of the castle, a local girl committed suicide after being shunned by him after their love affair. Though her mother then cursed the evil lord (resulting in his death, and that of the estate's future heir), the young lass still returns to the site.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: Several motorists are said to have encountered the phantom woman dressed in white that haunts this road, reporting their sightings to the police after driving through her.
properties.location_name: Nettlestone (Isle of Wight) - Road near the old church

properties.name: Bride
properties.description: This woman in white is said to be a bride murdered by her husband on their wedding night hundreds of years ago. She may have last been seen by a married couple who spotted a figure with folded arms standing amongst trees. Two years prior to this sighting, two woman passing Castle Hill observed the phantom woman in white.
properties.location_name: Newton le Willows (Merseyside) - Willow Park (and other meres and woods in the area)

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The woodland takes its name from a disused slaughterhouse. The white female form seen drifting in the area is thought to be the ghost of a woman involved in a car accident nearby.
properties.location_name: Newton Stewart (Dumfries and Galloway) - Slaughterhouse Woods

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: The staircase of this property is reputedly haunted by a benign white feminine spirit. The house may have been built over the graveyard of a nunnery.
properties.location_name: North Berwick (Lothian) - Private residence, Glenorchy Road

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A phantom white lady reputedly walks this area, as does a hooded figure said to be a former pageboy murdered in London.
properties.location_name: North Berwick (Lothian) - The Lodge (aka North Berwick House)

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Killed by soldiers when she was discovered in a chapel, the shade of this white lady has been reported many times since.

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: Seen wandering around the gate of the church, this woman in white is thought to be looking for an unknown item. The ruined church itself is home to a tall man who has been seen standing at the location the altar once stood.

properties.name: Children
properties.description: The ghosts of a white woman and of two children have been reported in this wooded area. While the woman in white has been named as Mary Chaworth Musters, the children are thought to have been murdered by their father William Saville, hanged for the crime in 1844. The sound of horses has also been reported.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This piece of woodland is reputedly haunted by a white lady, a nun and disembodied screaming.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Thought to be the same ghost encountered at the nearby church, this white woman is thought by some to be Mary Ann Chaworth-Musters reputedly Lord Byron's first love.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A bus driver passing through the village was left unnerved after watching a woman dressed in white cross the road ahead of him. She disappeared suddenly.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Doomed to repeat her actions for reasons unknown, the woman in white has been seen on countless occasions in the area. A lad out on his early morning paper round saw her crossing a field, vanishing without warning.
properties.location_name: Old Catton (Norfolk) - White Woman Lane, and George Hill

properties.name: Robert Leigh
properties.description: One of many ghosts belonging to this building, Leigh was reported to walk around the grounds carrying an unknown person's head under one arm. A woman in white rustling silk glides along the corridors of the building, the sad sounds of a harpsichord can be heard, and on occasion a black dog lurks outside.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Only appearing in front of children, this pale shade walks the hotel.

properties.name: Faceless Monk
properties.description: This giant figure is said to have had a skull for a head, a result of black magic rituals carried out in days gone by. He has made at least one witness drop dead of shock (although the death was also said to have been caused by a phantom old lady in black). The grounds are also haunted by a white lady, gliding effortlessly along.

properties.name: Former Master with Hounds
properties.description: Even though this place has been exorcised at least twice, the building still appears to possess several ghosts. One former owner has been seen dressed in traditional hunting gear, with his hounds around his feet. A white lady reportedly climbs the staircase located in the older part of the house, while the sounds associated with an old carriage have been heard coming from outside. Finally, poltergeist like activity, such as unusual electrical interference, has been reported.
properties.location_name: Oulton (Norfolk) - High House (now Manor House), Oulton Broad

properties.name: Lady Constance
properties.description: One of several ghosts reported here, Lady Constance killed herself after watching her husband die when a stag gored him. Her pale white shade now frequents the grounds. Lady Limerick reported seeing the ghostly Edward, the Black Prince during the First World War, and he popped up again in 1931. The spirit of a servant haunts the loft, while a poltergeist outbreak in 1950 resulted in furniture being tossed around.
properties.date: Constance unknown, Edward in October 1931, poltergeist 1950
properties.location_name: Outer London: Bexley (Greater London) - Hall Place

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A torso of a female murder victim was discovered here, with her legs found in a train carriage at Waterloo. Her ghost remained in the area for a while, manifesting as a tall woman wearing a white dress.
properties.location_name: Outer London: Brentford (Greater London) - Banks of the Grand Union Canal

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This area is rumoured to be haunted by a woman who committed infanticide.
properties.location_name: Panshanger Woods (Hertfordshire) - Area near an abandoned decoy air shelter

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Not much is known about the phantom woman who was said to haunt the road to Cork. A phantom coach and horses haunted other roads in the area, the noise of its passing causing household furnishings to shake.
properties.location_name: Passage West (County Cork) - Road towards Cork, and other roads through village

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This pale creature flitters around the building, having died of a broken heart.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Dressed all in white, this woman committed suicide in the pond where her ghost now vanishes.
properties.location_name: Penallt (Gwent) - Between the Argoed (private residence) and nearby pond

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A female phantom is said to have left the church via the east gate and walk down the hill. Author Eddie Brazil speculated that the phantom was Ruth Ellis, the last woman to have been executed in England.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A local legend says that this area is haunted by a white lady and a small child. Minor polt activity is also reported, such as rattling doors, flickering lights and glasses being smashed. The presence is described as playful.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: No one really has any idea who this phantom could be, though her appearance is said to warn against approaching bad luck and can bring about improved health and fortune. Some reports say she is green, rather than white.

properties.name: Thomas Hunter
properties.description: Sergeant Thomas Hunter, with the Anzacs during the First World War, died in the building 31 July 1916 when it was a hospital. Hunter was last seen drifting up the staircase, though he is blamed for moving items around the building at night or placing icy cold hands on people. Other entities said to haunt here include a scruffy man in the cellar, a white woman in the upper part of the building, a young girl in the former operating theatre, and a phantom Roman soldier who stands by a sword that is on display.

properties.name: Pale Lady
properties.description: Staff and visitors have reported seeing a phantom white lady around this building.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: These wards are reputed to be built on the site of a battle and numerous unexplained occurrences are said to have happened. One male staff member reported a white lady who disappeared into a room. On investigation, the room was found empty, but the patient the man was standing beside at the time of the sighting died before morning, even though he was not obviously unwell.
properties.location_name: Plymouth (Devon) - Geriatric wards, Mount Gould Hospital

properties.name: Monk
properties.description: This park is said to be haunted by a figure wearing a habit near the gates of the lodge, a white lady who drowned in a lake while eloping, and a strange animal with very large eyes.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Reported to float above the castle ruins, this phantom white lady could be Jane de Afan, the last occupant of the castle.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This phantom woman appears on or near the castle mound, located close to Saint Mary's churchyard.

properties.name: Lady in Brown
properties.description: Six sailors stayed at this hotel after their ship was unable to leave the docks due to bad weather. After spending time in the bar, the sailors headed to their rooms. Two of the men spotted a woman wearing a long brown Victorian style dress with a white bonnet and shawl on her shoulders standing outside of a room. The figure disappeared before being seen by the other four men.
properties.location_name: Portland (Dorset) - Portland Hotel, Portland Square (no longer standing)

properties.name: Man in Purple Cloak
properties.description: Named as either Peter Carey (an English invader) or Richard de Burgh (Earl of Antrim), this ethereal figure now walks around the broken towers of the castle. A white lady (possibly Mave Roe) also haunts the place; she died of a broken heart after being denied marrying her true love. Another story tells that, during a fierce storm, the castle's kitchens collapsed into the sea - the screams of the staff can be heard as they forever fall to their doom.

properties.name: Grey Figure
properties.description: This entity, clad in a grey cloak, is seen entering the churchyard before vanishing. A white lady has also been reported more recently on the site.

properties.name: Woman in Dress
properties.description: This lady wearing a white flowing dress has been reported quietly drifting around the grounds.
properties.location_name: Radcliffe on Trent (Nottinghamshire) - Grounds of Holme Pierrepont Hall

properties.name: The Ayloffs
properties.description: A few ghosts are reported to haunt this manor. Roman soldiers have been seen walking through walls in the basement, and a lady in white has been seen on the lower half of the stairs. Another lady seen on the stairs is thought to be Martha Ayloff, while Anne Ayloff, wearing a lavender dress, haunts the room adjacent to room 7.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pallid female wraith cursed the family with everlasting sorrow and misfortune. She is said to be quite tall and slender, with blue eyes and blonde hair. The figure was last seen in 1966, where the entity was said to be on her knees, crying, bathed in bluish-white light. A banshee has also been heard wailing at the castle; one man who heard the entity as he left the site died before his journey ended.
properties.location_name: Randalstown (County Antrim) - Shane's Castle (aka Shanes Castle)

properties.name: White Lady of Kilcosgriff
properties.description: The phantom white lady from Kilcosgriff Castle is said to have come to this building when a Major Langford died here. She was seen by a nurse treating the Major.

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: This ghostly woman in white could be heard moving things around the shop and was said to have touched the back of the neck of one witness. It is thought that the woman died in a house fire.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: It is not clear if the woman in white, a large black hound and a black hog which once haunted this area were all the same entity. The phantom (or phantoms) would all suddenly grow once seen, before pursuing any witness.
properties.location_name: Reagill (Cumbria) - Country lanes in the area

properties.name: Young Boy
properties.description: The swimming baths are reputedly to be haunted by two phantoms. One is a young boy who diving into the pool while wearing a First World War German helmet his father had brought back from the trenches. The lad landed badly and snapped his neck. The second ghost is that of a woman dressed in white.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A woman in white who haunted the area was said to have tried to locate a newborn baby, the kiss of which would release her from her fate.
properties.location_name: Rhiwsaeson (South Glamorgan) - Unknown road in the area

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This cave is haunted by a greedy woman who entered it looking for hidden treasure - a sudden storm blew up, trapping and drowning her.
properties.location_name: Rhosili (West Glamorgan) - Paviland Cave, between village and Port Eynon

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: The white figure of a lady was seen standing behind a wall, leaning over the top.
properties.location_name: Rhyl (Clwyd) - Start of the Dyserth Road bends

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This area is home to a phantom woman in white and a strange shadowy figure, speculated by some to be a former miner.
properties.location_name: Risca (Gwent) - Miner's graves near campsite in nearby forest

properties.name: White Woman & Coach
properties.description: It is said that a phantom white woman haunts this area, sometimes seen climbing into a ghostly horse-drawn coach which then drives off.

properties.name: Pale Lady
properties.description: This phantom woman in white who once frequently walked through walls and closed doors may be connected to the 'Boggart Room', where an infant's coffin was stored to avoid paying Shroud Tax.

properties.name: Arbella Stuart
properties.description: Some believe the phantom white lady said to haunt the abbey is the ghost of Arbella Stuart, although her connection to the site is a little unclear. A photograph hit the press in 2013 showing a 'white misty figure', although the image is open to interpretation.

properties.name: Dancing Woman
properties.description: Wearing a white gown, a phantom woman is said to dance alone in the ballroom. Other entities reputed to haunt the side include another woman who is fond of men, a woman in a black dress spotted on the staircase, a couple of children, three drinkers at the bar, and finally the friendly entity known only as Albert.
properties.location_name: Ryde (Isle of Wight) - Royal Victoria Yacht Club

properties.name: Restless Dead
properties.description: It is said that a former laird removed the headstones from nearby churchyards to use as construction material for his castle - hence, much of the area is haunted by those disturbed. Particularly noted are the shades of a monk and that of a white lady.
properties.location_name: Saddell (Argyll and Bute) - Abbey and castle

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Some people believe that this phantom figure is Margaret Radclyffe, Queen Elizabeth's favourite Maid of Honour. She died of a broken heart in 1559 shortly after the death of her brother.

properties.name: Phantom Footfalls
properties.description: The footfalls may belong to the owner of a severed hand which was discovered behind a secret panel within the pub. This ghost is also held responsible for moving dirty glasses in the bar. The hand was stolen from the pub in March 2004. Another phantom, that of a white lady, is said to appear at windows.
properties.date: Random days between 11.30 & midnight (footfalls), white lady unknown
properties.location_name: Salisbury (Wiltshire) - Haunch of Venison public house

properties.name: John Wilson
properties.description: Wilson was murdered just for being in the wrong place at the wrong time, and his ghost still protests. A blonde-haired lady dressed in white also haunts the castle - she was murdered in the late sixteenth century, though her body not discovered for almost three hundred years.

properties.name: Highwayman's Girlfriend
properties.description: This woman was murdered by a jealous partner after he discovered that she was having an affair with a highwayman; the ghostly lady in white now walks the staircase and the first floor.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This ghost is believed to be that of a woman who died of head trauma - a skull found in the 1830s near the site possessed a large hole caused by a blunt object. Shooter's Hill also made the news in 1963, as it was combed by police and soldiers looking for a puma-like creature that had been seen by members of the public.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: While travelling home, a driver spotted a woman in white clothing, with a pale face, standing by a gate by one of the bridge arches. Finding it strange for someone to be there, the driver slowed down to take a closer look. The figure was completely motionless. The driver passed her, and looking into the rear view mirror, realised the figure had vanished.
properties.location_name: Seaton (Leicestershire) - B672 Road, under the Seaton Viaduct bridge

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This traditional white figure supposedly haunts the grounds of both the hall and the abbey. The latter location is also the home to a spectral monk.
properties.location_name: Sheffield (South Yorkshire) - Beauchief Abbey and Hall

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: Wearing a flowing dress, this pale spirit has occasionally been reported.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Little is known about this phantom woman, other than an interesting point to note is that the road was once called Dead Man's Lane.

properties.name: Governess
properties.description: Now returning to the hall in the form of a white lady, the governess had her throat slit by the hall's Squire after she threatened to tell his wife of their affair. The sounds of their final argument can also be heard drifting around the hall at night. A man staying here also reported a phantom hound which awoke him at night.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Believed by staff at the prison to be the ghost of a woman executed there during the seventeenth century, this female presence was blamed for every cold spot and freak gust of wind within the building. More recently, staff have reported the ghost of an American serviceman, thought to be related to the period when the site was used by the US military.
properties.date: Mid twentieth century (White Lady), Circa 2008 (American Serviceman)
properties.location_name: Shepton Mallet (Somerset) - HMP Shepton Mallet (former prison)
