properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A white lady was said to haunt this little waterfall along the river, hovering while bathed in moonlight.
properties.location_name: Shinnel (aka Shinnell) Water (Dumfries and Galloway) - Paul's Pool

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: A headless woman in a white gown was said to haunt this location.
properties.location_name: Shipton Gorge (Dorset) - Private residence along Dorchester Road

properties.name: Nun
properties.description: This ghostly nun was once watched as she slowly dissolved into nothingness. She has also been heard walking in empty parts of the building, though the footsteps could belong to a white woman (thought to be a murdered maid) who also haunts the castle.

properties.name: Slim Woman
properties.description: This phantom woman in white died of the traditional broken heart after her father drove away her one true love. The entity has been reported exorcised. Another story attached to the site says that the Devil arrived one night to join a game of cards. Old Nick fled after one gambler spotted a cloven foot under the table.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: After a long period of absence, this phantom woman was spotted by a member of the public in 2006. Staff who work at the building report feeling a strange presence in some of the rooms in this hall.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Folklore said a phantom white woman haunted this road.

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: This pale, lost shade could belong to one of the mummified bodies that were removed from a nearby tower in the mid-nineteenth century. A monk has been seen on the staircase, offering to help anyone struggling to ascend the tower.

properties.name: Lady de Clare
properties.description: Rumoured to have been buried alive by her cruel husband who (incorrectly) believed she was having an affair, this white lady was once said to walk the castle and the surrounding area.
properties.location_name: St Athan (South Glamorgan) - Area around site of West Orchard castle

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: La blianche femme, or white woman, is reputed to haunt this road. She gave chase to a local man in 1900, who had to take shelter in a house close by.
properties.location_name: St Ouen (Jersey) - Rue a la Pendue

properties.name: Sarah Harrington
properties.description: Harrington was a former Maid of Honor to Queen Henrietta and is thought to be the phantom woman in white who haunts this building. A mighty oak which stood in the garden was thought to scream and bled when cut; unfortunately, the tree blew down during a gale in 1883.
properties.location_name: Staveley (Derbyshire) - The Hagge (aka Hagg) (former mansion, now a farmhouse)

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: The wife of a former owner of the local manor house, this white woman drifted down the road after dusk for many years after her death. Sometimes the stories colour her green, like so many other ghostly women in this part of the world.
properties.location_name: Stirling (Stirling) - Rownam Avenue, and area of former manor house

properties.name: Nun
properties.description: Dressed in white, the ghostly nun is said to haunt not only the church, but the entire village.
properties.location_name: Stratton St Margaret (Wiltshire) - Church and general area

properties.name: Steam Engine
properties.description: A film crew spending a night in the building in 2001 reported loud disembodied banging that climaxed as if the sounds of a stream train charged through the house. The house is also reported to be haunted by a white lady, and the park surrounding the building is home to a Roman soldier, a figure resembling a monk, two American servicemen in Second World War uniforms, a headless horse and a dwarf which somersaults along the driveway. In 2019, a witness watched a horse and carriage driven by a man wearing a tall Victorian hat pass through a locked gate.
properties.location_name: Stroud (Gloucestershire) - Woodchester Mansion, near the town

properties.name: Woman in White Coat
properties.description: A woman wearing a white coat and white trousers, with hair down to her shoulders, walked past a group of four friends, telling them, 'I have just been for a swim' before disappearing from view. Four years later, one of the group spoke to a couple who had experienced the exact same encounter in the same area.
properties.location_name: Studland (Dorset) - Pathway from Studland beach to road near The Banks Arms pub

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A woman who was betrayed by her lover drowned herself in a nearby pond (that no longer exists). Her ghost, dressed in white, wrings her hands as it drifts through the trees.
properties.date: Reputedly every night (story dates from nineteenth century)
properties.location_name: Sutton Coldfield (West Midlands) - Westwood Coppice (west side of Sutton Park)

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Sobbing for the loss of her lover, this white figure is seen moving across the grounds of Ranelagh and disappearing when she reaches the road.
properties.location_name: SW14 (Greater London) - Old Ranelagh and Lower Richmond Road

properties.name: Sarah
properties.description: Dressed in white, Sarah has been seen standing in the pub's bedrooms, her raven hair running over her shoulders. The ghost may be linked to the discovery of a secret, bricked up room in 1970.
properties.location_name: SW4 (Greater London) - Plough public house, Clapham Common

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The castle grounds are haunted by this crying white lady with a bloody back. Another story has the site haunted by a white horse.

properties.name: Black Lady of Tamworth
properties.description: Said to be the ghost of St Editha, this phantom nun has a room named after her within the building - she once appeared here and bullied a greedy landlord into returning stolen church lands. Another female, a white lady, also haunts the castle grounds.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A man driving a horse and cart through the woods was startled by the appearance of a white lady on the seat next to him - she vanished as he drove past the last tree of the forest. This story could be an early version of the phantom hitchhiker tales.
properties.date: Late nineteenth or early twentieth century

properties.name: Blue & White Ladies
properties.description: The blue lady took her own life after watching her lover die during a jousting contest outside the castle - she now patrols the battlements. The white lady is thought to be a nun from a priory which once stood nearby.

properties.name: Patrolling Woman
properties.description: A white woman has been spotted moving around the battlements at night.

properties.name: Hitchhiker
properties.description: This man dressed in a long grey coat stands by the roadside, and if an oncoming car does not stop to offer him a ride, he throws himself in front of the vehicle. Either way, he vanishes without trace, normally leaving the witness scared and confused. A white female version of the man has also been reported in the same area.
properties.location_name: Taunton (Somerset) - A38 road near the town

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: It is said this woman was once washed up dead on the beach - locals removed her expensive rings and threw the body back into the sea. She is seen standing on the coast, looking into the sea, whenever stormy weather takes a life.

properties.name: Monks
properties.description: Seen both inside and out, these figures drift forlornly about. Doors also open and close unaided by human hand, while the churchyard is said to be haunted by a screaming white woman.

properties.name: Black Dog
properties.description: This former pet has been seen on the landing, though it is not known whether the white woman who haunts a bedroom is its mistress.

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: Appearing quite real and substantial, the room that this sad shade haunts remained locked up for many years. She could be one of the nuns who once worked in the building.
properties.location_name: Thames Ditton (Surrey) - Home of Compassion church

properties.name: Lady in White
properties.description: The pale spectral form of a woman has been reported on the roadside near the ruins of the priory. She was last seen by a police officer in 1961.
properties.location_name: Thringstone (Leicestershire) - A512, near Grace Dieu Priory

properties.name: Whistling Ghost
properties.description: One of the spirits here can be heard playing what sounds like a tin whistle, while the other takes the form of a white lady who searches the building for her missing son.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Seen walking in this area, the lady is believed to be heading towards Gorwell Hall, the place where she died after having her throat cut.
properties.location_name: Tollesbury (Essex) - Road leading to the drive of Gorwell Hall

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pale figure is said to be one of a handful of ghosts reported here over the years, normally seen in the garden. A woman who starved to death in a bedroom and an old nurse also continue to reside here, while a headless horseman has been reported just outside the grounds of the building. In 1995 a member of staff spotted a glowing man wearing a cloak who faded away, remarkably like an event in 1976 where a young man watched a cloaked figure wearing a tricorn hat glide silently across the road outside the hall before passing effortlessly over the hedgerow.
properties.date: Mostly unknown, but cloaked figure October 1976 and circa March 1995

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A phantom woman dressed in white is reputed to haunt this area, possibly the same entity from the nearby Dartington Hall.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The ghostly lady that haunts the village has a connection to an old farmhouse, although the exact details are not known.
properties.location_name: Trawsfynydd (Gwynedd) - Farmhouse by edge of lake

properties.name: Young Girl in White Dress
properties.description: Believed to have been kidnapped, raped, and finally bricked up behind a wall in the 1600's, this white woman is said to haunt the first floor hallway of the club.

properties.name: Mrs Amphlett
properties.description: In his home along this street, Lord Lyttleton woke and saw a woman in white standing by his bed (named by some as being a Mrs Amphlett) who warned him he would be dead within three days. At 11pm on the third day, he dropped dead into his butler's arms at Pitt Place, in Epsom. The illustration is Lyttleton's encounter from the publication 'The Astrologer of the Nineteenth Century'.

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: A woman in a white nightdress vanished when a child asked 'mummy?'.
properties.location_name: W4 (Greater London) - Private residence, Blenheim Road

properties.name: Panicking Lady
properties.description: This white woman has been observed darting around, with body language suggesting she is in blind panic.
properties.date: The hours leading up to midnight

properties.name: White Woman
properties.description: An owner of the cottage claimed it was haunted by this lady in white.

properties.name: Murder Victim
properties.description: His life snuffed out at some point in the 1930s, this shade has remained earthbound since. The hall may also be haunted by a white Lady, thought to be Lady Isabella le Botelor.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: A phantom white woman is said to occasionally cross the road here.

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This phantom white woman would materialise to give warning of encroaching death.
properties.date: Before a death of the Lord of Hungerford

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: This pallid female would travel the building, ending her journey on the balcony where she would disappear.

properties.name: George
properties.description: George has been reported sitting next to patrons during performances - they claim to have felt someone take up position next to them, though the entity remains invisible while seated. George is said to be the theatre manager who hanged himself from the fly floor due to financial difficulties just after the building opened in 1912. A white woman has been seen in the building, but it is generally agreed that these are two different forces, rather than the same ghost.
properties.location_name: Westcliff on Sea (Essex) - Palace Theatre, currently an arts centre

properties.name: Young Woman
properties.description: The phantom figure of a woman in white was seen by a husband and wife as they drove out of Weston. It is thought that the phantom is a girl who drowned on her wedding day.

properties.name: Old Soldiers
properties.description: The spirits here reportedly include voices that call out to witnesses over the nearby River Carey, and horsemen who gallop silently along. People have also reported pockets of cold air, and watching a white woman glide over the marsh, near where her lover was murdered by Royalists. On the anniversary of the battle, sounds of fighting have been heard.

properties.name: Hooded Woman
properties.description: A phantom woman in white, with a hood covering her face, was seen to rush into a hedge. Following this, the sounds of a horse and cart could be heard travelling through woodland, though nothing could be seen.
properties.location_name: Wimborne St Giles (Dorset) - St Giles Estate

properties.name: Tall Man
properties.description: A tall man wearing a black cloak, a woman dressed in white, and a figure resembling a nun are all said to have haunted this property. Two exorcisms failed to remove the entities.
properties.location_name: Winchester (Hampshire) - Private house along Quarry Road

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: The misty white lady is not alone in her haunting of this building. A ghostly figure is also reported to sit by the fireplace smoking a pipe, a little phantom dog by his side.
properties.location_name: Woburn (Bedfordshire) - The Inn (was the Bedford Arms Inn)

properties.name: White Lady Forewarning Death
properties.description: Appearing to warn of trouble or a death in the family, the white lady is said to be a member of the Scamler family who once owned the hall.
properties.location_name: Wolterton (Norfolk) - Wolterton Hall, 3 miles northwest from Blickling

properties.name: White Lady
properties.description: Seen walking around the cellar, this phantom also enjoyed turning the beer pump taps off, sometimes up to twenty times a month. The entity disappeared after structural alterations were made to the building.
properties.location_name: Wolverhampton (West Midlands) - Stag's Head public house

properties.name: Woman in White
properties.description: Roger Froggatt, the landlord of this pub, was left terrified after encountering a phantom lady dressed in white in the ladies' toilet after investigating what he believed was a break in during the early hours of the morning. He described the figure as an elderly woman with her cheek and jaw structure missing.
properties.location_name: Wombwell (South Yorkshire) - Low Valley Arms public house

properties.name: Man in Tricorn Hat
properties.description: A man wearing a cloak and a tricorn hat is said to drift through this wooded area. A phantom woman in white and the sound of wailing have also been reported.

properties.name: Young Lady
properties.description: This woman dressed in white has been observed drifting along the beach, passing effortlessly over hazardous areas of sand. Sir William de Tracey also spends time on the beach, sorting sand as penance for the murder of Thomas Becket. When a storm rolls in and blows his work away, he can be heard screaming.
properties.date: Weather Dependent: Stormy weather (de Tracey), early twentieth century (woman)

properties.name: Bloodied Hands
properties.description: This witness was out with a group of friends, messing around in the cemetery. She turned away from the group and spotted a young girl dressed in white, holding out her hands and mouthing words that could not be heard. The witness held out her own hands, and for a fleeting moment perceived that they were covered in blood.

properties.name: White lady
properties.description: Legend says this lady has scared to death at least one young bell ringer, although she is now said to have a healing effect. Poltergeist activity has also been reported, around the 1950's. A photograph taken in 1975 by Peter Berthelot appears to show a misty white lady sitting behind his wife on a pew.

properties.name: Humming Woman
properties.description: This phantom white woman was seen moving slowly along, humming a tune to herself. The ghost would vanish near to the edge of a cliff.
properties.location_name: Wylfa (Gwynedd) - Area near the power station

properties.name: Legs
properties.description: Seen descending a staircase in the building, only the lower part of this male body appears. Two other spirits can be found in the bar area; that of a workman wearing a bowler hat (dressed in Victorian clothing) and a lady in a white dress who watches the fireplace.
